Name: Wycliffe Bible Translators
Description: Today, 1 in 5 people around the world are still waiting for a Bible in their own language. Wycliffe works with urgency to provide universal access to the Bible, giving all people the chance to read, hear and respond to God's word for themselves.

Name: KLIFE Ministries
Description: KLIFE is a small group discipleship ministry for students all over the country. KLIFE began in 1979 as a follow-up ministry to campers from Kanakuk Kamps. KLIFE is now in 25 cities serving thousands of students.

Name: Free Methodist Church USA
Description:
Free Methodists share a distinct call to scriptural holiness, resulting in a life of sacred service to God and people.

Mission 
Our mission is to love God, love people and make disciples.
 
Vision
Our vision is to bring wholeness to the world through healthy, biblical communities of holy people multiplying disciples, leaders, groups and churches.

Name: Springs Rescue Mission
Description: Springs Rescue Mission is a Christian nonprofit committed to helping men and women overcome homelessness, poverty and addiction by helping each guest regain their health, get back to work and find permanent housing in the community.

Name: Hoffmantown Church
Description: Hoffmantown Church

Name: Johnson Ferry Baptist Church
Description:
We want you to share in our exciting fellowship - a church that is open, friendly, and most of all centered on Jesus Christ. Our beliefs are from the Holy Bible, the inspired, authoritative record of God's plan and purpose for us. We understand these beliefs more fully as we study the life and teaching of Jesus Christ.

Our mission is clear — to love God and worship Him as we reach and disciple people for Jesus Christ.

Name: United Church of God
Description:
The mission of the United Church of God is to proclaim to the world the little-understood gospel taught by Jesus Christ—the good news of the coming Kingdom of God—and to prepare a people for that Kingdom. This message not only offers great hope for all of humanity, but encompasses the purpose of human existence—why we are here and where our world is headed. 

Vision Statement: A Church led by God's Holy Spirit, joined and knit together by what every member supplies, with all doing their share and growing in love to fulfill God's great purpose for humanity to bring many children to glory (Ephesians 4:16; Hebrews 2:10).

Visit our website ucg.org to read any of our publications, including issues of The Beyond Today magazine, dozens of free Bible study aid booklets and the 12 lesson Bible Study Course. Also watch Beyond Today videos and browse through the Bible FAQ.

Name: Immaculate Heart of Mary
Description:
Mission:
We are a welcoming Catholic community centered in the Eucharist, striving to live and share the Good News and grow in discipleship with the Lord.

Value Statement:
• Catholic – We find our identity in rich tradition of the sacraments and in full communion with the universal Roman Catholic Church and its timeless tradition.

• Community – We are the Body of Christ and help each other to grow in our personal relationship with Jesus Christ as a parish community to become God’s presence in this world.

• Sacraments – We celebrate the Eucharist and other sacraments initiated by Jesus Christ and receive God’s grace to live in God’s love.

• Engage/Evangelize – We proclaim God’s love by actively inviting and welcoming everyone to grow spiritually by living a life grounded in God’s sacred Word.

• Servant – We share Jesus’ love and become one with those who suffer and are in need expecting nothing in return.

• Disciples of Jesus – We seek to live as Jesus’ disciples by not accepting the false claims of the media and consumer culture.

Name: Archdiocese of Kansas City in Kansas
Description: The Archdiocese of Kansas City in Kansas serves the Catholic Church across 21 counties in northeast Kansas, with a vibrant community of approximately 200,000 parishioners. To better support this community, eight pastoral regions have been established. The Archdiocese consists of 107 parishes and 36 schools, along with various other entities that contribute to its mission. The Church offices are located in Kansas City, Kansas, where they provide leadership and resources for the entire Archdiocese.

Name: Episcopal Church Foundation
Description: ECF is an independent, lay-led and inclusive organization that helps build, vitalize and transform Episcopal faith communities focusing on formation, finance and resources.

Name: Living Waters
Description: Living Waters exists to inspire and equip Christians to fulfill the Great Commission.

Name: Jesus House
Description:
The church was founded as a church plant of Freedom Hall, a parish of the Redeemed Christian Church of God (RCCG), in Lagos, Nigeria in April 1994, and the progress we have made since those humble beginnings serves as a constant reminder that God can use the seemingly insignificant to make a difference.

Our passion for the growth and development of the individual is reflected in the vision of the Church, and we are committed to making a positive impact in our local and the international community.

Name: Mount Michael Benedictine School
Description:
Mount Michael Benedictine School is a Catholic College-Preparatory residential & day high school. At Mount Michael the core of the academic program enables students to become inquiring learners, appreciative of their heritage, responsive and committed to the challenges of the future. At Mount Michael the monks, lay faculty, and staff instill the value of moral leadership so that they may live in harmony with each other, their families, and the community at large. At Mount Michael the search for God is fundamental to understanding the meaning of life; Christian charity is grounded in our relationships with ourselves,our families, and all whom we meet. As gifts of God, individual talents are to be developed fully and shared generously. The school has grown steadily with an enrollment of just over 100 in the early 1970s to around 250 today highly qualified students. The school has become noted for its family atmosphere, spiritual environment, and high academic standards and expectations. Mount Michael was awarded 2012 National Blue Ribbon School.
http://www.mountmichael.com/Media/MountMichael/School/Admissions/School_Brochure.pdf

Name: Seekin Treasure
Description: This is an official page of seekin Treasure,  we are a follower of Christ Jesus and our mission is to share the Gospel of Christ Jesus, the solid Rock.

Name: Austin Ridge Bible Church
Description:
Austin Ridge Bible Church exists to pursue people at all costs, so they can know Jesus, grow in Him, and serve Him daily. 

We are one church with multiple campuses: one in Bee Cave, one in Dripping Springs, one in southwest Austin, and one coming soon in the Highland Lakes area. Each location offers a unique setting and environment that is part of a larger community committed to pursuing Jesus and loving people.

Join us on a Sunday for our services at 9am and 11am!

Name: Church of God of Prophecy (Official)
Description: The Church of God of Prophecy is a vibrant, worldwide body of believers, united in worship, working hand-in-hand to share God’s love and a message of hope to the broken-hearted. The Church of God of Prophecy has over one-and-a-half million members, worshipping in over 10,000 churches our missions in 125 nations of the world. Nearly 90% of our global membership is outside of North America. Our International Offices building is in Cleveland, Tennessee.

Name: Paloceras
Description: Serving design dreams accompanied with mimosas and sunshine.

Name: The Wilds Christian Camp & Conference Center
Description:
Using the unique benefits of Christian Camping, we serve people by presenting the Truth of God with the love of God so lives can be changed to the glory of God.

The Wilds Christian Association, Inc. operates The Wilds Christian Camp & Conference Center in Brevard, North Carolina and The Wilds of New England at the Deering Center, in Deering, New Hampshire.

Name: Hope Community Church
Description:
Hope Community Church — We are a family who loves God, follows Jesus, and shares Hope. We have two campus locations in Raleigh and Apex, North Carolina where we gather on Sundays, but we are actively involved in the surrounding communities and believe that we're called to much more than a Sunday morning.

Our hope is that our communities and all the world would know and experience the love of God and the life that Jesus came to make available.

At Hope, you’ll find authentic relationships, engaging worship, teaching that is firmly rooted in scripture, and opportunities to make a difference—both locally and globally.

If you're looking for a church home, we invite you to find hope, find purpose, and find family with us.

Join us this weekend—in person or online.
Visit www.gethope.net to learn more about locations, service times, and ways to get connected.

#HopeCommunityChurch #LovesGodFollowsJesusSharesHope #FindHope #ChurchInTheTriangle #JesusIsHope #RaleighChurch #OnlineChurch

Name: Archdiocese of Hartford
Description: The Archdiocese of Hartford comprises the counties of Hartford, Litchfield, and New Haven in Connecticut.  It includes approximately 469,000 Catholics, 300+ priests and 200+ deacons serve 130+ parishes as well as other apostolates. There are 29 Catholic schools in the archdiocese with an enrollment of more than 9,000 students.

Name: Hare Krishna Melbourne
Description:
The International Society for Krishna Consciousness (ISKCON) was founded in New York in 1966 by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada (1896–1977). 

Srila Prabhupada was a distinguished scholar, teacher and practitioner of Vedic religion, philosophy, and culture. He established ISKCON as a means of sharing Vedic culture with the world. He revealed a 5,000-year-old unbroken chain of spiritual teaching originating from the Supreme Lord Himself.

The Hare Krishna Movement fills the spiritual void in the world today. Its aim is to build a society based on the teachings of Lord Krishna, the Supreme Personality of Godhead. We believe that God is a person and that He has a name, His own family, and friends, that He performs transcendentally wonderful activities, and is cherished as the life and soul of His devotees. This Supreme Person is unimaginably beautiful, wealthy, strong, famous, intelligent and at the same time detached from everything. Although He has unlimited names, His original name is Krishna, meaning ‘all-attractive’.

Name: Kolbe Academy
Description: Dedicated to the pursuit of truth, wisdom, and virtue as the primary and prescriptive ends of education in the Catholic tradition, Kolbe Academy aids students in acquiring the skills of the liberal arts, assists in forming classically educated students to develop a mature intellect and cultivates openness to the call to holiness.

Name: Congregation of Sisters of St. Agnes
Description:
The Congregation of Sisters of St. Agnes (CSA) began in 1858, and its Motherhouse is located in Fond du Lac, Wisconsin within the Archdiocese of Milwaukee. There are more than 200 Sisters in the United States and Nicaragua.  Sisters serve in health care, education, pastoral ministry, and social service.

CSA was founded by three people: Father Caspar Rehrl, Mother Agnes Hazotte, and Father Francis Haas, OFM Cap.

#ReligiousLife 
#Nuns
#Sisters
#VowedLife
#CatholicChurch
#Catholic
#Wisconsin
#Vocations
#ComeAndSee
#CatholicNetworking
#PopeFrancis
#Prayer
#SpiritualDirection
#Education

Name: Kensington Community Church
Description: Come and test drive Kensington on Saturday or Sunday. You'll find the people are friendly (but not pushy) and the atmosphere is come-as-you-are casual. Each week's topic is different, but you'll always hear an encouraging message about a God who's crazy about you. Choose from 14 identical one-hour services with simultaneous kids programs birth through fifth grade. We're glad you're here.

Name: Scripture Union England and Wales
Description:
Scripture Union is a Christian charity, inviting children and young people to explore the difference Jesus can make to the challenges and adventures of life. 

Through a wide range of activities and initiatives, we provide opportunities for young people to explore the Bible, respond to Jesus and grow in faith. We also provide some great resources to help others do the same.

We believe every child should have the chance to discover Jesus. And, with an estimated 95% of children in England and Wales not part of a church, we're working harder than ever to take the good news of Jesus beyond the church in exciting and culturally relevant ways.

Scripture Union is a movement – a group of people with a common heart and desire to see Jesus' love shared with today's young people. The movement comprises volunteers, staff, like-minded partners, supporters and many more.

We have a long and rich heritage, having been established 150 years ago. Founded in England way back in 1867, Scripture Union is now a global movement active in over 120 countries.

Name: Faith Church
Description: We exist to Reach people and connect them to God and others.

Name: The Foundation of Jesus Christ
Description:
Make no mistake about it, Christ was never lost so technically we have never “found” Him. 

The truth is He found us because we were lost and like sheep had gone our own way. 

And even today He keeps finding us in places that He never meant for us to be, but still using our waywardness to further the gospel. 

God is amazing like that!

The Foundation of Jesus Christ is the ministry of reconciliation God speaks about in 2 Corinthians 5:18.

He has charged each of us to share our experiences of joy and pain, comfort and anguish, victory and defeat, that God has brought us to and brought us through for our good and His glory.

The logo was inspired by Luke 23:39-43 where Jesus was crucified between two criminals. 

It is a reminder that we can choose to be on the right side of Christ or the wrong side, that our choices either come with blessings or consequences, and that it does not matter where we are when He finds us.

That is why it is important to stay encouraged in Christ. (For our own encouragements go here: https://iampillows.us/collections/spiritual)

It is our prayer that through our experiences God will bring you love, joy, peace, and hope in knowing that He cares for you and has a plan and purpose for you before you leave earth and spend eternity with Him. 

Amen.

Name: Catholic Cemeteries & Funeral Services - Archdiocese of Toronto
Description:
Catholic Cemeteries & Funeral Services is a ministry of the Catholic Church and is operated under the auspices of the Archdiocese of Toronto.

Catholic Cemeteries & Funeral Services administers Holy Cross Catholic Funeral Home, Catholic Cremation Services and the following cemeteries:
Assumption Catholic Cemetery,
Christ the King Catholic Cemetery,
Holy Cross Catholic Cemetery,
Mount Hope Catholic Cemetery,
Queen of Heaven Catholic Cemetery, 
Resurrection Catholic Cemetery,
St. Mary's Catholic Cemetery in Barrie.

Name: Daughters of Charity of St. Vincent DePaul
Description:
The “Company of the Daughters of Charity of Saint Vincent de Paul, Servants of the Poor”
founded by Vincent de Paul and Louise de Marillac is an international community established as a Society of Apostolic Life (see Code of Canon Law cann. 731-755).

Daughters of Charity are called to serve Jesus Christ in the person of those who are poor and marginalized, with a spirit of humility, simplicity and charity.  Motivated by the love of Christ and sustained by a deep prayer life, we live together in community, supporting one another in our common mission of service, service which addresses the person in all human and spiritual dimensions.
 
We live and work in places of social priority:  hospitals, homes for troubled children, schools, day centers for the homeless, those with dependencies or handicapping conditions.  We would like to be available and ready to serve everywhere where there is need and where we can bring help and support, according to our strengths and possibilities, in collaboration with others in the Church and divers groups and organizations.

There are over 17,000 Daughters of Charity living and working in 94 countries throughout the world.

Name: Congregation Rodeph Sholom
Description: Upper West Side Reform Congregation and Day School

Name: OLAM Shrine of the Most Blessed Sacrament
Description: The Shrine of the Most Blessed Sacrament at Our Lady of the Angels Monastery exists for Adoration and the celebration of the Sacred Liturgy. This international pilgrimage site draws hundreds of thousands and serves as a place of rest and renewal of faith in Our Lord, Jesus Christ.

Name: Diocese of San Diego
Description: Roman Catholic jurisdiction encompasing San Diego and Imperial counties in California, USA. Currently there are 98 parishes, six high schools, two universities, forty four elementary schools, two hospitals and several social service agencies which include Catholic Charities Diocese of San Diego and St. Vincent de Paul, AKA Fr. Joe's Villages.

Name: Calvary Chapel Costa Mesa
Description:
Calvary Chapel Costa Mesa is a Christ-centered ministry that endeavoring to be a Jesus formed community on mission. The ministry began with a church located in Costa Mesa, CA and has flourished and expanded into a multifaceted ministry which includes Christian Schools, a Bible College, a School of Worship, Christian Radio Stations, Christian radio programs, Christian book distribution, and Christian bookstores.

Mission + Vision
We are endeavoring to be a Jesus formed community on mission.

Core Values
• Jesus Community
We are committed to following Jesus by being with Jesus, becoming like Jesus,
and doing what Jesus did.

• Biblically Fluent Community
We point people to God through the redemptive story in all of Scripture for the
transformation of lives.

• Spirit-Empowered Community
We pursue the presence, power, and gifts of the Holy Spirit in the life we lead
together.

• Kingdom-Minded Community
We seek to bring the presence of the Kingdom of God to impact our local
communities and beyond.

Name: Junta de Missões Nacionais da CBB
Description: Somos uma agência missionária da Convenção Batista Brasileira. Nossa razão de existir é Multiplicar Discípulos em todo país, transformando vidas e mudando realidades até alcançar todos com o evangelho de Cristo.
Location: Rio de Janeiro, Rio de Janeiro

Name: Trinity Christian Centre
Description:
Founded in Singapore in 1970, Trinity Christian Centre is an Assemblies of God church with three centers in Singapore and four centers in Australia, Indonesia, Thailand, and the United Kingdom. Led by Rev Gerald Tan, Trinity is a church for everyone, offering programs for children, youth and for adults in various languages including English, Chinese, Indonesian, Filipino, Thai and Japanese. 

A church of global impact, Trinity has reached more than 60 nations, partnering churches and humanitarian organizations in different nations to bless the communities they reside in. Our pastors and ministers train and provide consultancy to churches and leaders around the world. 

In Singapore, Trinity is committed to serving the underprivileged in society and established Care Community Services Society which operates as a registered charity offering programs for children, youth, the elderly, families, and ex-offenders. Trinity also empowers and develops the next generation of global leaders through TCA College – an accredited institution of higher learning that offers programs in theology, counseling, and leadership.

In Trinity, we love God passionately, love church zealously, and love souls relentlessly. We are a church where people will encounter God, experience life, and impact the world.

Name: Jüdische Gemeinde Frankfurt
Description: Die Jüdische Gemeinde Frankfurt zählt zu den vier größten Jüdischen Gemeinden Deutschlands. Ihren über 6000 Mitgliedern bietet sie alles, was für das moderne jüdische Leben nötig ist: Sie unterhält ein Gemeindezentrum, in dem sich auch ein koscheres Restaurant befindet, zwei Kindergärten, Krippen, eine Kindertagesstätte, die I. E. Lichtigfeld-Schule, eine eigene Beratungsstelle sowie ein Jugend- und ein Altenzentrum mit Tagespflege und einer Altenwohnanlage. Seit ihrer Neubegründung im Jahr 1948 ist die Jüdische Gemeinde kulturell und gesellschaftlich fest in der Stadt Frankfurt am Main verankert. Seit 1982 finden alljährlich Jüdische Kulturwochen statt, seit 2016 alternierend mit den Jüdischen Filmtagen. Sie werden von der Stadt Frankfurt gefördert und sind bei den Bürgerinnen und Bürgern und Gemeindemitgliedern ebenso beliebt wie die zahlreichen anderen Veranstaltungen der Gemeinde oder anderer jüdischer Organisationen.

Name: Erzbistum Berlin
Description: Wir sind die katholische Kirche in Berlin, Brandenburg und Vorpommern. Zusammen mit unserer Caritas, den Schulen und Kitas, in Pfarreien, Gemeinden, Vereinen und Einrichtungen engagieren wir uns für die Menschen in unserer Region. Wir sind ein verlässlicher und attraktiver Arbeitgeber.

Name: BAPS Mandir Swaminarayan Hindou de Paris
Description:
BAPS Mandir Swaminarayan Hindou de Paris

Le Premier Mandir Hindou Traditionnel en France

Créateur: Mahant Swami Maharaj, Chef Spirituel de @BAPS

Name: Shine Your Light
Description: Whether you are an individual joining with a group of friends and neighbours, a small church, larger gathering, we'd love to hear what you are doing. Don't forget to sign up!

Name: North New Zealand Conference of the Seventh-day Adventist Church
Description: North New Zealand Conference of the Seventh-day Adventist Church

Name: Bread Coffeehouse
Description: Bread Coffeehouse is a place unlike any other on campus… a place where good coffee flows freely, yummy treats are served daily, where residents and staff know your name, where you will be treated with love no matter what you believe, a place that never pushes Jesus on you but a place where the staff believes in serving Jesus by serving you and loving you. We have live bands, great dinners, quiet study times, deep conversations, great story tellers and people who will listen to you. And, we are a lot of fun! Come play ping pong or a board game, write quotes on the wall, swing in our indoor hammock, decorate a book page, play with our play dough or dance in the kitchen.

Name: Barefoot Republic Camp & Retreat Center
Description:
Established in fall of 2000, Barefoot Republic Camp is a non-profit organization with a multicultural ministry that seeks to facilitate the development of Christ-centered relationships among campers and staff from diverse racial, cultural and socio-economic backgrounds, through an equally diverse platform of artistic, athletic and team building programs. Since its inception, Barefoot Republic Camp has reached over 3,000 children across Middle Tennessee and the United States combined. Barefoot is designed to unite a diverse population of youth from the Middle Tennessee area with their parents, the community and the church to help define God’s eternal purpose for each of them. 

Our organization primarily serves four groups:

1) Middle Tennessee youth between the ages of 8-17, over 60% 
     from under-served households. 
 2) Young adults serving as counselors and volunteers
 3) Parents and families
 4) Other organizations including over 40 Middle-Tennessee churches, schools, state shelters and other youth oriented non-profits.

Name: St. Paul Center for Biblical Theology
Description:
The St. Paul Center for Biblical Theology is a non-profit research and educational institute that promotes life-transforming Scripture study in the Catholic tradition. The Center serves clergy and laity, students and scholars, with research and study tools — from books and publications to multimedia and on-line programming.

Our goal is to be a teacher of teachers. We want to raise up a new generation of priests who are fluent in the Bible and lay people who are biblically literate. For us, this means more than helping people to know their way around the Bible. It means equipping them to enter into the heart of the living Word of God and to be transformed and renewed by this encounter.

We read the Bible from the heart of the Church, in light of the Church’s Liturgy and living Tradition. In this way, we hope to help people experience the heart-to-heart encounter that Jesus’ disciples experienced on that first Easter night, when they knew Him in the breaking of the bread: "Did not our hearts burn within us while He talked to us…while He opened to us the Scriptures?" (see Luke 24:13-37)

Name: Greek Orthodox Metropolis of Chicago
Description: Tracing its roots back to 1923, the Greek Orthodox Metropolis of Chicago is the diocese of Greek Orthodox faithful in Illinois, Iowa, Minnesota, Wisconsin as well as northern Indiana and southeastern Missouri consisting of 58 parishes and two monastic communities. The Metropolis is one of nine regions in the Greek Orthodox Archdiocese of America and is presided over by His Eminence Metropolitan Nathanael. The general offices of the Metropolis are temporarily located in Elk Grove Village, Illinois. For more information, visit www.chicago.goarch.org.

Name: Walking by Faith Missions Church
Description: Walking By Faith Missions is a small missionary church which believes that ministering should be more than just words, it should be about actions. This is why we travel fulltime to show people what a Christ-centered lifestyle is all about. WBFM focuses on evangelism and discipleship as the cornerstones to develop a grounded sense of Christian community everywhere the Lord has us travel to. WBFM helps the local Christian community by developing customized personal discipleship programs to cultivate a deeper understanding and relationship with Jesus Christ. WBFM also partners up with local Christians to help them develop home churches to make a positive impact in their communities.

Name: The MET Church
Description: Be a part of a community that lifts you up, that accepts you, that believes in you, and that loves you. Join us and experience how the power of truth, transparency, faith, and love can transform your life. We invite you into a meaningful relationship with God and each other.

Name: Christ's Hope
Description: Christ's Hope is part of the global Body of Christ, discipling and caring for orphans and vulnerable children in Africa. Together, we are supporting their education, nurturing their physical and emotional well-being, keeping them in the care of their families and communities and, most importantly, teaching them the Gospel. Join us in empowering these children to break the cycles of extreme poverty!

Name: Reflective Spaces Ministry, Corp
Description: Our mission is to provide reflective spaces for transformational healing and total restoration in a faith-filled environment for survivors of human trafficking, sexual violence, domestic violence, and childhood adversity to thrive.

Name: Bistum Limburg
Description:
Die Geschichte des Bistums Limburg beginnt mit dem Zusammenbruch der weltlichen und kirchlichen Machtstrukturen in Europa als Folge der Französischen Revolution. Durch den Reichsdeputationshauptschluss im Jahre 1803, dem letzten Gesetz des Heiligen Römischen Reiches, werden Gebiete der alten geistlichen Kurfürstentümer Mainz und Trier den nassauischen Herzogtümern zugeschlagen, darunter das Gebiet um Limburg, das bis dahin den rechtsrheinischen Teil des Trierer Bistums darstellt. Das Herzogtum Nassau mit Regierungssitz in Wiesbaden initiiert zusammen mit der freien Reichsstadt Frankfurt die Errichtung eines eigenen Bistums.
Am 23. November 1827 ist es soweit: Das Bistum wird gegründet und der erste Bischof, Jakob Brand, nimmt seine Amtsgeschäfte auf. Das Bistum hat zum damaligen Zeitpunkt 134 Pfarreien mit 175.000 Katholiken. Die ehemalige Stiftskirche St. Georg, deren eigene Geschichte bis in das 10. Jahrhundert zurück reicht, wird zur Kathedrale, zur Kirche des Bischofs von Limburg.
Im Gebiet des Bistums leben heute rund 2,4 Millionen Menschen, davon sind etwa 580.000 Katholikinnen und Katholiken.

Name: Christ Fellowship
Description:
Helping you and your family follow Jesus. 
One church with many locations.
Join us on-campus or online.

Name: Thirdmill | Biblical Education. For the World. For Free.
Description:
Third Millennium Ministries (IIIM) is an Evangelical Christian parachurch ministry.
Our goal is to provide Christian education to hundreds of thousands of pastors around the world who lack sufficient training for ministry. We are meeting this goal by publishing and globally distributing a free multilingual, multimedia, digital seminary curriculum in English, Arabic, Chinese (Mandarin), Russian and Spanish. The curriculum is designed to be used in support of existing schools, as well as by groups and individuals.
(From thirdmill.org)

Name: Colégio Vicentino São José
Description: Há 78 anos oportunizando aprendizagens que transformam, valores que significam e vivências que aproximam. Educação Vicentina: um projeto de vida!

Name: Kanakuk Institute
Description: The Kanakuk Institute was founded in 2002 to provide a place for young men and women to intensively study the Word of God and experience life-change through biblical community and the power of the gospel. The Institute provides the applicable essentials of seminary in a way that prepares students to live a Christ-exalting life in any occupation. Over 1000 Alumni have entered the business world, medical field, local ministries, and world missions. Offering MBA and M.Div transfer credit, the Institute is a great way to prepare for your future both spiritually and professionally.

Name: Islamic Institute of Toronto
Description: Established in 1996, the Islamic Institute of Toronto is a premier Islamic Educational Institution, located in Toronto Canada offering post-secondary courses in Arabic Language, Islamic Jurisprudence, Ethics and Spirituality, Islamic History and Civilization, Comparative Religion and Civic Engagement among others.

Name: Pontifical College Josephinum
Description: Pontifical College Josephinum is a religious institution company based out of OH, United States. Motto: "Si Deus pro nobis, Quis contra nos? If God is for us, who can be against us?” Rom 8:31

Name: Sisters of Charity of the Incarnate Word
Description:
The Sisters of Charity of the Incarnate Word originated in Lyons, France, and was established in Galveston (1866), then moved to San Antonio (1869) by its founder, Claude Marie Dubuis, bishop of Galveston, whose diocese at that time comprised the entire state of Texas.
We are a Congregation of women religious of different ages, with diverse gifts and talents. Our mission is to actualize the saving and healing love of Jesus, the Incarnate Word.

Name: Lifeshape
Description: Lifeshape is a non-profit organization that exists to glorify God by opening doors for marketplace professionals to life-changing experiences by serving those in need around the world. We provide service opportunities that engage marketplace professionals, alleviate risks for vulnerable children, and make disciples.

Name: Acts2 Network
Description: Launching lifelong kingdom workers from every college town.

Name: Follower of One - Missions Reimagined
Description:
Where workplace ministers go for inspiration, ideas, and encouragement. We want to help you launch your own workplace ministry. Join Jesus as He makes Himself known in your workplace. You can lay up treasure in heaven every day while you're at work. 

We're a US non-profit energized to equip and mobilize full-time ministers in the workplace. We work to develop a missionary's mindset and live like a full-time minister in our family, our workplace, and our community. Join our community at https://followerofone.org.

Name: Chosen People Ministries
Description:
Chosen People Ministries exists to pray for, evangelize, disciple, and serve Jewish people everywhere and to help fellow believers do the same. The mission was founded in Brooklyn, New York in 1894 by Rabbi Leopold Cohn, a Hungarian Jewish immigrant with a zeal to share the knowledge of Yeshua (Jesus) the Messiah with God's chosen people.

Today, Chosen People Ministries serves in 20 countries across the globe. Our outreach programs include evangelism and discipleship, Messianic Centers and congregations, equipping the local church for Jewish evangelism, print and web publications, and benevolence work. With your help, we will continue proclaiming the Good News through Jesus the Messiah to Jewish people around the world.

Name: Anglican Church Southern Queensland
Description:
The Diocese includes an area of more than 500,000km2 and stretches from the borders of NT, SA & NSW  and extends north to Bundaberg.

Serving the estimated 750,000 Anglicans in the Diocese are 467 clergy in 305 churches. The clergy form an integral part of the faith community and includes Archdeacons, Canons, Priests, Deacons and lay people serving in various ministries. 

The Most Reverend Archbishop Dr Phillip Aspinall leads the organisation and also is the Primate of the Anglican Church of Australia. Dr Aspinall is assisted in the Church by The Right Reverend Cameron Venables, Bishop of the Western Region; The Right Reverend Jeremy Greaves, Bishop of the Northern Region; and The Right Reverend Alison Taylor, Bishop of the Southern Region. 

With the aim of the whole church engaged in the whole mission, the organisation provides much needed services for the community through its six departments: Anglican Schools Office; Anglicare Southern Queensland; Corporate Services; Financial Services; Ministry Education; and Local Church Mission.

Through their committed workers and volunteers the commissions provide ministry, outreach and care to Anglicans and the wider community. From parishes and schools to residential care facilities, community care and social service programs to theological education and financial services each commission undertakes a significant part of the Church’s work.

Name: Protestantse Kerk Amsterdam
Description:
De Protestantse Kerk Amsterdam is een gemeenschap van mensen die het christelijk geloof delen en van daaruit een bijdrage aan de samenleving leveren. Daarbij wil ze zoveel mogelijk mensen uit de stad betrekken.

Onze roeping: Gods liefde gestalte geven in de stad.

Information in English
The Protestant Church of Amsterdam is a diverse community of people who share the Christian belief and through this contribute to society. They want to involve many people in the city.

Mission: The Protestant Church of Amsterdam feels called to make manifest Gods love in this city.

Name: USPG - United Society Partners in the Gospel
Description: USPG is an Anglican mission agency that partners with the global Church in God’s mission to enliven faith, strengthen relationships, unlock potential and champion justice.

Name: Amate House
Description: Amate House is a year of intensive leadership development in Chicago. Our fellows are young adults ages 21-29 who commit to living in intentional community and serving under-resourced communities in Chicago through full time community service. Fellows are asked to engage thoughtfully throughout their experience with the Amate House tenets of community, social justice, stewardship, faith, and service. With a network of over fifty Chicago area non-profits, Amate House works diligently to place fellows in professional jobs geared toward their skills and interests, including positions at legal aid centers, low-income community health clinics, inner-city schools, parishes, and social service organizations.

Name: Catholic Christian Outreach
Description: Catholic Christian Outreach (CCO) is a university student movement dedicated to evangelization. We challenge students to live in the fullness of the Catholic faith with a strong emphasis on becoming leaders in the renewal of the world.

Name: Diocese of San Bernardino
Description:
The Diocese of San Bernardino works in partnership with 93 parishes in Riverside and San Bernardino counties of California to provide for the spiritual welfare of more than 1.6 million Roman Catholic faithful.

We work always to bring the Good News of Jesus Christ to all persons and all places in this vast and diverse diocese.

Name: Shiloh Baptist Church
Description:
Sunday Worship Service at 9:00am
SIBLE/Sunday School at 11:00am
Prayer Warriors Tuesdays at 6:00pm
Bible Study Tuesdays at 7:00pm and Wednesdays at 11:00am
The Lord's Kitchen Wednesdays and Thursdasy from 4:00pm - 6:00pm
Fitness Classes Mondays, Wednesdays & Thursdays from 7:00pm - 8:00m
Martial Arts Classes Saturdays 8:30am - 11:00am
Uplift Recover Meetings Fridays at 7:00pm

Name: Catholic Charities, Diocese of Pittsburgh
Description: Inspired by Jesus’ teachings and focused on lasting solutions, we serve our communities’ critical needs with compassion, healing and hope.

Name: Every Nation Philippines
Description: Every Nation is a worldwide family of churches and ministries that exists to honor God by establishing Christ-centered, Spirit-empowered, socially responsible churches and campus ministries in every nation.

Name: Forest Home
Description:
Forest Home’s Mission

Encounter Jesus. Experience Transformation. Engage the World.

75 Years of HIS-Story

Forest Home has a rich and moving history. It is sometimes said that our history is HIS-Story, and we believe that to be true. Forest Home has become a movement of God that has seen the winds of revival blow throughout Southern California and into the farthest reaches of the world.

Since 1938, we have watched Him move in the lives of countless millions as each guest has been faced with life-changing decisions. Our chapel is filled with books–literally thousands of pages of personal history where guests write out their decisions, prayers, and cries to God.

Name: Congregation Rodeph Sholom
Description: Upper West Side Reform Congregation and Day School

Name: Royal Mission
Description:
Setting forward Gods Kingdom through words, works and miracles.

"We are passionate to proclaim the Kingdom of God and to train others to do the same!"

Name: Servant Partners
Description:
- We seek Jesus together by living and serving among the world's urban poor. 
- We send, equip, and train those who want to share their lives with the urban poor and see urban slums transformed.
- We create churches that transform their urban poor communities.

Name: Christ In Youth
Description:
For more than 50 years, Christ In Youth (CIY) has called students and leaders to boldly live for Christ through the hope of the Gospel message and a call to Kingdom Work. By amplifying the call of Christ on student lives, three generations of servants, workers, missionaries and church leaders have responded to that call and populated the harvest field.

Today, CIY works in partnership with the local church, providing more than 100 annual program events for students and leaders across the United States and in more than ten foreign countries. CIY believes God has uniquely and specifically equipped every student for Kingdom Work. In response to His call, students can actively serve in the harvest fields of their families, schools, churches and communities- not someday- but TODAY.

Within the next decade, more than one million students and leaders will be impacted worldwide through CIY's programs, curriculum, and resources in an effort to turn the millennial generation into a movement of Kingdom Workers, responding in faith and in service to the call of Christ.

Name: Blended Kingdom Families
Description: Blended Kingdom Families exists to break the generational cycle of divorce, equip marriages and unite blended families with the truth of God’s Word.

Name: Sugar Creek Baptist Church
Description: Sugar Creek Baptist Church is a community of people who love God.  We gather on Sundays and many other times during the week to express our love for Him, learn to love like Him, and serve Him to the best of our ability. We are passionate about seeing everyone experience this life change because we know the good that comes from it:  hope is given, relationships are healed, families are strengthened, and purpose is found.  Our lives have been forever changed and we are consumed by a collective mission:  to love and lead all people to life change in Christ.  We currently have church campuses in Sugar Land and Missouri City, Texas but the vision God has given us will extend far beyond to many groups in many locations.  Whomever you are, wherever you’re from, whatever your past, we invite you to join us.  You will be loved, encouraged and empowered as we follow Christ together.

Name: North Coast Church
Description:
Making Disciples in a Healthy Church Environment

We are a Bible teaching church and would love to help you grow.  We offer many worship options on several campuses, each with a different worship style and ambiance.  Each weekend, regardless of campus or service time you choose, you will hear the same message presented by one of the pastors on our teaching team.

Being taught the Word of God on a regular basis is essential to growing in your Christian walk. You can find out more about our beliefs by reading our Statement of Faith.

Name: Divine Renovation Ministry
Description:
Parishes around the world are emptying, aging, sleeping, dying. It doesn’t need to be this way. Divine Renovation points a way forward. We don’t have all the answers but we believe we have many of the right questions. And we’ve experienced first-hand what it’s like to be a missional parish. Divine Renovation was birthed out of a real experience of renewal. Fr. James Mallon along with the team at Saint Benedict Parish in Halifax, Canada transformed their parish into a vibrant community of missionary disciples. Pastors, priests and leaders from around the world took notice. The Divine Renovation ministry with its events, books, coaching, and resources is a response to the incredible hunger for renewal. The ministry desires to inspire and equip every leader out there so that they can move their parish from maintenance to mission.


Over the past several years we’ve seen firsthand the transformations in parishes across the world as they move from maintenance to mission. Parishes of all shapes, sizes, and makeups are making missionary disciples through this work. We exist to help parishes make these transformative steps forward.

Name: Perimeter Church
Description: Perimeter Church is a community of people being transformed by the power of the gospel of Jesus. Founded in 1977 on the challenge to "Attempt something so great for God that it is doomed to failure, lest He be in it."

Name: Deutsche Kapuzinerprovinz
Description:
Die Gemeinschaft der Kapuziner ist ein katholischer Männerorden. Der Name des Orden lautet: Orden der Minderen Brüder Kapuziner, lateinisch Ordo Fratrum Minorum Capucinorum (OFMCap).

In der deutschen Kapuzinerprovinz sind die Ordensleute aus Deutschland, Belgien, Niederlande und West-Österreich organisiert. An der Spitze der Provinz steht ein auf Zeit gewählter Provinzial, zur Zeit ist dies Br. Helmut Rakowski. Die Verwaltung der Provinz ist in München. 

Der Name "Kapuziner" leitet sich von der markanten Kapuze des Habits ab. Die Kapuziner sind ein franziskanischer Orden und bilden neben den Minoriten und den Franziskanern einen der drei großen Zweige des ersten Ordens des Heiligen Franziskus. Die Kapuziner entstanden zu Beginn des 16. Jahrhunderts aus Reformbestrebungen innerhalb der franziskanischen Orden. Sie leben nach der Regel des Heiligen Franziskus.

Hier wollen wir, die Kapuziner in Deutschland, über unseren Orden, Franz von Assisi sowie unser Leben und Engagement in Kirche und Gesellschaft informieren - und mit Ihnen in den Austausch treten! 

Weitere Infos und das Impressum finden Sie auf kapuziner.org.

Name: Renaissance Unity
Description:
Renaissance Unity is a center for spiritual learning where everyone is welcome! Our community reflects diversity in every way, including sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, gender, age and socioeconomic status, and is exemplified in our leadership.

When engaging with our community, no matter the struggle or depth of faith, we provide an affirming environment that fosters positive self-actualization, personal responsibility, with unconditional love and acceptance. We encourage and nurture a sense of belonging and a recognition of wholeness.

Our gatherings are relaxed and comfortable, which is consistent with our desire to live transparently with no facades. However, we are intense about seeking, learning and applying spiritual practices for everyday living in alignment with Spirit.

Name: Menlo Church
Description:
Menlo Church has a rich history that dates back to 1873, when thirteen men and women founded Menlo Park Presbyterian Church. Now, it is a multi-campus community committed to thoughtful, Jesus-centered faith. Wherever you are on your journey of faith, and whatever questions you have about God, we hope to be a place where you can find grace and growth. We believe that all people matter to God, life is better in community, and that each of us is meant to bring God’s goodness to the world around us. 

Menlo Church also prioritizes serving the community, both as a way to give to our neighbors and as a way to develop spiritually. To that end, the church holds multiple Serve Your City events each year that touch the lives of thousands of neighbors. In addition, Menlo Church annually gives millions of dollars of financial support to local and global organizations, with a focus on justice and restoration, women and children in crisis, and urgent response to crises, to name a few. 

The church has campuses in Menlo Park, San Mateo, Mountain View, Saratoga, and San Jose. 

Our vision is to lead our generation into a transforming relationship with Jesus and authentic community with each other so that everyone in the Bay Area and beyond can flourish.

“Therefore go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit, and teaching them to obey everything I have commanded you.” Matthew 28:19-20

“By this everyone will know that you are my disciples, if you love one another.” John 13:35

“Jesus went throughout Galilee, teaching in their synagogues, proclaiming the good news of the kingdom, and healing every disease and sickness among the people.” Matthew 4:23 



Join us!

Name: Live Bible Verses
Description:
Description
Live Bible Verses -  We upload live Verses of the Living Bible.  These are pre-recorded audio tapes sync to our 3D Avatar Characters to make the living word more fun and interesting.  We will be live streaming Chapters and Verses from both the New and Old Testaments.

Name: University Presbyterian Church
Description: Founded in 1908 near the University of Washington in Seattle, we gather for traditional and contemporary worship on Sundays and in smaller communities and groups throughout the week.

Name: Business as Mission (BAM) Nederland
Description:
De wereld mooier en eerlijker maken middels je onderneming. Dat wil een groeiend aantal ondernemende mensen: op een sociale en duurzame manier bezig zijn. Met Business as Mission (BAM) komt daar een dimensie bij: een verlangen om je door God te laten inspireren en je bedrijf te zien als middel om Gods Koninkrijk zichtbaar te maken. Oftewel: sustainable +!

Als BAM NL brengen we ondernemers, maar ook mensen uit de kerk- en zendingswereld bij elkaar die zich hierin herkennen. We delen verhalen en ervaringen; we inspireren, bemoedigen en activeren elkaar. Samen leren we, want er is niet één ware formule voor Business as Mission. Je bedrijf kan op vele manieren een BAM-bedrijf zijn en soms ben je al meer `BAM’ dan je denkt!

Name: Living Faith Church
Description:
Our church exists SOLELY to connect people with Jesus and in turn connect ourselves to one another.  We invite you to experience the genuine worship, preaching, friendly people, and enjoyable atmosphere that are a part of our community.

We are people who struggle and hurt as we face the everyday pressures of life, but we are also people who are experiencing the joy of Jesus Christ in our daily lives.  We would like nothing more than to have you join us as we seek, learn, and grow together in our spiritual walks with God.  Whether you're seeking God, curious about our fellowship, or needing a place to come and be ministered to, there's something for you at our church.  We invite you to come and be as comfortable as you'd like in your attire when you visit us this Sunday.

Name: Alive to God
Description:
My name is Andrew Roebert (pronounced Ray-bert). I live in the city of Pretoria, South Africa, together with my two daughters, Caitlin and Amy. For three generations now, my family have been serving God in various capacities. I was born in Pretoria, South Africa in November of 1966. My father, Pastor Ed Roebert was the pastor of the Hatfield Christian Church in Pretoria until his death in 1997.

This church was not a 'normal' church, but it was a place where we discovered that God was alive and that He loved us and wanted us to know His presence in our lives as a living reality. This involved the active pursuit of the Holy Spirit and as a result many people were drawn to what was happening. Being brought up in this environment and with a Godly heritage, has been something special to me, and out of this background came a desire within me for more of God.

I currently serve as CEO of Impact Radio in Pretoria, South Africa. I host a regular radio programme on this station and write articles for certain Christian magazines.

THE IDEA BEHIND IT ALL
This ministry was started by my late wife Vanessa and I to help those individuals who would love to experience more of God. The Bible tells us that we can be 'Alive to God'. For many Christians this has been an element that they have failed to enjoy. Enjoying God and His presence in our lives is something that we can 'know' and experience in our lives.

Name: Al Tijaarat Al Raabehah
Description:
Under the guidance of al Duat al Mutlaqeen, the Dawoodi Bohra Community has experienced immeasurable economic prosperity across the world. The 52nd Dai al Mutlaq, Syedna Mohammed BurhanuddinRA, for over five decades, encouraged the community to establish trade and industry and be active participants in the development of local economies while adhering firmly to tenants of shariat mohammediyah. His Holiness’ message to the community to be business-minded has led the community to build profitable and sustainable businesses throughout India and Asia but to also branch out to develop opportunities in East Africa, Europe, and North America. Also, the community has diversified into many different industries and sectors ranging from the traditional trade of hardware, design and manufacturing of cutting edge technology, to providing services in the IT and Education.

Successor to the august office of Dai al Mutlaq, the 53rd incumbent, Syedna Mufaddal SaifuddinTUS continues his esteemed father’s legacy and see the community grow to even greater heights. In a time of increasing competitiveness and globalization, His Holiness teaches businessmen of the community that the key to growth and prosperity is to adhere the tenants of the Islamic faith, be ethical in all dealings, and work together in brotherhood with each other and with the global community towards achieving collective prosperity.

The office of al-Tijaarat al-Raabehah (under the auspices of Idaarah Hasanaat al-Qard al-Hasan al-Burhaniyah) aims to carry the vision and efforts of the 53rd Dai al Mutlaq to each and every member of the Dawoodi Bohra Community and foster sustainable growth, profitability, and prosperity for the business community.

Name: Christian Church (Disciples of Christ)
Description: The Christian Church (Disciples of Christ) in the US and Canada is a movement for wholeness in a fragmented world. As part of the one body of Christ we welcome all to the Lord’s Table as God has welcomed us. Our vision is to be a faithful, growing church, that demonstrates true community, deep Christian spirituality and a passion for justice.

Name: Kalamazoo Gospel Ministries
Description:
Homeless shelter, offering meals, shelter & recovery, work training, and discipleship tracks from a Christian perspective.
Our support ministries are:
Rescued Treasures & Simple Treasures Thrift shops - donated household items are sold and proceeds support the Kalamazoo Gospel Ministries with includes Kalamazoo Gospel Mission & Kalamazoo Deacons Conference.

Name: Maryknoll Fathers and Brothers
Description:
The Maryknoll Fathers and Brothers live amongst the poor and others in need in some of the most poverty stricken areas/regions in the Asia, Africa, Latin, South, and Central America as well as the United States.

All Catholics are called to mission through Baptism and Confirmation, and Maryknoll’s mission education outreach in parishes and schools throughout the country, engages U.S. Catholics in mission through vocations, prayer, donations, and as volunteers.
Pope Francis challenges the Catholic Church to create a culture of mission, in which all Christians are called to be “Missionary Disciples” — sharing compassion and God’s love with the poor and marginalized.  We invite and enable others to answer this call.  

Are you considering a Vocation?  
We are a community of Catholic missionaries serving overseas in more than 20 different countries of the world. We feed the hungry, heal the sick, and reach out to outcasts across many cultures, languages and traditions. If you are considering Life as a Maryknoller, click this link for more information - https://maryknollsociety.org/vocations/

To Help Us continue making a difference for those in need - https://goo.gl/a4CAGi

To keep up to date with Maryknoll, follow us on: 
Facebook - MaryknollSociety
Twitter - MaryknollFrsBrs
Instagram - MaryknollSociety
YouTube - MaryknollMR

Name: Global Disciples Canada
Description:
Global Disciples Canada is a Christian mission organization that trains local leaders living near least-reached communities to multiply disciples for Christ. 

One-third of our world hasn’t heard the Good News of Jesus. Yet. 

Global Disciples refers to these as “least-reached” people, and fewer than 10% of all missionaries work among these groups. We live in a time where many of these people are within reach of a local church. 

Through our simple and effective strategy of training and coaching, believers share the Gospel in their own nations and cultures. Jesus said, “Go and make disciples of all nations,” We’re committed to doing just that.

If you want a Christian mission organization to partner with to become a better disciple and help make disciples, connect with us today!

Name: Bishop Watterson High School
Description: Bishop Watterson, the first co-educational diocesan high school in Franklin County when it opened in 1954, seeks to challenge and nurture leaders for the 21st century by developing the religious, moral, intellectual, social and physical skills that they will need to achieve their fullest potential as productive and successful adults. Bishop Watterson endeavors to educate the whole person by focusing on the spiritual, intellectual and physical development of the students. The goal is to produce self-disciplined young men and women who have a commitment to essential Gospel values. The school's college preparatory curriculum offers more than 160 courses.

Name: Operation Mobilization USA
Description:
Operation Mobilization (OM), USA is a global organization working with 6,800 missionaries in 118 nations to build communities of Christ followers, provide humanitarian assistance and demonstrate compassion.
 
Though the Gospel has been with us for nearly 2,000 years, 2.8 billion people have never experienced its message of love and hope. In many cases, these same people are being crushed by unspeakable poverty, horrific injustice and cruel social and political systems.
 
With nearly six decades making inroads into the farthest corners of the world combined with a pioneering 21st century approach, OM is uniquely equipped to address these issues. Whether it’s rescuing enslaved women in Asia, assisting refugees in Europe, or developing bodies of believers in the the Middle East, we pinpoint ways to strengthen local communities and use the opportunities God presents us along the way to share The Bible’s life-giving message.
 
When you partner with OM USA, through your professional work, financial generosity, and/or prayer, you become an important member of a worldwide movement with eternal significance. With operations in nearly every region of the world, the sun never sets on our operations, meaning you will be part of a team serving the needy and spreading the Word of God every minute of every day. Few other organizations allow you to see your time and talent reach so far or impact so many.

Name: Winchester Cathedral
Description:
Winchester Cathedral is an inspiring space. We welcome thousands of people to the Cathedral a year to take part in a huge range of activities, including services and ceremonies, educational visits, guided tours, local festivals and a host of cultural events.

Like all major heritage attractions, there is an awful lot going on behind the scenes. Our community of clergy, staff and volunteers support the Cathedral’s mission, preserve and maintain the fabric of the building, and provide an excellent experience for visitors.

We employ around 80 permanent staff, offer seasonal vacancies for Christmas, and run a comprehensive apprenticeship programme. In addition to our paid workforce, the Cathedral is supported by around 700 volunteers. 

There can’t be many better locations to work. Situated in the heart of historic Winchester, the Cathedral is within easy reach of shops and places to eat, and has its own on-site Refectory. The city itself is beautiful and bustling, with excellent road and rail links.

We offer a benefits package, and actively invest in staff training and development. 

For job opportunities and current vacancies please visit the Cathedral website.

Charity No. 1202915

Name: Impact 360 Institute

Description:
Finde einen Beruf bei uns, der dich erfüllt. Wir brauchen starke Persönlichkeiten, die gemeinsam mit uns das Wir gestalten. Unsere aktuellen Stellenausschreibungen und Möglichkeiten des Einstiegs, Umstiegs, Wiedereinstiegs findest du hier und auf unserer Website.

In der Erzdiözese München und Freising oder einer ihrer Kirchenstiftungen gestalten rund 18.000 Mitarbeitende das Wir in den Feldern Seelsorge, Bildung/Beratung/Begleitung und Verwaltung.

Damit ist die katholische Kirche eine der größten und vielseitigsten Arbeitgeberinnen in der Region Oberbayern. 

Die zentrale Verwaltungsbehörde des Erzbistums ist das Erzbischöfliche Ordinariat München. Als kirchliche Verwaltungseinrichtung mit Aufsichts- und Servicefunktion ist das Ordinariat verantwortlich für die Betreuung und Unterstützung von 747 Pfarreien, 25 Erzbischöflichen Schulen, rund 400 Kindertagesstätten und zahlreichen weiteren kirchlichen Einrichtungen.

De IZB staat voor zending in Nederland. Het is ons verlangen dat mensen die Christus nog niet kennen, Hem vinden, samen met zijn gemeente Hem navolgen, en zo in rijke vreugde gaan delen.

Wij geloven dat zending Gods eigen werk is. Hij heeft zijn Zoon gezonden naar de wereld, en zijn  Geest gestuurd om de evangelieverkondiging voort te stuwen. In die beweging worden we als organisatie meegenomen; wij hopen eraan bij te dragen dat gemeenten en gemeenteleden zo gestalte geven aan hun geloof in Jezus Christus, dat mensen tot Hem worden getrokken.

Hoe we ons werk in een tijd van secularisatie moeten vormgeven is allerminst duidelijk. De zuigkracht van deze ontwikkelingen gaat namelijk ook ons niet voorbij. We worden uitgedaagd op nieuwe manieren onszelf en daarmee ons hele leven dagelijks af te stemmen op Jezus Christus.
Dat is de rode draad in het werk van onze organisatie.

Description:
World Changers Church International (WCCI), headquartered in Metro Atlanta, Georgia, is a non-profit Christian organization which is blanketing the world with the Gospel of Jesus Christ to meet the needs of mankind. Almost 500 highly skilled employees staff the facility of World Changers Church International, both at home and in our satellite offices in the United Kingdom, the Republic of South Africa, Australia and India; along with several United States locations. Through the leadership of our founders and pastors, Dr. Creflo Dollar and Taffi Dollar, we empower our staff members by helping them to realize their spiritual, personal and career objectives.

Name: Breslev Israel - ברסלב ישראל
Description:
Breslev Israel, the world’s leading Jewish website #Emuna focused – Real spiritual guidance from a Jewish point of view that is Universal, Soulful, Real Love and Life Changing towards #Effective #Happiness!

This is also the story about a small but most unique Yeshiva (and Kollel), ‘Chut Shel Chessed’ (a ‘Thread of Kindness’), whose main goal is to help Jews grow closer to the Torah and to G-D by teaching them the wisdom and beauty of Torah and by being a friendly ear and a guiding hand.
Patience, kindness, love, the strengthening of character and encouraging, spiritual support and often times even physical support are all synonyms with the name 'Chut Shel Chesed'.
In addition to learning the Torah and enhancing their minds spiritually, the students of 'Chut Shel Chesed' have taken it upon themselves to encourage other Jews to follow in the uplifted paths that have enriched the students’ lives with ultimate joy and fulfillment until this day.

Rav Shalom Arush our head! A special man with a noble soul, gentle and pleasant, whose eyes twinkle with kindness and sympathy, Rabbi Arush is well known for his big heart, overwhelmed by a genuine love for Am Yisrael. 

His incomparable sense of patience and tremendous empathy for people and the sufferings they endure penetrate the hearts of all those who encounter him (especially the hearts of his students) and one cannot help but sincerely love this man almost from the first minute they hear him speak. Full of reverence of Heaven, this beloved man spends his life serving G-D and helping his fellow Jews and beyond any way he can with the utmost humility and humbleness. 

There is a great mitzvah of giving charity and donating money to organizations that help people and bring them close to Hashem. The students of Chut Shel Chessed along with many other generous people spend much of their time distributing classes of Rabbi Arush’s lessons along with books of our Rebbe, zt”l, & thus have managed to strengthen the people! B'H!

Name: American Scientific Affiliation
Description:
American Scientific Affiliation, or ASA, was founded in 1941 as an international network of Christians in the sciences. As scientists, members of the ASA take part in humanity’s exploration of nature, its laws, and how it works. 

The ASA is not an advocacy organization. Where there is honest disagreement on an aspect of science, Christian faith, or the relationship between the two, the ASA strives to create a safe environment in which dialogue can flourish and diverse, even contrasting, ideas can be discussed with courtesy and respect.

Możliwe do wykonania przeze mnie czynności to:

- analizy rynku nieruchomości z wykorzystaniem regresji wielorakiej, w której tworzy się funkcję opisującą cenę ofertową oraz wyznacza czynniki mające na nią wpływ;

Name: San Francisco Zen Center
Description:
San Francisco Zen Center was established in 1962 by Shunryu Suzuki Roshi (1904-1971) and his American students. Suzuki Roshi is known to countless readers as the author of the modern spiritual classic, Zen Mind, Beginner's Mind.

The purpose of San Francisco Zen Center is to make accessible and embody the wisdom and compassion of the Buddha as expressed in the Soto Zen tradition established by Dogen Zenji in 13th-century Japan and conveyed to us by Suzuki Roshi and other Buddhist teachers. Our practice flows from the insight that all beings are Buddha, and that sitting in meditation is itself the realization of Buddha nature, or enlightenment.

Today, San Francisco Zen Center is one of the largest Buddhist sanghas outside Asia. It has three practice places: City Center, in the vibrant heart of San Francisco; Green Gulch Farm, whose organic fields meet the ocean in Marin County; and Tassajara Zen Mountain Center—the first Zen training monastery in the West—in the Ventana Wilderness inland from Big Sur. These three complementary practice centers offer daily meditation, regular monastic retreats and practice periods, classes, lectures, and workshops.

Zen Center is a practice place for a diverse population of students, visitors, lay people, priests, and monks guided by teachers who follow in Suzuki Roshi's style of warm hand and heart to warm hand and heart. All are welcome.

Zen Center programs also reach out to the community, helping prisoners, the homeless, and those in recovery; protecting the environment; and working for peace. See our outreach program for details. Suzuki Roshi's disciples and students of his disciples now lead dharma groups around the country.

Name: Self-Realization Fellowship
Description:
Self-Realization Fellowship is the non-profit and non-sectarian religious society founded by Paramahansa Yogananda in 1920 to disseminate his Kriya Yoga teachings and help people worldwide to realize their highest potential and deepest joy. 

Paramahansa Yogananda (1893 – 1952) is widely regarded as one of the preeminent spiritual figures of our time. Born in northern India, he came to the United States in 1920, where for more than thirty years he taught India’s ancient science of meditation, the art of balanced living, and the underlying unity of all great religions.  

Yogananda’s universal teachings are available through his acclaimed life story, "Autobiography of a Yogi," and his numerous other books, which have introduced millions to the perennial wisdom of the East. According to his express wishes, Self-Realization Fellowship publishes the complete works of Paramahansa Yogananda. 

And for those sincere seekers who want to learn the time-honored methods of meditation he taught for attaining direct realization of one’s true Self — the soul — Yogananda created his "Self-Realization Fellowship Lessons," an in-depth home-study series. The Lessons contain personal and comprehensive guidance and instruction from Yogananda on the techniques of yoga meditation he brought to the West — including the science of Kriya Yoga — and the principles of balanced spiritual living for well-being in body, mind, and soul. 

“Yoga is that science by which the soul gains mastery over the instruments of body and mind and uses them to attain Self-realization — the reawakened consciousness of its transcendent, immortal nature, one with Spirit.”  — Paramahansa Yogananda 

Today, Self-Realization Fellowship has grown to include more than 600 temples and centers around the world and has members in over 175 countries. In India and surrounding countries, Paramahansa Yogananda's work is known as Yogoda Satsanga Society of India (YSS), which has more than 200 centers, retreats, and ashrams.

Name: Rede Século 21
Description:
A Rede Século 21 é uma rede de telecomunicação católica com conteúdos religiosos e educativos, que oferece formação, informação, entretenimento e espiritualidade, 24 horas por dia, com uma programação bem variada para seus diversos públicos.
Foi inaugurada em 1999, como TV Século 21. Em 2013 foi criada a Rede Século 21, beneficiando milhares de pessoas. 

Localizada em Valinhos, região metropolitana de Campinas/SP, a emissora dispõe de cinco estúdios para produção de programas ao vivo, gravados, educação a distância, shows e dramaturgia.
A tecnologia é atual e integrada a modernos e potentes equipamentos e transmissores de última geração interligados aos sistemas operacionais que oferecem uma gestão dinâmica e atualizada.

 
COBERTURA CANAL ABERTO, TV A CABO E PARABÓLICA
A Rede Século 21 está presente em 12 estados e 144 municípios entregando conteúdo através de parabólicas, canal aberto e operadoras de TV, a mais de 38 milhões de pessoas.
Através das retransmissoras e 2 recepções via satélite, 25 milhões de lares em todo o território nacional recebem a programação.

Conheça um pouco mais de nossa história:

Description: For more than 50 years, Faith Comes By Hearing has been committed to providing all people with free access to the pure Word of God in their own language. Name: Saint Meinrad Seminary and School of Theology
Description:
Saint Meinrad Seminary and School of Theology forms priests, deacons, and lay people for service in the Roman Catholic Church throughout the United States and internationally.

The Seminary and School of Theology offers graduate-level degrees in philosophy and theology. Priesthood candidates work toward a Master's in Catholic Philosophical Studies or Master of Divinity. Graduate theology students can earn a Master of Arts (Theology) or a certificate in a specialized area of study.

Saint Meinrad also has:
-- a Permanent Deacon Formation Program, which provides dioceses with deacon formation for their candidates.
-- the Institute for Priests and Presbyterates, which offers continuing formation for priests in various stages of their ministry.
-- the Office of Continuing Formation, which offers opportunities for spiritual formation and academic learning.
-- the "One Bread, One Cup" program, a summer youth liturgical leadership program for high school youth and their adult youth ministers.
