Name: Redeemed Pillar of Fire Europe
Description:
Welcome to the Redeemed Pillar of Fire Church (RPF Ministry) - a bible believing church that is built on the matchless love of Jesus Christ. 

RPF is a church that believes in Jesus, a church that loves God and His people. The ministry is overwhelmed by the gift of deliverance and healing - found in Jesus Christ. 

RPF has a heart for spirit-filled worship, praise, fellowship & teachings and we engage in prayers in accordance to the biblical word of God. 

The ministry is led by God's servant, Senior Pastor Augustine E-Ben. 

Our Headquarters is located in Archway, however we also have branches and fellowships across the UK and Central Europe

Name: Pope John Paul the Great Catholic High School
Description:
Our Mission Statement and Philosophy

Rooted in the Dominican tradition of prayer, study, community and service, Pope John Paul the Great Catholic High School is committed to the formation of youth with special emphasis on the ethical development of the students.  We strive to provide an environment where students can grow in the image of Jesus Christ while developing intellectual, theological, and moral virtues.

In order to carry out this mission, the faculty and staff are committed to:
- Providing an environment in which the profound dignity of every student is affirmed, thus enabling them to grow in the knowledge of their worth as creatures made in God’s image and invited to share divine life with Him.
- Providing an environment in which the Catholic faith is vibrant so that students can grow in their relationship with Jesus Christ and are motivated to take an ever more active role in his Church.
- Cooperating with the parents and families of our students, recognizing them as the primary educators of their children.

Name: Novo Mission
Description: Novo is a band of creative missionaries sent to multiply movements of the gospel and mobilize the church for that mission around the world. Novo was previously known as CRM Empowering Leaders.

Name: New Hope Christian Fellowship
Description:
NHCF is a bible-believing, Christ-revering, non-denominational body of believers. 

WHAT WE BELIEVE
Mission Statement: “To do things Jesus’ way, by Jesus’ teaching, and for Jesus’ glory.”

Life Verse: “Bear one another’s burdens and thus fulfill the law of Christ.” (Galatians 6:2)

Philosophy of Ministry: New Hope Christian Fellowship is an independent body of believers, who have found comfort in biblical love, fellowship and spiritual unity. 

We practice New Testament Christianity in adherence to the Word of God, in faith, in obedience, and servanthood. 

We are dedicated to that which blesses the Lord Jesus Christ and lifts up His commandments, especially to love one another.

Name: Center For Radically Inclusive Judaism
Description: The Center for Radically Inclusive Judaism advocates for attitudes, policies, and practices that engage interfaith families in Jewish life and community. We send a monthly email newsletter re-capping media discussions on inclusion; write for the Forward, JTA, eJewishPhilanthropy, and other outlets on inclusion issues; conduct surveys on inclusion policies and practices (to date, among Reform, Reconstructionist, and Emerging Spiritual communities); lobby for inclusion initiatives (to date, for revocation of seminary admission policies that bar rabbinic students in interfaith relationships, and to set full inclusion as the goal of the Reform movement; and speak and consult on inclusion issues.

Name: Todos Ao Cubo
Description:
TODOS TODOS TODOS dá Todos Ao Cubo 

"Na Igreja, há lugar para todos. «Padre, mas para mim que sou um desgraçado, que sou uma desgraçada, também há lugar?» Há espaço para todos! Todos juntos… Peço a cada um que, na própria língua, repita comigo: «Todos, todos, todos». Não se ouve; outra vez! «Todos, todos, todos». E esta é a Igreja, a Mãe de todos. Há lugar para todos."

Name: Comunidade da Graça
Description:
"Uma igreja-familia, vivendo o amor de Cristo, Alcançando o próximo e formando discípulos."


UMA IGREJA FAMÍLIA

A igreja sede começou em 1979, quando o sonho de uma igreja-família nasceu no coração do Pastor Carlos Alberto de Quadros Bezerra. Ele, Suely e seus filhos, ao lado de seus companheiros de ministério, começaram a sonhar com uma igreja onde as pessoas pudessem viver como numa verdadeira família.
Hoje estabelecida na zona leste da cidade de São Paulo, a igreja-sede conta com mais de 6.000 membros em seus diversos cultos públicos. No lugar onde antigamente funcionava uma fundição existe, hoje, um belo auditório e edifícios anexos para, confortavelmente, acomodar crianças e adultos, tanto nas reuniões de fim de semana, como nas escolas e cursos de treinamento.
Com o crescimento da igreja-sede, muitas outras igrejas foram estabelecidas em várias cidades brasileiras. Também surgiram várias atividades e ministérios ligados ao trabalho da Igreja. Nossa mais nova sede é a Grace Community International Church, no Japão. 

"Ide, portanto, fazei discípulos de todas as nações." Mateus 28:19

Description:
Bridging cultures through conversations.
 Learn, ask questions, and gain insights into Islam.
For USA and beyond.

Name: The Well Community Church
Description:
In 2001, a group of college students and a 29-year-old pastor got together in a living room to talk about what church ought to be. We wrote on 3x5 cards what we thought was important and what we thought wasn’t. Looking back, we were crafting the philosophy of The Well in that room. And the verse that kept hounding us was Acts 2:42.

We became convinced that the best way to accomplish the Great Commission was one conversation at a time, and that the Christian life was not meant to be lived alone. Thousands of years of tradition have piled up on top of what the early church found so important. We have made our simple religion complicated and we wanted to go back to some of that early simplicity.

The other part of that verse is an unwavering commitment to Biblical teaching. We choose to teach through Scripture chunk by chunk. By walking through Scripture in order, the way God inspired it to be written, we can’t skip topics. Some think that hearing about issues like predestination and homosexuality will drive people away from the faith. Our experience has shown the exact opposite. When those who have dark and painful pasts come and hear a message filled with hope and love and grace, and when they look down the row and see others crying because of the scars of their past and know they are not alone, God moves. We teach the hard topics because they need to be taught, because it keeps us in right relation to our holy God and to each other.

Name: Church Initiative
Description:
Church Initiative is a nondenominational, nonprofit ministry, equipping churches for ministry to people facing the challenges of divorce, grief and single-parenting. Church Initiative provides a network of support group programs, which are found in more than 20,000 churches across the nation, Canada, and worldwide. 

Support group programs include:

- DivorceCare, www.divorcecare.org, a small group resource for those in separation/divorce 
- DivorceCare for Kids, www.DC4K.org, for children of divorce
- GriefShare, www.griefshare.org, helping people grieving a death
- Single & Parenting, www.singleandparenting.org, a care group resource to help and support single parents

Find a Christ-centered support group in your area by visiting one of our sites or calling 800-395-5755. If your church would like to start its own program, visit our sites or call the above number.

Name: Orthodox Christian Coaching
Description: We work side by side with clergy and ministry leaders to foster health in the communities they serve through professional executive coaching and leadership development.

Name: Shepherd's Grove | Hour of Power with Bobby Schuller
Description:
Shepherd’s Grove is a community of happy and whole students of Jesus. We’ve chosen to be a radically hospitable faith community focused on spiritual formation with God, our community with each other and our missional calling to the world we live in. We believe that God’s kingdom is flourishing and thriving, growing and bearing fruit. It’s our mission to join God in what he is already doing in the world he created with such love and care.

We would love to meet you and give you the opportunity to find out what Shepherd's Grove is all about! Check out our website, social media, or visit us on a Sunday to become a part of our family!

Name: Diocese of Salford
Description:
The Roman Catholic Diocese of Salford covers much of Greater Manchester north of the Mersey and Lancashire round Accrington, Blackburn, Burnley and Longridge. 

The diocese main objectives are the Promotion of the Catholic Faith, Education, Charity and Worship.

Name: Emmanuel Baptist Church
Description: We're a progressive group of worshippers seeking to glorify Our King by spreading unadulterated "Truth" (as He is the Person of Christ, cf. Jn.14:6) to all that will heed. We know prosperity as knowing, then doing the will of Holy God. Like the Bereans of Thessalonica (cf. Ac.17:11), we implore every congregant to be fervent in personal spiritual development.

Name: North Heights Church
Description:
Find faith. Discover purpose. Live eternal significance.

North Heights is one Holy Spirit-filled, Bible-believing, Christ-centered church with two locations: Arden Hills and Roseville, MN with a growing online campus. Whether you are young or young at heart, we will help you take your next step in faith. Join us on a life-changing adventure to know Jesus and make Him known through the power of the Holy Spirit.

North Heights Vision: Led by the Holy Spirit, North Heights equips all people to find faith in Jesus, discover their God-given purpose, and lead lives of eternal significance.

Name: Abyssinian Baptist Church
Description: The mission of Abyssinian Baptist Church is to win more souls for Christ through evangelism, pastoral care, Christian education, social service delivery, and community development. Our history conveys a rich legacy of leadership and faith, our activist commitment to serving the needs of the local and global community and a dedication to our mission of winning more souls for Christ.

Name: Leading Saints
Description: Leading Saints is dedicated to helping Latter-day Saints be better prepared to lead at home, in their careers, and in their church callings.

Name: Rolling Hills Community Church
Description: Rolling Hills Community Church exists to bring glory to God by reaching people for Christ and nurturing them in the faith through inspired worship, genuine community and passionate ministry that transforms lives in our neighborhoods and throughout the world.

Name: Spoken Worldwide
Description:
2/3 of the world’s population needs information to be spoken. Yet most of what is being offered is written. Literacy shouldn’t be a requirement to learn and receive life-saving Truth.

We focus 100% on "oral culture" people.  We serve them in a holistic manner as Jesus did -  helping them physically AND spiritually by getting practical health/water/agricultural trainings AND the Gospel message to them.

Oral recordings are carefully made in their rare languages so that they can know God and become a multiplying disciples. 

To find out more about how you can help "DELIVER TRUTH...WHERE WRITTEN WORDS CAN'T GO" please contact us at info@spoken.org.

Name: Cancer Companions
Description: Cancer Companions is a nonprofit which equips, trains and supports caring church volunteers to provide safe, supportive environments for cancer families to draw closer to Christ.  This is dome through volunteer led Christian cancer support groups and/or one to one meetings.  we impact people in treatment, post treatment and their loved ones.  An extensive training program is provided through both web based seminars and live trainings.  In order to find our locations around the country, go to http://www.cancer-companions.org/find-a-cancer-companion/

Name: Triumph Church
Description:
Triumph Church is a Christian megachurch located in Detroit, Michigan, USA. It was founded in the Fall of 1920 by Reverend Claude Cummings as the Triumph Missionary Baptist Church. Reverend Solomon W. Kinloch, Jr. is currently the Senior Pastor. The church has eight weekend and two midweek services.

More notably, the church is recognized for its exponential growth. According to Outreach Magazine, it is the #1 FASTEST GROWING church in the United States from 2012-2013, with more than 4,000 new members formalizing a commitment to the church during the past calendar years. With a weekly attendance exceeding 10,000 people, Outreach Magazine also named Triumph the #34 LARGEST OVERALL church in America.

Name: Ely Cathedral
Description:
Thousands of visitors come to Ely Cathedral each year, from all over the world. Few are ever disappointed, for there is something here for everyone. Lovers of architecture will rejoice in the many different styles, blending to make the whole. History abounds around every corner, and the beauty created by artists in wood, stone and glass is everywhere.

• Christian Place of Worship - the ongoing pattern of Morning Prayer, Holy Communion and Evensong is at the heart of life at the Cathedral.

• Learning -  a unique educational resource. Around ten thousand school children visit the Cathedral every year. For young people, we offer a variety of experiences and activities that support and enrich the National Curriculum

• Centre of Musical excellence - offered first and foremost as an expression of worship. Since the days of King Canute (c. 1000) music has played an important role in the on-going life of the Cathedral.

• Established filming and recording venue favoured by producers, artists, and film and TV companies alike.

• Corporate Dining/Private Hire - the combination of majestic architecture, sensitive lighting, sound and music, produces a unique occasion in an unforgettable location.

• Hospitality -  offered here since medieval times. Today, the tradition is continued at our 12c Almonry restaurant, where food of the highest quality is served, using fresh, local produce.

Name: Church of God in Christ, Urban Initiatives
Description: non profit

Name: Fielder Church
Description: Fielder Church is a bilingual, multiethnic, multisite church in Arlington, Grand Prairie & Mansfield, TX. Led by Lead Pastor Jason Paredes, this church has a 2026 vision to become a people who are radically generous, invest to send, pursue imperfect people, expand by planting, and celebrate differences. This is praying church, a sending church, and a diverse church that is seeking to advance the kingdom of God.

Name: Northside Baptist Church
Description: Independent Baptist Church

Name: Den Danske Kirke i Udlandet
Description:
”KOM HJEM UDE”. Vi er dansk kirke og kulturhus mere end 40 steder verden over. Danskerne rejser ud på kortere og længere ophold i forbindelse med kærlighed, arbejde, studier eller eventyrlyst. Vores præster og assistenter giver dem tryghed og kirkelig betjening, når de er langt væk.

Vi har for tiden 42 udsendte præster og assistenter til primært europæiske byer. Ud over Europa har vi også medarbejdere i New York, Hong Kong, Australien, Canada og Singapore.

Name: San Juan Diego Catholic High School
Description: San Juan Diego Catholic High School provides highly motivated students the opportunity to earn a Catholic, college-preparatory education enriched by participation in a corporate internship program.

Name: Catholic Diocese of Syracuse
Description:
The mission of the Roman Catholic Diocese of Syracuse is Evangelization:

To live our faith and freely share our faith with others.
To invite all people to hear the message of Jesus Christ as expressed in the Catholic parish.
To foster Gospel values in our society so that our nation and the world will be transformed by the saving power of Jesus. 

The Roman Catholic Diocese of Syracuse includes 237,546 Catholics residing in seven counties of Central and South Central New York State. The seven counties are Broome, Chenango, Cortland, Madison, Oneida, Onondaga and Oswego. The Roman Catholic Diocese of Syracuse is served by Bishop Robert J. Cunningham.

Name: Myers Park Presbyterian Church
Description:
Myers Park Presbyterian Church, based in Charlotte N.C., is a vibrant engaged community of faith. A community ready to foster your spiritual growth and enrich your faith life. A community ready to equip you with the heart and mind of Christ so you can serve alongside us at home, throughout our nation, and around the world. A community where every member has a ministry. 

For nearly a century, Myers Park Presbyterian has been home to people thoughtfully exploring their faith. Today it is an inclusive, spirit-filled church that just might defy your expectations. Those who are looking for a Sunday-only congregation are surprised to discover diverse opportunities offered on a daily basis. 

Here we are united as believers serving Christ; the greater the challenge, the better. Here each of us, as disciples and students of Jesus Christ, are learning, growing and keeping our hands in what has been referred to as the “mud of ministry”. Here Christianity is not merely academic—it is experiential.

Name: The Legionaries of Christ
Description:
The Legionaries of Christ are driven by the desire of Jesus’ heart to set the world ablaze, the religious priests of the Legionaries of Christ support each other and work in communion with other Regnum Christi members in their mission to form apostles, people who let the love of Christ the King transform their hearts, their families and the world.

The Legionaries of Christ are a Roman Catholic religious congregation of priests and seminarians, and part of the Regnum Christi Federation. Regnum Christi (Latin for “Kingdom of Christ”) is a Federation (within the Catholic Church) comprised of four vocations: Legionaries of Christ (the priestly congregation), the Consecrated Women of Regnum Christi, Lay Consecrated Men of Regnum Christi and the Lay Members of Regnum Christi. Regnum Christi has a Christ-Centered spirituality that is lived together in communion
with all members (all four vocations), in union with the whole Church, and through a mission to build the kingdom of Christ. Regnum Christi is made up of over 25,000 members throughout 38 countries worldwide.

The Legionaries of Christ live in communities that are made up of different nationalities, and make vows of poverty, chastity and obedience. The formation of a Legionary is a deep and transformative process that spans an average of 14 years, leading up to his priestly ordination. He is formed to be both contemplative and apostolic.

Legionaries go out as missionaries in today’s world. With great charity, joy and zeal, they invite people into the adventure of living for Christ and transforming the culture around them. Each year they impact thousands of lives through youth work, education, parishes, missions, the sacraments, and spiritual direction.

Name: Presbyterian Church In America
Description: As one communion in the worldwide church, the Presbyterian Church in America exists to glorify God by extending the kingdom of Jesus Christ over all individual lives through all areas of society and in all nations and cultures. To accomplish this end the PCA aims to fill the world with churches that are continually growing in vital worship, in theological depth, in true fellowship, in assertive evangelism and in deeds of compassion.

Name: Aish UK
Description:
For the past 25 years Aish UK has aimed to inspire a deeper connection to Judaism and to ensure a vibrant future for the Jewish people. Through diverse educational opportunities, immersive experiences and enduring relationships we share the wisdom and beauty of Jewish faith, practice and community – connecting with as many young Jews as we can, whatever their background, from when they are about to leave home until they build their own.

Today, Aish UK operates eight full time branches and provides educational opportunities at over 18 schools, 15 universities and 6 young professional hubs around the country, reaching and
impacting thousands of young Jews each year. We also regularly distribute educational and video content through magazine publications, online and through our social media channels.

Name: Vox Church
Description: Vox Church is a multi-site non-denominational Christian church. Led by Lead Pastor Justin Kendrick, Vox Church currently has 12 locations across CT and MA. Our mission is to spread the lifestyle of authentic Christianity for the glory of God and the transformation of our cities.

Name: The World Race
Description: The World Race is a stretching journey to serve “the least of these” while amongst real and raw community. This unique mission trip is a challenging adventure for young adults to abandon worldly possessions and a traditional lifestyle in exchange for an understanding that it's not about you; it's about the Kingdom.

Name: Christ Church IL
Description: Christ Church IL is a non-denominational, multisite church. We began as a gathering of 17 people in a home in Lake Forest, in 1980. Nine years later, we moved into our Lake Forest campus. In 2011, we partnered with the former Evangelical Church of Highland Park to create a second campus. Then in 2014, we partnered with Crossroads Church of Grayslake to add our third campus, Christ Church Crossroads.  And we’re still growing. Still committed to giving glory to God, loving people, studying the Bible and serving our communities.

Name: Inter-Religious Organisation, Singapore
Description:
The Inter-Religious Organisation, Singapore (IRO) was founded in 1949.  

The IRO’s objective is to promote inter-religious harmony among the various religious groups in Singapore. 

Ten (10) religions are represented in the IRO; Hindu, Jewish, Zoroastrian, Buddhist, Taoist, Jain, Christian, Islam, Sikh and Baha’i. A unique rotating annual presidency among the ten religions ensures that all member religions are given an equal opportunity to lead the IRO’s long-term efforts.

IRO’s activities include inter-faith prayers and blessings at launch ceremonies of public and private institutions in Singapore. It also engages with various religious communities in its efforts to further religious harmony in Singapore. 

IRO engages in ongoing dialogue with other similar global and local organisations (such as World Council on Religion and Peace (WCRP), the Asian Council on Religion and Peace (ACRP), Inter-Racial and Religious Confidence Circles (IRCC)) in the pursuit of fostering a collaborative approach towards inter-religious harmony.

IRO has published several books and pamphlets including the book “Religions in Singapore” and “Religious Customs and practices in Singapore”.

Name: Resonate Global Mission
Description:
Resonate Global Mission is here to equip the Christian Reformed Church to reach out and join God in what He is doing around the world—so that the good news of Jesus Christ goes out in every direction like an expanding, amplifying sound.  Together with you, we train missional leaders, guide new churches, and forge partnerships to proclaim and live out the good news of Jesus Christ worldwide.  

We are called to go “to the ends of the earth,” but the first step could be right outside your front door. Resonate Global Mission can help you discover how—and where—you can join God on mission.

What do we do?
Resonate will continue the good work of Home Missions and World Missions. For more than a century, these two agencies have been introducing people to Christ in North America and around the world. That means continuing our partnership with churches in the United States and Canada to plant churches, do campus ministry, train and equip pastors, and develop future leaders. Additionally, it means sending missionaries overseas, proclaiming the Gospel, and forging lasting partnerships.

In addition to this ongoing work, we also are tackling new opportunities for ministry. Here in North America, we need experience in international ministry to help churches effectively reach across cultural barriers and forge partnerships within new communities of faith. In other nations, we need expertise in church planting and campus ministry to help people in every nation discover God’s plan for their church, community, and life. With your prayers and financial support, as a single agency we will be able to equip all our churches for ministry and join together with what God is already doing around the world.

Name: Silverdale Baptist Academy
Description: Silverdale Baptist Academy is a preschool through 12th grade non-denominational Christian school committed to partnering with parents to raise children of outstanding character.

Name: Lichfield Cathedral
Description: Welcome to Lichfield Cathedral! We are one of the oldest places of Christian worship in Britain with over 1300 years of history in the only medieval three-spired Cathedral in the UK.

Name: Sisters of Saint Joseph of the Sacred Heart
Description:
The Sisters of Saint Joseph of the Sacred Heart are women who continue God’s mission by immersing themselves in the midst of life to empower others for life and bring hope. "I have come that you may have life… Jn 10:10"

The Congregation of the Sisters of Saint Joseph of the Sacred Heart (also known as Josephites) is a religious Congregation which was founded in Penola, South Australia in 1866, by an Australian woman, Mary MacKillop, and an English priest, Reverend Julian E Tenison Woods. The Sisters were a uniquely Australian group of women, who had been drawn from all levels of colonial society to meet a wide variety of needs. These included the education of children in outback areas, and the provision of homes for orphaned children, care for unmarried mothers and shelter for aged and homeless people.

The Sisters of Saint Joseph were significantly different from other groups of nuns living in Australia during the late nineteenth century. They went out in small groups of two or three to country areas where there was often no established Catholic Church structure. They continue to live among the people, sharing their daily lives and responding to today’s needs.

Today there are over 600 Sisters of Saint Joseph living in communities in Australia, New Zealand, Ireland, Peru, East Timor, Scotland and Brazil. These Sisters are engaged in pastoral work as well as in professional roles  which include teaching, administration, social services, law, psychology, nursing, aged care, community development, adult education and support for new arrivals in Australia and New Zealand.

Name: Hope Lutheran Church
Description:
Hope Lutheran is one congregation serving multiple locations unified in mission. This unity multiplies Hope Lutheran’s efforts to reach out to our community and our world with the good news of Jesus Christ.

Our mission is to encourage all people to know the love of Christ.

Name: Frelsens Hær
Description:
Frelsens Hær/The Salvation Army helps people in need through a wide variety of spiritual and humanitarian programs and is active in 129 countries. In Denmark our services are as wide-ranging and diverse as the areas of need in the community. 

The Salvation Army is a Christian movement dedicated to sharing the love of Jesus.  
We share the love of Jesus by: 
Caring for people
Creating faith pathway
Building healthy communities
Working for justice 

The Salvation Army, an international movement, is an evangelical part of the universal Christian church. Its message is based on the Bible. Its ministry is motivated by the love of God. Its mission is to preach the gospel of Jesus Christ and to meet human needs in His name without discrimination.

Name: PKWP
Description: PKWP (Pomoc Kościołowi w Potrzebie) is a Polish section of "Kirche In Not" - "Aid to the Church in Need" - an international pastoral aid organization of the Catholic Church, which yearly offers financial support to between 5 000 and 6 000 projects worldwide.

Name: ፈውስ መንፈሳዊ ጣና ቅ/ቂርቆስ ፬ መጻሕፍተ ጉባኤ ሚዲያ Fewus Menfesawi
Description: ይህ ፈውስ መንፈሳዊ የተሰኘ መንፈሳዊ ጉባኤ ሲሆን በርእሰ ገዳማት ወአድባራት ጣና ቅ/ቂርቆስ ገዳም የ ፬ መጻሕፍተ ጉባኤ ቤት ምሥክር መምህር በሆኑት በ ርእሰ ሊቃውንት የኔታ አባ ገብረኪዳን ግርማ የሚሰጡ ትምህርቶች ይተላለፉበታል።

Name: All Souls Church, Langham Place
Description:
All Souls Church is an Anglican Evangelical church in central London, situated in Marylebone at the north end of Regent Street, next to BBC Broadcasting House. 

As well as the core church membership, many hundreds of visitors come to All Souls, bringing the average number of those coming through the doors for Services on Sundays to around 2,500 every week. All Souls has an international congregation, with all ages represented.

Name: St. Joseph Catholic Church
Description: In 1989, the new church built in 1927 was deemed unusable for parish use and Bishop Gracida authorized demolition of the building on January 23, 1989. The parish hall and the small Queen of Peace Chapel were utilized for regularly scheduled masses. A fundraising campaign for the construction of a new church building through Catholic Sharing Appeal began February, 1991. Construction began August, 1991 and formal dedication of the new church building was October 3, 1992. Bishop Rene H. Gracida concelebrated the dedication service to the seven hundred and sixty nine registered parish families. The pastor at St. Joseph Catholic Church is Fr. Patrick Donohoe.

Name: Nur Al Nisa
Description:
Nural Nisa (The Light of the women) is a community-based initiative founded in 2025, dedicated to reviving hearts through Qur’anic knowledge and spiritual growth especially for women.
With a focus on Islamic literacy and identity building including faith, round table discussions, prayer, hijab, modesty, women in Islamic history, and the Qur’an cycle we also empower Muslim women through digital and tech skills, build confidence and life skills in young Muslim girls, offer counselling support for personal growth, and create leadership opportunities for women in the community.

Our mission is simple: to empower Muslim women and girls especially within refugee and host communities by providing faith-based guidance, life skills, and safe spaces to grow, connect, and lead. Whether you are seeking to strengthen your faith, gain valuable skills, or contribute to positive change, Nural Nisa is a place to grow spiritually, intellectually, and personally.

Name: Taizé Community
Description: Today, the Taizé Community is made up of over a hundred brothers, Catholics and from various Protestant backgrounds, coming from around thirty nations. By its very existence, the community is a “parable of community” that wants its life to be a sign of reconciliation between divided Christians and between separated peoples.

Name: دار الإفتاء المصرية | Egyp's Dar Al-Ifta
Description: دار الإفتاء المصرية أنشئت في عام 1895م/ 1313هـ، تعد في طليعة المؤسسات الإسلامية التي تتحدث بلسان الدين الإسلامي في جمهورية مصر العربية وتدعم البحث الفقهي بين المشتغلين به في كل بلدان العالم الإسلامي، حيث تقو بدورها التاريخي والحضاري من خلال وصل المسلمين المعاصرين بأصول دينهم وتوضيح معالم الإسلام وإزالة ما التبس من أحوال دينهم ودنياهم كاشفةً عن أحكام الإسلام في كل ما استجد على الحياة المعاصرة.

Name: Temple Israel of West Bloomfield
Description:
Our mission at Temple Israel is to create an inclusive center for our sacred community, framed through the lens of Reform Judaism. We are authentic and caring, fearless and dynamic, home to many different types of individuals and families searching for connection and meaning in their lives.  

Throughout the Covid-19 pandemic, we have re-visioned what it means to be a part of something greater than ourselves, shifting to creative virtual programming, innovative and intimate life cycle moments, and providing much needed communal support with expanded social services and medical care.  We will emerge stronger and closer than ever, ready to take on the challenges that the new world brings. We look forward to seeing you soon.

Name: Grace Chapel
Description:
Non-denominational Christian Church Located in Historic Lexington, MA.  

Our Mission: Transforming Lives with the Surprising Message of God's Grace.               
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         .

Name: Palmer Theological Seminary of Eastern University
Description: Palmer Theological Seminary is a company based out of 1300 Eagle Rd, St Davids, PA, United States.

Name: Roswell United Methodist Church
Description: A Methodist congregation of 6,500 located in Historic Roswell, Georgia.

Name: Paradisus Dei
Description: Paradisus Dei builds stronger marriages and families by helping individuals, couples and youth understand and fulfill their holy calling, putting them on a lifelong pathway to holiness. We do this through life-transforming programming for men, couples, and youth.

Name: Grace in Action
Description:
We partner with ministry leaders to provide leadership support, team development, and tools to serve your unique ministry setting. 
Your Partner in Moving Ministry Forward,
Grace in Action

Name: Sisters of Charity of Leavenworth
Description: Sisters of Charity of Leavenworth is a Roman Catholic congregation of women religious. Established in 1858 in Kansas, the Sisters of Charity live their mission in the spirit of their founders - Saint Vincent de Paul, Saint Louise de Marillac, and Mother Xavier Ross. The Sisters, as Gospel-centered women, share a tradition of responding to the needs of the time, ministering to the vulnerable and marginalized and caring for the fragile Earth. They share their mission in partnership and collaboration with those committed to working for peace and justice.

Name: Pleasant Valley Baptist Church
Description:
Our vision at Pleasant Valley Baptist Church (PV) is to unleash the transforming influence of Jesus at PV, the Northland and beyond. 

We unashamedly saturate everything with the Gospel.
John 3:16-18; Romans 1:16-17; 2 Corinthians 4:5-6

We consistently challenge people to take their “next step”.
Philippians 1:6; Philippians 3:12-14; 2 Peter 1:5-8

We boldly live to give ourselves away (practicing generosity).
Acts 4:32-35; 2 Corinthians 9:6-7; Philippians 2:3-7

We purposefully build biblical community for life transformation.
Acts 2:42-47; 1 Corinthians 12:12-14; 1 Thessalonians 2:8

We unapologetically invest in strengthening the family.
Deuteronomy 6:4-9; Nehemiah 4:14; Proverbs 24:3-4; Matthew 19:3-6

We passionately strive to reproduce ourselves.
Matthew 28:18-20; Acts 13:1-31; 1 Corinthians 11:1

Name: CAMA Zending
Description:
Nog steeds hebben 3 miljard mensen niet of nauwelijks van Jezus gehoord. CAMA Zending maakt Jezus zichtbaar waar Hij niet gezien wordt, door zendelingen uit te zenden en lokale gemeentestichters te ondersteunen.
CAMA Zending heeft de missie om zendingswerkers uit te zenden onder de minst bereikte volken. Het doel van zendingswerk is om lokale gemeenten te stichten of ondersteunen. Zowel gemeentewerkers als beroepsprofessionals worden uitgezonden om deze missie te volbrengen. CAMA Zending zendt zendelingen uit in samenwerking met uitzendende gemeenten en kerken in Nederland.

Het meest kostbare voor CAMA Zending zijn haar zendingswerkers. Daarom doen we er alles aan om hen zo goed mogelijk te ondersteunen. Op deze manier kunnen zij wereldwijd tot de grootst mogelijke betekenis zijn voor Gods Koninkrijk.

Name: Vineyard Community Church
Description: A creative, growing church on the northeast side of Indianapolis, IN passionate about people experiencing the love of God, and taking it out into the world. Apart of the Vineyard USA movement, which is a non-denominational Christian community of churches.

Name: Jerusalem Seminary - סמינר ירושלים
Description:
ֿVision: Making ministry training in the Land of the Bible essential and accessible.
Mission: The Jerusalem Seminary serves to equip and empower ministry leaders of the global Church — both Christians and Jews — to deepen faith and grow in Biblical knowledge in the Land of the Bible and through our Hebraic heritage.  As Micah 4:2 says, "Instruction will go forth from Jerusalem," the "center of the nations" (Ezekiel 5:5).  

Jerusalem Seminary has three schools that offer Bible-based, Jesus-centered, Spirit-empowered and Hebraic-oriented courses within a context of faith-based community and practice. 

Graduate School: provides short-term accredited courses (from Hebrew University of Jerusalem) and semester courses starting in January 2021 that will have three tracks: semester certificates, year-long diplomas and three year M.Div degrees. 

School of Hebrew: Inaugurated in 2018, JS’ School of Hebrew, also known as “Hebrew for the Nations” has trained 25 Israelis in Biblical Hebrew and Pedagogy, enabling them to serve the nationals globally in training Bible Translators and seminarians in Biblical Hebrew and Culture.

School of the Bible: provides educational opportunities beyond the tradition classroom for anyone through JS’s online video channel and rigorous non-accredited study-tours through the Jerusalem Biblical Studies Society (JBSS); both which seek to rejuvenate ministry and faith and extend broadly the vision of Jerusalem Seminary.

Anyone going into any kind of ministry is welcome to come and be transformed in and from Jerusalem, the "center of the world"!

Name: York Catholic High School
Description:
York Catholic Middle & High School is located in York, Pennsylvania, USA, and operated by the Roman Catholic Diocese of Harrisburg for boys and girls in the 7th through 12th grades.

York Catholic bases both its curriculum and extra-curricular activities on the Gospel values of Jesus as articulated in the Roman Catholic tradition. The school strives to provide a safe, family-oriented environment that will encourage sound academics and respect for self and others. York Catholic encourages and challenges all students to develop a mature, faith-filled and moral vision of themselves and their world by nurturing the spiritual, social, intellectual, emotional and physical needs.

Name: C-Suite for Christ Washington, DC, DMV
Description:
Our mission is to cover the world in Christ by unapologetically declaring to the world whose we are by proactively creating faith-centric cultures in the workplace.

We are a group of business executives who come together every month to share how including Christ in our daily lives has positively affected the work environment.

In sharing joys and struggles with others who walk similar paths, we are able to support and encourage our brothers and sisters in Christ to live courageously in witness to him. It is our hope to rejuvenate the working soul through fellowship and praise of God.

How is your soul today?

Name: Sisters of St Joseph of Boston
Description: The Sisters of St. Joseph of Boston is a Congregation of vowed religious women and associates who minister in the Greater Boston area and beyond. They trace their roots from LePuy, France (1650), Lyon, France (1807), St. Louis, MO (1836) and arrived in Boston, MA in 1873. They identify with the cries of a world, stunned by violence and seek to reopen communications in divided communities, to search for shared values, and to empower individuals to explore common ground for the healing of humankind. They are part of The U.S. Federation of the Sisters of St. Joseph composed of more than 4,500 members, 2,800 associates, with NGO status in the United Nations. For more information visit www.csjboston.org

Name: The Global Methodist Church
Description:
Our Mission is to make disciples of Jesus Christ who worship passionately, love extravagantly, and witness boldly.

In our worship, we desire to surrender to and be fully devoted to one God—the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit. We are passionate about what is important to God.

Jesus calls those who follow Him to love God wholeheartedly and love their neighbors as themselves. Our desire is to demonstrate to the world the extravagant love of God in the person of Jesus Christ.

We have been entrusted with a lifesaving and transforming message that we must share with others. Our witness is bold, compelling and fearless. Our mission advances when individuals become disciples of Jesus Christ and join God’s mission to make more disciples.

Our Vision is to join God in a journey of bringing new life, reconciliation, and the presence of Christ to all people, and to helping each person reflect the character of Christ.

Through our ministries, we desire to share the whole counsel of God with all peoples and to advance the presence and fulfillment of the Kingdom of God in every part of the world and at all levels of societies and cultures. The Global Methodist Church is committed to the Lordship of Jesus Christ, the inspiration and authority of the Scriptures, and the work of the Holy Spirit in conveying God’s truth and grace to all people.

We are a global church that recognizes and deploys the gifts and contributions of each part of the church, working as partners in the gospel with equal voice and leadership. Our witness to the world is marked by mutual love, concern, sharing, and a focus on those who are most vulnerable. We watch over one another in love and bear witness to the transforming power of the Good News as we humbly, but boldly, strive to serve others as ambassadors of Christ!

Name: Missions Etrangères de Paris (MEP)
Description:
Depuis 360 ans, des prêtres missionnaires sont envoyés à vie au service de l'Eglise en Asie et dans l'océan Indien.

Ces prêtres travaillent également à la création de nouvelles communautés chrétiennes ou de groupes de foi, à la formation des prêtres locaux, à des oeuvres caritatives dans l'éducation ou la santé, ils sont auprès de minorités ethniques ou de migrants, ils s'impliquent dans le dialogue interreligieux ou sont au service des communautés catholiques francophones d'Asie.

Description: Nijkleaster is a religious institutions company based out of Jorwert, Netherlands.

Description:
Vagabond is a Catholic apostolate that builds relationships with young people in
inner-city communities to help them fall in love with Jesus.

Description:
1st UMC exists to invite, equip, and deploy faithful followers of Jesus Christ. We are a multi-campus church, with 4 Sunday morning worship opportunities.

Downtown traditional worship: 8:30 and 11:00
Downtown contemporary worship: 11:00
Andover traditional worship: 11:00

Name: Watoto Church
Description:
Watoto Church is an exuberant cell-based, community church with campuses in Kampala and Gulu, Uganda; and Juba, South Sudan. It is the home of an international non-profit organisation that was initiated as a response to the overwhelming number of orphaned children and vulnerable women in Africa. 

Through this program, Watoto seeks to rescue an individual, raise each one as a leader in their chosen sphere of life, so they in turn will rebuild their nation. To date, Watoto has provided holistic, residential care for over 4,000 orphaned and vulnerable children (about 3,000 in current care). 

Through Watoto’s Living Hope initiative, dignity has been restored to more than 3,000 vulnerable women, who have been empowered with literacy, business and practical skills. Their children, more than 15,000 boys and girls, now have a better chance at a future. 

The renowned Watoto Children’s Choir travels the world to raise awareness about these initiatives.

Watoto Church is a member of the Pentecostal Assemblies of God, and works in affiliation with the Pentecostal Assemblies of God in Canada. We are also a member of the Evangelical Fellowships of Uganda. Watoto Church is a lively church where you can experience meaningful worship and the preaching of the whole counsel of God.

We are committed to maintaining a healthy spiritual atmosphere that will enable worshippers to experience the presence of God.

Name: Global Frontier Missions
Description: GFM is a movement of Christ-centered communities dedicated to mobilizing, training, and multiplying disciples and churches to meet the physical and spiritual needs of the least reached people groups of the earth.  We host short term mission trips, summer internships, and missionary training schools in several locations in the US and abroad.

Name: Holy Cross Family Ministries
Description:
Holy Cross Family Ministries is a family of Catholic ministries that inspires, promotes and fosters the prayer life and spiritual well-being of families throughout the world. HCFM has 24 mission centers in 17 countries on 5 continents, each continuing the mission of founder, Father Patrick Peyton, C.S.C. Father Peyton believed one of God’s greatest blessings is the family and he believed the best way to support the family is through prayer – particularly daily Rosary prayer. He knew that the family is the foundation of society and when the family maintains a deep and healthy mutual love, this in turn strengthens the family of the church and the entire family of humanity. Father Peyton’s messages,

“The family that prays together stays together” and “A world at prayer is a world at peace”

continue to be the focus of all the efforts of his continuing ministry. Families are served today through many forms of media and prayer events around the world. From apps to film, from social media to event, HCFM helps families pray. The work is carried out through Family Rosary, Family Theater Productions, Catholic Mom and the Father Peyton Family Institutes. Each ministry’s unique charism provides impactful resources that strengthen families.

Name: Kharis Church
Description:
We are a friendly, caring and exciting family church that seeks to influence our society with the reality of God’s love and the reliability of the His principles by ministering to the spiritual, physical, social and emotional needs of people who care to experience God in a dynamic and a living way.

While ministering to the total person, we seek to deepen people’s commitment to God and to spiritual values such as Holiness, Prayer, Evangelism, Christ-Centred Living, Bible Study and Fellowship – qualities we believe are essential to being an effective and fruitful disciple of Christ our Lord.

Description:
Waqaf An-Nur Corporation Berhad (“Waqaf An-Nur”) is a Malaysian company limited by guarantee established in 2000 to manage the assets and shares endowed (via waqf) by JCorp. As the corporate social responsibility arm of JCorp, Waqaf An-Nur continues to dutifully manage the waqf assets and funds for more than 20 years.

Waqaf An-Nur is recognised as a ‘Nazir Khas’ (special administrator) by Majlis Agama Islam Negeri Johor in 2005 to receive, manage and administer Islamic endowments.

Name: Parkview Christian Church
Description: We’re a non-denominational church that welcomes all people—no matter where you are on your faith journey. Our doors are open to everyone, and our culture is relaxed and welcoming. Whether you’re just exploring, or you’ve been walking with Jesus for years, we want you to feel at home. You can come as you are and know you belong.

Name: Religious Institution
Description: L’Ordre Antonin Maronite, dans sa forme actuelle, est né en 1700 sur l’initiative de Mgr Gabriel de Blawza, alors évêque d’Alep. Sur la colline d’Aramta, Mar Chaaya (Metn – Liban), à 20 Km de Beyrouth, les premiers pères fondent une Communauté monastique nouvelle professant les conseils évangéliques et œuvrant au service des besoins de l’Église dans les réalités sociales et prennent le nom d’ANTONINS. Les statuts de l’Ordre furent ratifiés le 17 janvier 1740 par le Pape Clément XII.

Name: Bent Tree Bible Fellowship
Description: We want you to not only experience the Life of Christ, but to also share his Life in the spaces where you are everyday!

Name: Grace Family Church
Description: Grace Family Church is a nondenominational, Bible-based church in Tampa, Florida. No matter your background, Grace Family Church is a place where you can explore Christianity at your own pace.

Name: Sisters Of The Holy Cross
Description: Our global ministries across eight countries focus on providing health care services and education, eradicating material poverty, and ending discrimination—giving witness to God's desire for the transformation of human hearts, human relationships, and all creation.

Name: Iskcon South Bengaluru
Description:
Iskcon South Bengaluru have their Lordships Jagannath, Baladeva & Devi Subhadra as their presiding deities. 
ISKCON South Bengaluru is part of International Society for Krishna Consciousness or popularly known as Hare Krishna Movement is a movement aiming at the spiritual reorientation of mankind through the simple process of chanting the holy names of God. The human life is meant for ending the miseries of material existence. Our present-day society is trying to do so by material progress. However, in spite of the extensive material progress, human society is not in a peaceful condition. The reason is that a human being is essentially a spirit soul and belongs to the spiritual world where he has an eternal and sweet relationship with the Supreme Lord. The Krishna Consciousness movement aims at awakening love of God within the heart of everyone and reviving their eternal relationship with God.

The Krsna consciousness movement is genuine, historically authorized, natural and transcendental due to its being based on Bhagavad-gita As It Is.  The knowledge of Krsna Consciousness has been transmitted through an unbroken chain of teachers and students stemming from the Lord Himself and has been presented in its original unadulterated form by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada. It is gradually becoming the most popular movement in the entire world, with more than 600 worldwide centers and literature in as many as 70 languages.

Name: St. John Paul II Newman Center
Description:
St. John Paul II Newman Center is where a student's spiritual life, residential life, and social life come together.  Whether worshipping in our beautiful St. John Paul II Oratory, living in Newman Hall, or hanging out in common spaces like Mary's Courtyard, college students feel welcome and encouraged to grow in their faith here.

BE BOLD. BE FAITHFUL. BE NOT AFRAID.

Name: Apostolic Ministerial International Network
Description:
Apostolic Ministerial International Network (AMIN) is an International Apostolic Council of Churches (IACC), a network for Pastors, Church Leaders, the Five-Fold Ministers ,Churches across the globe. The Apostolic Ministerial International Network is Registered in Ghana in 2009, Registered in South Africa in 2012 and in UK 2014.
The Apostolic Ministerial International Network is Accredited by the United Nations with Special Consultative Status (ECOSOC) in 2017. AMIN is also affiliated with IEP (Institute of Economics and Peace for Peace Dialogue.
The Apostolic Ministerial International Network wish to extend, restored and reform the Church to the Apostolic Pattern of Christ Jesus by gathering churches and Christian Leaders and bring them together to form a coalition to extending and expand the Kingdom of GOD. Ezekiel 37: 1-28, Isaiah 11:1-14.
We are therefore a living stone into the Building and Jesus Christ is the Chief conner stone of the Building, One Body 1Cor,12:12-26,.
We is therefore a body aim to trained the body of Christ and equip, mentor the Five-fold ministers.The training is for every Christian leader not only for AMIN members. We trained leaders to become a transformational leaders to bring changes and transformation in their life and in their communities. Acts 6 &7.
To fulfilled this great mandate, the church must be build on the foundation and pattern of the Apostles, there Bishop Dr Joseph Mihaye was inspired by the Holy Spirit to introduce this Apostolic Council or Association on the Apostolic Foundation and Patterns ( Eph. 2:20).

The Vision
1. To build the churches and ministers together on the Apostolic Foundation and Patterns of Christ Jesus and to bring decency and orderliness to the pulpit.
2. To empower, equipped our ministers, teams, and member churches from the apostolic network and send them to their communities to strengthen and establish churches and ministries both locally and globally.
The Mission.

Name: De Wittenberg
Description: De Wittenberg in Zeist is een community die overwegend bestaat uit jonge mensen tussen de 18 en 35 jaar. Wij zijn een leefgemeenschap, retraitecentrum en school in één. Jongeren kunnen hier wonen en deel uitmaken van de leefgemeenschap, een uitdagend tussenjaar van 5 maanden volgen (Inside-out) of een aantal dagen op retraite. Wij zijn een monastieke leefgemeenschap, wat onder andere betekent dat we leven op het dagritme van de getijdengebeden.

Name: Download Youth Ministry
Description:
The primary responsibility of a youth worker (someone who serves students primarily in a ministry context) is to support families by providing a safe environment for a student to be themselves and seek healthy relationships and guidance for their lives.

The primary way youth workers spend their time is preparing for weekly programs/events in an attempt to create that safe environment. Because youth workers are typically under-resourced, financially and relationally, they often spending a disproportionate amount of time on logistics rather than in relationships.

Download Youth Ministry (DYM) enables the youth worker to focus on their ministry to students and families by supporting them with high-quality, affordable resources they can plug into their ministry context quickly and easily. We help the youth worker look like a hero. 

We created Download Youth Ministry to help youth workers win in their vital role by providing resources that are:
High in Quality: we won’t waste your time with inferior resources.
Easy to Use: Our files are yours to edit and customize to your ministry context
Inexpensive: We know what it means to work from a limited budget, DYM won’t break the bank!
Trench-tested: practical tools that have already been proven in an actual youth ministry.

Here's how it works: a youth worker submits a digital resource he/she created for his/her own ministry (a sermon or game, for example). That resource goes through a light-weight vetting and editing process then is published on the DYM store. That youth worker will receive, on average, 50% royalty on every unit sold.

This model enables fresh, affordable, and seasonally- or culturally-relevant content year-round, which is critical for the youth worker connecting with his/her audience (teenagers).

About the team:
DYM was founded by two of Youth Ministry’s most influential thought leaders, writers, and speakers in the last couple of decades: Doug Fields and Josh Griffin.

Name: The Lovelady Center
Description: Empowering women and their children through Christ-based initiatives so that they can return to society as well equipped people of God. Our target is wholeness: spiritually, academically, financially, emotionally and physically. Given the tools to succeed in life, along with the empowerment of restoration to children and family, a woman can stand on her own and never return to a life of crime, addiction, domestic violence, or homelessness. It is our mission to help complete the steps between brokenness and wholeness by introducing the love of Christ, first and foremost, as well as the necessary tools that show her how to be a contributing member of society.

Visa anunciar o Evangelho Pleno de Cristo, proclamando a Palavra de Deus, fortalecidos pela oração, para servir a sociedade e assim florescer uma igreja fiel, com forte visão missionária e que seja reconhecida pela sua vida de integridade e santidade.

Name: Gateway Community Church
Description: We are a community of people who are in process. We have imperfect messy lives, but we are pursuing and experiencing a transforming relationship with Jesus and with each other. Everybody is welcome here, whatever your story, your questions, doubts or struggles. We’re all about doing life together in community, being intentional about our growth as people and serving one another and our broader community along the way.

Name: Hosea Life
Description: Faith Courtney Smith is the founder and Hosea Life came from the book of Hosea where a man “Hosea” which means My Salvation is a prophet man of God who is commanded by God to go marry a prostitute and he goes and saves her from being a slave in that place. This shows us the love that God has for us as we are married to Him and He has sent His Son Yeshua to die for us and save us from being enslaved of the things of this dark world.

The Diocese of Grand Rapids currently serves over 175,000 Catholics, 80 parishes, and 31 schools located within its 11-counties.

Name: Civil Air Patrol Chaplain Corps
Description: The Chaplain Corps of the Civil Air Patrol, the official auxiliary of the United States Air Force, is the home of volunteer ordained Chaplains and Character Development Instructors.  CAP has a vital interest in the moral development, and spiritual welfare and resilience of its members. The CAP chaplaincy is an organization of clergy who are appointed and endorsed as CAP chaplains. They represent their various denominations and support the pluralistic ministry of the CAP Chaplain Corps.  Character Development Instructors are the moral guides for the CAP cadet program.  The CAP Chaplain Corps College offers professional training and education, a new journal, and academic opportunities to advance personal and professional competencies.  The college's think tank, the Center for Religion, Ethics, and Excellence in Leadership (CREEL) provides opportunities for creative development of curriculum across multiple mediums.  The Chaplain Emergency Services School (ChESS) is part of CAP's National Emergency Services Academy (NESA) which provides opportunities to support local crisis situations and national disaster responses.

Name: Lambeth Palace
Description:
Lambeth Palace, on the south bank of the River Thames opposite Parliament, has been a historic London residence of the Archbishops of Canterbury since the 13th century.

It acts as a home for the Archbishop and their family when in London and as the central office for their national and international ministry.

The Archbishop employs several dozen staff to support them in their work there. In addition to the Archbishop's senior advisers and administrative staff, the Palace is serviced by a Facilities team, Events and Engagement Team, Gardening Team, and Gatekeepers and Security, all of whom take care of the historic building and its grounds.

Name: A+W Aus- und Weiterbildung der reformierten Pfarrerinnen und Pfarrer
Description:
Die Arbeitsstelle A+W, Aus- und Weiterbildung für Pfarrerinnen und Pfarrer ist beauftragt für Bildung, Entwicklung und Beratung für das evangelisch-reformierte Pfarramt. 

Ausbildung
In der Schweiz gibt es drei unterschiedliche Ausbildungssysteme für die kirchliche Ausbildung und Ordination der reformierten Pfarrerinnen und Pfarrer. In der deutschschweiz sind es das Konkordat für die Ausbildung der evangelisch-reformierten Pfarrerinnen und Pfarrer und ihre Zulassung zum Kirchendienst, welchem die deutschschweizer Landeskirchen mit Ausnahme der Kirchen Bern, Jura, Solothurn (BeJuSo) angeschlossen sind. Bei letzteren ist die Koordinationsstelle für die Praktikumbezogene Theologische Ausbildung (KOPTA) zuständig. Die KOPTA wird getragen von der Universität Bern, den Reformierten Kirchen Bern-Jura-Solothurn und dem Kanton Bern, und arbeitet eng mit dem Ausbildungskonkordat der anderen deutschschweizerischen reformierten Landeskirchen zusammen. In der Romandie ist das Office protestant de la formation zuständing.

Weiterbildung
Die Fachstellen für Weiterbildung der evangelisch-reformierten Kirche des Kantons Zürich (Aus- und Weiterbildung A+W), der Conférence des Eglises réformées (Office Protestant de Formation opf) und der Reformierten Kirchen Bern-Jura-Solothurn (Pfarrweiterbildung pwb) arbeiten seit 2004 zusammen und koordinieren ihre Angebote. Die Angebote von „Weiterbildung Schweiz“ dienen der fachlichen Weiterbildung der Pfarrerinnen und Pfarrer sowie der persönlichen Entwicklung der Spiritualität, der Vernetzung  und dem Wissenstransfer unter der Pfarrschaft. 

Gemeinsam treten sie unter der Marke Bildungkirche auf. www.bildungkirche.ch

Name: ACTS XXIX
Description:
ACTS XXIX is a 501 (c)(3) Catholic ministry founded to help get God’s world back and to mobilize the Church for mission. Our name comes from the New Testament book the Acts of the Apostles, which has 28 chapters. The book ends with St. Paul under house arrest in Rome, still boldly preaching the Gospel. According to Detroit Archbishop Allen Vigneron, “Luke ends without finishing the story because the story of the Church’s mission continues in every age. We are living the 29th chapter of Acts!”

We unleash the power of the gospel and equip ordained and lay leaders with our three essential principles to mobilize the Church for mission.

ACTS XXIX was founded by Fr. John Riccardo in 2019 in response to the Archdiocese of Detroit’s Synod 16 call to Unleash the Gospel. ACTS XXIX has brought more than 2,500 priests on retreat throughout the country and is working with more than a dozen bishops and dioceses. We operate from the biblical principle, “freely [we] have received, freely [we] give (cf. Mt. 10:8), and so our ministry is free of charge.

In August 2022, we launched The Rescue Project, a free eight-week video experience that proposes a biblical way of seeing reality. Most significantly, why is there something rather than nothing? Why is everything so obviously messed up? What, if anything, has God done about it? And how should I respond if He has? The Rescue Project is ideally offered in small group communities, including parishes, homes, schools and workplaces. 

As one of our patrons, St. Joan of Arc, quipped, “I am not afraid. God is with me. I was born for this.” We are not alive right now by chance. God has created us for this moment. Join us on God’s adventure!

Visit rescueproject.us to learn more and prepare to be overwhelmed by the gospel!

Description: The mission of The Crossing is to move the hearts and minds of more people to believe that Jesus is more.

Name: Washington Conference of Seventh-day Adventists
Description:
Washington Conference of Seventh-day Adventists located in Federal Way, Washington, serves as church leadership headquarters and as a spiritual training resource center for members in the western Washington region.

OUR MISSION
Believing in the imminent return of Christ, our mission in Washington Conference is to proclaim the everlasting Gospel* of Jesus Christ to every person in the Puget Sound region inviting them to become fully devoted disciples of Jesus. *See Revelation 14:6-12

OUR VISION
Every member, church and school reflecting the image of Jesus and working together to proclaim the everlasting Gospel of Jesus Christ and His soon return.

OUR CORE VALUES
We value spiritual growth.
We value discipleship.
We value reaching lost people. (See Luke 15)
We value excellence, transparency and integrity.

Email us at info@washingtonconference.org

Name: Catholic Information Center
Description: Located in the heart of Washington, D.C., the Catholic Information Center is committed to making the Catholic Church alive in the hearts and minds  of men and women living and working in our nation’s capital. Through a variety of spiritual, intellectual, and professional programs, the CIC offers the tools to live an integrated life and to engage in all areas of human endeavors.

Name: Kulaqua Retreat and Conference Center
Description:
Kulaqua, situated among the natural beauty of northern Florida and adorned with a style that will create a lasting impression for you and your guests, is the preeminent destination for your next educational or ministry event. 

We offer an extraordinary array of amenities that are perfectly tailored to meet your professional needs. Our custom blend of retreat options are expertly designed to ensure that your next event at Kulaqua will set the benchmark for quality and professionalism. 

Located in lush northern Florida, about two hours from Tallahassee and Tampa and less than two hours from Jacksonville, Kulaqua is a haven from the every day is blessed with natural beauty, a variety of activities and facilities in a second-to-none environment that is ideal for large and small events. 

This incomparable property has been enjoyed by thousands of guests for decades, and we are now proud to announce we have completely refurbished and expanded our offerings – in every way.

Since our modest beginnings in 1953, Kulaqua Retreat and Conference Center has offered people and groups of all ages a sanctuary to come together and bond with one another and God, away from common stresses and distractions, nestled on a blanket of God’s green canvas in north central Florida. T

This sprawling retreat and conference center is the ideal backdrop for company events, meetings and conferences, conventions and many other types of gatherings, both professional and private.  

Kulaqua’s mission is to serve the constituents of the Florida Conference of Seventh-day Adventists with a friendly and quality camping and retreat facility that sets an atmosphere allowing for the Holy Spirit to touch as many lives as possible.

Secondarily, to use Camp Kulaqua as an outreach to non-members who can abide by the policies of the Seventh-Day Adventist Church and the principles of Christianity.

May all be to the honor and glory of God.

Name: CBMC Central Midwest
Description: Our mission is clear: empower high impact leaders with wisdom for life, helping them discover, develop, and deploy their unique gifts for kingdom purposes. Why CBMC? Click on www.cmw.cbmc.com to get connected with us.

Name: Daily Audio Bible
Description:
Guiding Scripture
2 Timothy 3:16-17 (New International Version)
All Scripture is God-breathed and is useful for teaching, rebuking, correcting and training in righteousness, so that the man of God may be thoroughly equipped for every good work. 

Vision Statement 
Our ultimate goal is to provide the spoken Word of God to as many people as will listen in every region of the world that God will allow using whatever means God will allow for as long as God will allow. To be people of the Scriptures, fervent in prayer and having a daily and intimate walk with God. To build stable and Christ honoring community as we take our place in the global advancement of the Kingdom of God.
 
Mission Statement
The Daily Audio Bible is committed to guiding Christian's worldwide into an intimate and daily friendship with the Bible.  This is accomplished by providing the spoken Word of God freely in multiple languages and by the creation and establishment of communities that have no geographical limitations.  Our efforts are devoted to exposing the rich texture and heritage of the Bible's life changing power by educating the Believer on how to engage in and interact with the ancient Scriptures in a future world.

Name: Willesden Temple

From humble beginnings, it has grown to become an inspiration to thousands, keeping our culture, music and language alive with the hint of the British lifestyle we have grown up with. It continues to teach the core foundations of our religion; compassion, thirst for knowledge, morality, and respect for all human beings. As an important part of the local community, Willesden Temple has become a place where lifelong friendships have been made.
