Description: Trinity Episcopal Church has been serving Houston TX since 1893

Name: Compass Bible Church
Description: Compass Bible Church exists to make disciples of Christ by seeing as many people as possible reached for Christ, taught to be like Christ, and trained to serve Christ.

Name: Park Cities Baptist Church
Description:
We exist to lead all generations to love Jesus. At PCBC, we believe in the boundless Gospel, inexhaustible grace, overflowing generosity, relentless urgency, and courageous innovation. We believe that everybody is somebody, wholly loved by God—and you’re invited to join us!

Join us for worship and fellowship on Sundays at 9:30, 11:00, or 12:30!

To find out more about Park Cities, come join us on Sunday morning, visit pcbc.org, or give us a call (214.860.1500)!

Name: Evangel Church
Description: We are a church that is focused on pouring into peoples lives so that they will know Christ, grow in community with one another and go out and serve the world in the name of Jesus.  The church has a deep history, starting as a small german speaking church in Elizabeth into finally moving to Scotch Plains where the church has grown steadily, now averaging around 1,500 congregants per Sunday.

Name: Taylors First Baptist Church
Description: In order to bring glory to God, we seek to bring all people to a saving knowledge of Jesus Christ and active participation in His Kingdom, to help them grow in conformity to the image of Christ, and to equip them for ministry and mission in our community, our nation, and among the nations.

Name: Agapè Netherlands
Description: Agapé in the Netherlands is a national operating movement of christians that share Gods faith, hope and love in their neighbourhood, on the campus and in cities.

Name: Earlham School of Religion
Description: Earlham School of Religion is a Christian graduate theological school in the Quaker tradition. ESR prepares women and men for leadership that empowers and for ministry that serves. This mission grows out of our Christian belief that God calls everyone to ministry. Using a transformative model of education, ESR encourages students to explore the intellectual, spiritual, and practical dimensions of their calls to ministry.

Name: Oblates of the Virgin Mary
Description:
The Oblates, whose name means “offering,” are a group of Catholic priests and brothers founded early in 1816 by the Venerable Fr. Pio Bruno Lanteri.  Father Lanteri lived in northern Italy in a time of great moral confusion and spiritual discouragement. He formed the Oblates to be both contemplatives and apostles, men of study, prayer, and compassionate pastors. Today, serving in nine countries around the world, the Oblates bring hope for a new beginning.  This hope springs from five key attributes essential to Oblate Spirituality:  Mercy, Mary, Fidelity, Discernment, and Zeal for souls in today’s world.

Oblate Works

The ministries of the Oblates include spiritual direction, guided retreats, provide the sacraments, publishing, preaching and teaching as well as working with the poor and needy.

Oblate Spirituality

The spirituality of the Oblates of the Virgin Mary flows from the Spiritual Exercises of St. Ignatius of Loyola. They approach life with a contemplative spirit, finding God in all things by careful discernment of the movements of the heart. In guiding others to friendship with Christ, the Oblates know how to address the whole person, forming free men and women able to discern the will of God in concrete situations. As experts in spiritual direction, they cherish opportunities to offer retreats and parish missions, and to vitalize the lay apostolate.

Name: Westwood Community Church
Description:
Westwood began in 1995 in an unlikely location. The Chanhassen Dinner Theatre was an unconventional setting that fostered a friendliness that still characterizes Westwood today. In 2003, the church moved to 57 acres of rolling farmland in Chanhassen. People come from all over the Twin Cities and from many faith backgrounds. Whether, like many that visit, you are exploring the Christian faith for the first time or  are seeking to deepen an existing relationship with God, you are welcome at Westwood! At all our services the messages present biblical truth in a way that you can apply to your life situation.

Community is a vital part of Westwood. You will find many avenues to develop friendships and find support. Our kids and students are a high priority. In partnership with parents, we want our kids and students to know God personally and have their lives shaped by His love and grace. Our Preschool, Children's Ministry (birth-grade 5) and Student Ministry (grades 6-12) are designed to be a great resource for your family.

To explore Westwood further, take a look at www.westwoodcc.org.

Name: ក្រុមជំនុំជីវិតថ្មីកម្ពុជា New Life Fellowship, Cambodia
Description:
New Life Fellowship Phnom Penh is not a political organization, nor a government organization, nor a business.
New Life Fellowship is a Christian based and humanitarian organization.

Name: Shincheonji Church of Jesus
Description: "Enhance your ministry with our free, engaging Biblical courses. Fresh content for all levels, pleasing God through continuous learning."

Name: Lansing Catholic High School
Description: The Lansing Catholic High School community forms students spiritually, intellectually, and socially into faithful disciples of Jesus Christ.

Name: Ayngaran institute of spiritual sciences
Description:
In physics, we have moved from molecules to atoms to the sub-atomic world and identified many fundamental forces. However, these forces only attempt to explain how matter is formed. They do not explain the composition and nature of the life giving substance itself. Due to this reason, there is still a huge divide between spiritual sciences and physical sciences.

Is their a unified view somewhere? 
Thus, to understand the fundamental difference between the living and the non-living, we might need to look in the space beyond the known physical universe. We might need to differentiate between 'energy and intelligent energy', 'motion and emotion', 'mind and matter'. 
Given that the same carbon atom that makes charcoal also makes a human being, it is clear that the science of matter cannot explain the fundamental difference between living and non-living. Several questions arise when we leave known physical dimensions and move into the unknown space of life energies.

        What is the composition of soul? Does soul have a periodic table? What needs to be added to solar energy to make it 'soular energy'? How many layers beyond the physical body are we composed of? Are there any scientific means to explore spiritual dimensions? What are the technological innovations needed to capture each layer? Can we photograph mind and soul? What is their address? Does the address remain static or does it change? 

        We know about DNA, RNA and proteins. We are also familiar with terms like genotype and phenotype, genomics, proteomics, metabolomics and so on. Currently, biological science deals with the space between molecular inventory of an organism and its phenotypic outcome. The trouble is that DNA, RNA and protein molecules are 'practically dead' on arrival. They can be chemically synthesized and crystallised. What is chemically synthesized can be part of the living system but cannot be the 'life giving substance' itself.

Name: The Pines Catholic Camp
Description: The Pines is the premiere Catholic camp and retreat center in the Southwest. Located on 130 acres of beautiful piney woods in East Texas, The Pines helps youth develop a closer relationship with Christ and His Church through outdoor relational ministry.

Name: Central Christian Church of Arizona
Description:
Central is a nondenominational church that has been serving people in the Valley for over 50 years. Our mission is to be a church where all people can discover Jesus as Lord and Savior, develop their faith and purpose in Him, and deploy as committed believers to serve their families, friends, church, community, and world.

Our staff motto is to Work Hard, Work Smart, and Work Together. We value each individual employee and strive to maintain an environment based upon commitment, teamwork, loyalty, and trust. Therefore, we are looking for bright, talented employees who enthusiastically love the Lord, have a heart for ministry, and conduct their lives with the highest standards of honesty, integrity, and Christian ethics.

Central Christian Church of Arizona is a Certified Best Christian Workplace of 2018.

Name: Justice & Mercy International, Inc
Description: Justice & Mercy International exists to make justice personal for the poor, the orphaned, and the forgotten people of the world. JMI primarily focuses on the needs of the orphaned and acutely at-risk children in Moldova and the forgotten people in the villages along the Amazon River in Brazil. JMI administers sponsorship programs for children and families in the communities in which it serves. JMI leads volunteer mission trips to these locations.

Name: OHIO Hillel
Description: Welcome to the official website of Ohio University Hillel! Hillel strives to create a dynamic Jewish environment on campus where everyone is welcome. We serve the Ohio University campus community in areas of Jewish culture, education, history, social service, Israel advocacy, and communal experiences. Through involvement in Hillel, Jewish students learn valuable leadership skills, enhance their Jewish identity, and diversify the religious and cultural campus community.

Name: Kentwood Community Church
Description:
MISSION
To know Him and make Him known

VISION
We aspire to live as disciple-making, leader-raising Christ-followers who multiply healthy, multi-ethnic church expressions around the world.

CORE VALUES
Externally Focused  |  Lifestyle of Worship  |  Unity in Diversity  |  Loving Relationships  |  Servant Leadership

Name: Inter-Religious Council of Kenya (IRCK)
Description: The Inter-Religious Council of Kenya (IRCK) is a coalition of all major faith communities in Kenya that works together to deepen interfaith dialogue and collaboration among members for a common endeavor to mobilize the unique moral and social resources.

Name: St. John's Episcopal Church
Description:
We are a diverse Christian congregation of 250 families set in a resort community with deeply religious roots and a strong manufacturing and agricultural base.  Our congregation covers a broad spectrum of age and economic means.  While many of us are lifelong Episcopalians, some members have come from other faith  communities, finding St. John's a welcoming family. 
 
Our Sunday attendance averages 170, with many snowbirds migrating south in the winter and returning when the resort season begins. We have numerous summer visitors, including some cottage owners who return to St. John's annually.  We also draw from the larger population of Ottawa County and other nearby counties.

Name: Comunidade Cristã Videira
Description:
Somos uma igreja que segue princípios bíblicos, levando pessoas a seguirem a Jesus e a viverem o cristianismo fora das quatro paredes do templo, através de ações de generosidade que ajudam a transformar a história de outras vidas.

Pilares
A Videira é sustentada em cinco princípios básicos: comunhão, adoração, serviço, evangelismo e edificação.

Comunhão
As pessoas se relacionam não somente na igreja, mas durante a semana através dos Grupos de Crescimento, uma das melhores ferramentas para relacionamentos saudáveis serem construídos.

Adoração
Não é sobre cantar, louvar ou fazer música. Mas é sobre atitudes de indivíduos que obedecem a Deus, e são submissos à palavra Dele.

Serviço
Servir na comunidade, na igreja local e na sociedade. A nossa "África" está no Instituto Vida Videira. A nossa China está no olhar de cada membro da nossa igreja local.

Evangelismo
Alcançar o outro por meio de relacionamentos com pessoas. Nossa meta não é "pregar" Jesus, mas permitir que as pessoas O vejam através do nosso relacionamento sincero.

Edificação
Pessoas que vão ser edificadas na palavra, quando forem encorajados a crescer em um relacionamento pessoal com Jesus, através da leitura da bíblia e da oração pessoal.

Visão
“Ser uma igreja relevante na sociedade”.

Missão
“Amar a Deus e servir ao próximo”. Viver, amar e servir.

Name: Bistum Aachen
Description:
Das Bistum Aachen ist ein lebendiger Teil der katholischen Kirche in Deutschland – mit rund 1 Millionen Katholikinnen und Katholiken, über 300 Pfarreien und zahlreichen sozialen, pastoralen und gesellschaftlichen Initiativen.

Name: All Saints Catholic Church - Dallas, TX
Description:
All Saints Catholic Community is located in North Dallas and is part of the Catholic Diocese of Dallas.

All Saints is a vibrant, faith-filled, and loving community of over 2,000 Roman Catholic families.  We worship, live, love, and serve together, keeping the Holy Trinity at our center.  We know and attest that Jesus Christ is truly present in the Eucharistic. We submit that He graces all that we do and accomplish in His name!

We are a parish of many generations – from the newborn to the very founders of our parish; we offer our loving embrace to our parishioners of all ages.  We worship together as one family in liturgies that are reverent and inspiring, glorified by the uplifting song of our choirs.  

We learn together in Bible studies and faith formation programs; we serve together in aiding others in need throughout our community; we join together in parish events and meals; we gather together as we welcome new parishioners in baptism and RCIA; and we celebrate those that have joined our Lord in their resurrection.  Truly, our parish offers something for everyone, and provides the one thing we all need: Christ in our life.

Come join us at the table of our Lord at All Saints, where we praise God together for His gifts to us of faith, family, and children, and give thanks to Him for the many blessings He bestows on us as a people and a parish of faith!

Name: Diocese of Fall River
Description: Established on March 12, 1904 by Pope Pius X, the Diocese of Fall River covers 1,194 square miles in Southeastern Massachusetts, comprising Bristol County; Barnstable County – recognizable on any map as Cape Cod; Dukes County or Martha’s Vineyard; Nantucket County; and the towns of Mattapoisett, Marion and Wareham in Plymouth County. The Most Reverend Edgar M. da Cunha, S.D.V., D.D., became the Eighth Bishop of Fall River on September 24, 2014. He leads a Catholic community numbering 279,000 persons who worship in 80 parishes and 11 mission churches. The Diocese is divided into five territories or deaneries with one priest in each appointed by the Bishop to serve as Dean to assist him in the coordination of ministry in that area.

Name: CatechismClass.com
Description:
CatechismClass.com was founded by Fr. James Zatalava in March 2004 to the highest quality Catholic catechesis online through www.CatechismClass.com.  

CatechismClass.com exists to spread the Catholic faith and to make its students fully informed Catholics.  Fr. Zatalava created CatechismClass.com as an online Catholic Education apostolate - dedicated to providing catechesis to Catholics from the cradle to the grave.  CatechismClass.com currently provides a wide breadth of products, including courses for Adults as well as a complete Sacramental Preparation Program, juvenile programs based on individual grade levels, short courses on moral issues of contemporary importance, an extensive lineup of book summaries taken from the best Catholic classic and contemporary literary works, and a Catholic Liturgical Year Course.  CatechismClass.com's main offerings are its innovative Baptism Preparation Programs and its online RCIA courses.

CatechismClass.com operates with the singular goal of being the best online Catholic catechesis program in the world.  All actions of the organization are ultimately directed to the purpose of saving souls.  In the words of the holy apostle Paul, “And how can they believe in him of whom they have not heard?”  (Romans 10:14)

Name: VKB Kerkrentmeesters
Description:
Welkom bij VKB kerkrentmeesters! Als kerkrentmeester houd je je bezig met de middelen van en voor jouw kerk. We onderscheiden binnen de VKB kerkrentmeesters 4 werkvelden (gebouwen, geld, mensen en organisatie). Je kunt denken aan de financiën van de kerk, inkomsten vergaren, gebouwen en begraafplaatsen beheren, toerusting van vrijwilligers en werkgeverschap van personeel dat in jouw kerk actief is. Wat je ook doet: waarschijnlijk is er elders in het land iemand met precies hetzelfde bezig (geweest). Bij de VKB vinden jullie elkaar. We zetten hiervoor diverse (communicatie)kanalen in:
- magazine Kerkbeheer (verschijnt 6x per jaar)
- website www.kerkrentmeester.nl (met een uitgebreide kennisbank)
- podcast Collectieve Wijsheid (o.a. via Spotify)
- VKB-app, te downloaden via www.kerkrentmeester.nl/vkb-app
- digitale nieuwsbrief
- adviseurs via ons kantoor: info@kerkrentmeesters.nl of 078-6396660
- via regionale afdelingen van de VKB opgezet voor en door kerkrentmeesters

Dit alles hebben we zo georganiseerd om ervaringen en tips uit te wisselen, om elkaar te helpen, zodat niet ieder voor zich het wiel opnieuw hoeft uit te vinden en om je denkproces op gang te helpen en je te helpen bij het ontwikkelen van een toekomstgerichte kerkvisie. 
Waarden van de VKB vind je in onze aanpak terug: gelijkwaardig, jou ondersteunend, maar ook van jou lerend, om zo elkaar te inspireren. 
Voel je meer dan welkom om jouw kennis, ervaring en expertise ruim te delen met andere kerkrentmeesters. Zo staan we samen sterk voor de kerk!

Name: The Life Center Church
Description:
What is now The Life Center Church was formerly known as The Mt. Carmel Institutional Missionary Baptist Church, founded by the late Rev. J. A. Johnson in 1957. The initial church building was a small wooden structure located at 109 Washington St., Eatonville. The structure was built by neighbors and other community citizens to accommodate 75 and had a beginning membership of one adult and eight children. 

In August 1976, Bishop Ronald F. Kimble, Sr. received his ministerial license and following the May 1977 resignation of Rev. Johnson, became the interim pastor of the church. In October of the same year, Bishop Kimble was ordained and installed as the official pastor of the church. Under the leadership of Bishop Kimble, the church experienced another significant growth period and has achieved many milestones including a name change (The Life Center Church), the establishment and expansion of a variety of ministries and the continued development of ministry facilities.  Four additional churches have also been established under Bishop Kimble's leadership. They are: The Life Center Church Ocala, FL; Titusville, FL; Monrovia, Liberia. 

The Life Center complex consists of a worship center, educational facilities, administrative offices, a multi-purpose facility, a bookstore, and a prayer chapel. 

The Life Academy (formerly Eatonville Christian Academy) was established in 1982 and currently services students K4-9th grade.  It is located at 107 Wymore Rd., Eatonville, FL.

The Life Center complex functions for ministry to the local congregation and the Central Florida Community.  It is designed for edification, education, administration, and recreation.

Name: PRAYING PELICAN MISSIONS
Description:
PPM exists to build up, encourage, and assist the local Church in serving and reaching their communities for Christ.

As an interdenominational ministry, we seek to recognize the diversity of the church while leveraging what binds us together as followers of Jesus Christ. There is no more powerful example of God's love to a community than when His people come together to serve and advance the Gospel, all in the name of Jesus.

Praying Pelican Missions’ vision is to see a united global Church reaching its fullest potential for the glory of God. We believe the best way to accomplish our mission and vision is to support the local Church 365 days a year. We do this in three ways: short-term mission trips, our sponsorship program PPM365, and disaster response.

Name: Mercy Hand Ministries-Non Profit
Description: We our a ministry that believes in living out God's word ( Luke 4:18 ) by serving people where they are, we go to women shelters, children shelters, men shelters , homeless shelters and to communities in need. We our a non-profit ministry that does every thing for free, we have been blessed with a food concession trailer to feed the less fortunate, we offer fresh grilled hamburgers, hotdogs, all the fixings, cookies or ice cream for desert and bottled water, we donate clothes and bibles ( all for free ) . We have a team that have regular jobs and we pitch in every month to do a outreach. We have a servants heart and we all have a story on how Jesus has changed our hearts. We basically go to a shelter or community and do a mini revival of about 4 hours, we feed, we have live music and share some heart felt testimonies and have prayer time and fellowship. God Always shows up at these events because he anoints our ministry for loving him and doing his work. Our goal is to do this for the rest of our lives, we would love to grow and reach more people by bringing Jesus town to town. We all have a God given gift to share with this world, so come and join us get involved, also pray about donated to the Ministry so we can keep moving forward. God Bless!  We our a ministry that does not judge people, we our called to love one another, Amen...

Name: Our Lady of Perpetual Help Parish
Racine St. Catherine's
Description:
St. Catherine's is a faith community in the Catholic tradition committed to the Christian education of young men and women. It is sponsored by the Racine Dominicans and bears witness to the
mission, values and commitment of its sponsors.

St. Catherine's welcomes students of diverse backgrounds and aims to provide an excellent academic program that challenges all students to develop their God-given talents and achieve their highest potential. Programs and environment also nurture the spiritual and social development of students to enable them to engage in healthy relationships with all life and learn to cherish and celebrate that life.

St. Catherine's aims to graduate socially responsible women and men committed to gospel-value living and life-long learning; prepared to enter college and/or the world of work; and willing to contribute leadership and service to their communities.

Name: C-Suite for Christ Denver
Description:
Our mission is to cover the world in Christ by unapologetically declaring to the world whose we are by proactively creating faith-centric cultures in the workplace.

We are a group of business executives who come together every month to share how including Christ in our daily lives has positively affected the work environment.

In sharing joys and struggles with others who walk similar paths, we are able to support and encourage our brothers and sisters in Christ to live courageously in witness to him. It is our hope to rejuvenate the working soul through fellowship and praise of God.

How is your soul today?

To become a member of the C-Suite for Christ Denver Chapter, or for all questions in general, please contact Kristin Rapert, C-Suite for Christ Denver Chapter President, at krapert@csuiteforchrist.com

Name: St. Michael's Abbey
Description:
St. Michael’s Abbey is an autonomous abbey of the Norbertine Order. The Abbot is assisted in his governance of the community by members of his council. The canonry currently has 47 priests, and 18 seminarians studying for the priesthood. All members of the abbey live a full priestly and monastic common life; with daily attendance at the choral Office in the abbey church and life in community.

Priests from St. Michael’s Abbey give spiritual direction and retreats to many different religious communities, both in California and internationally.

Confreres assist in many Catholic organizations locally and nationwide, prison ministry and staffing of parishes in the dioceses of Orange and Los Angeles. Confreres regularly give retreats; spiritual direction and counseling; sacramental care to the dying; and assistance to Catholic cemeteries. Confreres – who have a variety of graduate degrees from Roman, American and European institutions – also write and publish books and articles.

Name: Partners in Torah
Description:
Partners in Torah’s signature phone study program offers Jewish adults of all backgrounds across North America a cost-free learning opportunity to discover Judaism – its culture, history, and traditions – at their pace and their schedule. Jewish men and women with an interest in acquiring specific skills (ex: Hebrew reading or Talmud) or who simply want to build on their Jewish knowledge-base, are matched, one-to-one, with a carefully selected personal Torah trainer or “mentor” for up to an hour a week of over-the-phone study and discussion. Participants can choose any topic including Hebrew, Jewish philosophy, or history. In its decade and a half of operation, Partners in Torah has engaged more than 30,000 intellectually curious Jews across North America, and today supports some 13,000 weekly study partnerships.

Partners in Torah also runs a full range of exciting study programs including personalized community-based learning, celebrated Shabbatons and unforgettable retreats.  A cohesive team of 26 qualified professionals work out of offices in New Jersey and Jerusalem.  One of the most important aspects of the study program is clearly Partners in Torah’s involvement and ongoing support offered to each of its participants – week in and week out. In addition to making Jewish knowledge accessible to Jews no matter where they may live, Partners in Torah aims to foster unity among Jews of all backgrounds and denominations.

Name: IQRA
Description:

Our courses are for Muslim audiences based in the tri-state area (Greater Philadelphia area, South Jersey, and Delaware).

We offer single weekend courses, continuing courses, and soon will be offering certificate programs.

IQRA Community

IQRA Community seeks to unite Muslims together through multi-community gatherings that are fun and enjoyable, and are within the bounds of the deen.  This is done through social events, sports, Quran competitions, Iftars, as well as da’wah activities.

IQRA Academy

IQRA Academy aims to develop our youth to be outstanding Muslims and strong leaders that have a commitment to individual excellence, family, community, and the Ummah at large.

Name: Kirchlicher Dienst in der Arbeitswelt der Evang.-Luth. Kirche in Bayern
Description:

Wir verbinden diese arbeitsweltlichen Erfahrungen mit dem kirchlichen Leben und sprechen die dahinterstehenden theologischen Themen an.

Wir machen Bildungsangebote rund um Arbeit und Beruf, begleiten Menschen in betrieblichen Alltags- und Krisenzeiten und schaffen Freiräume zum geistlichen Auftanken. 

Darüber hinaus engagieren wir uns mit der Aktion „1 plus 1“ für Jugendliche ohne Schulabschluss und Langzeitarbeitslose. Wir sind in zahlreichen Bündnissen und Initiativen aktiv wie der bayerischen Allianz für den freien Sonntag, dem Sozialen Netz Bayern oder der Aktion „5000 Brote“.

Der kda ist mit seiner Zentrale in Nürnberg und sechs weiteren Regionalstellen in ganz Bayern erreichbar:

Augsburg
Coburg
München
Nürnberg
Regensburg
Schweinfurt

Wir freuen uns auf Austausch, Diskussionen und gemeinsame Projekte mit Ihnen!

Description:
We are a Messianic synagogue based upon a traditional Conservative model. If you are looking for a Jewish experience, we may be for you. We conduct our services in Hebrew, utilizing Conservative siddurim and chumashim; we do a full-complement of Torah aliyot, chanting Torah, Haftorah, and the B'rit Chadashah in Hebrew.

 

We welcome intermarried couples and families, and offer a unique opportunity for those who are Jewish and unfamiliar with traditional synagogues to learn about your heritage in a friendly, welcoming space. Come and see what we have to offer.



The MJBI equips leaders who will establish Messianic Jewish congregations and ministries in Jewish communities worldwide. Additionally, the MJBI seeks to equip the Church in its responsibility to take the Good News to the Jew first (Romans 1:16). Like Paul, the MJBI helps educate Christians in their role to provoke the Jewish people to jealousy and thus save some of them (Romans 11:11-14).

Name: Archdiocese of New Orleans
Description: Archdiocese of New Orleans is dedicated to promoting the Kingdom of God in the Metro New Orleans area.

Name: Maryland Hillel
Description: Maryland Hillel serves one of the largest and most dynamic Jewish campus communities in the country. The organization actively seeks to engage Jewish students on their own terms by providing students with meaningful Jewish opportunities, which are empowering and engaging. Through a creative mix of leadership training, education, volunteerism, social, spiritual, and cultural opportunities, students are inspired to realize their potential and become the best versions of themselves. Each year, Maryland Hillel impacts 4,500 unique students.

Name: Healing Place Church
Description: A vibrant community of believers whose heart and soul is to be a healing place for a hurting world. We invite you to come as you are, leave different than you came and expect the unexpected! HPC is a church with one heart, to be a healing place for a hurting world, at locations across the globe.

Name: Kingdom Business and Professionals Forum
Description:
Kingdom Business Forum and Professionals Forum (KBPF) is a non-denominational, non-political network of Christ-minded entrepreneurs, professionals, leaders and emerging leaders who are connected by a common commitment to personal, professional, and business growth for greater purpose and impact, in line with the vision and mission set forth below.

Vision
To raise a new generation of entrepreneurs, professionals, and leaders united by a commitment to pursuing Christ-like values and principles in their businesses and careers, and who empower and support each other to build sustainable inter-generational wealth and to better support the Kingdom of God.
This vision, as conceptualized by Archbishop Nicholas Duncan-Williams and Lady Rosa Whitaker Duncan-Williams, is based on a strong conviction that “We are better together than alone!” and as admonished by the Holy Bible in Proverbs 27:17, “Iron sharpeneth Iron”.

Mission
Hosted by ACI Prayer Cathedral, KBF will provide a rare platform where Kingdom-minded entrepreneurs, professionals, and leaders connect, network, fellowship, and together build a strong and dynamic business and professional community that empowers and supports them to create sustainable inter-generational wealth and value, and through that be able to better support the work of the church and impact society.

The Kingdom Business Forum is more than just a network; it’s a God-centered community dedicated to equipping and empowering business leaders to make an eternal impact. By becoming a member, you join a movement of believers committed to glorifying God in the marketplace and transforming lives through Kingdom-focused business practices.

Name: White's Chapel Methodist Church
Description: As much as we try to avoid the label (and all the baggage that comes with it), White’s Chapel is admittedly a big church.  And we know that, with hundreds of opportunities to grow and serve, it’s easy to get overwhelmed; but we’re here to help every step of the way to make WC the “biggest small church” around: to make WC not just a church, but your church.  Wherever you are on your journey of faith, White's Chapel has a place for you to worship, study, grow, heal, and serve.

Name: The Beautiful Life Ministry
Description: Experience The Lord Jesus

Name: Calvary Church
Description:
We’re a group of imperfect people, loved by a perfect God and called to something greater than we can even imagine. We seek to be a community of Christ-followers, loving Jesus and others. And as an outpouring of God’s overwhelming love and work in our own lives, we want to be a group of people who are making a difference in the lives of others in our homes, city and world.  
Calvary is a member of the Evangelical Free Church of America, an association of autonomous churches united around certain theological convictions.

Description: "We are a faith-centered, welcoming congregation that serves God, our community, and the world through diverse ministry and mission work. We are real people, with real lives, and real faith." We dig deep into the Word so that we are prepared to work in the world & to help change lives in the name of Jesus Christ.

We are committed to making disciples of all age groups for the Lord Jesus Christ through evangelism, teaching and training so that churches are established and the Church strengthened.

We are a local church with a global mission to lead people into transforming relationships with Jesus, and a vision to lead an Only God movement that transforms the soul, the city, and the world. 

Soul City values creating moments that lead to movement so that people can come, connect, contribute, and change.  We are committed to working with excellence as we seek to create a  7 >1 daily transforming environment where everyone is both accepted and expected to grow in a holistic (heart, mind, body, soul) way. 

You can learn more at our website or visit us for a Sunday gathering - we would love to meet you and share more.

Name: The Journey Church - NYC

When you attend The Journey you can expect to be welcomed warmly, to hear lively music and to find the teachings relevant to your life. We hope you take the opportunity to visit us on a Sunday in the near future to experience The Journey for yourself.

Name: Passport Camps
Description:
With Christ as our foundation, Passport empowers students to have fresh encounters with God, embrace community, and extend grace to the world.

Passport, Inc. is a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit ministry whose primary goal is to work for integrated, ecumenical, and inspirational weeks of summer camp that model important lessons like service in the name of Christ, creation care, and respect for our neighbors. Passport’s National Team is responsible for developing camp themes and content, hiring and training exceptional summer staff, and executing the overall camp experience. In addition to camps, Passport also produces a devotional website and app, d365, that offers a devotion for each day of the year.

Name: St. Paul's Lutheran Church of Muskego
Description:
St. Paul's is a Christian church of approximately 2400 members from Muskego, WI and the surrounding communities.     

We offer five weekend worship opportunities in both traditional and contemporary settings.

We operate an accredited elementary school, and provide childcare from birth to age 5. These and other youth ministries serve the families of over 300 children from our church and community. 

St. Paul's is a member of the WELS, and a proud partner of several local, national and international mission efforts, including:

- Victory of the Lamb Lutheran church in Franklin, WI (our daughter congregation)
- Garden Homes Lutheran Church (a partner congregation serving those in an urban Milwaukee setting)
- Wisconsin Lutheran High School (Miwaukee, WI)
- Wisconsin Lutheran Seminary (Mequon, WI)
- Savior of the Nations (a sister mission congregation in Vancouver)
- Missionary John Holtz (Malawi, Africa)

Description:
At WestGate Church, we’re trying our hardest to understand what it looks like for us to become fully devoted followers of Jesus Christ. We want to love and live like Jesus. And we want to do this together in community.

“The student is not above the teacher, but everyone who is fully trained will be like their teacher.”

LUKE 6:40

WestGate Church has been certified as a “Best Christian Workplace” according to the Best Christian Workplaces Institute in 2021.

Name: Northwoods Community Church
Description: We hope you’ll find Northwoods to be an exciting place that fuels your spiritual journey—a place where you can find practical help for the needs in your life, as well as tools to build healthy relationships. We believe everyone, through the supernatural power of Jesus Christ, can find freedom to become all that God created us to be. We are one church that meets in multiple locations! Weekend services—creative, relevant, and understandable—are held on campuses in Peoria, Galesburg, and Chillicothe, Illinois. Live worship teams lead each local service, and then we join together for teaching, which is live in Peoria and shared via high-definition video in Galesburg and Chillicothe. We strive to offer something catalytic for people at every stage on their spiritual journey, from those simply exploring Christianity to those learning to live a Christian life to those fully centered around Jesus.
