Name: Catholic Diocese of Lansing
Description:
The Diocese of Lansing, originally comprised of 15 Southern Michigan counties, was established by Pope Pius XI by proclamation dated May 22, 1937. Previously, these counties had been part of the Archdiocese of Detroit and the Diocese of Grand Rapids. In May 1938 with the establishment of the Diocese of Saginaw, the counties of Allegan, Barry and Ionia were annexed from the Diocese of Lansing to the Diocese of Grand Rapids, and the counties of Genesee, Livingston and Shiawassee were annexed from the Archdiocese of Detroit to the Diocese of Lansing. In July 1971 from the Dioceses of Lansing, Grand Rapid and Saginaw, the Dioceses of Kalamazoo and Gaylord were formed. At that time, Washtenaw and Lenawee Counties were annexed to the Diocese of Lansing from the Archdiocese of Detroit.

The Diocese of Lansing is currently comprised of 10 counties covering 6,218 square miles: Clinton, Eaton, Genesee, Hillsdale, Ingham, Jackson, Lenawee, Livingston, Shiawassee and Washtenaw counties. Major cities are Lansing, Adrian, Ann Arbor, Flint, Jackson, Owosso and Ypsilanti. 2013 figures provided by the U.S. Census Bureau estimate the total population in the 10 county area at 1,795,538. The Official Catholic Directory 2014 approximates the diocesan Catholic population at 195,858.

Name: Online Prasad
Description:
OnlinePrasad.com aims to connect people to their faith by offering and home-delivering Prasad from various temple locations across the country. We are one of the hottest start-ups in the country. We have been covered by ‘The Times of India’, ‘Economic Times’, ‘Mail Today’ and many top-notch media houses.

Today’s India is undergoing momentous change. We Indians are progressing fast. But, the best part of this progess is that we stay true to our roots and culture. 
At the heart of our culture lies faith and devotion. OnlinePrasad.com aims to bring people closer to their faith. We do this by offering and delivering Prasad to you from the temple of your choice. Thus, using technology to make life convenient for you.

You can read more at http://onlineprasad.com/pages/about-us/
Also check out our blog (http://blog.onlineprasad.com) for detailed information and media mentions.

Name: Salesians of Don Bosco
Description: We, the Salesians of Don Bosco, officially known as the Society of Saint Francis de Sales, are recognised in the Church as a clerical religious institute of pontifical right, dedicated to apostolic works. We are a male religious congregation dedicated to apostolic and missionary activity and to the many works that Christian charity has given rise to, but above all at the service of young people, especially the poorest and most abandoned.

Name: Outpost Centers International
Description: Outpost Centers International (OCI) is an umbrella organization that networks and nurtures  Seventh-day Adventist supporting ministries around the world. These ministries represent a significant portion of the organized gospel outreach conducted by the laity of the Adventist Church. OCI is a spiritual brotherhood of ministries striving towards the common goal of taking the gospel to the world.

Name: Australian Baha'i Community
Description:
Founded in 1920, the Baha’i community is spread throughout all regions of Australia. Australian Baha’is come from a wide variety of backgrounds and walks of life, and are united by a shared commitment to translate into reality the teachings of Baha’u’llah, the founder of the Baha’i Faith.

The Australian Baha’i Community seeks to be a learning community, reflecting on how to better foster spiritual life and contribute to the progress of Australian society. Baha’is work alongside other Australians to enhance our individual and collective spiritual lives and to improve the neighbourhoods in which we live.

Name: Stichting Evangelisatie Sjofar
Description:
De belangrijkste doelstelling van Stichting Evangelisatie Sjofar is het verkondigen van het Evangelie van Jezus Christus. Dit doen wij op verschillende manieren:

* Evangeliseren op markten, in winkelcentra, bij evenementen en op voorlichtingsavonden.
* Verspreiden van de Bijbel.
* Ondersteunen en helpen opzetten van evangelisatieacties van kerken en gemeenten.
* Toerusten van kerken, gemeenten en individuele gemeenteleden.
* Het waarschuwen voor onchristelijke ontwikkelingen in de maatschappij.

Name: Bais HaVaad
Description: With over 50 Dayanim, Poskim and Kollel members – a group of some of the most accomplished Talmidei Chachamim in the United States today – the Bais Havaad has become the epicenter for the study and analysis of Halacha and its application in today’s world.

Name: Mitra Diocesana de Jundiaí (Cúria)
Description:
Criada a 7 de novembro de 1966, pela Bula “Quantum Conferat” do Papa Paulo VI, a Diocese de Jundiaí foi instalada canonicamente no dia 6 de janeiro de 1967, numa memorável celebração festiva que contou com imensa multidão de fiéis e numerosas autoridades eclesiásticas, civis e militares. Tomou posse, naquele dia, seu primeiro Bispo, Dom Gabriel Paulino Bueno Couto, OCarm, natural da cidade de Itu. Desde o Jubileu de Prata, em 1992, foi reconhecida pela Santa Sé como Padroeira Diocesana, Nossa Senhora do Desterro, que é também patrona da cidade de Jundiaí desde 1615. O território da Diocese foi praticamente todo desmembrado da Arquidiocese de São Paulo, com exceção de Louveira, que pertencia a Arquidiocese de Campinas.

Fazem parte da Diocese de Jundiaí 66 paróquias e uma área pastoral, em onze cidades, a saber: Jundiaí, Cabreúva, Cajamar, Campo Limpo Paulista, Itu, Itupeva, Louveira, Pirapora do Bom Jesus, Salto, Santana de Parnaíba e Várzea Paulista. A Diocese tem uma população de mais de um milhão de habitantes, dos quais cerca de 66% professam a fé católica. Hoje a Diocese de Jundiaí conta com 87 sacerdotes incardinados, 25 sacerdotes membros de Institutos Religiosos e 91 diáconos permanentes e 50 seminaristas.

Name: Athey Creek Church
Description: Athey Creek Christian Fellowship is a body of believers desiring to know the Lord in an ever-increasing measure. We are a non-denominational fellowship that studies the Bible together, verse by verse, and chapter by chapter.

Name: Bridgeway Christian Church
Description:
Bridgeway Christian Church is a Scripture-soaked, Sprit-led community expectantly seeking transformation into our identity in Jesus. We are passionate about equipping one another to bring the wholeness of Jesus to a broken world.

Bridgeway was founded in 1996 when three churches in the Sacramento region realized that they were all drawing people from the Roseville area.  Twenty some families from Arcade Church in Sacramento, Lakeside Church in Folsom, and Sun River Church in Rancho Cordova served as the core group called to be the church planters of Bridgeway. In 2016 Bridgeway moved into it's current home at 8150 Industrial Ave. Bldg. A, Roseville, CA 95678.

You are encouraged to come to Bridgeway just as you are. We are an authentic community of Christ followers, and we would love to have you join us!

Name: CrossNet Ministries
Description:
CrossNet Ministries exists to offer help and hope in the name of Jesus Christ by empowering people in the ELANCO community. CrossNet Ministries provides programs in the area of Youth, Food & Nutrition, Social Services, and Community.

CrossNet Ministries programs focus on two areas:
1. Empower people to build their resources
2.Develop relationships to see lives changed by Jesus Christ

Name: Camp Ramah in Wisconsin
Description:
Camp Ramah is 150 acres of magical space, tucked away in the heart of Wisconsin’s Northwoods.  In this space, campers and staff members from around the Midwest and Israel join together each summer to create an exceptional Jewish community.  For campers, Ramah is a second home where they have fun, learn new skills, gain independence and build lifelong friendships.

For nearly 80 years Camp Ramah in Wisconsin has provided opportunities for growth and joy in the context of meaningful Jewish experiences and community.

Name: Potomac Conference Corporation of Seventh-day Adventists
Description: The Potomac Conference is a regional headquarters of the Seventh-day Adventist church, encompassing Virginia, Washington D.C. and a small section of Maryland. The Potomac Conference exists to grow healthy, disciple-making churches

Name: The Colossian Forum
Description:
In a time of deep division between Christians in areas of faith and culture, The Colossian Forum exists as a sign of hope. We take our name and inspiration from the Apostle Paul’s proclamation in Colossians 1:17: All things hold together in Christ. 

Since 2011, The Colossian Forum reclaims, shares, and practices this liberating truth with Christians who are divided.

We equip leaders to transform messy cultural conflicts into opportunities for spiritual growth and witness. Our vision is of a Christian community that acts like Christ, especially in the face of conflict. 

For more information, please visit www.colossianforum.org.

Name: Hebrew Seminary: A Rabbinical School for Deaf & Hearing
Description:
Hebrew Seminary is a pluralistic, egalitarian rabbinical school that educates people to become rabbis and pararbbis for Jewish and multifaith communities. Hebrew Seminary was founded in 1992 to meet the spiritual needs of the Jewish Deaf and hearing. As an outgrowth of its commitment to inclusiveness for all underserved communities, Hebrew Seminary has expanded its mission to serve the richly diverse Jewish community in all its facets. We believe that the Jewish community as a whole is strengthened when we recognize the validity of all those who contribute to our vibrant Jewish heritage. Our curriculum is grounded in Bible, Hebrew, and Talmud, and incorporates Jewish ethics and history. Through elective classes and hevrutah study students are encouraged to follow their own interests and passions, especially as they reflect today’s dynamic Jewish communities.Hebrew Seminary’s curriculum differs from the programs of all other American rabbinical schools by offering traditional Jewish healing meditations and practices, and American Sign Language and Signed English translations of Jewish liturgy and life-cycle events. Our founder, Rabbi Dr. Douglas Goldhamer, z”l wrote extensively on these subjects. Our online courses attract a diverse student body from across North America. While all required coursework for our programs can be completed online, we also host learning opportunities onsite in Chicago. The rabbinic program is a 5-year course of study for full-time students and the pararabbinic program is a 2 ½-year course of study for full-time students. Students may also pursue their studies for either program on a part-time basis. American Sign Language interpreters are provided for students and faculty whenever needed.
Whether you are just starting out on your career path or you’ve always wanted to guide and inspire others along a Jewish path, Hebrew Seminary is here to guide you and support you along your journey to Jewish leadership.

Name: Immaculate Conception Catholic Church
Description: Immaculate Conception is a Roman Catholic community in downtown Durham pastored by Franciscan Friars. We are a multi-cultural and stewardship parish called, gifted, and sent to proclaim the Gospel of Jesus Christ through worship, parish-wide faith formation, our parish school, pastoral care, and living the church's social teaching.

Name: Kloster Engelberg, Obwalden
Description:
Die Benediktinerabtei Engelberg besteht seit 1120 und in ungebrochener Tradition leben seither Mönche im Hochtal am Fuss des Engelberg und des Titlis in der Schule des christlichen Lebens nach Benedikt von Nursia.

Zum Kloster Engelberg gehören eine Schule, es nimmt Gäste in seinen Gastbetrieben auf und verpflegt diese und betreibt verschiedene Handwerksbetriebe sowie ein eigenes Rebgut am Bielersee. Die Engelberger Klosterbetriebe werden heute weitgehend von angestellten Kräften geführt, die je in ihren Berufen bestens ausgebildet sind, über grosse Erfahrung verfügen und sehr kompetent arbeiten. Die Klosterbetriebe arbeiten selbstverständlich nicht nur für das Kloster. Ein grosser Teil der Arbeiten werden für Auftraggeber von ausserhalb ausgeführt - eine wichtige Einnahmequelle insbesondere für den Unterhalt des Klosters, da Benediktiner als Spezialfall in der katholischen Kirche keine Einnahmen durch Kirchensteuern, dem Bistum oder Rom haben, sondern sich ihren Unterhalt möglichst selbst erwirtschaften.

Mit über 140 weltlichen Angestellten ist das Kloster Engelberg einer der grössten Arbeitgeber im Engelbergertal.

Name: Grace Bible Church
Description: Helping people find and follow Jesus

Name: The Anglican Diocese of Leeds
Description:
The Anglican Diocese of Leeds is a diocese (administrative division) of the Church of England, in the Province of York. It is the largest diocese in England by area, comprising much of western Yorkshire: almost the whole of West Yorkshire, the western part of North Yorkshire, the town of Barnsley in South Yorkshire, and most of the parts of County Durham, Cumbria and Lancashire which lie within the historic boundaries of Yorkshire. It includes the cities of Leeds, Bradford, Wakefield and Ripon. It was created on 20 April 2014 following a review of the dioceses in Yorkshire and the dissolution of the dioceses of Bradford, Ripon and Leeds, and Wakefield.

The diocese is led by the Anglican Bishop of Leeds and has three cathedrals of equal status: Ripon, Wakefield, and Bradford. There are five episcopal areas within the diocese, each led by an area bishop: Leeds, Ripon, Wakefield, Bradford and Huddersfield.

Name: Crosspoint Church
Description: Our mission is to share God’s love with people in McKinney and beyond so that they can become fully developing followers of Christ.

Name: The Church at Brook Hills
Description: The Church at Brook Hills is completely committed to glorifying God by making disciples of all nations. We have many ministries working together to see this vision, mission, and goal fulfilled, and strive to both care for our members well and to share, show, and teach the gospel of Jesus Christ through serving the world both locally and globally. You’re welcome here. Come and go with us.

Name: Women in Apologetics
Description:
Women in Apologetics (WIA) is a Christian non-profit organization whose sole focus is to educate, equip, and encourage women in Christian apologetics. We have a passion for encouraging and equipping women who are interested in learning how to think more deeply and intelligently about their faith. Here at Women in Apologetics we strive to live into Luke 10:27:

He answered, “You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, and with all your soul, and with all your strength, and with all your mind; and your neighbor as yourself.” (NRSV)

Name: TEAM Mobile Seminary
Description: Great Commission church planting through providing national pastors in Latin America with tools for them to train their countrymen.

Name: Vrije Baptistengemeente Bethel
Description:
De Vrije Baptistengemeente Bethel te Drachten is een kerkelijke gemeenschap. Wij hebben ontdekt dat een relatie met Jezus Christus het meest waardevol is in dit leven. Daarom willen we in de samenkomsten, in de onderlinge relaties en naar buiten toe de blijdschap van het geloof in Jezus Christus tonen.

Voorganger van de gemeente is ds. Jacob Folkerts

Name: Northeastern Conference of Seventh-Day Adventist
Description: Since the inception of our conference in 1945, our mission has been to punch holes in the moral darkness into which sin has plunged us, by vigorously engaging in the spread of the Good News of Salvation. That evangelistic fervor has catapulted our conference to be among one of the largest conferences in the North American Division of Seventh-day Adventists.

Name: Biblical Leadership for Excellence
Description:
BIBLICAL LEADERSHIP FOR EXCELLENCE (BLE) IS A NONPROFIT, NONDENOMINATIONAL CHRISTIAN ORGANIZATION FOUNDED IN 1985. 
 
BLE helps people connect with God's greater purpose in their work
through one-to-one discipleship, mentoring, and small group studies.

Name: Católico Sou
Description:
Somos um site católico no qual queremos ajudar a todos os fieis a vivenciarem e entenderem, a fé Católica e suas riquezas. Oferecemos muito mais que produtos, aqui você encontra notícias, eventos, produtos e parcerias.
Entre em contato e ajude a construir.

Name: Calvary Bible Church
Description: Building a committed, worshiping community that knows the Word of God, grows in the knowledge and application of the Word of God, and show the Word of God through service and evangelism.

Name: Briarwood Presbyterian Church
Description: Briarwood Presbyterian Church, Presbyterian Church in America (PCA), is located in Birmingham, Alabama. Our Purpose is: "For God's glory, Briarwood is committed to equipping Christians to worship God and to reach Birmingham to reach the world for Christ."

Name: Hope Center Ministries, Inc.
Description: Hope Center Ministries is a growing nonprofit discipleship recovery program with multiple centers across the U.S. Hope Center Ministries exists to restore lives, restore families, and restore communities. We do this by helping those individuals bound by drug addiction and/or alcoholism find lasting freedom and sobriety through a relationship with Jesus Christ, which translates into seeing the lost saved, the saved freed, the freed restored, and the restored fulfilled.

Name: Merkos 302 - Chabad World Headquarters
Description:
Merkos 302 is an umbrella organization that supplies services and support for the 4,000 centers of the Chabad Lubavitch global network, as well as launching new centers. 

Some of Merkos 302's programs include: 

Beis Hamedrash l’Shluchim: Online lectures, learning, and live farbrengens accessible to Shluchim around the world.

CKids: Interactive lessons for Shabbat, holidays, and more that aim to inspire children with enthusiasm and excitement for Judaism. 

CTeen: Dynamic programming for Jewish teenagers that transforms their formative years into a time of purpose and self-discovery. 

JNet: Provides people around the world with personalized, one-on-one Torah learning through an expansive network of study partners. 

Keren HaShluchim: Financial support for shluchim in times of emergencies, tragedies, and for general assistance. 

Kinus: The International Conference of Shluchim, the annual reunion of Chabad’s dedicated Jewish leaders from across the globe.

Machon l'Shlichus: Provides training and resources to enhance and expand the Shliach’s initiatives.

Merkos Shlichus: Roving Rabbis program that sends hundreds of young rabbinical students across the globe to make Jewish experiences and education available to Jews in remote locales.

Moshiach Campaign: Spreads the message about Moshiach through newsletters, brochures, stories, and sermons available for download.

MyShliach: Cares for the wellbeing of shluchim’s children while instilling in them core Chassidic values through a mentoring program, book library, online rallies, and other resources.

Shluchim Exchange: Provides shluchim and shluchos with a global forum to share ideas, access resources, and interact with fellow shluchim 24 hours a day.

Yaldei: Provides emotional and logistical support to shluchim with special needs children through service coordination, advocacy, financial assistance, family and respite support, and more.

Name: Notre-Dame Basilica of Montréal
Description:
The Notre-Dame Basilica of Montréal is the first Neo-Gothic Church in Canada. Its history is intrinsically linked to the foundation of the city of Montréal.

Acting as a sacred place for prayer and, thus, the religious celebration of Catholicism, Notre-Dame has always been a beautiful venue for hosting grandiose celebrations and events. A shining jewel amongst Quebec parishes, the Basilica greets over one million tourists annually. 

Since March 2017, The AURA experience, produced by Moment Factory, is a spectacular light show highlighting the Basilica's astounding 17th century architecture using 21st century technology, making it a one of kind immersive experience.

The Basilica is managed by the non-profit organization La Fabrique de la Paroisse Notre-Dame de Montréal, which also includes the Notre-Dame-des-Neiges Cemetery. The Fabrique offers a complete range of competitive benefits to its 150-250 employees.

Name: Tenth Presbyterian Church
Description: Tenth Presbyterian Church, Philadelphia, was established in 1829 and has consistently stood on the inerrant Word of God as foundation for life and practice. Therefore it teaches the entire bible--the whole council of God--and has a ministry of mercy, ACTS Ministries (Active Compassion Through Service) which employs the gifts and talents of approximately 300 volunteer staff.

Name: Sisters of Notre Dame
Description:
The Sisters of Notre Dame are called to be apostolic women religious. We witness to God’s goodness and provident care through a consecrated life of chastity, poverty and obedience. Centered in Jesus Christ, united as a community in his love and in the spirit of Mary, we share a life of faith and joyful simplicity, ready to take up our cross daily in love.

Consecrated for mission in the Church, we serve people, especially those who experience poverty in its various forms, through education and other ministries. We place special emphasis on catechesis so that we may help others to grow in faith and confidence in God our Father.

Name: Preekpunt
Description:
We willen het Evangelie brengen op plekken waar geen (vaste) voorganger is. We helpen daarbij om een dienst in te vullen door een preek via video aan te bieden. Ook goed te gebruiken als noodpakket - als een voorganger onverhoopt niet komt opdagen.

Via Preekpunt bieden we preken, series en aanvullende materialen aan die je preekvoorziening een stuk eenvoudiger gaan maken. Concreet gaat het om: een videopreek, voorbeeld voor het programma van de dienst, gespreksvragen voor daarna en ook aanvullende materialen.

Name: Church of God, a Worldwide Association
Description:
We are a vibrant community of mission-driven believers from around the world, dedicated to giving all humanity the good news of the Kingdom of God and teaching Christ’s disciples. Learning God’s purpose for your life will be your greatest discovery!

The mission of the Church of God, a Worldwide Association, is drawn from Christ’s promise that His Church would preach a specific “gospel” (good news) message, continuing the theme of all inspired Scripture: “And this gospel of the kingdom will be preached in all the world as a witness to all the nations, and then the end will come” (Matthew 24:14).

Also included in our mission is Christ’s charge to the early Church leaders: “Go therefore and make disciples of all the nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit, teaching them to observe all things that I have commanded you” (Matthew 28:19-20). “Making disciples” involves educating, training and caring for believers.

Our values are developed from those of the New Testament Church. We teach the living Word of God from both the Old and New Testaments, which transforms peoples’ lives.

The Church of God, a Worldwide Association, teaches and practices the same way of life Jesus Christ and the first-century Church of God followed. We observe the seventh-day Sabbath and the annual Sabbaths or Holy Days, as did Jesus and the early Church.

Learn more about what we believe and teach at https://lifehopeandtruth.com.

Name: Household of David
Description:
We believe that we were created for one purpose: bringing God pleasure all the days of our lives. We do this through our worship.

We are committed to bringing lost souls into the fold and we will continue to do this until we meet our Master on the last day.

At HOD, we do church differently. Beyond the weekly gatherings, we are integrated as a family, with systems in place to be present in the lives of our members.

The Christian life is a journey. We have a mandate to help every member grow into Christ, hand-holding them from their salvation experience through to maturity.

We believe that every HOD member is a minister of the gospel, with our pulpits ranging from the altar to the marketplace. Our greatest desire is to see everyone discover where God has called them and to stay there.

Name: B-B-T
Description:
BBT provides Biblical leadership development and curriculum to small-group Bible study leaders, helping them grow in their own relationship with Christ and equipping them to pour into the lives of others.  
     The ministry focus of BBT is developing group leaders for small-group Bible studies.   BBT groups meet at work, near work (e.g., restaurants, coffee shops, etc.), or at a local church.  BBT provides leaders with curriculum that is focused on practical work situations to help participants directly apply Biblical principles to what they do every day … but that’s just the beginning!
     BBT is more than a Bible study – it’s an individualized Biblical leadership development process for group leaders.  BBT coaches work with group leaders to assess, equip and encourage them to lead in every area of life (we call this the Leadership Flywheel ).  We believe that leaders who are growing in their relationship with Christ will lead thriving, Christ-centered small groups.
     BBT is a non-profit 501(c)(3) organization which exists to help people apply Biblical principles at work. 


For more information, or to find a Biblical Business Training (BBT) group near you, visit us at www.B-B-T.org or email us at info@B-B-T.org.

Name: Chabad at Yale
Description: Chabad At Yale has been at the heart of the Yale Jewish experience for thousands of students since the early 2000s. The vision for Chabad at Yale is a vibrant, modern center that provides educational, social, and recreational programming for students and faculty. We strive to convey a compelling, rich, and meaningful Judaism that will inspire all who come in contact with it.

Name: Balaji
Description: Salasar Balaji or Salasar Dham in India is a place of religious importance for the devotees of ... concerning the origins of Salasar Balaji. History claims the discovery of the site by Ginthala Jats of Asota village in Nagaur district of Rajasthan.

Name: COOL Ministries, Inc.
Description:
Who We Are
Boyd and Jan Harrell founded C.O.O.L. (Christ Over Our Life) Ministries, Inc. in 1999. We are a multi-faceted outreach ministry-focused on results in recovery-God's way. Some of the activities sponsored by C.O.O.L. Ministries are:

C.O.O.L Prison Ministry  
C.O.O.L. Church 
C.O.O.L. Youth  
C.O.O.L. Counseling - Belief Therapy 

Our Mission
C.O.O.L. (Christ Over Our Life) Ministries, Inc. is dedicated to delivering the Word of God and the Gospel of Jesus Christ worldwide, and maturing disciples for Jesus Christ. We believe in the true and living God. We carry the message of hope and that recovery from all forms of sin is possible through a complete surrender to Jesus Christ.

Name: Pastors for Revival
Description: We are here for the calling of Revival in America and beyond. God's plan for this ministry is to reach the Pastor's, Saints and lost sheep.  Through this ministry, You will find rejuvenation, inspiration and a burning for revival and Salvation sown into your heart. Throughout the year Pastors for Revival hosts events like feeding the homeless and poor while also attending to children with terminal illnesses.

Name: Wifaq-ul-Madaris
Description: Wifaq ul Madaris Al-Arabia, Pakistan (Urdu: وفاق المدارس العربیہ) is the largest federation of Islamic Seminaries in Pakistan, founded in 1959. More than 10,000 Seminaries and 23,000 Madaris across the Islamic Republic of Pakistan are affiliated with the federation. It controls all the seminaries which are run by Deoband School of thought.

Name: L'Oratoire Saint-Joseph du Mont-Royal
Description: Saint Joseph’s Oratory of Mount Royal, founded by Saint Brother André in 1904, is a Catholic shrine dedicated to Saint Joseph. Place of expression of faith, prayer, healing and peace, the Oratory welcomes pilgrims and visitors in a spirit of openness and compassion. A must-see in Montreal, the Oratory receives 2 million visitors per year.

Name: A Igreja de Jesus Cristo dos Santos dos Últimos Dias
Description:
Este trabalho é tão libertador: ser empregado em uma organização onde temos a liberdade máxima de utilizar os verdadeiros princípios do evangelho restaurado de Jesus Cristo em nosso trabalho diário. Ter acesso a toda a verdade e aplicá-la em nosso desempenho diário é a coisa mais libertadora que eu conheço. Este é verdadeiramente o trabalho do reino de Deus." - Élder David A. Bednar, do Quórum dos Doze Apóstolos.

Os funcionários da Igreja encontram alegria e satisfação ao usar seus talentos e habilidades únicas para promover a obra do Senhor. Desde o profissional de TI que desenvolve um aplicativo que leva a mensagem do evangelho para o mundo todo, até o gerente de instalações que mantém nossos edifícios, proporcionando lugares de adoração, ensino, aprendizado e recebimento de ordenanças sagradas para os membros da Igreja, nossos funcionários buscam maneiras inovadoras de compartilhar o evangelho de Jesus Cristo com o mundo. Eles estão literalmente trabalhando no Reino.

Name: Congregation Beth Elohim
Description: Congregation Beth Elohim, founded in 1861, is one of the fastest growing and most vibrant synagogues in New York City. CBE, also known as Garfield Temple, was recently named “one of America’s 25 most vibrant congregations” by Newsweek, which hailed CBE for “quickly adapting to Brooklyn’s exciting, young population.” Open to people from a variety of backgrounds who honor our traditions and values, CBE serves as a hub for members and for the surrounding community as well. It is a place of worship, dedicated to honoring Jewish traditions, and also a place of learning, a place to socialize, to celebrate and to actively engage with the world, a synagogue center energizing and enriching our Jewish community through study, ritual and acts of loving-kindness.

Name: Ripon Cathedral
Description: Ripon Cathedral is a music company based out of Cathedral Office Liberty Courthouse Minster Road, RIPON, United Kingdom.

Name: Hickory Grove Christian School
Description: As a ministry of Hickory Grove Baptist Church, we exist "To know Christ and to make Him known through Christian education." As a school, our mission is "To equip students to serve God in the 21st century by providing an excellent academic program founded upon a biblical worldview."

Name: Carmel Baptist Church
Description:
Carmel Baptist Church is committed to making disciples of those Nearest, our Neighbors and the Nations.

We have ministries for all age groups designed to teach the love of Christ and the what it means to follow Him.  Join us for one of our three services in the Worship Center each Sunday. Services begin at 8:00am, 9:30am and 11:00am.  In addition, we have Chinese and Japanese language worship services. 

Carmel is the home of WEE School and Carmel Christian School which provide Christian care and education for preschoolers (6 weeks - 5 years) and children (K - 12).

Name: GOOD NEWS For Everyone!
Description: The official Facebook Page of Good News For Everyone (formerly GideonsUK). Introducing everyone to the Lord Jesus Christ.

Name: The Anglican Communion Office
Description: The Anglican Communion Office, based in London, England, at St Andrew's House, is the permanent secretariat for the Instruments of Communion. It serves Anglican Consultative Council, the Primates' Meetings and the Lambeth Conference, as well commissions, committees and groups that emerge from time to time, as the need arises. The Rt Revd Anthony Poggo serves as Secretary General of the Anglican Communion.

Name: Hebrew Seminary: A Rabbinical School for Deaf & Hearing
Description:
Hebrew Seminary is a pluralistic, egalitarian rabbinical school that educates people to become rabbis and pararbbis for Jewish and multifaith communities. Hebrew Seminary was founded in 1992 to meet the spiritual needs of the Jewish Deaf and hearing. As an outgrowth of its commitment to inclusiveness for all underserved communities, Hebrew Seminary has expanded its mission to serve the richly diverse Jewish community in all its facets. We believe that the Jewish community as a whole is strengthened when we recognize the validity of all those who contribute to our vibrant Jewish heritage. Our curriculum is grounded in Bible, Hebrew, and Talmud, and incorporates Jewish ethics and history. Through elective classes and hevrutah study students are encouraged to follow their own interests and passions, especially as they reflect today’s dynamic Jewish communities.Hebrew Seminary’s curriculum differs from the programs of all other American rabbinical schools by offering traditional Jewish healing meditations and practices, and American Sign Language and Signed English translations of Jewish liturgy and life-cycle events. Our founder, Rabbi Dr. Douglas Goldhamer, z”l wrote extensively on these subjects. Our online courses attract a diverse student body from across North America. While all required coursework for our programs can be completed online, we also host learning opportunities onsite in Chicago. The rabbinic program is a 5-year course of study for full-time students and the pararabbinic program is a 2 ½-year course of study for full-time students. Students may also pursue their studies for either program on a part-time basis. American Sign Language interpreters are provided for students and faculty whenever needed.
Whether you are just starting out on your career path or you’ve always wanted to guide and inspire others along a Jewish path, Hebrew Seminary is here to guide you and support you along your journey to Jewish leadership.

Name: Christ Episcopal Church
Description:
First, all of our beliefs are founded upon faith in Jesus Christ. While we are a community of faith that finds its source in the Holy Scriptures and the traditions of the church, we also highly value human reason. We hold fast to a few simple truths: God made us. God loves us. Jesus was the fulfillment and perfect embodiment of that love. God wants us to love everyone else in the same way. And in the end, in Christ, all will be well.


Worship experience

At Christ Church our worship seeks to adore the living God with classical liturgies that are beautiful, sacred, and traditional - modeled after the worship forms of the earliest Christians. While our worship is classical, it is not formal – come as you are and enter into the beauty of holiness.

We are Gospel-inspired and Eucharist-centered, but also enjoy Morning Prayer. We share our Anglican heritage with seasonal Choral Evensong services when additional voices from our community join our choir to present very moving beautiful settings of psalms, anthems, and hymns as an outreach to our neighboring churches and community.

We have a weekday morning service of Holy Eucharist and healing prayers with a homily on the saints of the Church.  Special services have included the Crèche service for children and families near Christmas, Stations of the Cross, Tenebrae, and Festive Choral Eucharist on high holy days. Our services are reverent, but welcoming and friendly with a bulletin designed to help visitors follow the form of the service. 

Whoever you are, wherever you are on your faith journey, you are welcomed here!

Name: Christ Community Chapel
Description:
CCC is a nondenominational church in Hudson, Ohio, that exists to help people reimagine life because of Jesus.

View our messages:
ccchapel.com/messages

Check out our upcoming events:
ccchapel.com/events

Name: BookMyPooja
Description: BookMyPooja is India's Most Trusted Pooja Booking Platform

Name: Faith Fitness Ministry
Description: Faith Fitness Ministry is a Catholic ministry for young adults in their 20s and 30s, dedicated to the evangelization of Jesus’s message and teachings. Through our monthly brewery and fitness events in DFW, we aspire to equip young adults with faith-based knowledge, empowering them to embrace and embody their beliefs with unwavering conviction.

Name: City Bible Church
Description: City Bible Church is a multi-cultural, multi-generational, multi-campus church and we meet in a number of different locations in the Portland/Vancouver metro area.  For more information on who we are and where we are going, please check out our website at www.citybiblechurch.org

Name: Association of Christian Media
Description:
The Association of Christian Media’s purpose is to be an authority that inspires, serves and supports Christian Media in Southern Africa.

The ACM is a network of Christian media organisations that share a vision of reaching people with the Gospel. It is a platform for those involved in Christian media to pool resources, expertise and ideas for the purpose of uplifting each other. The ACM offers its members the unique chance to support and be supported by each other.

The ACM was founded in 1994, for the purpose of supporting Christian Broadcasters. Last year the name (formerly the Association of Christian Broadcasters) was changed to reflect a growing number of print and electronic media members and also to accommodate the vision to include Christian individuals who work in secular media and church media departments. 

The ACM currently has over 75 members. These members include One Gospel TV, Joy magazine, My Faith Magazine, CCFM, Christian Art Media, Focus on the Family, God TV, Heartlines, Impact Radio, Joyce Meyer Ministries, Kenneth Copeland Ministries, Kingfisher FM, Link FM, Radio Pulpit, Transworld Radio, Radio Tygerberg, Faith Broadcasting Network and many more.

Name: WinShape College Program
Description:
The WinShape College Program guides students on a proven path that leads to spiritual growth and life-defining development.

Our mission is to glorify God by cultivating college students to develop disciples, create community, and launch leaders. Through our Residential Communities and Discipleship Intensives, participants gradually build a fuller identity in Christ and strengthen their leadership ability.

Our four-year Residential Communities are available at Berry College, Belmont University, NC State University, and the University of Central Florida. Students live in biblical community and participate in weekly core development, experience life-changing catalytic events, and serve their communities through hands-on team ministry.

Discipleship Intensives are 3- and 5-day discipleship experiences that are hosted by our Ministry Partners throughout U.S., and impact hundreds of participants each year. Ministry Partners send students or host an event in order to equip & commission college-aged students and young adults to make disciples in their communities.

You are worthy. Are you ready? Find out more at winshapecollegeprogram.org

Name: Marathon Messiah's Praise
Description:
About thirteen years ago, Marathon Messiah’s Praise, the World’s longest non-stop annual gospel musical event began at the Redemption Camp, an emerging City along Lagos/Ibadan Expressway Nigeria. It is an event that is organized with two objectives: as a birthday gift to appreciate God for His mercies and faithfulness over the life of Pastor E.A. Adeboye, The General Overseer of The Redeemed Christian Church of God; and as a gospel musical event that will birth a new pattern of worship in this dispensation. Since inception in 2012, eleven successful editions have so far been held from 70 Hours Marathon Messiah’s Praise to 71 Hours, 72 Hours, 73 Hours till 81 Hours. Today, Marathon Messiah’s Praise has become an annual event that is growing in leaps and bounds. It expanded to other nations outside Nigeria in 2016 as 74 hours Marathon Messiah’s Praise (themed PRAISE BEYOND BORDERS) was held across three countries on two different continents to become a global event that was held in about 50 countries across the world in 2023. 

Welcome to 82 Hours marathon Messiah's Praise - JESUS THE HELPER!
#JesusTheHelper #JTH #82HoursMMPRAISE

Name: Catholic Cemetery Conference
Description:
The Conference is committed to the following objectives:

To provide a forum for the discussion of, and dissemination of information concerning all phases of Catholic cemetery development, operation and maintenance.

To assist Catholic cemetery personnel in the understanding and solution of their cemetery problems by the assembling of accurate information with reference thereto.

To assist Catholic cemetery personnel in the continuous improvement of cemetery services in the respective Archdioceses and Dioceses served by them.

To foster and promote the religious, charitable and educational interests of Catholic cemeteries and their beneficiaries in cemetery service.

The Catholic Cemetery Conference serves as the vehicle through which individual members can share problems, explore solutions, work together and communicate with each other to meet today’s challenges.

Name: Bradford Cathedral
Description:
Bradford Cathedral is a hidden jewel waiting to be discovered. Set in tranquil gardens we are Bradford’s oldest place of worship and house the story of the City.

We are an Anglican Cathedral, holding services, events and educational visits.

We are open Mon - Sat 10am – 4pm for visiting and prayer. Discover our Morris & Co textiles and stained glass and monuments marking Bradford’s history.

Visit us online for more or call 01274 77 77 20.

Welcome to Yorkshire ‘Small Visitor Attraction of the Year’ 2020
Eco congregation status
Museums+Heritage Awards ‘Best Use of Digital - UK’ (2022, shortlisted), 'Culture Award' (2023, shortlisted).

We are committed to safeguarding: bradfordcathedral.org/about-us/safeguarding.

Name: Glenstal Abbey
Description: Glenstal Abbey is a Benedictine monastery in County Limerick on the south-west coast of Ireland. A 500 acre estate with streams, lakes and woodland paths, surrounds a castle built in the romantic Norman style. The Abbey, which is dedicated to Saints Joseph and Columba, is home to a vibrant community of monks. Our prayer and liturgical celebration combine with managing a boarding school for boys, a farm, and a guest house. The monks assemble in Church four times a day for the Divine Office and the Mass. Benedictine worship emphasises beauty and harmony, celebrating God’s presence, while it evokes a response of loving reverence in the monk.

Name: St. Maria Goretti
Description: Details on the location and what the community offers. Additionally, it has all the adminstrative staff with pictures.  It list current events with all the ministries and the chairs of the ministries.  It has documentation in PDF formats.

Name: Abbazia di San Giorgio Maggiore - Benedicti Claustra Onlus
Description:
♰ Benedictine Monastery founded in 982, restored by A. Palladio.
• Promoting a dialogue between Church and Contemporary Art since 2011.

Name: Spaanse Evangelische Zending (SEZ)
Description:
Wie wij zijn

De Spaanse Evangelische Zending is een kleinschalige, reformatorische zendingsorganisatie voor Spaanstalige landen.

Wat wij doen
De SEZ werd in 1952 opgericht om de onderdrukte protestantse minderheid in Spanje te ondersteunen. Nu is er in Spanje vrijheid van godsdienst. De protestantse kerken zijn vaak nog wel afhankelijk van buitenlandse hulp. Sinds het eind van de vorige eeuw geeft de SEZ financiële prioriteit aan de nood van de Cubaanse kerken, die zwaar te lijden hebben en grote nood kennen. 

Boekenverspreiding
Cubaanse predikanten en theologiestudenten ontvangen boeken uit de reformatorische en puriteinse traditie op usb-sticks, via de post of gedrukt in Cuba zelf. Wekelijks versturen we vanuit Nederland kleine boekenpakketjes.

Reformatorisch netwerk Cuba
Elbert Verboom, onze Cubacoördinator, geeft vanuit Nederland steun aan predikanten en theologiestudenten in Cuba.

Onderwijs
Ds. Berend Coster werkt sinds 1992 in Spanje. Hij is docent en decaan aan o.a. de Facultad de Teología, IBSTE in Castelldefels, en predikant van de Iglesia Cristiana Reformada, Mataró.

Bijbelcursussen

Name: Renovaré
Description:
Renovaré is a Christian nonprofit that models, resources, and advocates fullness of life with God experienced, by grace, through the spiritual practices of Jesus and of the historical Church. 

Christian in commitment, ecumenical in breadth, and international in scope, Renovaré helps people in becoming more like Jesus. 

What We Do
Renovaré publishes print and online resources, including books, articles, messages and podcasts. Retreats, spiritual formation groups, and educational initiatives like the two-year Renovaré Institute provide community and experience where that knowledge can move from head to heart.

Name: Seventh-Day Adventist Church - Santiago, Chile
Description: Headquarters of the Seventh-Day Adventist Church that embrace more than 110 churches around the Metropolitan Region of Chile, 4 Adventists Schools and 6 Life Hope Centers (support community centers).

Name: Substance Church
Description: A fast growing non- denominational church with multiple sites around the Twin Cities area. We believe in the importance of community and empowerment for all of our members. Our subgroups exceed our Sunday service attendance! We are open to anyone that wants to check church out again or for the first time.

Name: National Association of the Catechesis of the Good Shepherd (CGSUSA)
Description:
The Catechesis of the Good Shepherd is centered on nurturing the 3-to-12-year old child's relationship with God.  Over the span of 50-plus years of careful and loving observation and experimentation, first by the work's founders, Dr. Sofia Cavalletti and Gianna Gobbi in Rome, Italy, and then slowly by trained catechists around the globe, the Catechesis of the Good Shepherd has become an respected voice on the spiritual life of children.  

Almost 20 years ago, catechists in the United States recognized the need for an association that could assist catechists, parents, and others in the Church and beyond in their understanding of the religious potential of the child. They already shared a vision: a world where the religious life of the child was honored. It was not surprising that the first stated aim of the association was: “To involve adults and children in a religious experience in which the religious values of the child are predominant, keeping in mind that the contemplative nature of the child indicates to the adult a certain way of drawing near to God.”

 Thus was formed the National Association of the Catechesis of the Good Shepherd in the USA.  Since then,CGSUSA has developed 74 publishcation and offered hundrends of formation courses for catechists.  CGSUSA currently counts approximately1600 catechists and friends as members.  Today, there are over 1025 atria operating in this country, serving thousands of children in parishes, homes and schools.

Name: First Friday Club of Cleveland
Description:
The First Friday Club of Cleveland is a unique forum for Catholic thought and inspiration. he First Friday Club of Cleveland provides a wonderful opportunity for nourishment of mind and spirit. Listen to inspiring and thought provoking speakers as you enjoy a buffet luncheon and the fellowship of friends and colleagues.
Please join us and invite a friend, relative, or colleague to a First Friday Club program. The Club meets at The Cleveland City Club, 850 Euclid Avenue in downtown Cleveland.

Name: Shri Bodhivinayak Temple
Description:
Sasi Krishnasamy is a spiritual guru and social activist who is the founder of Ayngaran Foundation, a non-profit organisation dedicated to promoting spiritual principles and values . He was He was born in Palani, Tamil Nadu, India on January 22nd, 1984  . Ganesh Chaturthi is a Hindu festival that celebrates the birth of Ganesha, the elephant-headed god of wisdom and prosperity  It is observed by
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             installing clay idols of Ganesha in homes and public places, offering prayers and sweets, and immersing the idols in water after ten days . The festival falls between August and September every yearAyngaran Foundation celebrates Ganesh Chaturthi
Ganesh Chaturthi, also known as Vinayaka Chaturthi, is a festival that honors the elephant-headed god Ganesha, who is believed to remove obstacles and bring good fortune. The festival is celebrated with great devotion and enthusiasm by Hindus across India and around the world.

Name: Oak Pointe Church
Description: The vision of Oak Pointe Church is simple. Our desire is to be a place where anyone can find God, no matter where they are on their spiritual journey. We believe in the idea of “no shame, no same”. There’s no shame in where you’ve been, but we’re passionate to see you grow. We also have a heart to be part of the larger Church in Metro Detroit. We want to plant new churches and partner with existing churches. We want to see a movement of people throughout this region who know Jesus and make Him known.

Name: Zephyr Point Presbyterian Conference Center
Description: Zephyr Point has welcomed groups across the world to enjoy our Presbyterian Conference Center. Nestled in the Sierra Nevada, on a half-mile of Lake Tahoe shoreline, Zephyr Point provides reasonably priced cabins year round. Since 1924 this Presbyterian Conference Center has provided rest, rejuvenation and recreation for thousands. As we come upon our 100th year anniversary, we look back at the times when guests camped in tents on a rugged lakeside, Zephyr Point has grown into one of the leading cross-cultural and ecumenical Christian Centers in the nation. Zephyr Point accepts guests of all religions, ethnicity, and sexuality, and we take pride in providing a safe space for all groups to enjoy. A recent guest, a journalist from the Chicago Tribune wrote that Zephyr Point is “a haven where God’s presence seems to reach out from the snowy mountains and the deep blue lake to drape the world-weary visitors in a profound peace.”

Name: World Hope Ministries International
Description:
World Hope Ministries International (WHMI) is passionately engaged in the critical Great Commission task of "teaching them to observe all things...". We provide essential theological training to pastors and church leaders, empowering and equipping them to lead healthy churches that can affect gospel transformation. 

WHMI is a 501(c)(3) organization and an accredited member of the Evangelical Council for Financial Accountability.

Name: Church of England - Diocese of Gloucester
Description:
The Diocese of Gloucester is part of the Church of England and covers most of Gloucestershire, as well as parts of neighbouring counties. It is home to about 600,000 people.

The Diocese is led by the Right Revd Rachel Treweek, who made history by becoming the first female diocesan bishop and the first female bishop in the House of Lords. She is supported by the Bishop of Tewkesbury, the Rt Revd Robert Springett.

In the Diocese our communities are served by some 327 parishes, 390 churches and 117 church schools. About 140 stipendiary clergy offer spiritual and pastoral care, together with retired and non-stipendiary clergy, Readers, local ministry teams and other lay ministers.

Our vision is LIFE Together and to share the transforming Gospel of Jesus Christ, so that people may know life in all its fullness.

Name: JLE (Jewish Learning Exchange)
Description:
JLE is striving to inspire young Jews across the UK through learning and education and to make Judaism relevant & meaningful in the 21st Century.

Our aim is to show the significance and deeper meaning of Judaism to 21st century Jews and to enable them to make informed life decisions by providing the highest quality educational and social opportunities in a relevant and welcoming environment.

For 34 years, JLE has been developing meaningful relationships and delivering world-class Jewish Education to thousands of Jews, primarily in the United Kingdom. Today, the JLE centre has become a hub for Jews with a thirst to understand more about their heritage. We run programmes and initiatives within schools, on university campuses, for young professionals and programmes for men and women.  Within the centre, our students can also experience Shabbat Hospitality and attend one of the many social events occurring throughout the year. 

The JLE is a non-judgemental, warm and welcoming environment that allows young Jews a space to develop and enrich their Jewish knowledge and identity. Being both a social, communal and teaching space, the JLE is able to offer participants a chance to experience a sense of “belonging” whilst providing the skills to increase their day-to-day knowledge of Judaism.

Name: Holy Family School of Faith
Description:
Holy Family School of Faith leads people to meaning, hope, and happiness through deep friendship with Jesus and others. 

We’re a movement of people who live in deep friendship with Christ and others and constantly invite others into this way of life. 

Founded in 2005 by Dr. Mike Scherschligt and Dr. Troy Hinkel, Holy Family School of Faith is a public association of the lay faithful. Under the guidance of the Holy Spirit and the Blessed Mother, our team daily witnesses the fruits in their work with parishes, Catholic school faculties, and mentors through the Catholic Spiritual Mentorship Program. 

We annually form over 10,000 people by sharing the fullness of the Catholicism through live classes, pilgrimages, audio Rosaries, podcasts, and videos.

Name: SCQ Librería
Description: Librería y Cafetería que forma parte del Seminario Conciliar de Querétaro.

Name: JKI Injil Kerajaan - Gospel Of The Kingdom Church
Description: Gospel Of The Kingdom Church

Name: Nederlandse Zendingsraad (NZR)
Description: De Nederlandse Zendingsraad (NZR) is een netwerk waar kerken en zendingsorganisaties vanuit de breedte en diversiteit van Christelijk Nederland, elkaar ontmoeten, bevragen en uitdagen. We zijn verbonden door het evangelie van Jezus Christus. In de wereld van vandaag zoeken we samen naar inspiratie en wijsheid en moedigen we elkaar aan tot participatie in de Missio Dei in Nederland, Europa en wereldwijd.

Name: Hope Fellowship Church
Description:
We think you matter. 

At Hope, people matter to God and they matter to us. Everything we do from programming to hiring staff stems from that statement. We see a greater North Dallas area thriving with life, family, love, compassion, and faith all around us and believe that no matter what background you come from, your existence matters in all of it.
At every stage in life, you can find something for you at Hope. Our clean and safe kids areas allow you to drop off your kids with confidence while you enjoy our modern services that build upon the past and look to the future that God is building in our areas. 
There’s something for students, singles, young adults, married couples, those recovering from addiction, parents with kids with special needs, and more. We hope you will join us at a service soon to see for yourself.
Frisco East Campus (9950 Rolater Rd. Frisco, TX) meets on Saturdays at 5:00 PM and Sundays at 9:30 AM and 11:15 AM every week. 
Frisco West Campus (309 W. Main St. Frisco, TX) meets on Saturdays at 5:00 PM and Sundays at 9:30 AM and 11:15 AM every week. 
McKinney Campus (1702 University Dr. McKinney, TX) meets on Sundays at 9:30 AM and 11:15 AM every week. 
Prosper Campus (301 Eagle Dr. Prosper, TX) meets on Sundays at 9:30 AM and 11:15 AM every week (mobile campus at Prosper High School Auditorium).
Go to hopefellowship.net to see more about our church and we hope to see you at the Welcome Center this weekend!

Name: Mecklenburg Community Church
Description:
Mecklenburg Community Church (Meck) believes that being a part of the local church is not about religion, but a relationship with Jesus.  We are excited to be on the front lines of what God is doing in Charlotte, NC, and around the world.  For the past twenty years we have welcomed people to join us as they are; there isn't a mold you have to fit before you walk through our doors.  And it's a place where you can come with all of your questions, no matter what they are.

Meck is more than a weekend service.  We are a Bible study with friends, an online course in the Meck Institute, a misison trip to the Philippines to bring healing to girls rescued from human trafficking, and an hour during a weekend service investing in the life of a child. At Meck, we value community and believe that you'll find it here.

Meck is one church in multiple locations.  Just find the location that is closest to you, choose a service time, and we'll see you this weekend!

Name: Mission Hills Church
Description: Mission Hills is a place where you can be accepted as you are, experience freedom, receive grace, and heal from your past pain. We know that life can be messy, but you don’t have to navigate it alone. No matter where you are, or where you’ve been, you are welcome here. Don’t settle for incomplete; you were created for so much more.

Name: Baptist International Missions, Inc
Description:
A fundamental, Baptist Mission board serving and assisting the local church in sending missionaries around the world.  "Devoted to the commission, Dedicated to the call serving missionaries and local churches".

Understanding the importance of the Great Commission, our purpose is to assist independent Baptist churches in the establishment of like churches around the world. BIMI exists to serve the local church as it sends missionaries into God's harvest field. As the church's delegated servant, we provide accountability, training and assistance to our missionaries as they carry out their pre-field, on-field and furlough ministries. It is our desire to glorify God through the pursuit of excellence in every area of this worldwide ministry of faith.

Name: Pontifical Oriental Institute
Description:
The Pontifical Oriental Institute (Latin: Pontificium Institutum Orientalium Studiorum, Italian: Pontificio Istituto Orientale) or "Orientale" is the premier center for the study of Eastern Christianity in Rome, Italy.

The pontifical institute was established in 1917 by Pope Benedict XV. Pope Pius XI entrusted the Institute to the Society of Jesus in 1922, and with the 1928 encyclical "Rerum Orientalium" encouraged bishops to send students to the Institute to be formed as future professors in Oriental studies. In that same year, Pius XI associated the Institute with the Pontifical Gregorian University and the Pontifical Biblical Institute, thus forming the Gregorian Consortium.

Name: Arquidiocese de Joinville
Description:
A Arquidiocese de Joinville é composta por 18 municípios do Norte Catarinense, sendo eles: Joinville, Garuva, Itapoá, Itaiópolis, Mafra, Campo Alegre, Rio Negrinho, São Bento do Sul, Corupá, Guaramirim, Jaraguá do Sul, Massaranduba, Schroeder, Araquari, Balneário Barra do Sul, Barra Velha, São Francisco do Sul e São João do Itaperiú. 


Criado em janeiro de 1927 e elevada à arquidiocese em 5 de novembro de 2024, possui 67 paróquias e 490 comunidades, 79 diáconos e 148 padres entre diocesanos, religiosos, residentes e aposentados. 

Seu primeiro arcebispo é Dom Francisco Carlos Bach. 

Somos uma arquidiocese comprometida com ações evangelizadoras e que trabalha para incentivar as comunidade eclesiais missionárias. Desde 2017, com a chegada de Dom Francisco, há três palavras-chaves que norteiam a ação pastoral da nossa Igreja local: Unidade, Pastoreio e Comprometimento.

Nossa sede fica localizada no município de São José, estado de Santa Catarina e é responsável por atuar em todo o sul e sudeste do estado de Santa Catarina.

Name: Mennonite Mission Network
Description:
At Mennonite Mission Network, we are committed to partnering with local communities and congregations to embody God’s love and work for peace, justice, and reconciliation in the world. Rooted in the Anabaptist faith tradition, we walk alongside individuals and churches, both locally and globally, to nurture transformative relationships and serve where God leads.

We believe that God invites, Jesus sends, and the Holy Spirit empowers each of us to participate in God's mission. Whether through service, support, learning, or leadership, we are part of a growing movement that seeks to make Christ’s healing and hope visible in every context.

We’re always looking for compassionate, mission-driven individuals to join our team. Explore how your gifts can contribute to a meaningful global mission.

Come be a part of the story. Your journey starts here!

Name: Shalom Harvest Outreach Ministry
Description:
SHALOM HARVEST OUTREACH MINISTRY
Is a non denominational Evangelistic ministry. We proclaim the Gospel of the Jesus Christ Our vision is to reach with the Gospel to those who don't know Jesus. Our goal is not to establish a new church denomination, we want to save people those are under the yoke of Satan, those are sick, those are in poverty. We kneel down and pray for them. We affiliated with Pentecostal faith but our faith statement is written below.

FAITH STATEMENT
The One Triune God
We Believe in one God, Maker of all things and being in Trinity of Father, Son and Holy Spirit. We believe that in the unity of the Godhead there are three persons, equal in every divine perfections and executing distinct but harmonious offices in the great work of redemption.
The Scriptures
We Believe the Bible to be the inspired Word of God, as the rule of faith and conduct. We believe that the Holy Scriptures are from God, written by men divinely inspired by the Holy Spirit. This scripture are true without error, and reveals the principles by which God will judge us, and therefore shall remain to the end of the world.

Name: Mina Metrics
Description: Mina Metrics is a digital discipleship assessment tool intended for Christian institutions that provides precision in discipleship, resources for spiritual growth, and strategic clarity for leaders.

Name: Evangelical Fellowship of Zimbabwe
Description:
In 1962, a few Evangelical Church leaders came together to form what is now known as the Evangelical Fellowship of Zimbabwe (EFZ). Over the years its membership has grown to over 900 member bodies comprising Denominations, Church Bodies, Ministries and Para-Church Organizations. The Fellowship represents over 4.5 million individual members.

Over the years the EFZ has become a strong, growing and unifying platform for preaching the Gospel, equipping church leaders and facilitating fellowship and joint ministry, meeting the needs of vulnerable communities and being a positive influence in nation-building efforts. Local and International funding partners have played a significant role in assisting the EFZ to fulfil its holistic mandate of being salt and light to the nation; and some of the partners included IOM, Mercy Corps, Higher Life International, Tearfund, Living Water International, World Vision, EU, ISO23, Africare, CCSF and EPOIZ among many others.

Name: Midtown Church
Description:
HOPE, HEALTH, and HEALING. 

Midtown Covenant Church is a multiethnic, multiplying, reconciling, and disciple-making church. We are a church for the unchurched, those who have been burned by the church in the past, and those wondering if they are passionately welcomed back into the church after being gone. We care deeply about our city, the nation, and the world. We believe that Christ changes everything and provides us with the power and authority to make a transformative difference in the world. We share Christ’s heart for the vulnerable, marginalized, lost, and broken. We're committed to being a bridge of empowerment, unity, and love in a divided world.

Name: Eastside Christian Church - Anaheim, CA
Description:
Eastside is a non-denominational church with 4 locations in California, including Anaheim, Bellflower, Irvine, and Redlands. As well as Park Rapids, Minnesota, and Las Vegas, NV. We are committed to pursuing God, building community, and unleashing compassion both locally and globally. With engaging music, great kids and students services, and the personal teaching style of Pastor Gene Appel, we're a place where you can discover and take the next step on your spiritual journey.

For service times and locations, visit eastside.com/locations




Follow us! 
Facebook: eastsidecc
Instagram: eastside_cc

Name: Christian Revival Church
Description:
Christian Revival Church (CRC) is a dynamic, multi-site church founded by Senior Pastors At and Nyretta Boshoff in 1994, with a bold mission to win the lost at any cost. As one church in many locations, CRC has a powerful presence in South Africa:

Bloemfontein: 69 Vereeniging Drive, Fleurdal
Pretoria: 185 Lynnwood Road, Pretoria East
Johannesburg: Cnr Witkoppen & Riverbend Rd, Fourways

Transforming lives and spreading hope, CRC continues to impact communities nationwide.

Name: Diocese of Houma-Thibodaux
Description:
The Roman Catholic Diocese of Houma-Thibodaux was formed on June 5, 1977. The area covers the civil parishes of Terrebonne, Lafourche, parts of St. Mary (Morgan City & Amelia), and part of Jefferson (Grand Isle). The diocese includes 38 Church Parishes, 6 Mission Communities of Faith, 9 Catholic Elementary Schools, 3 Catholic High Schools, and several Diocesan Offices (including Catholic Charities) that serve and support our churches and schools.

Following Jesus’ instruction presented in Matthew 28:19, the mission of the Diocese of Houma-Thibodaux, simply put, is to form missionary disciples of Jesus Christ.

Name: ACN United Kingdom - Aid to the Church in Need
Description: Founded in 1947 as a Catholic aid organization for war refugees and recognized as a papal foundation since 2011, ACN is dedicated to the service of Christians around the world, through information, prayer and action, wherever they are persecuted or oppressed or suffering material need. ACN supports every year an average of 6000 projects in close to 150 countries, thanks to private donations, as the foundation receives no public funding.

Name: Trinity Presbyterian Church
Description: We are a 200-member church associated with the PC(USA), located in Northeast Raleigh. We invite lay leadership in our worship, seek to humbly offer witness to Christ in the world, and benefit local and international missions.

Name: Peace Lutheran Church
Description: A Family Gathered Around Christ to Eternally Influence Our Community

Name: Illinois Baptist State Association
Description: The Illinois Baptist State Association is an association of messengers from  nearly 1,000 Illinois Southern Baptist churches and missions cooperating to achieve common goals. The purpose of IBSA is to assist in establishing and developing effective churches in their context.

Name: Valley Creek Church
Description: Valley Creek Church is a Jesus-focused, Spirit-filled, Life-giving church. We are one church that meets in multiple campuses that carries hope to thousands of locations.

Name: مجمع خادم الحرمين الشريفين الملك سلمان بن عبدالعزيز آل سعود للحديث النبوي الشريف
International Training Alliance (ITA)
Description:
On October 15, 2005, in the Atlanta headquarters of Operation Mobilization USA, the leaders of eight different organizations discussed the critical need for leadership development across the worldwide mission scene. They decided to establish a partnership of organizations, with the aim to help each other in the training of leaders. They signed an agreement, giving birth to the International Training Alliance (ITA).

“Our vision is to help develop mission leaders” said the elected executive director Joop Strietman from the Netherlands, when he carefully explained the path that to the formation of the Alliance. “The dream is to see a network of ten to twenty organizations come into being, enabling the training of hundreds of missionary leaders and upcoming leaders each year all around the globe.” 

As the new CEO, Jeff Turnbough states that "our vision is to continue to energize leaders on mission globally with the same high quality training that ITA has been known to give."

Over 3,000 mission leaders have completed the training in the more than 100 courses that have been offered in over 40 nations. Participants from over 190 mission agencies working in over 100 nations have participated, and these numbers are growing every year.

The focus of the training is to give basic skills in such vital areas as building effective relationships, management, public relations, communication and personal life planning. The response of one of the participants, a fifty-year-old career missionary in France was: “This was the most intensive but also the most rewarding course I have ever attended.” 

More information about the background, the vision, the content and the upcoming courses is available on the website: www.wetrainleaders.org

Name: International Catholic Association of Exorcist
Description:
The International Catholic Association Of Exorcists is an organization for Catholic and Orthodox Catholic Churches, Clergy, Exorcists & Demonologists  

Our Mission is to serve the Catholic and Orthodox Catholic Churches of the world, to be a vital resource in fighting Satan and his minions.  

We accomplish this task by providing resources and assist in training Exorcists & Demonologists.  
Our goal is to ensure that they are adequately prepared to help their faith communities in Spiritual Warfare.  

If you are a Catholic or Orthodox Catholic Church or Clergy(Priests & Bishops) Please visit membership page for information on joining the Association.

Name: YM360 - YouthMinistry360
Description:
Bible Study Resources
At YM360 our desire is to see teenagers transformed through a deeper understanding of God and His Word. YM360 is committed to bringing you an ever-expanding offering of Bible-centered resources that are innovative, creative, and relevant. Whether you need resources for Sunday school, Wednesday nights, small groups, or retreats, YM360 has you covered.

https://youthministry360.com/


Training
YM360 is committed to continually providing training for you through the YM360 Blog. We’ll bring to the table the voices of experienced youth ministers and youth ministry leaders. These are the men and women who are in the trenches every day. Learn from them as they share with you the insights of their experience.

https://youthministry360.com/blogs/all


Community
You know too well the challenges of being a youth worker. The support of a strong community is invaluable. At YM360 we are bringing together a community of individuals whom each share the call to minister to teenagers. Our desire is to see you connect with other great youth workers through our social networks, special promotions, and events. Join us at the worlds largest online community of youth workers.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/youthpastorsonly/


Networking
There are a lot of excellent organizations doing an exceptional job of ministering to teenagers, and youth workers. At YM360 we’ll use the ym360 Blog and social media to highlight these ministries in order to show you the best of what’s out there.

Name: Brown Missionary Baptist Church
Description: Brown is committed to Changing Lives and Making A Difference while creating a community that loves God and people above all else.

Name: Jesuits USA Central and Southern Province
Description:
The Jesuits of the USA Central and Southern Province are one group within the worldwide Society of Jesus. Known especially for their schools, Jesuit ministries extend beyond the classroom, from hospitals to refugee camps, from retreat houses to parishes in all parts of the world. 
Jesuits—together with their lay collaborators—are called to do all things for the Greater Glory of God.

Name: Alleluia Ministries International
Description: Alleluia Ministries International is a Bible believing Christian Church, with its foundation rooted on the word of God. The Church started on the 24th of February 2002 in Lyndhurst, Johannessburg South Africa.

Name: IGMG Zuid-Nederland

Wij geloven dat de beste onder de mensen, de gene zijn die het goede voor de mensheid willen.
