Name: Congregation Emanu-El of the City of New York
Description:
Founded in 1845 as the first Reform Jewish Congregation in New York City, Temple Emanu-El is among the largest Jewish Houses of Worship in the world. We continue to build upon the legacy of leadership and the timeless values of Jewish tradition as we strive toward a more just, ethical and compassionate world.  Temple Emanu-El offers a robust religious school program for children of all ages, a first-rate nursery school, the Bernard Museum of Judaica, the Stettenheim Library, a range of community events through the Streicker Center, and more.   In addition, our Tikkun Olam Committee and Philanthropic Fund support a wide range of humanitarian, educational, and cultural causes, as well as initiatives that advance Reform Judaism and strengthen our connection to Israel.

We are a community of warmth and awareness where every individual matters. We embrace our diverse membership and offer all who comprise it a meaningful life grounded in Judaism. We commit our spiritual, intellectual, physical, and financial resources to strengthen our community today for a vital tomorrow.

Name: Archdiocese of Dubuque
Description:
We the Church of the Archdiocese of Dubuque, are God’s people gathering together as a Eucharistic community to worship, sharing the Word of God, affirming traditional and emerging leadership roles and supporting families to model Catholic faith and morality for the next generation.  

We commit ourselves to lifelong faith formation and to the service of humanity because we believe in the Risen Christ, in the wisdom and kindness of God, the author of life and in the love the Holy Spirit bestows.

Name: Disciples Church Extension Fund
Description:
Disciples Church Extension Fund (DCEF) helps ministries of the Christian Church (Disciples of Christ) in the United States and Canada as well as other denominations grow their mission through our faith-based transformational and financial services, ministry expertise, and a shared commitment to God’s vision for the Church. 

DCEF is an Equal Opportunity Employer committed to providing a workplace free from discrimination or harassment. We expect every member of the DCEF community to do their part to cultivate and maintain an environment where everyone feels included and is afforded the respect and dignity they deserve. We are committed to providing equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, and local laws.

Name: AFES (Australian Fellowship of Evangelical Students)
Description:
Proclaiming Jesus Christ at university to present everyone mature in him.

Universities in Australia are full of students who are poised to make life-changing decisions about what they believe and where they are heading. AFES works in this God-given window of opportunity to reach students for Christ.

AFES engages staff to help and encourage students in their Christian growth and witness, and has student groups on campuses in every state and territory in Australia. Groups hold a variety of activities, such as regular public Bible talks, smaller Bible studies, prayer groups, mid-year conferences and outreach events.

Name: Philly Frassati
Description: We at Philly Frassati envision a community of young adults who desire to share life together while pursuing that which is true, good and beautiful in order that they may experience life to the fullest.

Name: New International
Description:
New International is a mission sending agency that exists to proclaim Christ and make disciples globally. We join with God to restore His dominion on earth, as evidenced by people of all nations worshiping God, experiencing salvation and continuous life transformation, fulfilling their God-given purpose and fostering the emergence of Jesus-following communities. 

New International is always looking for individuals and coupes who feel burden for the lost and God's calling on their life for missions and cross-cultural ministry. The question often asked asked is "What is calling you to do?". With 3 billion people around the world still unreached by the Gospel there is a lace for everyone to be actively involved in the Great Commission. Short-term or long-term, join us! 

Founded in 1989, NMSI today is an international community of 200+ missionaries serving in 26 countries in ministries of church multiplication, community transformation, global youth ministry and marketplace ministry. Opportunities to serve overseas and in our home office vary in length: 2 weeks short-term projects, 6+ months apprenticeships, and long-term career missions (commitment of 3+ years). 

Learn more about what drives us, why we do what we do, our leadership by visiting our site. Find out what makes NMSI unique among other sending agencies by watching testimonies of current NMSI-ers. 

NMSI is a faith-based non-profit organization and offers no salaried positions. All NMSI missionaries and home office staff raise support. 

Go. Pray. Volunteer. Give. Advocate. What is God calling you to do?

Name: Creation Justice Ministries
Description:
Creation Justice Ministries educates, equips and mobilizes individuals, congregations, and communions/denominations to protect, restore, and rightly share God's Creation.  Based on the priorities of its 38 member Christian communions/denominations, with a particular concern for the vulnerable and marginalized, Creation Justice Ministries provides collaborative opportunities to build ecumenical community, guides people of faith and faith communities towards eco-justice transformations, and raises a collective witness in the public arena echoing Christ's call for just relationships among all of Creation.  

Though our ministry is explicitly Christian, we are not exclusively Christian and welcome partnership and collaboration with those feel called to care of God's Creation regardless of religious affiliation.

Creation Justice Ministries was born out of the National Council of Churches Eco-Justice Programs, which was founded in 1983.

Name: FCA Central & Western Mass
Description:
The Fellowship of Christian Athletes is touching millions of lives…one heart at a time. Since 1954, FCA has been challenging coaches and athletes on the professional, college, high school, junior high and youth levels to use the powerful platform of sport to reach every coach and every athlete with the transforming power of Jesus Christ. FCA focuses on serving local communities around the globe by engaging, equipping and empowering coaches and athletes to unite, inspire and change the world through the gospel. 

VISION

To see the world transformed by Jesus Christ through the influence of coaches and athletes.

MISSION

To lead every coach and athlete into a growing relationship with Jesus Christ and His church.

We pursue our vision and mission through the strategy of to and through the coach. We seek ministry first to coaches hearts, marriages and families.   Then, when ready, we minister through coaches to their fellow coaches, teams and athlete leaders.

Billy Graham said, “A coach will impact more people in one year than the average person will in an entire lifetime.” With the influence of a coach, FCA recognizes the most strategic way to reach more athletes is to first reach the coach.

Name: Finding Purpose Ministry
Description: Finding Purpose is a ministry that offers men opportunities to gather for fellowship, prayer, study, and to fuel a passion for God’s Word. Since 2003, we have pursued our mission to glorify God by making Him known and guiding men toward their life’s purpose. We believe the only way to understand one’s purpose in life is through a personal relationship with Jesus Christ.

Name: River Pointe & West End Church
Description: River Pointe & West End Church is a safe place where real people do life together and discover how hopeful life can be in a relationship with Christ.

Name: Covenant Presbyterian Church
Description:
Covenant's Identity:
Who are we?
We are people striving together to live
In gratitude for the Almighty‘s grace
In relationship with Jesus Christ
In union with the Holy Spirit
In accordance with Holy Scripture

Covenant’s Mission:
What are we here to do?
We are here to
Nurture a loving and inclusive family
Make new followers of Christ
Foster growth in believers
Serve the people of our urban setting
and the world beyond
Honor God in authentic worship – and all we do!

Name: Linfield Christian School
Description:
The Vision of Linfield Christian School is to be the standard of excellence in Christ-centered, college preparatory education.

Linfield Christian School is an independent college preparatory school that exists to challenge boys and girls and young men and women ---

To know Jesus Christ as Lord

To love others as themselves

To grow in knowledge and skill

--- in order that they may serve the Lord and the world through their character and leadership.

Name: Centenary Methodist Church
Description:
Centenary is a community of faith that seeks to worship God.  We believe that the Bible is our guide for our faith and practice, and that God has provided a way for people to have a relationship with Himself through the person of Jesus Christ.   

Centenary is a Methodist congregation in the Wesleyan tradition.  That is, we believe that God desires all people to come into a relationship with God through Christ.  As Wesleyans, we interpret the Bible in light of relationships.  Our mission statement reflects our emphasis on relationships: "To strengthen and transform lives by living and sharing Christ's love."  This is grounded in the New Testament words of Jesus who said in Matthew 25:37-39, "Love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind."  And, "Love your neighbor as yourself."

Centenary offers multiple opportunities for Bible study and life in the community with other Christians.  

Sunday School small groups meet at 10:00 a.m. and are available for all ages.

We have two Sunday morning worship services, the traditional service meets at 11:15 a.m. and a more contemporary service is offered at 9.00 a.m.

Name: British Union Conference of Seventh-day Adventists
Description:
The British Union Conference is comprised of 2 conferences and 3 missions.

Our mission is to make disciples of Jesus Christ who live as His loving witnesses and proclaim to all people the everlasting gospel of the Three Angels' Messages in preparation for His soon return.

Name: Hope Bible Church
Description:
Hope Bible Church Oakville began early 2003.
God placed a calling and a dream within a small group of people to see a Harvest Bible Chapel come to the Greater Toronto Area. Through supernatural circumstances, a core group of people was formed and the momentum was building. It wasn’t long before ideas were being articulated, prayer meetings were being scheduled and plans were underway for the launch of the second Canadian Harvest Bible Chapel.

A Bible study began in the summer of 2003 in response to the demand from people who were passionate about this church plant. The first Bible study met in a local office space. When those quarters became too tight, the Bible study was moved to a church basement in Mississauga. By the grace of God, this core group continued to grow and develop and attract high-quality disciples of Christ. It was in the early fall of 2003 that God clearly led the leadership from Harvest Oakville to be connected with the leadership from Calvary Baptist Church in Oakville.

In what can only be described as miraculous, God arranged for Calvary Baptist Church to become Harvest Bible Chapel Oakville which included giving Harvest their church building, property and most importantly their people. This was an incredible process that presented many challenges. However, we believe this kind of unprecedented event in our area is a sign of what God is up to and what He will do to accomplish His will in building His Church! In 2018 Harvest Bible Chapel officially joined the family of like-minded churches affiliated with the Great Commission Collective. Their desire for planting churches, for connection and relationship in the gospel, and for world-wide evangelism really struck a chord with our leadership team as something our church family should be part of. As of Easter 2019, we are officially Hope Bible Church Oakville, and we are excited to see what the Lord has in store.

Name: Rama Baktha
Description:
This website is devoted to Rama, Hanuman and Spirituality as a whole. The Knowledge and visions of the ancient sages and Siddhas is shared with devotion and love. As we all know the enlightened experience is beyond books and the human mind itself. But our limited minds need to be fed with the knowledge from the minds who have had the experience. 

The inability of people to understand the power of the human mind and its supernatural powers has created a lot of divisions among each other. If man learns to scientifically understand the subconscious powers of the human mind this will create a clearer view of the universe. The truth is one we just call in it in many names and we give it some philosophical meaning.

The Truth is what we create for ourselves, what we believe becomes our reality. All changes outside and within you is experienced by you. You are not the body nor the mind you are pure consciousness. The only connecting factor between your existence and consciousness is the “I”. 

Your existence and experience of living is the projection of the intelligent universe. Intelligence is pure consciousness, which in turn is raw energy.  This energy in its absolute state is a compressive stock force. The universal energy functions in the form of circular motion which is the most basic form of intelligence. All living forms in this universe are a manifestation of this energy functioning in various levels frequencies. 

This universal intelligent energy is never born nor it dies it’s under a constant eternal change. This energy exists within empty space, but remember nothing can come out of empty space nor become it. This Empty space and Energy co-exist as the absolute reality. This Empty space is the absolute eternal nothingness and the energy is the transformational eternal force, which brings this empty space also to the understanding of the human mind.

Name: Manna Church
Description:
Our mission is to glorify God by equipping His people to change their world and by planting churches with the same world-changing vision.

Manna Church has been serving military communities for over 40 years and is strategically planting expressions of Manna Church along the Military Highway. We believe that the Church exists wherever God’s people gather. An expression of Manna Church could meet in its own designated building, a local school or gym, a home, or even online.

Name: Diocese of Norwich (Church of England)
Description:
We are the Church of England across the areas of Norfolk and Waveney - a Christian presence serving every community through a family of 650 churches and 110 schools and academies; together Committed to Growth.

Our churches play a key role in major life events such as weddings and funerals.

The Diocese of Norwich serves over a hundred Church Schools and Academies - almost a quarter of the primary schools in Norfolk and Waveney.

Name: ICF Zurich
Description: ICF is a free, non-denominational church with a biblical foundation. Authentic, relevant and excited about life with Jesus.

Name: Christian Life Church
Description: A non-denominational church in Chester, (Chesterfield) Virginia VA. An established and growing church leading people into life changing relationships with Jesus. We are located off I-95 and easily accessible from Richmond, Chesterfield, Colonial Heights, Petersburg, Hopewell and Fort Lee.

Name: Buckfast Abbey
Description:
Buckfast Abbey - Home to a community of Benedictine Monks, nestled in the shadow of Dartmoor, in a beautiful wooded valley beside the River Dart.

Since time immemorial, deer have been drawn to graze in the sheltered meadows which line the banks of the river Dart in this Devonshire valley and quench their thirst in the cooling waters which flow here from high up on the moors and down towards the sea.

For nearly a thousand years, people have been drawn here, too. They have come to search for God. For God who is everywhere, certainly, but as they happened upon this place, they sensed that its beauty, its silence and tranquillity, its community of people who were similarly searching, made it a place where God might easily be found.

Name: Diocese of Sacramento
Description: We, the People of God of the Catholic Diocese of Sacramento, guided by the Holy Spirit, are called by Christ to proclaim the Good News of the Kingdom of God through prayer, praise and sacraments and to witness the Gospel values of love, justice, forgiveness and service to all.

Name: South African Theological Seminary
Description:
The South African Theological Seminary (SATS) which is a fully accredited and registered, online education institution. We focus on equipping you for ministry, right where you are in your local church.

- Bible-based, Christ-centred, Spirit-led
- Credits for prior learning and experience
- Enrol at any time of the year
- Pay as you go.

Name: The Belonging Co
Description: Encounter over Entertainment. Intimacy over Industry. Presence over Presentation. People over Position. Jesus over Everything.

Name: Sisters of the Order of Saint Benedict
Description:
We are a monastic community of women who seek God in our daily lives according to the Rule of Benedict. Through our ministry of prayer, work and community living, we listen and respond to the needs of the Church and the world. 

Saint Benedict’s Monastery is located in St. Joseph, Minn., and Saint Scholastica Convent, our retirement and assisted living facility, is located in St. Cloud, Minn. We have more than 100 employees across both campuses who serve in a variety of roles, including administration, development, food services, health care, wellness and environmental services. Employees get the opportunity to work among the religious women who live here.

Our ministries include education, health care, pastoral ministry and social ministry, among many others. Our donors, volunteers and employees help make our ministries possible by giving gifts of money, time and compassion. If you are interested in serving alongside the Sisters of the Order of Saint Benedict and helping to further our ministries, stay connected and reach out to us.

Name: Ministry Leadership Center
Description: The Ministry Leadership Center equips leaders and faith communities for transformational ministry so future generations embody Christ’s love in the world.

Name: Baltimore-Washington Conference of The United Methodist Church
Description:
Under the leadership of Bishop LaTrelle Easterling and Lay Leader Delores Martin, the Baltimore-Washington Conference of The United Methodist Church is made up of 1,024 clergy and 177,031 members in 642 churches in Maryland, Washington, D.C., the panhandle of West Virginia and Bermuda.

As a connectional church, the Baltimore-Washington Conference joins in ministry with churches around the world in 68 other annual conferences that make up the denomination, which totals more than 12 million members. The mission of the conference is to call, equip, send and support spiritual leaders to make disciples for the transformation of the world.

The conference staff of more than 50 people does its ministry at the BWC’s Mission Center in Fulton, Md., at a satellite office at 12 S. Walnut St. in Hagerstown, and at three camps near Annapolis, Harper’s Ferry and Berkeley Springs, W.Va.

Name: CROSSROADS CHURCH
Description: CROSSROADS Church is a place where faith and life meet.  People come to CROSSROADS to hear inspiring music and motivating messages.  Small groups are also a huge connecting point for members at CROSSROADS.

Name: Evangelische Kirche von Westfalen
Description: Evangelische Kirche von Westfalen is an internet company based out of Germany.

Name: Congregation Emanu-El of San Francisco
Description:
Congregation Emanu-El is dedicated to advancing our members' lifelong involvement in Judaism, engaging them in communal prayer (avodah), the study of sacred texts (Torah), and the performance of compassionate deeds (g'milut hasadim).

When intrepid Jews journeyed around the Horn or overland in 1848 as part of the migration west, little did they imagine that they would be helping to found one of the most dynamic Jewish communities in America. San Francisco—the city that sparked the imagination of a nation—is the home of Northern California's landmark temple, Congregation Emanu-El. Officially established in 1850, our congregation has over 2,100 households, many of whom have been involved with the congregation since its founding.  We are the oldest congregation west of the Mississippi.

Name: جمعية الدعوة بالروضة
Description: لا يخفى على مسلم أهمية الدعوة إلى الله تعالى فهي الطريق إلى صلاح البلاد والعباد، وهي وظيفة صفوة الخلق من الأنبياء و المرسلين عليهم الصلاة و السلام، وقد دلت نصوص الكتاب و السنة على أهميتها و بينت جزيل ثواب من قام بها، فمما ورد في الكتاب العزيز قوله تعالى : (( وادع إلى ربك )) وقوله عز وجل : (( ادع إلى سبيل ربك بالحكمة والموعظة الحسنة )) وقوله تعالى : (( ولتكن منكم أمة يدعون إلى الخير ويأمرون بالمعروف وينهون عن المنكر وأولئك هم المفلحون )) [آل عمران104]. قال ابن القيم -رحمه الله-: "والداعون إلى الخير هم الداعون إلى كتاب الله وسنة رسوله - صلى الله عليه وسلم -، لا الداعون إلى رأي فلان وفلان". وقوله تعالى : (( قل هذه سبيلي أدعو إلى الله على بصيرة أنا ومن اتبعني وسبحان الله وما أنا من المشركين )) [يوسف 108]. قال ابن كثير - رحمه الله -: "يقول تعالى لرسوله - صلى الله عليه وسلم - إلى الثقلين الجن والإنس آمراً له أن يخبر الناس أن هذه سبيله أي طريقته ومسلكه وسنته، وهي الدعوة إلى شهادة أن لا إله إلا الله وحده لا شريك له، يدعو بها على بصيرة من ذلك ويقين وبرهان هو وكل من اتبعه يدعو إلى ما دعا إليه رسول الله - صلى الله عليه وسلم - "على بصيرة ويقين وبرهان عقلي شرعي"

Name: Saint Patrick's Cathedral, Dublin
Description:
Built in honour of Ireland’s patron saint, Saint Patrick’s Cathedral stands adjacent to the famous well where tradition has it Saint Patrick baptised converts on his visit to Dublin.

The parish church of Saint Patrick on this site was granted collegiate status in 1191, and raised to cathedral status in 1224. The present building dates from 1220. The Cathedral is today the National Cathedral of the Church of Ireland (a church of the Anglican communion) and also serves as a popular tourist attraction in Ireland.

Name: Anglican Mission in the Americas
Description: The Anglican Mission in the Americas (The Mission) exists to raise, release and support leaders and communities of faith to reach the lost for Jesus Christ in North America. Rooted in the Celtic missionary tradition and nourished by the three streams of Scripture, the Sacramental Life and the Holy Spirit, The Mission has catalyzed over 200 church plants since its establishment in 2000. Through a culture of creativity, flexibility, boldness, learning and leadership, The Mission is committed and poised to build on its rich history.

Name: St. Anthony Shrine
Description: The “Church on Arch Street” is a center for the Roman Catholic ministries in Boston, MA, staffed by the Franciscan friars of Holy Name Province. The central focus of the Shrine’s ministry has remained constant for 70+ years: providing a convenient and prayerful setting for people desiring to praise and worship our God – currently through 12 weekend and seven weekday Masses, as well as an extensive schedule for the Sacrament of Reconciliation. The Shrine also has over 25 outreach and evangelical ministries

Name: Stonebriar Community Church
Description:
Stonebriar exists to encourage all people to pursue a lifelong, joyous relationship with Jesus Christ. 

Our history dates back to 1998, when a small group of people, including Senior Pastor Chuck Swindoll, founded a church in Frisco, Texas. 

We are a nondenominational, evangelical Christian church. That means that we are governed by our own elders, not an outside organization. About 3,500 people worship at Stonebriar each Sunday.

Name: Christ Church Cathedral Dublin
Description:
Situated in the heart of the city, Christ Church Cathedral is Dublin’s oldest working structure, and has welcomed pilgrims and visitors for almost 1,000 years. A working cathedral and a registered charity (RCN20005658 / CHY4271), Christ Church is renowned for its magnificent architecture and fascinating history. Admire the stunning stained glass and unique floor tiles of the cathedral nave. Learn the secrets of Strongbow’s tomb and uncover the amazing story of the heart of St. Laurence O’Toole, Dublin’s patron saint. Explore the Medieval Crypt, the largest in Ireland, and visit the Treasures of Christ Church exhibition featuring priceless silver and Ireland’s first copy of Magna Carta. Take a guided tour, climb up the medieval staircase to the Belfry and ring the famous Christ Church bells. Before departing, visit the Foxy Friar gift shop and browse the wide variety of beautiful gifts and crafts exclusive to Christ Church Cathedral.
The Cathedral stages many events throughout the year from free lunchtime concerts to lectures on a diverse range of subjects. There are also regular night-time events, including extra special performances such as the ‘Carols by Candlelight’ events at Christmas time which features the Cathedral Choir. The Cathedral’s historic nature offer a perfect setting for any corporate event you wish to host, including, receptions, dinners, product launches and conferences.

Name: Institute for Jewish Spirituality
Description:
The Institute for Jewish Spirituality (IJS) envisions a world in which spiritual practice is a vital part of Jewish life, leading to greater peace, compassion and justice. IJS’s mission is to develop and teach Jewish spiritual practices so that individuals and communities may experience awareness, purpose, and interconnection.

Founded in 1999, IJS has led the development of our core practices of mindfulness meditation, tikkun middot (the cultivation of sacred character traits), Torah study, prayer, and embodied practices such as yoga. We are currently developing two additional core practices of Sacred Listening and Ritual Practice. Grounded in Jewish values and thought, these practices enable participants to deepen their inner lives and connect more mindfully with others, Judaism, and the sacred.

In 2022, IJS announced a strategic plan outlining growth in three major program domains: serving the spiritual needs of people across the lifespan, leadership development, and building the field of Jewish spirituality. In support of the strategic plan, IJS has secured over $3.6 million in new multi-year commitments from individuals and foundations. These investments are the foundation of a combined $16 million in grants, individual contributions, and program fees the Institute seeks to raise and earn over the next four years to achieve the plan’s goals. The Institute seeks to expand and diversify its staff as a cornerstone investment in fulfilling its mission and strategic objectives, and as such we encourage candidates from diverse backgrounds to apply.

Name: Creating a Culture of Renewal
Description:
As a church leader, you deal with division within your church and society, a seemingly endless amount of people who are hurting and need help, and struggle to receive the support you need from your congregation emotionally, physically, and financially.

Creating a Culture of Renewal helps church leaders and their congregations unite in purpose and vision, thereby reducing conflict, increasing the flow of resources, and reversing the decline of congregation numbers.  
We understand the myriad challenges you face as a church leader. We’ve helped hundreds of church leaders and their congregations create audacious visions and make them a reality! We call it dreaming like Jesus.

Through the group leadership program, Creating a Culture of Renewal, you can interrupt church decline, and be empowered to do the impossible while you deepen your faith. All so that your church can dream and lead like Jesus again!

You’ll learn to: 
•	Craft a bold vision that exponentially expands what you and your congregation(s) thought possible. 
•	Dramatically increase the number of people actively engaged in ministry. 
•	Transform a risk-averse culture into a congregation willing to dream like Jesus, enthusiastically tackling new initiatives and launching new ministries.

Creating a Culture of Renewal was founded by Rev. Rebekah Simon-Peter, in 2011. She recognized that many church leaders were dealing with people problems and the struggle to maintain congregational vitality, instead of being successful in their desire to bring the Kingdom to life. Creating a Culture of Renewal has empowered thousands of church leaders refocus their congregations on the love of Jesus.
When a church leader is in the thick of things, making a significant positive impact on the community can be near impossible. Creating a Culture of Renewal can help you achieve the impossible.

Download your free DARE to Dream Like Jesus Plan:
https://rebekahsimonpeter.com/dream-like-jesus

Name: Maryknoll Lay Missioners
Description:
Maryknoll Lay Missioners is a Catholic mission-sending community committed to nonviolence through prevention, intervention, reconciliation and the restoration of all creation.
Compelled by faith to engage with people across cultures and ethnicities, we live, love and work with communities on the margins to promote active nonviolence and healing.
We are long-term missioners and missioner families, serving in nine African, Asian and American countries. Striving to create an inclusive and anti-racist world, we live and work with those most excluded in society to transform unjust structures together.
Jesus’ call to love our neighbors as ourselves extends throughout our interconnected world. And Pope Francis’ call to “go to the margins” means embracing the Gospel’s preferential option for the poor throughout our global “neighborhood.” 
For half a century, Maryknoll Lay Missioners has sent almost 1,000 men, women and families to serve in 34 countries. Thousands more have walked with us by lending their financial, moral and prayerful support. 
Being missioners today is different from the missionary models of previous centuries. 
Today our missioners work with local partners, building their capacity to provide for their own communities and to work for more just and compassionate societies. 
We foster greater global solidarity by accompanying people from different cultures, races, languages and religions.
Listening to each other’s realities, we build relationships of mutual friendship and respect to help heal today’s fractured world.
Maryknoll lay missioners 
•	commit to and practice our core values of simple living, community, joy, inclusion and humility. 
•	include single women and men, married couples and families with children.
•	range from young people in their 20s to those who have joined after retirement.
•	serve in Bolivia, Brazil, El Salvador, Haiti, Kenya, Tanzania, South Sudan, Cambodia and at the U.S.-Mexico border.
•	work in education, healthcare, justice and pe

Name: The Gideons International in South Africa
Description:
The Gideons International is an Association of Christian business and professional men and their wives dedicated to telling people about Jesus through associating together for service, sharing personal testimony, and providing Bibles and New Testaments.

While we are often recognized for our work with hotels, we also place and distribute Scriptures in strategic locations so they are available to those who want them, as well as to those who may not know they need them.

Name: Terry Modica, Good News Ministries
Description: Good News Ministries of Tampa Bay is a Catholic adult faith formation and evangelization ministry in the Diocese of St. Petersburg. We offer online and in-parish seminars, retreats, courses, and parish missions here and around the world. Terry Modica is an international speaker who builds faith, heals broken hearts, and changes lives for Christ. Our online services include daily Good News Reflections on the readings Catholic Mass, WordByte articles on living the faith and how to evangelize, prayer resources, and books. As a non-profit charitable organization, GNM charges no fees but accepts donations as support for its ministries.

Name: Roman Catholic Diocese of Brooklyn
Description: Founded in 1853, the Roman Catholic Diocese of Brooklyn serves the boroughs of Brooklyn and Queens, New York. The boroughs’ combined population stands at more than 4.9 million, of which 1.5 million identify themselves as Catholics. Often dubbed the “Diocese of Immigrants,” members come from 167 countries where Mass is regularly offered in two dozen languages.

Name: Igreja Unasp SP
Description:
Fundada em 1917, a Igreja Unasp SP é um dos maiores templos adventistas da América Latina, tanto em tamanho, quanto em importância. Com mais de 4 mil membros, possui uma vasta estrutura, oferecendo comodidade acústica e estética. Com um ambiente agradável, a igreja proporciona um lugar perfeito para encontro com amigos, familiares e principalmente, com Deus.

A presença virtual da Igreja Unasp SP é notável: com mais de 300 mil seguidores em suas redes sociais, em sua denominação é a igreja local com maior número de seguidores em todo o mundo.

Name: Bhakti-Link
Description:
-Bhakti-Link – Your Divine Connection
BhaktLink is an innovative platform designed to bridge the gap between devotees and religious places by offering seamless access to spiritual experiences. Our mission is to enhance devotion through technology, making it easier for people to connect with their faith from anywhere in the world.

With BhaktiLink, devotees can:
 Experience live streaming of aartis, poojas, and religious ceremonies
 Book prasad & receive blessings directly at their doorstep
 Get real-time updates about temple events and schedules
 Connect with verified travel guides for religious tourism
 Make secure donations and prevent fraudulent activities
 Access historical and mythological information about sacred places
 Video call priests or temple guides for personalized spiritual guidance

Whether you’re seeking divine blessings, exploring the rich heritage of religious places, or supporting temple activities, BhaktiLink ensures a seamless and authentic spiritual journey.

 Stay connected to your faith, anytime, anywhere – with BhaktiLink.

Name: BRAVE Church
Description: We exist to Challenging BRAVE Warriors for Courageous Kingdom Advancement.

Name: Valley Creek Church
Description: Valley Creek Church is a Jesus-focused, Spirit-filled, Live-giving church. We are one church that meets in multiple campuses that carries hope to thousands of locations.

Name: Chabad Piazza Bologna, Rome
Description:
Chabad Piazza Bologna in Rome, Italy offers the following services: Religious, educational, holidays, lifecycle and multimedia for locals, students, tourists and youth.
For information on Jewish Rome visit www.JewishRome.com, for information on Jewish and Kosher Italy visit www.Jewish.it.

Name: International Quran Reciter Academy
Description:
International Quran Reciter Academy is an online Quran academy, which provides the online premier Islamic and Quran education to Muslim students all over the world.
Our academy is the online premier Islamic academy for individuals who want to learn Islamic and Quran education on the web with the help of multiple courses. We have started a great educational program to learn the Quran and Islamic education.
Our online tutors use web-based learning devices and combine old and current educational technologies. Learn Quran and Islam through our online Islamic academy and experience it for yourself.

Name: Command Church
Description:
Command Church stands as an embodiment of unwavering commitment to the bedrock of divine wisdom, the Bible. Our guiding principles find root in the practices elucidated within the New Testament, approached with contextual understanding. Central to our faith is an unshakable belief in the potency inherent in the appellation of Jesus. Miracles, signs, and wonders are not mere notions but integral facets of our conviction.

With steadfast hearts, we embrace the imperative of hearing and obeying the sacred word of God. Our devotion rests in acknowledging its entirety, without selective discernment. We hold resolute that the veracity of the scriptures is unadulterated truth, impervious to the sway of external opinions.

Name: Carlisle Cathedral
Description:
Discover, experience and enjoy: over 900 years of extraordinary history; our famous starlight ceiling; our treasury exhibition; active family trails; an oasis of peace and prayer in the heart of the city with beautiful monastic gardens, award-winning café and unique gift shop.

There are fascinating stories of faith, hope and love to discover here. They point to God who is at work among us now and who welcomes all. Whether you are of this faith, another faith, or no faith at all, we look forward to welcoming you.

Name: FAMBRAS
Description:
A Federação das Associações Muçulmanas do Brasil – FAMBRAS, foi fundada em 1979 pelo visionário Hajj Hussein Mohamed El Zoghbi. À época, os mais importantes representantes de associações islâmicas do país se reuniram em Brasília. Manifestaram, na ocasião, total apoio na criação de uma entidade que se comprometesse com as causas ligadas à comunidade e suas instituições.

A FAMBRAS atua nos âmbitos social, humanitário, religioso, econômico e diplomático, com projetos que visam à divulgação do Islam e ações de desenvolvimento humano. A entidade também foi pioneira na implantação do conceito e sistema Halal no Brasil, se configurando hoje como a mais importante referência sobre o Islam no país e um dos principais modelos em certificação Halal no mundo.

Na área social e humanitária, a FAMBRAS atua de maneira exemplar, tanto em benefício da comunidade muçulmana quanto das comunidades carentes, oferecendo doação de alimentos, atendimentos médicos e bolsas de estudos, entre outras importantes ações.

Por ser uma organização filantrópica, a Federação possui sólidas parcerias com entidades nacionais e internacionais, que a auxiliam na concretização dos projetos. Ao longo dos 40 anos de existência, a entidade tem buscado construir uma sociedade mais humana.

Segura de suas ações e responsabilidades, assim como mantendo intactos os valores religiosos e princípio de seu fundador, a FAMBRAS continua escrevendo sua história, incentivando o conhecimento da religião e cultura islâmica e preconizando a paz aos seres humanos para que assim o mundo se torne melhor.

Name: Ahmadiyya Muslim Jama`at Canada
Description:
This is the official LinkedIn page of the Ahmadiyya Muslim Jama`at Canada.

The Ahmadiyya Muslim Jama‘at (AMJ) is a dynamic, fast growing international revival movement within Islam. Founded in 1889, AMJ spans over 200 countries with membership exceeding tens of millions.

Name: Evangelisch-reformierte Kirche Schweiz EKS
Description:
Die EKS ist die nationale Dachorganisation der evangelisch-reformierten Kirchen der Schweiz. Wir sind gemeinsam mit den Kirchgemeinden, den Mitgliedkirchen und den Werken als Kirche berufen, in diesem Land Gottes Liebe zu der Welt, zu allen Geschöpfen und jedem einzelnen Menschen zu bezeugen, vorzuleben und aus der Hoffnung auf Gottes Gnade zu wirken. 
Die EKS ist ein Garant für ausgewogene und durchdachte Positionen aus theologisch-ethischer Perspektive zu relevanten Gesellschaftsfragen.
Die EKS repräsentiert die evangelischen Kirchen der Schweiz und bringt ihr reformatorisches Erbe aktiv in die Ökumene als Lern-, Lebens,- Zeugnis- und Hoffnungsgemeinschaft ein.
Die EKS ist die zentrale Auskunftsstelle für Fragen zu Religion, Ethik, Kirche aus reformierter Perspektive.

Our guiding principles find root in the practices elucidated within the New Testament, approached with contextual understanding. Central to our faith is an unshakable belief in the potency inherent in the appellation of Jesus. Miracles, signs, and wonders are not mere notions but integral facets of our conviction.

Name: Christ Fellowship McKinney
Description:
We are a non-denominational, Bible-based church with contemporary worship. Our family-focused ministry offers support groups, Bible study, a Christian preschool, life groups, parenting and marriage courses – all in both English and Spanish. We are one church, two languages.

We are a diverse community of people from McKinney, Allen, Prosper, Celina, Frisco, and other North Texas communities, to around the globe with our online ministry. Wherever you are, we welcome you!

Christ Fellowship • 2801 Orchid Drive,  McKinney, TX • info@cfhome.org

Name: Congregation B'nai Israel of Boca Raton
Description: Congregation B’nai Israel is an inclusive Reform congregation that offers a nurturing home to all who seek a meaningful and enduring connection to Judaism. We strive to serve the needs of families and singles by embracing Jews from all backgrounds. Our services, led by our clergy, are innovative, diverse and filled with song, joy and spirituality. From preschool education, guest speakers and social groups to philanthropic initiatives that unite us, we offer a multitude of engaging opportunities rooted in faith and togetherness.

Name: WoodsEdge Community Church
Description: WoodsEdge is a nondenominational Christian Church founded by lead pastor Jeff Wells in 1993. Located on a beautiful 65-acre campus, WoodsEdge serves a diverse and growing community in The Woodlands and Spring, Texas. With a mission to Love Jesus, Journey Together, and Bring Hope to the World, WoodsEdge focuses on prayer, discipleship, missional living, and church planting. The church’s vision is to see Houston become a city of God. WoodsEdge offers worship services every weekend in both English and Spanish, as well as a Wednesday night prayer service. Description: Encuentro Juvenil es un movimiento laico de jóvenes, para jóvenes, sin ánimo de lucro, católico, no excluyente, que trata de hacer realidad el sueño de cambiar el mundo empezando por nosotros mismos.

Name: Sisters of Providence of Saint Mary-of-the-Woods
Description: The Sisters of Providence of Saint Mary-of-the-Woods, Indiana, are a congregation of Roman Catholic women religious (sisters) who minister throughout the United States, in Taiwan  and China. Saint Mother Theodore Guerin founded the Sisters of Providence in 1840. The congregation has a mission of being God's Providence in the world by committing to performing works of love, mercy and justice in service among God's people. Lay employees assist in the day-to-day affairs of the congregation.

Name: Reconstructing Judaism
Description:
Reconstructing Judaism is the central organization of the Reconstructionist movement. We train the next generation of rabbis, support and uplift congregations and havurot, foster emerging expressions of Jewish life, and encourage people to be their best selves — always helping to shape what it means to be Jewish today and to imagine the Jewish future.

Reconstructionists approach Judaism — and life — with deep consideration of the past and a passion to relate it to the present. We have originated many of the core innovations of today’s Judaism and lead efforts to make our congregations and havurot even more groundbreaking, inclusive and relevant.

Name: M.A.D Max, Productions
Description:
"The Certifiably TRUE Ravings Of A Sectioned Philosopher", is a droller take on the subjects of mental health, political issues and Nietzschean, Christian, Jungian, existentialist and post-modern philosophy. Don’t be afraid to question your world view, don’t be afraid to think you might be a bit ‘mad’. Who isn’t?

It is based on the author, Max J. Lewy’s, own experience as an oh-sopatient patient in the N.H.S. Mental Health System. Veritably knocked off his horse by two out-of-control, gaslighting shrinks at the tender age of 23, his writings trace his recovery from this lifechanging, iatrogenic incident over the next 12 or so years, exploring the ‘mad’ identity that was placed upon him and the truly insane, or certainly very flawed and eye-brow raising System which so unfortunately often does such things to quite healthy and relatively rational people. 
***
Madness has always fascinated and terrified the mind of man, in equal measure. In today's medicalized, 'normalized' world, it has come to be seen simply as a 'disease', an ugly blight on the smooth, cog-like operation of the social organism. Our very language has become impoverished by the steady stream of scientistic neologisms which have rushed to take its stead, leaving us with only the cold apparatus of an all-too suspect, bullying a

Name: WatersEdge
Description:
WatersEdge exists to financially empower donors and ministries to transform lives with the Gospel of Jesus Christ. We accomplish this through:
