Name: The Summit Church
Description:
The Summit Church is not about a building, a program, or a method. It's about people!  People of all ages who need a purpose in life.

The mission of The Summit Church is simple: To cooperate with God in developing  multiplying communities of fully devoted followers of Jesus Christ.

The Summit is committed to reaching the next generation for Christ. Understanding the times, we believe that the next generation can be reached for God's glory through truth and grace.

The Summit Church is longing for more than the status quo. We are a community on mission with God sharing the only true hope for this world: the life-changing power of Jesus Christ.

The Summit Church is a place where God and people meet...
a place where people become what they were meant to be.

Name: Aid To The Church in Need Australia & New Zealand (ACN)
Description:
In 1947 Fr Werenfried van Straaten (the bacon Priest) founded Aid to the Church in Need. Today it has 600,000 benefactors in 16 countries. The organisation approves over 8,000 requests for aid annually from Eastern Europe, the former Soviet Union and the Third World. It has been granted the status of a Universal Public Association by the Holy See.

A Catholic Charity dependent on the Holy See, providing pastoral relief to needy and oppressed churches.
Please visit our website www.aidtochurch.org

Name: Bethany Community Church
Description: Founded in 1916, Bethany is a diverse group of people whose primary aim is to give expression to the hope found in Christ. We have seen people drawing near to Jesus through Sunday services, family ministries, spiritual formation practices, small groups, and serving others in creative ways.  These elements combine to create an environment where individually, as families, and as a church we can know transformation and better represent the spirit and heart of Jesus. We look forward to you becoming a part of the story of hope God is writing through Bethany Community Church.

Name: Via
Description:
Today, 3 billion people have no access to the gospel.

And there are too few missionaries to reach them.

Via provides resources and training to Pastors, Ministries, Parents, Leaders and Missionaries to mobilize the most strategic regions in Asia, Africa and South America to help them send more missionaries to the unreached.

In 2021 alone, 2,402 new missionaries were deployed by our staff and partners.

Join us as we serve the global church in multiplying national mission movements. With teams in 10+ countries, we are passionate about glorifying Jesus Christ raising up the next generation of missionaries to take the Gospel to the unreached.

Name: Saddleback Valley Community Church
Description: Saddleback Church serves the Southern California community through more than 200 ministries, eight worship venues, a variety of counseling and support programs, Bible studies and seminars, local and global outreach programs, and a broad network of small groups meeting in local homes. Its purpose is to lead people to Jesus and membership in his family, teach them to worship the Lord and magnify his name, develop them to Christlike maturity, and equip them for ministry in the church and a mission in the world.

Name: Bund Freier evangelischer Gemeinden in Deutschland KdöR
Description: Der Bund Freier evangelischer Gemeinden gehört in Deutschland zu den klassischen Freikirchen des Protestantismus. Gegründet wurde die erste Freie evangelische Gemeinde 1854 in Wuppertal. Ziel war es, eine kirchliche Alternative zum Modell der damaligen Staatskirche zu entwickeln. Wesentliche Merkmale der Freien evangelischen Gemeinden sind: die Glaubenstaufe (im Gegensatz zur Kindertaufe) und das Prinzip der Freiwilligkeitsgemeinde (Mitgliedschaft aufgrund einer persönlichen Entscheidung).

Name: New Covenant Bible Church
Description:
A non denomination church located in Cedar Rapids, Iowa. New Covenant Bible Church desires to lead people into a personal, growing relationship with Jesus Christ. 

We have services at 9:00 AM and 10:30 AM on Sundays.

We also have a variety of ministries that meet throughout the week.

Name: C-Suite for Christ Dayton
Description:
Our mission is to cover the world in Christ by unapologetically declaring to the world whose we are by proactively creating faith-centric cultures in the workplace.

We are a group of business executives who come together every month to share how including Christ in our daily lives has positively affected the work environment.

In sharing joys and struggles with others who walk similar paths, we are able to support and encourage our brothers and sisters in Christ to live courageously in witness to him. It is our hope to rejuvenate the working soul through fellowship and praise of God.

How is your soul today?

Name: Catholic Diocese of Youngstown
Description:
The Roman Catholic Diocese of Youngstown serves the Ohio counties of Ashtabula, Columbiana,  Mahoning, Portage, Stark and Trumbull. 

Learn more and connect with us at:
The Catholic Echo: https://catholicecho.org
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/DOYoungstown
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/DOYoungstown/
YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@DOYoungstown

The Diocese of Youngstown provides a range of fulfilling career opportunities for individuals seeking to contribute to the mission of the Catholic Church and make a positive impact in their community. Serving as the administrative center for the Catholic Church in the region, the Diocese plays a pivotal role in coordinating and supporting various spiritual, educational, and social initiatives that cater to the needs of the local Catholic community.

One potential career path within the Diocese of Youngstown involves roles that contribute to pastoral ministry. Professionals in these roles work closely with parishes, schools, and other Catholic institutions to facilitate spiritual growth, community engagement, and religious education. This may involve coordinating programs, leading worship services, and providing pastoral care to individuals and families. Positions within this domain necessitate a strong commitment to the teachings of the Catholic Church and a genuine desire to support the spiritual well-being of others.

Moreover, the Diocese of Youngstown offers diverse career opportunities in areas such as education, administration, social services, and communications. Professionals in these roles play integral parts in ensuring the effective functioning of various departments and programs, contributing to the overall mission of the Diocese. Employees find fulfillment in knowing that their work directly influences the lives of individuals and families, fostering a sense of community and spiritual growth.

Name: Diocese of Metuchen
Description:
On November 19, 1981, His Holiness John Paul II established the Roman Catholic Diocese of Metuchen. The diocese is comprised of a Catholic population of 650,000 and encompasses Middlesex, Hunterdon, Somerset and Warren counties, which span through the upper central and western parts of New Jersey.

This Bishop’s seat is the Cathedral of St. Francis of Assisi in Metuchen, NJ.

OUR MISSION is to evangelize all people, at all times, and in all places. We are to proclaim the MESSAGE of Jesus; to teach His TRUTH; to extend His LOVE to people of all ages.

Name: Alliance of Confessing Evangelicals, Inc.
Description:
The Alliance of Confessing Evangelicals is a Bible-believing media, publishing, and event ministry, as well as a collaborative ministry network.

“Alliance” signals that we are a collaborative ministry network of church leaders and members, deliberately devoted to associate with one another in the service of the Church. “Confessing” indicates our joint commitment to believe, publicly declare, and live out a biblical faith and to do ministry in accord with the historic confessions of the reformed and evangelical churches. “Evangelical” is the earliest self-designation used by the Protestant Reformers of the sixteenth-century church, as well as their designation of their doctrine. We aim to stand with them in their faithful proclamation of the Gospel of Grace.

Name: Bethel AG Church
Description:
Bethel Assembly of God Church, Bengaluru, Karnataka, is one of the largest mega churches in India and one of Asia's most influential churches. 

The move of God is evident in Healing and Deliverance at Bethel.

A place where unadulterated teaching from the Word of God and strict adherence to the doctrines of the Holy Bible is a never compromised

A place where the principles, policies and kingdom values are held in high esteem

Located in Bengaluru, Bethel AG Church is one of the fastest growing churches in the city. Rev. Dr. M A Varughese is the Senior Pastor of the Church

To raise a new generation of believers looking unto God and trusting in his power.

Name: Morning Star Church
Description: Founded in 1999, Morning Star Church seeks to meet people right where they are and help them take their next step on their spiritual journey. Morning Star provides a wide variety of opportunities for spiritual and emotional growth, from support groups to classes, all of which are available to members as well as to the community. Under the leadership of Lead Pastor Mike Schreiner, Morning Star has grown from its initial attendance of 200 people to over 5,000 during 2014 Easter weekend.

Name: Eastview Christian Church
Description: Eastview Christian Church has a vision to be a fearless church of Christ-followers whose ridiculous love and dangerous witness are irresistible.

Name: Diocese of Rockville Centre
Description: The Roman Catholic Diocese of Rockville Centre on Long Island, New York was formed in 1957 and covers 1,198 square miles in Nassau and Suffolk Counties.  The diocese serves approximately 1.3 million baptized Catholics* (total population in both counties is approximately 2.5 million). There are 132 parishes plus 1 campus parish in 115 towns.

Name: Blue Letter Bible
Description:
To proclaim the Gospel of Jesus by making the written Word of God available across the globe, in order to know the divine Word of God, Jesus Christ.

To equip people with powerful tools to read, study, and rightly divide the written Word of God, the Holy Bible.  Such tools would include, but not necessarily be limited to:
1. A website containing in-depth Bible study tools and aids.
2. Mobile apps to bring essential tools to users wherever they might be.
3. Bible courses specifically designed to delve deeper into specific subject matters.
4. Partnerships with other persons and/or ministries to further our vision/mission on a co-laboring basis.

Name: Cáritas Diocesana de Córdoba
Description: Perfil oficial en Linkedin de Cáritas Diocesana de Córdoba, la institución cauce de la Iglesia de Córdoba para la acción caritativa y social.

Name: SOUTH AMERICA MISSION, INC.
Description:
We are a mission organization longing to see dynamic churches multiplied across Latin America that transform local communities and the world by embodying the Kingdom of God. Following the example of Jesus, SAM centers its mission and ministry on:

Our greatest need – Redeemed Lives.
Our greatest responsibility – Beautiful Churches.
Our greatest privilege – Transformed Communities.

Since 1914, we have served in South America, offering the hope of the gospel through discipleship, theological training, education, aviation, church planting, and more. We aim to enable and equip Latin Americans for ministry.

Name: Diözese Innsbruck
Description:
Die Diözese Innsbruck umfasst rund 365.000 Katholikinnen und Katholiken (Stand 31.12.2021) und gliedert sich in 75 Seelsorgeräume, 16 Dekanate sowie 292 Pfarren und Seelsorgestellen. 
Wir bauen auf die Strahlkraft des lebendigen Glaubens, die in der Frohbotschaft Jesu begründet ist und aus den zahlreichen Quellen unseres Glaubens schöpft.

Name: Faculdade Evangélica Bíblica de Teologia Reformada
Description: Holding forth the Word of Life

Name: Denton Bible Church
Description: Equipping the Saints to Do the Work of the Ministry.  It is our prayer and purpose to help people grow closer to Jesus Christ. If you are looking for a church home, or have questions about who Jesus Christ is, we want to serve you in any way we can.  Contact us via email at dbc@dentonbible.org

Name: Hour of Power Nederland
Description:
Hour of Power is een televisiekerkdienst. Elke zondag om 8 en 9 uur op RTL5 en om 17 uur op Family7.

Hour of Power geeft de liefde van Jezus door en laat mensen kennismaken met God. We bieden handvatten voor een leven en relatie met Hem. Dat doen we met onze televisiekerkdienst, die wordt uitgezonden vanuit Shepherd’s Grove in Californië en geleid door Bobby Schuller, maar ook met onze HOOPlijn, het gebedsteam, evenementen en onze webshop.

In de Nederlandse uitzending interviewt presentator Jan van den Bosch elke week een Nederlandse gast over zijn of haar geloof.

Hour of Power is een organisatie met een wereldwijde missie. De televisiekerkdiensten worden opgenomen in de gemeente Shepherd’s Grove in Garden Grove, Californië. Hier is ook het hoofdkantoor gevestigd. Hour of Power telt daarnaast zeven internationale kantoren in Australië, Canada, Duitsland, Hong Kong, Nederland, Nieuw-Zeeland en Zwitserland. Hour of Power Nederland bestaat sinds 1990 en is gevestigd in Baarn. Met ongeveer twintig medewerkers en vrijwilligers zorgen wij ervoor dat de kerkdiensten te zien zijn op de Nederlandse televisie. Deze uitzendingen worden volledig gefinancierd door donateurs.

Ga voor meer informatie naar www.hourofpower.nl.

Name: WORLD MISSION AGENCY
Description: WORLD MISSION AGENCY-WINNERS CHAPEL INTERNATIONAL is a religious Organisation based out of GREEN STREET GREEN ROAD, DARTFORD, United Kingdom.

Name: Frontier Ventures
Description:
Since its inception in 1976, Frontier Ventures has served alongside pioneering leaders serving the least reached peoples of the earth.

We are a Missional Order, which means that we grow as men and women in Christ through formation and community, and from that depth of life we respond to God's invitation to pursue the fullness of God's blessing for all peoples and the reconciliation of all things in Christ.

We create spaces for spiritual formation and community as a pathway to become our truest most alive selves for the sake of the world. We welcome and celebrate the distinctiveness of our collective humanity, made in the image of God, and pursue becoming a people marked by depth, openhandedness, belonging, faithfulness and vulnerability.

Our initiatives are shaped by our focus on nurturing new ways for least-reached peoples to experience the fullness of life in Jesus, leaning our work towards the socio-religious peoples with fewer than .1% known followers of Jesus.
We are executing a multi-year transition from a single North American hub to a multi-centralized network of Hubs that are closer to the frontiers. This will posture our community to be more diverse, closely engaged with practitioners, and connected to the contexts we hope to influence.

We will continue to shape environments for pioneering leaders to continue to engage the emerging world with missiological insight, collaboration, innovation, training, mobilization, and publishing to see breakthroughs of the gospel and movements to Jesus among all peoples.

Name: 7 Hills Church
Description:
Whether you're new to church, have been a Christian for many years, or are looking for a fresh start, you're welcome here. Our hope is to give vou a place where you experience a fresh, enjoyable connection to God and a community of people.

We keep our focus simple so we can have the biggest impact possible. Everything we do as a church is filtered through our focus on helping every person live the full life for which God created all of us.

Come visit us anytime at one of our incredible services:
Sunday 9a | 11a

Name: Alliance Church
Description:
Connection. That's a word you'll hear a lot around here. Our top priority is to create space for you to connect with God because God desires to connect with you. No matter who you are, God desires a relationship with you. So, we invite you to come connect with God and one another.
We offer Sunday services at three locations—Appleton, Hortonville, and Neenah. Each location provides nursery care and age-appropriate classes for children from 6 weeks through sixth grade.

Name: Fondazione Migrantes
Oregon Conference of Seventh-day Adventists
Description:
Our Church Mission
The mission of the Seventh-day Adventist Church is to proclaim to all peoples the everlasting gospel in the context of the Three Angels' messages of Revelation 14:6-12, leading them to accept Jesus as their personal Savior and to unite with His church, and nurturing them in preparation for His soon return.

Our Oregon Conference Mission & Vision
The main focus of the Oregon Conference is the Gospel Commission to “Go and make disciples of all nations.” We see the immensity of the task and realize that it will take all of us working together under the Holy Spirit’s direction to reach our world. As a result our Vision is: “Powered to Serve -- Members in Ministry.” Every member is important and gifted in some special way to be a part of God’s mission.

Name: Holy Myrrhbearers Orthodox Christian Church
Description:
The angel came to the myrrhbearing women at the tomb and said: Myrrh is fitting for the dead, but Christ has shown himself a stranger to corruption! So proclaim: The Lord is risen, granting the world great mercy.

With the blessing of the First Hierarch of the Russian Church Abroad, His Eminence, Metropolitan Hilarion, the first divine services were held at the newly opened Holy Myrrhbearers Orthodox 'Mission' in Harrisonburg, Virginia, on February 3, 2012.

As be begin the process of building out new church we invite you to view the latest photos of the our church family and the new land as they are posted. 
Please consider helping us financially in the new endeavor.

Name: Christian Life Center, Dayton
Description:
We are a multi-generational and multi-cultural church who believes that truth matters and that, through God, we can make a difference in our communities and the world around us. Young, old, single, families, long-time believers, new to the faith, or just exploring . . . There is a place for you at Christian Life Center.

Since our beginning in 1968, Christian Life Center (formerly called Northwest Assembly) has been a place that has taken bold steps to reach our community. Our campus sits on Little York Road located on the north side of Dayton, Ohio. Lead Pastor, Jordan Hansen, was installed as the new Lead Pastor at CLC in 2024. Our church is fueled by the God-Sized Vision. This vision has led us to relocate, build/expand facilities, go multi-site, and plant churches in places across the globe. Locally, we impact our communities by empowering congregants to reach out to others in their community, and we take part in transforming the urban Dayton area by partnering with inner-city churches and ministries.

We are a church of approximately 4,000 and are affiliated with the General Council of the Assemblies of God; however, believers from many denominations call CLC home. CLC is about sharing God’s love, God’s word, and God’s resources in practical and tangible ways. The leadership is leaned into engaging millennials and know that our future depends on engaging the current and next generation.

Our mission is to know God, be His people, value others and change our world. CLC is a unified and relevant church, and we are looking for staff who are dedicated to the God-Sized vision and who desire to be a part of an excellent, authentic team.

Name: Guru Nanak Darbar Gurdwara
Description: One of the biggest Gurdwara in Europe with over 750 volunteers. The Gurdwara hosts fantastic facilities for Sikhs and the wider community, including three Meditation Halls, two Langar Halls, fully equipped Lecture Theatre seating 140 people, ICT suite, Library, Meeting Room, Board Rooms etc. The complex also includes Sports fields, Outdoor Gym and Sports Hall (home of Guru Nanak Football Club, Kabaddi, Karate, Wrestling, Boxing, Basketball, etc), Guru Nanak Day Centre for the Elderly, and a Punjabi School at weekends.

Name: Gateway Resource Library
Description:
Gateway Resource Library | gatewayresourcelibrary.com

Gateway Resource Library is an ever-growing collection of over 2,500 resources created and curated with pastors, church staff and volunteers, and ministry leaders in mind. We are regularly adding new resources to the already robust collection, highlighting kid’s ministry curriculum, worship and music, leadership training, Jewish ministry, stewardship, church operations, and teaching series. Whether you are just getting started in ministry or have served faithfully for years, we invite you to use these free resources to help your church thrive, no matter the size!

We are committed to coming alongside you to provide relevant, spiritual, and practical resources, and we are honored to join you on your journey.

Sign up for free access today!
Connecting companies seeking Growth and Innovation with cutting edge Digital Transformation Solutions

Name: Preston Taylor Ministries
Description: Preston Taylor Ministries empowers Preston Taylor children and youth to discover and live their God-inspired dreams, develop a love for learning, and build joy-filled friendships that glorify Christ Jesus.

Name: BattleCreek Church
Description: Helping people Know God, Find Freedom, Discover their Purpose, and Make a Difference.

Name: Bible League Canada
Description:
For over 50 years, Bible League Canada has been committed to doing missions differently. How? By strategically building extraordinary international partnerships with national ministries in over 40 countries, equipping local believers through Bible-centric discipleship programs to become champions for the Gospel, committed to reaching their own communities with the Living Word of God. 

Bible League Canada diligently tracks each copy of God's Word placed, connecting Canadian donor champions with global champions to see maximum impact and multiplying transformation.

Name: Yeshivat Chovevei Torah
Description:
We at Yeshivat Chovevei Torah Rabbinical School (YCT) believe that the future of Orthodoxy depends on our becoming a movement that expands outward non-dogmatically and cooperatively to encompass the needs of the larger Jewish community and the world. For this vision to succeed, we require a new breed of leaders - rabbis who are open, non-judgmental, knowledgeable, empathetic, and eager to transform Orthodoxy into a movement that meaningfully and respectfully interacts with all Jews, regardless of affiliation, commitment, or background.

YCT is committed to training and placing open Modern Orthodox rabbis. We seek the best and the brightest that Modern Orthodoxy has to offer. Each student accepted into our family is chosen through a rigorous process that employs the highest academic standards and scrutinizes every applicant's character, make-up, and overall ability to perform and excel as a leader of the Jewish community. Our Modern Orthodox rabbinical school cultivates a love of Torah, a philosophy of inclusiveness, and a passion for leadership. The entire curriculum is taught at the highest levels of academic excellence by leading scholars and talmidei chakhamim. Upon ordination, each graduate commits to serving in the rabbinate.

Name: The Catholic Church Zurich City
Description:
Catholic life in Switzerland’s largest city falls under the “Catholic Church Zurich City” umbrella.

The association of Roman Catholic parishes and the deanery of Zurich City are the pillars of Catholic Church Zurich City. The association embodies the institution set up under the laws governing church-state relations and the Zurich City deanery encompasses the inner-church or pastoral issues.

This structure exemplifies the “dual system”, a specific feature of Switzerland. The bodies set up under the laws governing church-state relations see to financial resources and create the structures that enable pastoral bodies to fulfil their duties. 

This dual system is reflected even at the smallest level: the parish (pastoral) ensures church life, providing offerings and activities. The church community (established church) is responsible for the parish administration.

There are 23 church communities and 23 parishes in the city of Zurich.

The association of parish foundations (VPKS) operates under the umbrella of the Catholic Church Zurich City.

The VPKS brings together the 23 respective church foundations of the parishes.

Name: The Worship Center Christian Church
Description:
The Worship Center Christian Church is a fun, non-traditional, bible-based ministry. We are one family in multiple locations. Whether you’re hungry or hurt, lonely or lost, full of questions or looking for a home, there’s a place for you at TWC!
We embrace freedom in worship, and we believe that Jesus died and rose for all, so all are welcome here. We are a multi-ethnic, multi-cultural ministry impacting the world in which we live with the life-changing message of Jesus Christ. Through our relationship with Christ, we are healed, renewed, redeemed, forgiven, set free, transformed, and loved!
We are committed to the spirit of excellence! We are not a perfect family, but every day we are working towards it for God’s Glory. We are concerned about the total person; striving to become an oasis of hope within the Birmingham and surrounding communities by promoting and providing education, awareness, and financial independence. We desire to reach the lost at any cost, equip and empower the people of God, and provide holistic ministry to our community and the world. 
Our foundation is the Word of God, and we believe it in its entirety. The Word is the authority on which we base our faith, our behavior, our talk and out thinking. We are who The Word says we are, we can do what The Word says we can do and we can have what The Word says we can have!
And if you can’t remember all of that, just remember this:
We are the HUB! We exist to Honor God, Unify Communities and Build People!

Name: North Coast Calvary Chapel
Description: A Jesus community of transformed people, transforming our world. We meet Sundays at 8:45am, 10:45am & 6pm.

Name: Scalabrinianas
Description:
A Congregação das Irmãs Missionárias de São Carlos Borromeo, Scalabrinianas, foi fundada pelo bispo, o bem-aventurado João Batista Scalabrini, em Piacenza, aos 25 de outubro de 1895, e tem como cofundadores os irmãos: venerável Padre José Marchetti e a bem-aventurada  Assunta Marchetti.
As Scalabrinianas desenvolvem sua missão através de atividades nos âmbitos da catequese, educação, pastoral, saúde e social. Atualmente marcam presença em organismos eclesiais e políticos internacionais.

Name: جمعية تحفيظ القرآن الكريم بمركز بني يزيد
Description: تعليم وتحفيظ كتاب الله للطلاب والطالبات من خلال مهر المعلمين والمعلمات وباستخدام أحدث أساليب التعليم.

Name: Family Church Network
Description:
Family Church is one church in many neighborhoods speaking the neighborhood language on mission to build families by helping them discover and pursue God’s design. Our vision is to take the gospel to every resident in every neighborhood in South Florida. We pull people in, lift them up, and bring them home to be a part of God’s family.

As a multisite, multicultural, and multigenerational church, the coronavirus pandemic (COVID-19) continues to change reality as we know it and has significantly impacted the way we do church. One thing hasn’t changed – we will continue to be the church. 

Guided by our Lead Pastor, Jimmy Scroggins, we continue to find creative ways to stay connected to you and creating additional content for adults, students, and kids that you'll find on our website. The purpose of this content is to stay connected to our people through our digital platforms. We're calling this Family Church at Home.

Name: National Catholic Partnership on Disability
Description:
Rooted in Gospel values that affirm the dignity of every person, the National Catholic Partnership on Disability (NCPD) works collaboratively to ensure meaningful participation of people with disabilities in all aspects of the life of the Church and society.

Initiatives:
- Working with the United States Conference of Catholic Bishops to revise the U.S. Bishops Guidelines on the Celebration of the Sacraments for Persons with Disabilities.
 
- Offering a disability perspective to dioceses and national Catholic organizations through regional meetings, partnerships, and trainings.

- Facilitating a Vatican conference entitled "Catechesis and Persons with Disabilities" with the Pontifical Council for the New Evangelization and the Kairos Forum.

- Running the National Catholic Partnership on Disability Council on Mental Illness and Council on Intellectual and Developmental Disabilities.

-Planning a theology and disability symposium for pastoral leaders, clergy, and students.

Name: Redeemer Austin
Description: Redeemer Austin is family church dedicated to Following Jesus Together through Loving God, loving each other and loving our neighbors.  Through worship, fellowship and service we strive to make a positive impact in Austin and beyond.

Name: G3 MINISTRIES INC
Description: G3 MINISTRIES INC is a religious institutions company based out of 76 WESTFIELD DR, Pawleys Island, South Carolina, United States.

Name: St. Charles Borromeo School
Description: The finest Catholic/Academic Institution in Monroe County, Indiana. I am proud to be one of  the faculty in this school of dedicated, caring professionals.

Name: Buddhism.Net
Description:
Friends,

Our goal is to reach one billion people.  We work towards helping one billion people gain a solid understanding of Buddhism, and more importantly, become daily practitioners. One of the most wonderful features of the life-changing practices taught by the Buddha is they are secular enough to be practiced by anybody, whether or not they have a religion, and regardless of what their religion is.

Why one billion people? First of all, because the Buddha’s teachings are extremely effective at solving personal suffering, and we want to help one billion people with their suffering. But just as importantly, because one billion could be the tipping point. One billion people constitutes roughly 14% of humanity, and it may take as little as 10% of a population to create a tipping point. With as little as one billion practitioners, we can tip the world into goodness. The renewed rise of fascism, increasing conflict, and the resort to violence all around the world threaten civilized society, while the climate crisis threatens all lives. All the problems in the world are caused by greed, hatred, and delusion. The teachings of the Buddha are the antidote to greed, hatred, and delusion. Therefore, even with only one billion people practicing what the Buddha taught, we can begin to solve all the most serious problems in the world. We can begin to create the conditions for world peace.

We invite you to join us on this journey together.

Welcome to Buddhism.net, my friends.

Chade-Meng Tan
On behalf of the Buddhism.net 

TL;DR
Mission
To enlighten humanity, by making the teachings of the Buddha accessible to everyone in the world, with early Buddhism at the core.

Core Values
Not to do evil.
To cultivate good.
To purify our minds.
To learn the Buddha’s teachings.

We are also on Facebook, Instagram, Twitter and Youtube, with handle @buddhismdotnet if you prefer to follow us there.

Name: Rabbinical Assembly
Description:
The Rabbinical Assembly is the international association of Conservative rabbis. Since its founding in 1901, the Assembly has been the creative force shaping the ideology, programs, and practices of the Conservative movement,
and is committed to building and strengthening the totality of Jewish life.

Name: Vipassana for Leaders
Description:
Official LinkedIn Page for Vipassana Meditation* for Leaders and Executives

*Vipassana Meditation As Taught By S. N. Goenka in the tradition of Sayagyi U Ba Khin

The technique of Vipassana meditation that is taught in Executive courses is identical to that taught in regular 10-day courses. The only difference is that in an Executive course there is an additional talk and two sets of questions and answers by Mr. Goenka, who was a businessman himself for many years. In these, he discusses using the principles of Vipassana to deal with the challenges and stresses of the business world.

For more details, please visit: www.executive.dhamma.org

Name: YWAM Thailand
Description: Youth With A Mission Thailand is committed to communicating the Gospel and making disciples of Christ among Thailand's unreached peoples and those of surrounding nations through training, evangelism, church planting, mercy ministries and partnership with local churches and other Christian organizations.

Name: Police Chaplaincy UK
Description:
Police Chaplaincy UK is the professional body for the 500 chaplains operating in UK Police Forces. We deliver training, CPD, conferences, advice and guidance to all our chaplains. We are a registered charity with a Chair and Board of Trustees and our CEO is the National Police Chaplain.   

Working in today’s police service is increasingly challenging, but now there is a greater awareness of the effects of prolonged  exposure to stressful situations. It is recognised that, more than ever, at times individuals may need some help to be able to keep things in perspective. It is recognised, too, that everybody is a complete person: body, mind and spirit. Police work can affect us, our physical and mental well-being, as well as, perhaps, our own deeply held standards and beliefs and chaplains are here to care for all in the police. The basic role of the chaplain is to care: to care for the individuals within each force and to care for the organisation itself. Chaplains are in a unique position because they are not part of the structure, nor are they required to report in detail what they do. Their work is totally confidential.
-Chaplains make themselves available to people who need someone to talk to someone in confidence.
- By their on-going presence, they become aware of the stresses & strains of life in their force and try to help staff at all levels by being a critical friend.
- All chaplains will pray regularly for their force and for the situations and members of staff they are involved with.
- If appropriate, they visit staff who are ill at home or in hospital and they offer care to families and friends.
- They get involved if a member of staff dies, particularly if this is whilst in service.
- They are available as an operational resource to officers; for example, in situations of distress or death.
- Chaplains don’t try to convert you!

Name: First Presbyterian Church of Dallas
Description: Three ministries define our congregation: worship, education and service. Our worship experience is deeply rooted in the Christian tradition, yet vibrant and alive in its relevance to our contemporary world. Our education ministry offers many ways for people of all ages to grow in relationship with God through exploring the Bible and its Word to our world today.  With one of the largest social ministries in the nation, First Presbyterian Church offers a unique opportunity to meet Christ by serving the least of these in our midst at the Stewpot.

Name: Romemu
Description:
Romemu is attempting to transform the way Judaism is practiced and experienced by infusing aspects of Eastern spiritual practices with traditional Orthodox influences, so the ta’am or “taste” is unmistakably Jewish.

Committed to powerful prayer and transformative spiritual practices, Romemu attempts to engage the heart, mind and body in everything we do, helping us to foster greater levels of compassion.

We believe that Judaism offers spiritual seekers and skeptics alike a path that celebrates our wholeness and provides practical, grounded ways to heal our brokenness. We are committed to helping to birth a post-denominational Judaism that transcends ideologies and labels, that is joy-based and contemplative, ecstatic and reverential, connected to our tradition, yet open to truth, wherever it is found.

What is Romemu?

...a welcoming home where men and women participate equally and fully in song, dance, learning and praying

...an oasis in New York City where you can focus on your heart and catch your breath

...a place where we chant Hebrew prayers together, sing powerful wordless melodies, and have moments of deep silence

...a space that honors insights and practices found in many eastern spiritual paths, and that offers meditation and yoga within a deeply Jewish practice

...a center dedicated to a Yiddishkeit — Judaism — that opens body, heart, mind and spirit to experience greater compassion, courage, and joy in our lives.

Romemu (roh·meh·moo) seeks to integrate body, mind, and soul in Jewish practice. Unabashedly eclectic, we engage in body practices like yoga, infuse traditional liturgy with the energy of ecstatic chant, and ground our practice with meditation and contemplation. This is a Judaism that will ignite your Spirit.

We are a progressive, fully egalitarian community committed to tikkun olam, or social action, and to service that flows from an identification with the sacredness of all life.

Name: Adventist Frontier Missions
Description:
WHO WE ARE
We are a Seventh-day Adventist Christian lay ministry dedicated to establishing church-planting movements among people groups with no Adventist presence.

WHY WE SERVE
There are over 1.5 billion people who will never hear the gospel unless someone goes and tells them. Jesus Christ called us to go and share the gospel with these unreached people. Our frontier missions agency seeks to augment the ministry of the Seventh-day Adventist Church by establishing church-planting movements among people groups where there is no Adventist presence.

Name: Scottish Bible Society
Description:
The Scottish Bible Society believe that life is transformed through the Bible because the Bible leads us to Jesus Christ, the Saviour of the World.  We are committed to encouraging people of all faiths and none to engage with the Bible with an open heart and mind.  We believe that God uses his Word, by the power of his Spirit, to speak his love into people’s lives.

Thanks to the commitment and generosity of our supporters, the Scottish Bible Society help millions of people in over 200 countries and territories around the world to receive Bibles in a language they understand and a format they can use.  We also support transformative projects that teach people how to read using the Bible, contribute to education programmes for children and much more!

Name: Pray Chattanooga
Description: We are ministry serving the city of Chattanooga, working to unite churches and believers in prayer, worship, and service to the community.

Name: Eagle's Landing Christian Academy
Description: Eagles Landing Christian Academy is a private, Christian school located at 2400 Highway 42 N, McDonough, Georgia, United States.

Name: Assembly of Catholic Professionals OKC
Description:
The Assembly of Catholic Professionals is building a network of leaders coming together in an atmosphere of fellowship and faith. Archbishop Coakley has created this group to engage business and professional leaders in central and western Oklahoma more fully in the life of the Church. Membership is open to anyone who is interested in learning about and promoting the gospel of Jesus Christ in the Archdiocese of Oklahoma City.

Business and professional women and men are invited to attend a quarterly luncheon in Oklahoma City where they will meet and build relationships with each other, Archbishop Paul Coakley, clergy and lay leaders of the Archdiocese. It is a way to encounter people whose faith has had a positive impact in their daily and business lives. At each luncheon, the Archbishop will conduct the opening prayer, offer a few words about current issues facing the Church in Oklahoma and socialize with attendees.

Membership in the Assembly of Catholic Professionals is $500 each year and will cover event, program and overhead costs. Sponsorships, which help defray the expenses of the ACP, also are available. Please contact Peter de Keratry at acp@archokc.org for more information on sponsorships.

Name: Aldersgate United Methodist Church
Description:
We are glad you found us! Redford Aldersgate, is doing whatever it takes to make Christ's love visible through inclusivity, hospitality, and service. We are black and white, LGBTQ and straight, rich and poor, and differently abled. Come as you are and visit us sometime soon!

ALL are welcome - Come as you are.

Name: SolVivor: Rise. Rebuild. Recapture.
Description: SolVivor presents 'Rise. Rebuild. Recapture. Stories of Transformation,' a faith-based journey that guides survivors through three transformative stages—Rising from adversity, Rebuilding with purpose, and Recapturing their strength. Originally launched with a 316-piece NFT collection (inspired by John 3:16), the project has evolved into a comprehensive platform featuring published books, inspiring merchandise, and a supportive community that helps transform personal struggles into testimonies of triumph.

Name: Qalam Seminary
Description: Since 2013, the Qalam Foundation has been able to provide an Islamic Studies program known as the Qalam Seminary. Qalam strives to develop Islamic thought and community leaders by means of education, practical training, and mentorship. Today, many of our graduates serve as community leaders, imams, and teachers across the country.

Name: First Free Church
Description:
First Free Church is a growing community passionate about worshiping God, reaching the lost, growing spiritually, praying continually, deepening community, and developing leaders who continue those pursuits in every area of life.

Distinctive Values:

Jesus is the Difference
He’s not just the gateway to heaven, he’s the difference in our lives and our access to God’s kingdom now. Jesus is the difference between a life of guilt or grace. He’s the difference between a life of emptiness or fulfillment. He’s the difference between wandering and purpose. He’s the difference between discouragement and hope. 

Love is a Verb
The only way to follow Jesus is by putting love in action. If we say we love God and others but do nothing as a result, we don’t really love.

Heaven is Big 
We understand that God’s kingdom is made up of many diverse people and different cultures. We choose to unite around the most important beliefs and not divide over secondary issues.

Outreach is for Everyone 
The gospel came to you on its way to someone else. Every believer should learn to be a missional Christian, living out their faith in public and introducing people to Jesus wherever they are.

Community is Core 
Groups are the hub for everything we do: community, care, service, and growing with God. God designed His children for close relationships with each other. We believe everyone in our church should belong to a group. Groups are a safe place for broken people.

Leadership is Learned 
We develop leaders who impact the world for Jesus in all walks of life. Everyone can grow to be a better leader. Leadership development is a lifelong process directed by a sovereign God. It happens both continually and episodically, often through challenges. Sometimes through training or trying leadership opportunities.

Name: Saint Rose of Lima R.C. Church
Description: Roman Catholic Parish

Name: Inspiration Travel
Description: Inspiration Travel is a Christian travel management company specializing in group travel experiences for Christian ministries and churches since 1981. As seasoned travel advisors, we partner with ministry and church leaders to prepare once-in-a-lifetime Christian cruises and Christian tours for like-minded people to get away and meet God in a unique setting. We care deeply about your travel experience.

Name: A Place of Change Ministry
Description:
A Place of Change Ministry is not your usual church! No need for preliminaries or hierarchies. Just thus saith the Lord in the most practical, and applicable way possible...Just try it once!

Our ministry is founded on LOVE, GROWTH, SERVICE

From the Pastoral staff, to the members, love rests, rules & abides at A Place Of Change Ministries! Everything we do at APOC, we do because He Loved us first! When you spend a little time with us you’ll see that we are much more than a “church family.” At APOC, our love for one another makes us all truly, “family!”

Growth is what we do! You’ll find that each week, we are challenged never to remain the same! The practical, real-life approach to biblical study, as presented by our Pastoral staff,creates an atmosphere where personal growth is encouraged. From the Praise team’s uplifting music ministry to the Word offered by our Pastors, growth and change are on the menu at APOC, every single week! The Word says that “by beholding we become changed.” At APOC, we behold and are changed!

Service. At APOC, we live to serve! A Place of Change Ministries is not your average church! We get our hands dirty, we get in and make a difference wherever and whenever we can! From, clothing drives for and feeding the homeless, Back-to-school drives for students of every age, mobile blessing ministries for groceries, gas, you name it, we do it! We are the church that serves!

Follow us as we share the Love of Christ in a Safe Environment, where you can grow and serve.

Name: Missions Door
Description:
Missions Door is a Christian mission organization that supports and promotes indigenous missionaries who minister in their local communities. Missions Door is currently represented in over 25 nations and works with over 250 missionaries. 

Join us in 
- Following Jesus Christ 
- Working together as culturally-diverse people 
- & Generating faith communities!

Missions Door began in 1950. Since then it has been called the Conservative Baptist Home Mission Society, Mission to the Americas, and finally, Missions Door. The organization has worked fervently to use local missionaries to 
-Demonstrate our love for Jesus Christ through servant leadership
-Experience cultural, racial, gender and economic diversity
-Develop relevant ministries that are vision and opportunity driven
-Minister among the poor, the culturally diverse, the next generation and all unreached people
-And to impact our world for Christ and His Kingdom.

WHY INDIGENOUS MINISTRY
People from the areas they’re serving are often the best missionaries. They know the culture and language. They understand aspects that someone outside might not. And they’ve experienced firsthand the joys and hardships unique to that community.

Whether it’s a small group, a church in a small town, or university, these are missionaries who love their community and want to bring Jesus to them.

Name: Christ the King Catholic Church
Description:
A Brief History: In 1981, a group of Catholics in the "Charismatic Renewal" met weekly for Mass and reception of the sacraments with the blessing of the Bishop of Lansing. In 1986, Bishop Povish established us as a Lay Association of the Faithful and we were given the name Christ the King Catholic Association. In 1991, we began the process of becoming a personal parish of the Diocese of Lansing, a process that was completed in 1997. We were a 'pilgrim people' functioning without a permanent building; by God's grace, we dedicated our current church in June 2001. Today, many people come to Christ the King Catholic Church with various spiritual experiences; the main mission of the parish continues... to provide formation and pastoral care of all persons, leading them closer to God, especially God the Holy Spirit. (Click here for a more detailed history.)

Charismatic Prayer and The Mass: A charismatic style of prayer is common at Christ the King. People are free to raise their hands in prayer and during songs, many pray their own prayers audibly, some pray in tongues, etc.... We pray with expressive or charismatic prayer at monthly parish prayer meetings, at the beginning of parish meetings, and most especially during certain moments in the Holy Mass. These are some of the external markers of a charismatic parish. Internal markers include a radical surrender to the Lordship of Jesus Christ in all parts of life, a strong adherance to the Gospel and the teachings of the Catholic Church, and the pursuit of strong friendships centered on Christ. As Catholics, this obviously includes abiding in the heart of the Church in union with our beloved Bishop and Pope Benedict XVI.

Name: Carver Bible College
Description: Carver College was established to meet the need for an institution of higher learning for those desiring biblical and theological training. Since God opened the doors of the institution in 1943, over one thousand men and women have graduated. The graduates now serve God around the world in countries like Kenya, Ghana, Jamaica, and the United States. The graduates are educated in Bible and Theology, Business Management and Psychology.

Name: Igreja Adventista do Sétimo dia - União Sudeste Brasileira
Description: Perfil oficial da Igreja Adventista do Sétimo Dia para a região Sudeste (MG, RJ e ES)

Name: Grace Episcopal Church
Description: A growing and vibrant community in Charleston, South Carolina, where we strive to be "a light in the city".

Name: Diocese of Canterbury
Description:
Canterbury Diocese is the oldest diocese in England, stretching from Maidstone to Thanet, the Isle of Sheppey to the Romney Marsh. We have 350 miles of coastline with historic ports and seaside resorts, alongside rural communities, market towns and commuter-belt urban developments. Affluent areas often sit alongside pockets of major deprivation, offering an exciting and challenging mission context. 

At the heart of all we do is a vision of transformation for ourselves and our communities: no one can encounter God and remain unchanged. In the Diocese of Canterbury, we want to increasingly become a Christian community transformed through encounter with Christ, growing and overflowing to transform and bless the families, homes and communities we serve: Changed Lives, Changing Lives.

Name: Gentle Shepherd Ministries
Description: Gentle Shepherd Ministries in an evangelical, non-denominational ministry that reaches out in Christ's love and ministers to seniors in care facilities and inmates in county jails throughout south-central Minnesota. We are associated with InFaith, an American Mission.  Currently we are in 57 facilities providing ministry. Our ministry includes one-to-one visits, Bible Studies, Church Services, and "Holy Land Trips" (showing DVDs of the Holy Land to residents of senior care facilities.

Name: Le repos Saint-François d'Assise
Description: Le repos Saint-François d'Assise est un des plus importants cimetières catholiques au Québec et même au Canada. Par son statut d’organisme à but non lucratif, d’institution centenaire et par son caractère sacré, Le repos Saint-François d’Assise propose aux utilisateurs – clients, un ensemble de concessions, de biens et de services en accord avec les besoins de chacun et de concert avec les rituels funéraires témoignant de la foi chrétienne.

Name: Sinai Temple
Description:
“Sinai Temple is dedicated to providing a welcoming and caring community for its members, devoted to Jewish learning and support for Israel and the Jewish people, and committed to improving ourselves and the world through a growing relationship with God from generation to generation.

We are committed to the principles and values of Conservative Judaism and we welcome Jews of all backgrounds and affiliations to observe life cycle events, and pursue our mission through prayer, rituals, learning, music and the other gifts God has given us.

Name: Solidair Friesland
Description:
Solidair Friesland ondersteunt mensen die iets voor anderen willen betekenen en zich willen inzetten voor een rechtvaardige en duurzame toekomst voor iedereen. Solidair Friesland doet dat door vrijwilligers te ondersteunen bij de ontwikkeling en uitvoering van projecten in het sociale domein.
Voorbeelden zijn de scholing en ondersteuning van RK Caritas-instellingen en van diaconieën van PKN- en andere kerken, maatjesprojecten voor nieuwkomers, voor mensen met een verstandelijke beperking, voor mensen met problemen met budgetteren en voor ex-gedetineerden. Daarnaast voert Solidair Friesland projecten uit als Kinderen en Armoede, Geloof, Duurzaamheid & Toekomst en The Action en ondersteunt Solidair Friesland organisaties als Kleurrijk Fryslân (dialoog en samenwerking vanuit diverse levensbeschouwelijke gemeenschappen), Arme Kant Fryslân, het Fries Vredesplatform en de werkgroep Kerk en Landbouw Fryslân.

Name: Bethany Church
Description:
Mission

It is our Mission to go into all the world and make disciples. We are dedicated to supporting and sending out missionaries both at home and abroad. We accept the personal charge that each of us are missionaries for the evangelism of our world.

It is our Mission to bring healing to the sick, hope to the hurting, food to the hungry, and love to the lost. We will purposefully provide ministries that express the personal care of Jesus Christ by ministering to the whole person--physically, emotionally, and spiritually.

It is our Mission to encourage its members according to God's mandate for a five-fold ministry, some to be prophets, some to be evangelists, and some to be pastors and teachers, for the maturing of the body for works of service, and expression of the fullness of God's supernatural gifts.

It is our Mission to welcome and pursue the Presence of God. We are  committed to the purpose of being a Spirit led house of prayer and worship for all nations.

As a congregation, it is our mission, our purpose and privilege to bring people to Jesus through evangelization, and that all may be encouraged, edified, and equipped to exalt Him through worship and ministry.

Name: Movimiento de Vida Cristiana
Description:
El Movimiento de Vida Cristiana (MVC) es un movimiento eclesial con una espiritualidad y un estilo propios dentro de la comunión de la Iglesia. Es una Asociación Internacional de Fieles de Derecho Pontificio, aprobada en el año 1994 por San Juan Pablo II.

El MVC constituye un espacio comunitario de encuentro con el Señor Jesús, en el que se busca experimentar una auténtica y comprometida vida cristiana.  Como porción de la comunidad eclesial, se inserta activamente en la misión de la Iglesia. Por ello aspira a proyectarse apostólicamente a través de la vida testimonial, el anuncio de la fe y la promoción humana integral, a la luz del Evangelio y de las enseñanzas de la Iglesia. Su identidad está sellada por su eclesialidad y por su vocación al apostolado, que marcan la vida y el compromiso de sus integrantes.

El MVC está conformado por hombres y mujeres, de diversos estados de vida, que se vinculan en una misión apostólica común. Esta vinculación puede ser a título personal o en forma colectiva, y lleva a constituir comunidades, grupos, instituciones, asociaciones y servicios de diverso tipo y con distintas finalidades apostólicas concretas.

This is accomplished by providing youth with opportunities for skill development, mentorship, referrals and community spaces for youth to discover their possibilities.

Name: Rʏғ
Description:
In the Name of Allah, the Most Gracious and Most Merciful.

In today's world, we are bombarded with an overwhelming amount of information at all times. It's crucial in the journey of life and in the journey to Allah, that we have direction, purpose, and don't get lost along the way. This can even happen in our pursuit of sacred knowledge. There are many different types of knowledge and numerous subjects and areas we can explore. However, we must begin by prioritizing the most important types of knowledge that fulfill our obligations, and then continue along a specific curriculum or path of learning that is sound and reliable.

If we embark on a journey through the wilderness, we want to take someone with us who knows the way and can guide us, showing us what to avoid, where to find water, and where to set up our tent so we can reach our destination. Having teachers and following a curriculum helps us make progress and not become overwhelmed by the abundance of information and various subjects and classes available. Instead, we should focus on what we should be studying at our current level.

Therefore, following a reliable curriculum, such as the one offered by institutions like "Rekindle Your Faith" and others, can help us make significant progress in our learning journey, ultimately bringing us closer to Allah. May Allah guide us to the truth and enable us to follow it. May He bless us with beneficial knowledge that we put into practice, which is accepted by Him and draws us nearer to Him. Amen. Yarabal Alamin.

Allow me to introduce you to Rekindle Your Faith (RYF) if you're new to our organization. RYF is a non-profit Islamic institution with a steadfast commitment to serving the Muslim community. Our overarching mission is to facilitate the spiritual enrichment of Muslims, fostering profound inner growth and strengthening their divine connection. By doing so, we aspire to nurture individuals who are earnest students of knowledge.

Through a posture of listening and serving, we help local church leaders develop relevant leadership trainings that fit their cultural contexts. We’ve based our training resources on years of hands-on leadership experience. At the same time we seek to understand and respect local church leadership needs. Our desire is to help church leaders become even better at what they do.

Our goal is to equip indigenous leaders to start and sustain movements of transformation. Once we equip the first set of leaders, they go on to train more indigenous leaders. As this process continues, leaders multiply. This calling comes from 2 Timothy 2:2: "And the things you have heard me say in the presence of many witnesses entrust to reliable people who will also be qualified to teach others."

Name: Scoan.org
Description:
The Holy Spirit worked with the Father and the Son to create the world. The Father gave His Spirit to make us like His Son, Jesus Christ. The Jesus Christ we know is Jesus in the power of the Holy Spirit. He made a wonderful promise in John 14:16-17: “I will ask the Father, and He will give you another Counselor to be with you forever — the Spirit of Truth. The world cannot accept Him, because it neither sees Him nor knows Him. But you know Him, for He lives with you and will be in you.”The Holy Spirit is to be with us forever. He is not known or received by everyone, but only by those who are prepared for Him. The Holy Spirit shows us how wrong our sins are. He helps us to accept Jesus Christ as our Saviour. He completely changes our lives. This is called being converted or born again.

    Jesus Christ is a soul-winner. That is what He came for, lived for, died for and rose again for. He came to restore the relationship and fellowship between God and man. As to His human nature, Jesus Christ was a descendant of David. As to His divine nature, He was shown with great power to be the Son of God by being raised from death on the third day. Now He sits at the right hand of the throne of God (Hebrews 12:2; Romans 1:2-4). He was at all points tempted just as we are, yet was without sin. Jesus Christ loves us, died for us, reigns in power for us and still prays for us.

statement_of_faith_icon_3

    Holy men of God were carried along by the Holy Spirit as they spoke the message that came from God. The Holy Bible is more than long-ago events and ancient wisdom. It is God’s message of grace and truth to us today (2 Timothy 3:16; 2 Peter 1:21).

    Sin points one to eternal death and destruction but God’s Word points one to life. If Christ Jesus is our Lord and Saviour, a new body, a new soul and a new spirit await us one day. God’s Spirit joins Himself to our spirit to declare that we are God’s children (Romans 8:16).

Name: Ethiopian Graduate School of Theology
Description:
EGST was jointly founded in 1997 by the Ethiopian Evangelical Church Mekane Yesus (EECMY), the Ethiopian Kale Heywet Church (EKHC) and the Evangelical Churches Fellowship of Ethiopia (ECFE). The founding vision was born from the growing need, perceived within the Ethiopian evangelical churches, for contextually relevant and affordable graduate level theological education in Ethiopia.

EGST opened its door to the first students in January of 1998. EGST operated initially from a single room at the EKHC head office and then moved to a spacious bungalow. In 2002 EGST was able to buy a site in a prime location in Addis Ababa. Classes and offices were located in a house on this property. In August 2009 a purpose-built 6-story building on this property became the new home for EGST, with EGST occupying the upper three floors. In that same year EGST was accredited by the Accrediting Council for Theological Education in Africa (ACTEA). In March of 2014 a purpose-built 7-story building located on the same property was dedicated and is now the new home of EGST.

Twelve students, including one female, were enrolled at EGST when the school began. Since then enrollment has grown steadily. In 2008-2009 there were 96 students enrolled. For the academic year of 2013-2014 about 200 students were enrolled.

The first class graduated in 2001. As of September, 2013, EGST has graduated 256 students who are currently serving the Church and society in Ethiopia in various ways.

Faculty has also expanded. Five full-time faculty, along with regular part-time faculty and visiting faculty currently provide instruction at the school.

Dr. Peter Cotterell, former Principal of London School of Theology, served as founding Director of the school. 

From humble beginnings, EGST looks back on all that God has done since 1997. We praise God for what he continues to do through the lives and ministries of our graduates as they serve the Church and society in Ethiopia and beyond.

The church empowered people of all ages, socio-economic groups and ethnicities to be awesome in their world. Name: Fellowship Bible Church Nashville
Description:
Welcome to Fellowship Bible Church and FellowshipNashville.org! Our hope is that you will find this family of faith a place where you can worship freely, relate authentically and grow spiritually.

We are a group of people who believe the stakes are too high to sit on the sidelines when it comes to things in life that really matter. Life is too short to coast through, and people are too important to take for granted.

In the winter of 1997, a small group of people set out on a journey to become a community of faith where growth was as deep as it was wide, and where life-change was experienced and not just talked about.

Today that small group has become a worshiping community of influence we call Fellowship Bible Church, with campuses in Brentwood and Franklin.

Name: Wells Cathedral
Description:
Built between 1175 and 1490 Wells Cathedral has been described as “the most poetic of the English Cathedrals”. Set in the medieval heart of England’s smallest city, Wells is the earliest English Cathedral to be built in the Gothic style. The current building is a significant landmark in the South West of England, has an international reputation and is TripAdvisor ranked number one for things to do in Wells and in Somerset.

Wells Cathedral is a centre of excellence for choral music and over 1,000 services take place in the building every year ranging in style and length from very simple acts of worship to larger, more ceremonial occasions. These are open to all and first time visitors are especially welcome.

Wells Cathedral has unique features that separate it from other English cathedrals including its iconic West Front and the beautiful ‘scissor arches’ supporting the central tower, as well as one of the largest collections of historic stained glass in the country. The Cathedral also boasts the famous Wells Clock (which is considered to be the second oldest clock mechanism in Great Britain), the fascinating octagonal Chapter House and one of only four chained libraries in the UK.

Vicars’ Close:
Adjoining the Cathedral is Vicars’ Close, believed to be the only complete medieval street left in England. Vicars’ Close was designed to provide accommodation for the Vicars Choral, who sing the daily services, and this is still the case today. 

Entry:
English Cathedrals receive no regular funding from the Government or the Church of England towards maintenance, restoration and development work. Here at Wells that means we have to raise £4,500 per day which we must do through our own endeavours and with the help of our visitors, congregation, supporters, The Friends of Wells Cathedral and grant making trusts. While admission to the Cathedral is free, donations are therefore very welcome.

Name: JustMoney Movement
Description:
JustMoney Movement has a vision of a world where money shapes a fairer, greener future. 
 
What we do with money, how wealth is shared, including through our tax system, and how money is used by investors, banks and companies, determines the kind of world we live in. JustMoney Movement aims to be the go-to organisation for Christians and churches who want to connect faith, money and justice.  
 
We educate, campaign and advocate, resourcing a movement of Christians and churches to act differently with their own money and speak out about money.  
 
We believe an active movement like this can lead to changes in the way businesses and banks behave, in the policies set by government, and in people’s attitudes, all of which can help shape a fairer greener future.

Description:

First Presbyterian Church of Orlando has been in the heart of the City Beautiful for over 135 years, serving, teaching, and shaping lives. Our church family has seen it all: trains cut through Florida’s jungle, miles of orange groves, the opening of Disney World, and now the advent of SunRail (back to trains!). Things may change, but the Word of God endures for ever.

Name: Marist Association of St Marcellin Champagnat
Description:
The Marist Association of St Marcellin Champagnat Limited (MASMC Ltd) is a not-for-profit company limited by guarantee which was formed by the Trustees of the Marist Brothers to provide governance as well as spiritual leadership and formation for those engaged in or associated with the apostolic works, activities and operations of the Marists in Australia. 

Anyone familiar with the life of St Marcellin Champagnat would be aware of the encounter that has become known as ‘The story of the dying boy’. The young priest in 19th century rural France was deeply affected by tending to this youth who knew nothing of God’s love for him. It was a huge impetus in the founding, by Marcellin, of the Marist Brothers to educate and minister to young people -  particularly those on the margins.

St Marcellin Champagnat’s ministry, and the spirituality that underpins it, is alive and well today all over the world. As well as the Marist Brothers, thousands of lay Marists are living out Marcellin’s vision. 

One great expression of this in Australia is the Marist Association. It is a ‘spiritual family’ that is spread across the country and currently has over 1,000 Members – and growing. These are comprised of Brothers and lay people, males and females, young and not so young. 

Members of the Association seek to live out their vocation in ways that are inspired by Marist spirituality and values. This means we seek to follow Jesus in the way of Mary.

The Marist Association has over 50 Local Groups located around Australia, in cities and regional areas. Many, but not all, are situated in Marist schools, and are eager to welcome new Members into the Association family. For a complete list of locations, FAQs, the Membership Application Form, and lots of other information, please go to our website.

Name: Church of the City New York
Description:
We are a church located in the heart of Manhattan with a desire to see revival come again in our city and generation. The cry of the ancient prophet Habakkuk is still our cry today — to see the fame and deeds of God renewed and known in our time (Habakkuk 3:2). To this end, we want to be missional disciples who are pursuing the tangible presence of God, practicing counter-formation in the way of Jesus, and living on sacrificial mission for the renewal of the city, all in community. 

For open roles, please visit https://www.church.nyc/jobs

Name: Bethel Church of Morristown
Description:
From our humble beginnings, Bethel has strived to remain true to God’s calling for our church. We’ve sought to be willing to step out in visionary faith and take risks for God, and He has blessed us for that. In looking back at Bethel’s history, we can clearly see God at work. This gives us hope for the future and inspires us to continue to seek to impact Morris County and beyond with the love of Jesus Christ.

In 1841, several families left the Presbyterian Church of Morristown under the leadership of Mother Frances Freeman Ray to form the Bethel Mite Society, which was later incorporated as the Bethel African Methodist Episcopal Church of Morristown in 1843. Bethel’s first Pastor was Bishop Willis Nazery, who was born a slave in 1803 in Isle of Wight County in Virginia. For eight years the congregation met in each other’s homes until funds were raised to construct the Carpenter Gothic church on Spring Street in 1849. Bethel’s first building was dedicated with Bishop Paul Quinn officiating, assisted by Pastor Thomas Oliver.

Bethel served as the only school for Colored and Native American children in Morris County and needed to expand its facilities. In 1874, a new lot on Spring Street was purchased from John R. and Cornelia Piper for the sum of $2,000. A new church was constructed at an estimated cost of $3,000. Gifts from Mrs. Mary Anne Cobb (wife of George T. Cobb, first Mayor of Morristown) of pews, doors and windows from the old Methodist Church [on the Morristown Green] helped in this great work. The existing house on the lot was moved next door and used as a parsonage. The church remained in that building for nearly 100 years.

Under leadership of Pastor A. Lewis Williams, groundbreaking ceremony was held for a new church on August 12, 1967. The congregation worshipped in the Lafayette School auditorium for two and one half years until the construction of the church was completed in 1970.

Name: Gereja Kristen Indonesia
Description: The Indonesian Christian Church (GKI, originally: Gereja Kristen Indonesia) is an Indonesian Church of Presbyterian denomination. The church's theology is Calvinist. GKI was established in continuity with the Indonesian Christian Church in West Java, Central Java and East Java. These three denominations were originally independent, each arising from separate missionary initiatives conducted by local and foreign missionaries. The first of these denominations to be established, later coming to be known as the Indonesian Christian Church East Java, was incorporated on 22 February 1934. Six years later, and coming to be known as the Indonesian Christian Church West Java, the second denomination was incorporated on 24 March 1940. Finally, on 8 August 1945, the Indonesian Christian Churches Central Java was incorporated. Since 27 March 1962, the three denominations have been united as the Indonesian Christian Church, with the overall governing responsibility maintained by the General Synod (Sinode Am), which aims to co-ordinate united efforts towards common goals.

Description:

Name: ISKCON FARIDABAD
Description:
Hare Krishna! 
Welcome to ISKCON Faridabad  (Sri Sri Radha Govind Temple) – one of the most visited spiritual landmarks in Delhi-NCR.

A Loving Spiritual Community, based in Faridabad, based on Service & Connection for Peace, Pleasure and Purpose of Life.

Engage with us for
