As the largest Islamic complex in the Tri-Valley, our Friday prayer service draws about 500 worshipers from across the East Bay. The Center also sponsors public lectures, social service projects, and participates in interfaith activities through regional and national religious organizations.

We welcome you to our center where each day we are strengthening faith through community within the precious walls that hold our future.

Explore MCC East Bay: 
Events Calendar: http://www.mcceastbay.org/calendar
Weekly Updates: http://www.mcceastbay.org/newsletter
Support: https://www.mcceastbay.org/sadaqa
Facebook: http://www.facebook.com/facebook
YouTube: http://mcceastbay.org/live
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/mcceastbay

Name: Forgotten Children Worldwide
Description:
FCW Vision Statement: 
It is our vision to safeguard orphans and vulnerable, forgotten children from poverty, abandonment, and the evils of human trafficking as clearly directed in the Bible.

Mission Statement:
Forgotten Children Worldwide seeks to provide a loving and nurturing environment for orphans and vulnerable children by protecting them from becoming victims of extreme poverty and child trafficking so they can grow to fully understand the love of Jesus Christ. We strive to empower forgotten children through the strategic use of sponsorships, clothing distribution, self-sustainability initiatives, orphan home construction and other life-improving projects by partnering with indigenous Christians worldwide.

Forgotten Children Worldwide assists orphans and vulnerable children worldwide through:

Child Sponsorship - helping children escape the grips of poverty

Clothing Collection and Distribution - making a difference in hygiene, health and illness prevention

Self-sustainability Projects - making a long-term impact on communities caring for orphans

Child Trafficking Prevention - changing futures for vulnerable children

Orphan Home Construction Grants - building hope-filled opportunities for more children in need

Name: Alislam.org - Ahmadiyya Muslim Community
Description:
Alislam is the official Website of the Ahmadiyya Muslim Community. Alislam aims to be both broad and international in its scope.

IT Professionals belonging to the Ahmadiyya Muslim Community interested in serious voluntary work can contact directly via E-Mail: msi@ahmadiyya-islam.org

Some Projects:

Khalifa of Islam
A Website about His Holiness Mirza Masroor Ahmad, fifth Khalifa (Caliph) of the Ahmadiyya Muslim Community and collection of his publications, sermons, speeches and highlights of his efforts to bring ultimate world peace and to unite humanity.

Ask Islam
The Ask Islam website contains a vast library of the popular question and answer sessions held with Hadhrat Mirza Tahir Ahmad rh. This is a valuable resource and visitors can download answers for offline use.

The Holy Quran
Read The Holy Quran online with multiple translations available.

Name: Christians Engaged in Faith Formation
Description: The mission of CEF is to develop leaders in faith formation to equip people of all ages to live as disciples of Jesus Christ. The vision of CEF is of leaders connected to one another and to God, energized for the work of faith formation. (Guided by Acts 2:42--"The Believers devoted themselves to the apostles' teaching, to the community, to their shared meals, and to their prayers" CEB.)

Name: Hindu Vahini
Description:
Welcome to HinduVahini.com – your gateway to a world of Hindu devotion, knowledge, and spirituality. Our mission is to celebrate and share the depth and diversity of Hindu culture, from ancient teachings to modern interpretations, fostering a strong sense of faith and community.

At Hindu Vahini, we believe that spirituality and devotion bring us closer to inner peace and universal harmony. Our website offers a rich variety of content: from devotional songs and inspiring stories of gods and goddesses to insightful articles on Hindu philosophy, rituals, and festivals. With a focus on creating a contentful experience, we provide visitors with resources and tools to deepen their connection to Hindu culture and spirituality.

Join us in this journey of devotion and self-discovery, as we continue to build a community rooted in shared beliefs and timeless values.

Name: African Methodist Episcopal Church (Ecumenical & Urban Affairs)
Description:
The Mission

The Mission of the AME Church is to minister to the social, spiritual, and physical development of all people.
The Vision

At every level of the Connection and in every local church, the AME Church shall engage in carrying out the spirit of the original Free African Society, out of which the AME Church evolved:  that is, to seek out and save the lost, and to serve the needy.  It is also the duty of Church to continue to encourage all members to become involved in all aspects of church training.

The Purposes

The ultimate purposes are:  (1) make available God’s biblical principles, (2) spread Christ’s liberating gospel, and (3) provide continuing programs which will enhance the entire social development of all people.

The Objective

In order to meet the needs of every level of the Connection and in every local church, the AME Church shall implement strategies to train all members in: (1) Christian discipleship, (2) Christian leadership, (3) current teaching methods and materials, (4) the history and significance of the AME Church, (5) God’s biblical principles, and (6) social development to which all should be applied to daily living.

Name: CBE International
Description:
Founded in 1988, CBE International (CBE), also known as Christians for Biblical Equality, is a nonprofit organization that advances the gospel by equipping Christians to use their God-given talents in leadership and service regardless of gender, ethnicity, or class. Based on Scriptures such as Galatians 3:28, the CBE community believes that the Bible, properly interpreted, calls women and men to lead and serve as equals.

With supporters and ministry partners in over 100 denominations and 65 countries, CBE engages Christians nationally and globally with conferences, adult and youth curricula, multi-media resources, award-winning publications, a blog, and more. 

CBE collaborates with pastors, churches, schools, and NGOs around the world to eliminate gender-based violence and human trafficking, and improve girls’ access to education by raising the status of women.

Member of ECFA, EPA, GuideStar Platinum Participant

Name: First Presbyterian Church Houston
Description: Presbyterian Church USA with 4500+ members.  Sunday morning traditional worship times are 8:30 AM, 9:30 AM, 10:45 AM.  Sunday morning contemporary worship is at 11:05 AM.

Name: Adrian Dominican Sisters
Description:
Adrian Dominican Sisters is a Congregation of 400 Catholic Sisters who are committed to preaching the Word of God and acting for the common good of the whole Earth community. About 200 Associates (married and single men and women) share our Mission and efforts to “seek truth, make peace, and reverence life.” We engage in a wide variety of ministries in 25 states and five countries and sponsor several institutions of education, literacy, and healthcare. 

Our Motherhouse is in Adrian, Michigan (Diocese of Lansing). 

Websites:
Congregation: http://adriandominicans.org
Weber Retreat and Conference Center: https://webercenter.org/

Name: HHJO
Description: De Hersteld Hervormde Jongerenorganisatie (HHJO) houdt zich intensief bezig met het organiseren van activiteiten voor jongeren. Door middel van appeldagen, conferenties en landelijke acties betrekken we jongeren actief bij de kerk. Daarnaast bieden we ambtsdragers, leidinggevenden en ouders een luisterend oor, instructie en advies in de omgang met jongeren. De HHJO is een organisatie die werkt vanuit het grondvlak, om het hart van jongeren. In afhankelijkheid van de Heere, die Zelf in staat voor Zijn werk. Verdieping in het Woord van God, lezingen over Bijbelse en actuele thema’s en (gezellige) ontmoeting zijn daarbij belangrijk. Onze jongeren houvast bieden voor het leven!

Name: Lexington Rescue Mission Inc
Description: Lexington Rescue Mission exists to glorify God through a Christ-centered ministry that meets the physical, emotional, and spiritual needs of hurting people int he greater Lexington area.

Name: First Pentecostal Church
Description:
Praise the Lord!

First Pentecostal Church (FPC) is a Phoenix, Arizona based apostolic/pentecostal faith based church dedicated to Winning and Keeping Souls for Jesus Christ in accordance with Acts 2:38. FPC is a member of Pentecostal Assemblies of the World.

Love Never Fails!

Name: Salt Lake City Mission
Description:
"The Salt Lake City Mission seeks to serve the homeless community by providing emergency services and long-term recovery programs, the Mission helps to meet the physical, mental, emotional, and spiritual needs of individuals in the greater Salt Lake area. Through daily outreaches providing food, clothing, hygiene items, counseling, life skills, education, ministry and most importantly "A WAY OUT!"" - Pastor Wilson

Our Mission Statement

Salt Lake City Mission is a non-profit Inner-City Church Rescue Mission Ministry. We are called to Minister in Salt Lake City to men, women and children caught in the cycles of poverty, hopelessness, dysfuctionalities and dependencies of many kinds. We provide: food; clothing; blankets; sleeping bags; hygiene items; crisis intervention and counseling; job counseling and placement; telephone access and message services; educational assistance; and rehabilitation programs to the destitute of skid row since 1994. We also coordinate all of our efforts with all other organizations to obtain the maximum help possible for our clientele. We are committed: to helping the needy people we serve change their lives and again become self-sufficient, productive members of society. We hope and pray for the lives of those we serve to be transformed through the love and power of Jesus Christ. We offer and make available all types of help and assistance that we can to all who have a genuine need, without discrimination...

Name: Jesus Agape
Description:
Why we should collaborate?

 Keynote Speaker: As a dynamic and inspiring speaker, I am the best choice in helping businesses elevate their employees' work performance and productivity. Churches to learn rarely explained biblical truths and apply its wisdom in all aspects of their lives for personal growth and success. Organizations to inspire and empower their members with positive mindsets and personal transformations. My messages resonate with all types of audiences to inspire.

 Harvard Certified Theology: Certified in courses of study in Christian theology. This academic foundation, combined with my extensive experience, WCM minister certifications, Biblical studies and social services training ensures sound teachings.

 Diverse Educational Background: I hold a Bachelors of Science in Business Administration and Management, a degree in Electronics Engineering Technology, AI Marketing and other degrees. This diverse educational equips me to connect and equally communicate with the same knowledge of diverse audiences from various disciplines and fields of study.

 Pastoral Experience: As founder and Minister of Jesus Agape Ministry, I gained invaluable insights into spiritual leadership, community-building, faith based growth, counseling and powerful communications.

 Author & Resource Creator: I am the author of two impactful books, in both English and Spanish, on the importance of families, relationships and marriage founded on the principals of morality, virtues and faith. Additionally, I've established a comprehensive online platform that offers learning, counseling, and resources to help all people.

 YouTube Channel: Explore a wealth of inspirational content on my new YouTube channel, where I weekly share videos on personal development, positive mindset, spirituality, health and success.

 Collaboration Opportunities: If you're seeking a Public Speaker to elevate your event or organization, or you're interested in collaboration just Contact me!

Name: Vaad Meleches HaKodesh
Description: Our mission is to ensure the perpetuation and kashrus of STA”M in Jewish communities worldwide.

Name: Folkekirken | Aalborg Stift
Description:
Siden drives af Aalborg Stift.

Aalborg stift er det nordligste og geografisk største af de ti stifter i Den Danske Folkekirke. Det strækker sig fra Skagen i nord til Agger mod vest over til Læsø mod øst til Mariager Fjord mod syd.  

På LinkedIn viser vi folkekirkens bredde som organisation, som professionel arbejdsplads for provster, præster og kirkefunktionærer og som mødested for kirkens mange frivillige og menigheder.

Thomas Reinholdt Rasmussen er biskop over Aalborg stift, som omfatter 14 provstier, 296 sogne, 140 pastorater og 330 kirker.

Stiftet har en høj tilslutning og medlemsprocent. 

Stiftsadministrationen har adresse på Thulebakken 1, 9000 Aalborg.

Name: Inspire
Description: We inspire confidence in the Bible through Christ-centered experiences and resources.

Name: Onda Dura
Description:
Bem-vindo ao LinkedIn da Onda, uma igreja bíblica, acolhedora e extraordinária 

A Onda Dura surgiu em 2007, em Joinville, uma cidade no Sul do Brasil, a partir do descontentamento de um grupo de jovens que desejavam viver igreja de forma bíblica e contemporânea. Nosso nome tem como significa do “a Onda de Deus dura para sempre”.

Somos uma igreja que acredita na missão de plantação de igrejas. Temos mais de 20 campus espalhados pelo Brasil, Europa e Estados Unidos e centenas de pessoas que vivem igreja de forma remota através da Onda Dura Online.

Name: PIB do Brasil
Description:
Primeira Igreja Batista do Brasil. Amando Deus, Amando Pessoas. Líderes Apóstolos Milton @miltonebenezer & Niara @apniara . Salvador, BA

Cadastre seu currículo em nosso Banco de Talentos para concorrer a futuras vagas de emprego:

https://pibdobrasilnovostalentos.solides.jobs/

Name: University of the Nations
Description:
Youth with a Mission (YWAM) / Ungdom Med Uppgift (UMU) is an international and interdenominational missionary organisation. Our motto and calling is “To Know God and Make Him Known”. YWAM divides its efforts in three main focuses: Evangelism, Training and Mercy Ministries. YWAM is present in more than 1,000 locations, over 150 countries with about 16,000 people serving as staff.

Youth With A Mission Sweden was started in 1976 and has trained and sent out thousands of young and old people into long term or short term missions. The first centre was established 1980 at Restenäs (Ljungskile), north of Göteborg. Centres have since then been established in Stockholm (1995), Vilhelmina (2003), Dalarna (2003) and Linköping (2008). We also have smaller ministry teams in Malmö, and Borås. From our centres we are ministering in Sweden and reaching out to the nations. There are around 120 full time staff in Sweden and every year hundreds of people participate in schools, mission trips, projects and ministries.

As a large inter-denominational mission movement, one of our values is 'doing new things in new ways'. We want to explore what God is doing in the business as mission movement and to release those called to outwork their ministry through and in business.

Name: Oak Hills Church
Description:
Pastored by Lead Minister Travis Eades and Teaching Minister Max Lucado in San Antonio, Texas, Oak Hills Church is a dynamic, non-denominational congregation of disciples who make disciples by guiding all people to follow Jesus moment by moment. 

From the earliest days, God gave our church a vision of reaching the city of San Antonio for Christ, and God is still calling Oak Hills Church toward that vision. We envision a community of disciples courageously engaging culture with the hope of Jesus where we live, work, learn, and play. 

Step into this journey of faith with us as we set our sights on following Jesus. To learn more about Oak Hills Church, call 210.698.6868 or visit http://www.oakhillschurch.com/

Name: Diocèse de Lille
Description: Les catholiques du Nord de la France.

Name: Jesuitas Ecuador
Description:
La Compañía de Jesús fue fundada en 1540 por San Ignacio de Loyola y sus compañeros. La misión jesuita es una misión de reconciliación, que trabaja para que las mujeres y los hombres puedan reconciliarse con Dios, consigo mismos, con los demás, y con la creación de Dios.

La Provincia Ecuatoriana de la Compañía de Jesús se funda en 1983.

Name: Svenska kyrkan Stockholms stift
Description:
Svenska kyrkan har ca 5.5 miljoner medlemmar. Under 2023 döptes 40 086 personer och 20 procent av alla 15-åringar valde att konfirmera sig. Under 2023 gjordes bland annat 10 129 476 gudstjänstbesök och det finns 25 351 barnkörer och mycket mer. 
Stockholms stift är en del av Svenska kyrkan. Det är det yngsta av Svenska kyrkans tretton stift. Geografiskt är stiftet något mindre än Stockholms län.
Svenska kyrkans uppdrag är att förkunna evangelium om Jesus Kristus i ord och handling. Alla församlingars grundläggande uppgift är att fira gudstjänst, bedriva undervisning, diakoni och mission. Mötet, var man än befinner sig i livet, är det som Svenska kyrkan vill ta vara på. Det kan vara i ett enskilt samtal, i engagemanget för internationellt arbete eller i ungdomsarbete på skolor. Svenska kyrkan finns också på många sjukhus, i arbete med kulturmöten och på plats vid kris och katastrof.

Name: C-Suite for Christ St. Louis
Description: C-Suite for Christ is an international fellowship for Christian business executives. There are many ways to join our movement. Help us in our mission of being open about our faith and covering the world in Christ.

Name: The CornerStone Initiative
Description: The CornerStone Initiative is a Christ centered non-profit serving the low-income community in Madison County, Alabama. We encourage and equip individuals as they navigate a unique pathway from situations of crisis to places of stability and ultimately flourishing.

Name: GLFT
Description:
We realize that it is the Authority of God, the Faith of God, and the Power of God in which we are able to operate and carry out His will.



Greater Love Fellowship Tabernacle Inc. affirms the Holy Bible is the inspired, infallible, inerrant Word of God and is the basis for any statement of faith.  The church subscribes to the doctrinal statement of "The Holy Bible" as the inerrant, infallible word of God.  We band ourselves together as one body of baptized believers in the name of Jesus Christ personally committed to sharing the good news of salvation to lost mankind.



The ordinances of the church are baptism and the Lord's Supper. The realization of the New Testament Church is an independent ecclesiastical organization that operates by the direction of the Holy Spirit without interference and/or subordination by any association or convention.

Name: Young Catholic Professionals - NYC Chapter
Description: New York City Chapter of the Young Catholic Professionals organization. @YCPNYC across all social media channels

Name: Iglesia VidaReal.tv
Description: Jesús es el centro de todo lo que hacemos, somos una familia en múltiples puntos.

Name: City Church
Description: City Church is a union of two Madison, WI churches - Lake City Church and Mad City Church.   We want people at City Church to: Come as You Are, Connect with God, Connect with People, and Contend for More of the Presence of God.  City Church, located on the east side of Madison, WI, also includes Abundant Life Christian School and Campus For Kids Learning Center.

Name: Beth Tfiloh Congregation & Schools
Description:
BETH TFILOH celebrates the joy and love of Judaism within a caring community that strives to nurture every Jewish soul and embrace Jews from all levels of observance. Serving as a vibrant hub of the Baltimore Jewish Community, Beth Tfiloh has a rich offering of religious services, classes and year-round Jewish programming for children, families, young adults and seniors.

BETH TFILOH DAHAN COMMUNITY SCHOOL is recognized as one of the top college preparatory Jewish day schools in the country. Offering a complete continuum of education from Pre-School through High School, our school embraces families from all branches of Judaism as part of a caring community that celebrates the joy of Judaism, emphasizes values and mutual respect, and champions each child. We provide not only outstanding academics but a wealth of opportunities for students to excel – in the arts, athletics, technology and leadership. Our graduates are extremely well rounded, respectful women and men with a broad world view who have distinguished themselves as leaders on college campuses and beyond.
 
BETH TFILOH CONGREGATION is the largest Modern Orthodox Synagogue in America, with over 1,300 members representing a wide range of religious observance. Our dynamic rabbis and world-class Chazzan lead inspiring religious services, officiate at life cycle events and provide ongoing adult education classes through MERCAZ DAHAN CENTER for Jewish Life and Learning. An extensive calendar of youth and family programs are offered through our YOUTH CENTER. Our CAMP is one of the area’s most popular summer day camps, with a top rated aquatics program on its own 40-acre campsite.
 
Centrally located in Baltimore County, the Congregation’s extensive religious facilities and banquet halls are among the most popular Kosher catering venues in the Baltimore metropolitan area.

Name: Africa Hopes in Christ
Description:
Africa Hopes In Christ is a charity designed to play its part in Africa's spiritual and commercial development

Does your heart bleed when you think of Africa’s insurmountable problems?

Do you want to do something but worried about transparency of finances and activities and your money being spent wisely?

Are your values Integrity, Love, Provision?

Africa has had a bad deal, slave trade, colonisation, caught between the cold war and blatant racism in high places. Yet Africans are strong, intelligent and overcomers despite the hand they have been dealt.


We aim tp provide: 
Business Loans and Mentoring
Infra- structure funding
Health systems funding
Marriage and Family support
Local churches overseeing all local activities. 

Make a difference! Together we can.

Name: Igreja Videira
Description: Nosso ENCARGO é edificar uma IGREJA de VENCEDORES aonde cada crente é um SACERDOTE e cada casa uma extensão da IGREJA conquistando assim a nossa geração para CRISTO através de CÉLULAS que se multiplicam uma vez ao ano.

Name: Diocese of Venice in Florida
Description: The Diocese of Venice in Florida is a Roman Catholic diocese, founded on July 17, 1984.  The Diocese includes the 10 Florida counties of Charlotte, Collier, DeSoto, Glades, Hardee, Hendry, Highlands, Lee, Manatee, and Sarasota. The Bishop of the Diocese is The Most Rev. Frank J. Dewane.

Name: Lutheran Church Charities
Description: Originally headquartered in Addison, Illinois, and currently in Northbrook, Illinois, Lutheran Church Charities (LCC) was founded in 1947 as a 501(c)(3) nonprofit ministry to help support Christian human care ministries of the Church. LCC works throughout the United States and Internationally in bringing the Mercy, Compassion, Presence and Proclamation of Jesus Christ to those Suffering and in Need. LCC works with and in partnership with our churches. LCC is an RSO (Recognized Service Organization) of the Lutheran Church - Missouri Synod. We operate with a Dollar In – Dollar Out funding process where EVERY dollar given for a cause goes directly to that cause or need, with LCC acknowledging the gift and overseeing that the money is used as intended.

Name: Unitalsi
Description:
Quella dell’Unitalsi è una “storia di servizio” che dal 1903, anno della sua fondazione, si è sempre alimentata del desiderio di essere uno “strumento” nelle mani di Dio, per portare la speranza dove c’è disperazione, un sorriso dove regna la tristezza.

È una missione semplice che si nutre del desiderio di vivere il Vangelo nella quotidianità, offrendo, ciascuno secondo le proprie possibilità, un contributo fondamentale per costruire una società dove ci sia spazio per la carità. 
Questa è la nostra missione che si costruisce ogni giorno grazie all’impegno smisurato di quanti abbracciano il cuore della nostra associazione che, partendo dai pellegrinaggi, con l’aiuto della Provvidenza, ha realizzato una serie numerosa di progetti in grado di offrire risposte concrete ai bisogni di ammalati, disabili, persone in difficoltà.

Vogliamo essere uno strumento di “carità operativa” e di “carità creativa” nelle mani del Signore. Vogliamo condividere un impegno per costruire la speranza. I molteplici ’Progetti Unitalsi’ rappresentano una opportunità per quanti vogliono condividere con noi questa missione di carità, scegliendo l’ambito più vicino alla propria sensibilità e alle proprie attitudini.

Name: Order of Carmelites
Description:
The Carmelite Province of the Most Pure Heart of Mary is a religious community of men in the CHurch, whose members responding to the hunger for God in themselves and in the world, answer an interior call to prayer, community and ministry.

We embrace an eight century contemplative, prophetic tradition of Gospel living, patterned on the Carmelite Rule and inspired by Elijah and Mary. As contemplatives, we seek to live consciously in the presence of God and to affirm and challenge one another by living a prayerful life in common. As prophets, we walk with people, announcing God's presence, denouncing oppression, and promoting the well being of the human family, as we serve in pastoral, educational, spiritual, and other creative ministries in the United States, Canada, Mexico and Peru.

Name: Jesuits of Canada
Description:
The Jesuits are an order of Catholic priests and brothers founded nearly 500 years ago. Today, thousands of Jesuits along with lay colleagues work around the world and in Canada, in the promotion of truth, reconciliation and justice. 
Our long-standing spiritual tradition emboldens us to walk alongside one another in our unique spiritual journeys – regardless of our gender, sexual orientation, identity, race, economic status or faith. Through our spirituality and retreat centres, parishes, schools, universities and social justice centres, among others, we create welcoming and inclusive spaces that put faith into action for the common good of all.

Name: Rochester Youth For Christ
Description: Rochester Youth for Christ is a not-for-profit registered affiliate with the world wide Youth for Christ movement. Our mission is to change kids lives through relationships, life coaching, leadership development, and a living faith in Jesus Christ.

Name: Logos Aflame Ministries
Description: Logos Aflame Ministries (LAM) was incorporated in Nigeria.

Name: HKBP (Huria Kristen Batak Protestan)
Description:
HKBP has stood alone, in accordance with the government's recognition through a decision letter dated June 11, 1931 no 48, which was published in staatsblad 1932 no 360.
re-enactment of the republican government of Indonesia dated april 2, 1968, no dd / dak / d / 135/68. re-enactment of Indonesia Republic  cq Indonesia Republic of religion department no 33 dated february 6, 1988.

HKBP (Huria Kristen Batak Protestan) is a communion container of people of all groups, people and tribes throughout Indonesia, and throughout this world, who are baptized into the name of God the Father, the Son of the Lord Jesus Christ and the Holy Spirit.
The church is a manifestation of the Body of Christ, witnessing the unity of believers throughout the world.

We have churches spread around the world. Many of them at Indonesia, Malaysia, Singapore, United States of America, and also HKBP join some national and international institutions like CCA (Conference Council of Asia). 
With the Lord Jesus Christ let us spread joy and peace and blessings.

Name: Christ the King Church
Description: Helping people know, love, and obey Jesus as Lord over all of life.

Name: Eglise évangélique réformée du canton de Vaud (EERV)
Description:
L’Église réformée vaudoise (EERV), Église de droit public - reconnue par l'État de Vaud - est au service de toutes et tous sur l’ensemble du canton, dans les régions, les paroisses protestantes réformées, les services communautaires et en aumônerie. 
Elle compte près de 290 collaborateurs et collaboratrices et plus de 2000 personnes assumant notamment des responsabilités électives.

Notre mission se déploie en relation et en lien au service de toutes et tous dans le canton de Vaud dans les domaines suivants  :
•	vie communautaire et cultuelle 
•	santé et solidarités 
•	communication et dialogue
•	formation et accompagnement 
•	participation au dialogue interreligieux

L’Église réformée vaudoise est structurée en 11 régions qui regroupent 86 paroisses et se déploient sur l’ensemble du territoire vaudois.

Pour toutes et tous 
l’Eglise réformée vaudoise et ses activités viennent en soutien quotidien, accompagnent et entourent dans la vie. Notre Église propose et apporte ses services à toutes et tous. En solidarité et dialogue avec chacun et chacune.
Une Église à vivre et partager

Vous recherchez un environnement empreint de sens, en transition, dans une institution riche par ses valeurs et sa culture ?
Rejoignez-nous ! 
Travailler en Eglise => https://eerv.ch/nous-rejoindre

Name: Amazing Discoveries
Description:
Amazing Discoveries is a registered non-profit organization in Canada and the US (501c3) committed to exposing deceptions and errors in the religious realm, history, science, media, and health. We accomplish this mission through seminars around the world, as well as through publications and broadcasting via internet, satellite, and television. Our material attempts to provide life-changing information and to reveal evidence that allows people to make better decisions for their lives.

We are committed to sharing the truth about God and His promises. Amazing Discoveries began as a way for us to further spread this truth.
 
In 1992 we met scientist and speaker Professor Walter J. Veith. After hearing his life story and seeing his passion for truth, we invited him to hold his first public Canadian seminars at the Massey Theatre in New Westminster, BC. Over 1200 people attended his fascinating seminars on science and religion.
 
Seeing the success of Professor Veith’s series, we formed Amazing Discoveries in 1993 as a way to expand our audience and consolidate resources. Several more seminars followed throughout the years.
 
Since then, Walter Veith and Amazing Discoveries have gone through several updates of the series, expanded to include the US and Europe, and added additional speakers and topics. Seminars are now available on DVD and on the Web.

Name: Diocese of Bristol
Description:
The Diocese of Bristol is the Church of England across Bristol, South Glos, North Wilts and Swindon. There are 30,000 people worshiping regularly in more than 200 churches, led by hundreds of clergy who we train and provide. Around 15,000 children and young people attend our 69 Church schools, while chaplains serve in institutions across our region.

Rt Revd Vivienne Frances Faull the Bishop of Bristol, is the Diocesan Bishop supported by Rt Revd Dr Lee Rayfield, the Suffragan Bishop of Swindon. Our diocesan identity and vision of “Creatingconnections: connected with God, each other and our communities”, helps shape the work of ministers, parishes and schools. Our priorities as a diocese at the current time are making disciples, growing leaders and engaging younger generations.

The Diocesan Support Services exist to support our identity, vision and priorities. Based at the Diocesan Office to the north of Bristol and made up of 50 employees and volunteers, we serve ministers, parishes and schools by expressing vision, strengthening relationships, empowering people, maximizing resources and developing structures.

We offer training, consultancy, advice in a range of areas, highlight and share best practice across the
Diocese and manage different aspects of the life of the Diocese. Come and join a strong
and committed team.

Name: First Christian Church
Description: FCC is a non-denominational Christian Church in Moncks Corner, South Carolina. We believe that we are not the only Christians, but we only call ourselves by the name of Christ. We are Christ-focused, led by Elders as our spiritual overseers, and driven by the purposes for which the church of Jesus was made.

Name: Cathedral of Hope
Description: Cathedral of Hope UCC, based in Dallas, Texas, is the world's largest liberal Christian church with a primary outreach to lesbian, gay, bisexual and transgender people. Local and national church ministries, outreach programs, pastoral counseling, Internet and television media touch thousands of lives each day. Cathedral of Hope is a religious institution in which religious considerations permeate all aspects of the employment relationship and govern the employee selection process. Employees in every position serve as role models for the sincerely held Christian values of Cathedral of Hope.

Name: University Baptist Church
Description: The purpose of University Baptist Church is to be the body of Jesus Christ worshipping God together nurturing God’s family building God’s kingdom, sharing God’s love.

Name: CALVARY ATX
Description:
Mission:  

Love God. Love People. Serve the Community.

We’ll do this by seeking the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit to lead us as we preach Jesus, make disciples and develop and equip them for the work of ministry, the edification of the body, and to serve in the mission field that starts in our community.

Vision:

Make Jesus Known in the heart of Austin, other Urban Communities, and the World.

Name: The Restorers' Church
Description:
The Restorers' Church is a ministry built or outreach and committed to Isaiah 58:12:

"And they that shall be of thee shall build the old waste places: thou shalt raise up the foundations of many generations; and thou shalt be called, The repairer of the breach, The restorer of paths to dwell in."

We are located in Dolton, IL  and would love to partner with you.  Our info is the following:

Physical Address:
820 California Ave
Dolton, IL  60419

Mailing Address:
P.O. Box 468
Dolton, IL  60419

Sunday Services are 2:30PM (Prayer) & 3PM (Worship Service)
Thursday Night Empowerment is at 8PM

Follow us on all social media platforms

Name: The Lighthouse Church and Ministries
Description:
The Lighthouse Church and Ministries, founded by bestselling author and renowned spiritual leader Keion D. Henderson, is one of America’s fastest-growing churches headquartered in Houston, Texas. Keion D. Henderson, CEO and Senior Pastor, began his spiritual journey at a young age and established The Lighthouse Church in 2009. The church has since expanded to five campuses across Houston, including a thriving online ministry, LH2.0.

Pastor Henderson's dynamic leadership and profound teachings have drawn a diverse, multigenerational congregation of over 19,000 members and reached over 900,000 weekly viewers worldwide. With over 26 years in active ministry, he continues to educate, nurture, and equip his followers through various initiatives, including the L3 accelerator for entrepreneurship.

The Lighthouse Church is a global community committed to impactful outreach and humanitarian efforts. Notable initiatives include partnerships for quarterly Anti-Hunger Campaigns, Grab & Go Meals, and Annual Easter Community Outreach. During the COVID-19 crisis, the church partnered with Congresswoman Sheila Jackson-Lee to provide crucial support and testing.

Pastor Henderson is celebrated for his inspirational oratory, transformative teachings, and entrepreneurial spirit. He is a sought-after speaker at major conferences and has been recognized by The John Maxwell Institute as one of the Top 250 Leaders in the nation. His humanitarian efforts have earned him numerous accolades, including a nomination for the CNN Heroes Award for his Hurricane Harvey Relief Initiative.

In 2022, The Lighthouse Church opened The Dream Center Houston, a state-of-the-art facility dedicated to fostering educational, cultural, and artistic excellence. Pastor Henderson, originally from Gary, Indiana, is a devoted father and husband, steering The Lighthouse Church toward becoming a leading global faith-based institution.

Name: Kingsland Baptist Church
Description: Kingsland Baptist Church is a Biblical community of believers doing life together. Kingsland is committed to aligning with God's Truth, the Bible.

Name: Islamischer Kantonalverband Bern (IKB)
Description:
Verschiedene muslimische Organisationen im Kanton Bern haben sich entschieden, einen Islamischen Kantonalverband zu gründen, um ihre gemeinsamen Interessen zu bündeln und diese koordiniert und zielgerichtet zu vertreten.

Am 18. Oktober 2020 wurde die Gründung des Islamischen Kantonalverbands Bern, kurz IKB, in Bern von den Delegierten der sechzehn Mitgliederorganisationen mit der Verabschiedung der Statuten offiziell vollzogen. 

Der IKB setzt sich dafür ein, die Interessen und Anliegen der islamischen Gemeinschaften im Kanton Bern auf vielfältige Weisen zu unterstützen und zu vertreten. Wir streben danach, ein verlässlicher Ansprechpartner für islamische Belange gegenüber den Behörden und Institutionen im Kanton Bern zu sein. 

Der IKB ist daran interessiert, aktiv an der Gesellschaft teilzuhaben und einen Beitrag zum friedlichen Zusammenleben durch inklusive Projekte mit Institutionen im Kanton Bern zu leisten. Dies beinhaltet die verstärkte Kooperation in Bereichen wie Seelsorge, Medienarbeit, Sicherheit und sowie das Bestattungswesen. Darüber hinaus engagieren wir uns nachdrücklich für die Förderung der interreligiösen und interkulturellen Zusammenarbeit.

Name: United Presbyterian Church
Description: Vigorous church community in small town that is within commuting distance of the Omaha/Lincoln Nebraska area.

Name: Apostolic Intercessors Network
Description: Professional Apostolic Intercessors

Name: 365 Christian Men
Description:
365 Christian Men. Inspirational Stories Of Real Men. These stories span more than 2000 years. From the abolitionist, to the actor, and the addict. From the baker to the barber. From the educator to the evangelist and the executive. From the pastor to the pill pushers, even presidents. From the tank operator to the truck driver. 
Built on the foundation of Hebrews 12:1. We have one life to live and one opportunity to make it count. These short stories will both challenge and inspire you.

Name: SAINT MARY'S COLLEGE HIGH SCHOOL OF BERKELEY, INC.
Description: SAINT MARY'S COLLEGE HIGH SCHOOL OF BERKELEY, INC. is a religious institutions company based out of 1294 ALBINA AVE, BERKELEY, California, United States.

Name: Navigators studentenvereniging Ede
Description:
Hoe is het allemaal begonnen?
Onze voorvaderen zijn Amerikaanse mariniers, die geraakt werden door het evangelie. Vanaf 1930 vertelden ze overal waar ze kwamen over Jezus. Wat ongeorganiseerd begon, werd later vormgegeven in Bijbelstudies en lezingen over de hele wereld. Nu is ‘De Navigators’ een interkerkelijke en internationale beweging met het volgende doel: Christus kennen en Hem bekend maken.

Waar staan we nu?
Navigators kent inmiddels verschillende takken en doelgroepen in 120 landen, verspreid over de wereld. Navigators Studentenvereniging is daar een onderdeel van. In de jaren ’70 is het studentenwerk in Nederland opgezet. Inmiddels zijn er 15 Navigator Studentenverenigingen. NSEde is één van deze verenigingen. Wij startten in 2006 als een kleine groep enthousiastelingen en we zijn inmiddels uitgegroeid tot een vereniging met ongeveer 200 leden.

Waar houden wij ons mee bezig?
Net als onze leeftijdsgenoten zitten wij vol hormonen, onzekere toekomstplannen, lege portemonnees, veeel wilde plannen, gebrek aan tijd, vervelende tentamens, geloofstwijfels en levensvragen. Binnen de vereniging kun je je vragen stellen en samen met anderen ontdekken wie Jezus is en hoe het leven met Hem eruit ziet.

Name: GODcentre Gouda
Description:
WELKOM!
Wij zijn een jonge frisse kerk waar Jezus centraal staat in alles wat we doen. Wij geloven dat je gemaakt bent om van God, van mensen en van jezelf te houden en daar willen we je graag bij helpen. Jij bent welkom in onze celebrations, we hopen je snel te zien!


Gods rijke zegen,

Jeroen & Petra Dorstijn

Name: Georgia-Cumberland Academy
Description:
Georgia-Cumberland Academy (GCA) is a coeducational day and boarding school established in 1964. At GCA, we do more than prepare our students for college. We prepare them for life by emphasizing the pursuit of individual excellence, spiritual growth, and service in a diverse learning environment. Our students are the center of all that we do. Together, we challenge them to learn with passion, act with integrity, and serve with respect.
Each year, GCA enrolls approximately 240 students. Our school community represents an average of 12 states and 10 countries worldwide. Stretching for more than 500 acres, GCA’s campus is nestled next to the Chattahoochee National Forest in Calhoun, Ga., about one hour from Atlanta or Chattanooga, Tenn.

Name: Center for Rural Ministry at Grove City College
Description: We connect students and faculty with pastors and congregations around shared vision to see churches in rural and small town areas thrive. We seek to amplify the voices of pastors, resource innovative ministry, and build the capacity of current and future generations of pastors and lay leaders.

Name: Sheffield Cathedral: A Place for All People
Description:
Sheffield Cathedral aspires to be ‘A Place for all People’ (and dogs!). It even has its own tram stop!

People have been gathering on the site of Sheffield Cathedral for over a thousand years, evidenced by a Saxon Cross on display in the British Museum. Granted Cathedral status in 1914, the cathedral is the oldest building in Sheffield to be open 365 days a year. It is a Grade 1 listed building with significant local, regional and national importance.

At its heart Sheffield Cathedral is a place of worship with a growing congregation of people originating from countries across the world. It is a holy place where prayers are said daily and where people can seek quiet space and sanctuary within its ancient walls.

Sitting at the heart of Sheffield, the cathedral also tells the story of the origins of the city from the early 12th century, its development through the years, its industrial heritage and an association with the cutlery and silver industries. Today the Cathedral meets the needs of a multicultural community, welcoming thousands of visitors every year for services, guided tours and a range of cultural events, concerts and exhibitions to enjoy, as well as providing educational and musical opportunities for children and young people. There are also meeting rooms and affordable event space for hire. 

Within the cathedral is the award-winning family friendly 1554 Coffee and Gift Shop stocked full of locally sourced gifts, food and beverages, including the Heavenly Coffee! The Cathedral even has its own Sheffield ‘mark’.

Name: Christian Bible Institute & Seminary
Description:
CBIS is fully accredited and is exempt through the State of Florida by the Florida Department of Education’s Commission for Independent Education, (850) 245-3200, under Florida Statute 1005.06(1)(f). Its purpose is to prepare students for religious vocations only as ministers, professionals, or laypersons in the category of theology. CBIS is exempt from licensure and the jurisdiction or purview of the commission pursuant to F.S. 1005.06(1)(f); as a state-authorized (rather than regionally-accredited) theological institution, CBIS accepts no governmental grants or financial aid.

The studies offered by Christian Bible Institute & Seminary are not in competition with the secular field of training or employment. CBIS was established to train men and women of God and equip them with the tools necessary to be mature, productive, and functioning parts of the local Body of Christ.
You can earn a recognized, creditable degree in a timely fashion through one of our accelerated degree programs. You can transfer up to 90 credits from an acceptable college or seminary and complete your final year with CBIS and earn your Bachelor’s degree. Most of our students earn their degrees withing 24 months or less. Once you have completed the Bachelor’s degree program, you can enroll into the seminary division and earn your Master’s and Doctoral degree.

Name: The School Of Creation Community
Description:
If you are looking to connect with Father, Son and Holy Spirit and find a deeper purpose for your life, then quite possibly! 
What you can expect: 
Moggerhanger Park has been a place of spiritual discovery since the time the local 12th century Cistercian Abbey farmed unproductive land here. 
Some of Moggerhanger Park’s historical significance comes from it’s architect Sir John Soane whose other projects included No.s 10 and 11 Downing Street and the Bank of England – buildings of national and spiritual significance. 
You will be part of the residential community who support the work of Moggerhanger Park, our guests and our grounds. By supporting others and enjoying the support you receive in turn, we believe you will discover your identity in Christ more deeply and the impactful purpose of your life. 
Work and worship get reconnected. Work has been redeemed by Christ’s death on the cross. Our prayer is that you will find freedom and joy in the ministry of your work here. 
More than just supporting the estate, you will be enhancing a place of significance for all who visit. 
Our vision is for you to play a part in restoring historic gardens and home. This is an opportunity to model God, the Creator of nature and beauty. 
Cultivating is a key theme - cultivating the land, new relationships and cultivating your own walk with God.

Name: Konsultasi Syariah Islam
Description:
Konsultasi Syariah Islam Al-Khoirot  (KSIA) adalah layanan konsultasi agama Islam gratis secara online melalui internet. Kirim pertanyaan via email ke: alkhoirot@gmail.com, kunjungi artikel terbaru di www.alkhoirot.net.

Layanan ini memakai bahasa Indonesia dan dapat difahami oleh pemakai bahasa Melayu (Malay) seperti Malaysia, Brunei, Singapore dan Pathani.  KSI dibimbing oleh Majelis Fatwa Al-Khoirot yang anggotanya terdiri dari Pimpinan Pondok Pesantren Al-Khoirot Malang - salah satu pesantren terbaik di Indonesia. 

Jawaban berdasarkan Al-Quran, Hadits sahih dan kitab-kitab fiqih madzhab Syafi'i dan ketiga madzhab yang lain bilamana diperlukan serta pendapat ulama fiqih kontemporer.

Name: Calvary Church of Albuquerque
Description: Everything we do is about creating life change and we want to build and provide opportunities for change in every realm of life: spiritually, relationally, professionally, and emotionally. We want to see people give their lives to Jesus Christ and become better husbands and wives, mothers and fathers, and employees and business owners. A relationship with Jesus makes life better—and it makes you better at life.

Name: The Messianic Vision Inc.
Description:
Sharing the Good News

Sid Roth started Messianic Vision in 1977 with the desire to reach the world with the good news of Messiah. It is our passion to reach “the Jew first” (Romans 1:16). By following this “law of evangelism,” God opens a supernatural door to reach greater numbers of Gentiles.

In 1996 Sid launched the It’s Supernatural TV program featuring guests who have experienced extraordinary healings, miracles and personal encounters with God. For more on the history of our ministry see “Twelve Stones of Remembrance.”

Equipping All Believers for “Greater” Works

Through our It’s Supernatural TV show, Messianic Vision radio show and ISN (the It’s Supernatural Network) we are committed to teaching you how you can do the same works Jesus did and even greater (John 14:12). Thanks to your support we are able to continue producing It’s Supernatural!, Messianic Vision and ISN to mentor you and other believers around the world.

What We Are Doing

There are many things we are doing that we can’t talk about on our It’s Supernatural! TV show and Messianic Vision radio broadcasts because we are trying to reach the unsaved. But we thought you might like to know some of these recent, exciting ministry highlights:

Through Project 77 we have mailed over 1.4 million copies of Sid’s evangelistic book to Jewish families in North America.
We have also distributed 1.3 million copies of the Russian version of Sid’s evangelistic book to Russian Jews in the former Soviet Union, Germany and the United States.
Our It’s Supernatural! Network (ISN) is available free on every smartphone, tablet and computer in HD throughout the world.
Each week we receive testimonies of miracles and salvations from viewers and listeners around the world.
Our TV show, It’s Supernatural!, airs 37 times a week in Israel. We also broadcast in other Middle Eastern countries like Iran, Iraq and Afghanistan. In addition, we air 10 times a week in Arabic and English on the most viewed broadcast TV station for the Palestinian people.
The Russian-language version of It’s Supernatural! airs on a Russian TV network in 182 countries and is the most viewed program on the network.
We have developed Jewish evangelistic websites in English, Hebrew and Russian.
Sid continues to conduct “Lectures on the Supernatural” worldwide and large numbers of Jewish people are being saved.
We are airing our show across the Spanish-speaking world.
In September 2016 we acquired Middle East Television which broadcasts the Good News to 25 countries in the Middle East with a potential audience of 150 million people.
In February 2017, we celebrated 40 years of ministry! Take a look back at all God has done for us.
Messianic Vision is a Charter Member of the Evangelical Council for Financial Accountability and adheres to its fundraising guidelines. Gifts are tax deductible as allowed by law.

Name: Village Missions
Description:
Village Missions is dedicated to keeping country churches alive across the United States and Canada. 

We send missionary pastors to struggling churches in rural North America where little-to-no Gospel witness exists. We supplement whatever salary and health benefits the congregation cannot afford and will continue supplying leadership as the church grows. 

Since 1948, Village Missions has been sending “Village Missionaries” to rural communities across North America with the mission: “Preach the Word and love the people.” Village Missionaries are true shepherds who build and maintain relationships with people of all ages in the church. Village Missionary couples are also extremely involved in the community, and many couples serve in facets of the community such as fire departments, schools, athletics, hospitals, music and farms. 
Through modeling and teaching, Village Missionaries help the local body of Christ become what they should be in reaching their community for Christ. 

We are seeking men and couples who are willing to invest their lives into reaching people across rural North America with the Gospel of Christ. 

Village Missions currently serves in over 200 communities across the United States and Canada. Is God calling you to join? 


OPPORTUNITIES:
1. Full-time Village Missionary
2. One-year Residency
3. Interim Pastor Role (Associate Missionary)

MORE INFORMATION:
Check out our website for more information, or contact us about an application or any additional questions. Check out vmcontenders.org to learn about our free 6-course biblical training: Contenders Discipleship Initiative.

Name: St. Thomas Aquinas Catholic Center at Purdue
Description: St. Thomas Aquinas is the Roman Catholic presence at Purdue University. We are a vibrant and diverse community of students and resident parishioners. We are welcoming to all, a beacon of peace and God’s love, and a model of Jesus Christ to the local community at Purdue University, the Greater Lafayette area, and the world. We value spiritual and intellectual formation and take action to be a source of church leadership.

Name: The Brook Ministries, Inc.
Description:
The Brook Ministries is a dynamic partner in strengthening Latin American churches through the Christ-centered renewal, Biblical training and resourcing of Latin American pastors and their communities. 

By investing in pastors we help grow healthy churches. And healthy churches plant more churches, send more missionaries, and fight poverty and injustice in their own communities.

The Brook operates as a partner organization. We work with churches, seminaries, training ministries, and non-governmental organizations (NGOs). We believe that we are better together.

The Brook is a strengthening ministry. Our greatest investment for God’s purposes in found in providing physical and spiritual renewal, as well as practical ministry training to those already serving in ministry.

The Brook is a resourcing agency. We’ve found that aligning resources to meet the needs identified by pastors has the greatest ministry impact.

The Brook is also a faith-based ministry, relying on the generous financial support of individual donors and churches around the world.

Name: Trent Vineyard Church
Description: Trent Vineyard is a Nottingham based church which seeks to worship God and communicate the gospel with compassion and generosity. We meet on Sundays at 09:30, 11:15 (also live streamed) and 19:00.

Name: The Diocese of Truro
Description:
The Diocese of Truro is one of 42 dioceses in the Church of England. It includes parishes in the whole of Cornwall, Isles of Scilly and two in Devon.
We want to encourage others to come and join us in the exciting adventure that God has for us. In Cornwall especially, there are many signs of God at work in the world.

Name: Center Against Anti-Semitism
Description: Combating global anti-semitism. Resources for the Jewish community.

Name: Парохија Заплањска
Description: Српска православна Парохија заплањска, Епархије нишке, настаје 2015 год, спајањем Великокрчимирске, Горњодушничке и Горњобарбешке парохије.

Name: Schulwerk der Diözese Augsburg
Description:
GRÜSS GOTT IM SCHULWERK DER DIÖZESE AUGSBURG!
Wir sind ein Träger von 46 freien Schulen im Bereich des Bistums Augsburg, dessen Auftrag und Aufgabe die gute Erziehung und Bildung von Kindern, Jugendlichen und jungen Erwachsenen im Geist des Evangeliums ist.
Unsere Schulen bieten eine kirchlich geprägte Alternative zum öffentlichen Schulwesen. Als staatlich anerkannte Schulen verleihen sie dieselben Abschlüsse und Berechtigungen wie öffentliche Schulen.

Wir bemühen uns um die notwendigen personellen, materiellen und räumlichen Voraussetzungen für einen erfolgreichen Schulbetrieb. Denn die Entfaltung und Förderung der Anlagen und der Leistungsfähigkeit der Schülerinnen und Schüler ist unser wesentliches Anliegen.

Wir erfüllen die klassischen Aufgaben eines Schulträgers (Finanzierung, Personalplanung, Besoldung, Bauunterhalt, etc.). Wir unterstützen darüber hinaus unsere Schulen in ihrer Sorge um einen qualifizierten Unterricht, eine familiäre Atmosphäre und ein hohes Maß an Zuwendung zu den Schülerinnen und Schülern, ganz im Sinn des christlichen Menschenbildes. Die Orientierung der schulischen Erziehungsarbeit an christlichen Werten ist für uns selbstverständlich.

Name: Cedar Springs Presbyterian Church
Description: Cedar Springs is a Presbyterian Church in Knoxville, TN.

Name: The Foundation for Evangelism
Description: The Foundation for Evangelism works globally to educate and invite people into the Christian faith.  Grants to pan-Wesleyan/Methodist leaders, churches, seminaries, and denominations impact Christian leaders in Russia, Europe, several African countries, and the United States.

Name: United Evangelical Mission (UEM)
Description:
The United Evangelical Mission (UEM) is an international and equitable communion with 39 members, including 38 Protestant churches in Africa, Asia, and Germany, plus the v. Bodelschwingh Foundations Bethel.
The UEM is steadfast in its pursuit of a holistic understanding of mission.

Name: Arkansas Conference of the United Methodist Church
Description:
Our Mission:

To make disciples of Jesus Christ equipped to transform the world with excellence and passion.

Our four areas of focus, not just for the Arkansas Conference, but for all United Methodists are:

Developing principled Christian leaders;
Creating new places for new people by starting congregations and renewing existing ones;
Engaging in ministry with the poor; and
Stamping out killer diseases by improving health globally.

Name: SCOM (Student Christian Organization of Malawi)
Description:
Student Christian Organization of Malawi (SCOM) is a faith based independent national NGO. 

Vision: To be a vibrant student movement on every campus capable of impacting the whole society for God.

Mission: SCOM is dedicated to building a well coordinated and financially stable organization that is committed to the evangelism, discipleship and training of students and equipping them to live as witnesses of Jesus Christ on campus and beyond

Name: Archdiocese of Winnipeg
Description:
A diocese is a portion of the People of God, entrusted to a Bishop to be nurtured by him,
with the cooperation of the priests, in such a way that, remaining close to the pastor and
gathered by him through the Gospel and the Eucharist in the Holy Spirit, it constitutes a
local church. In this church, the one, holy, catholic and apostolic Church of Christ truly exists
and functions. (Canon 369).

The Archdiocese of Winnipeg was erected on December 4, 1915. It comprises that portion
of the Province of Manitoba lying south of the line dividing Townships 44 and 45 and
west of Lake Winnipeg and the Red River, except the territory south of the line dividing
Townships 9 and 10 and east of the Meridian between the 13th and 14th Ranges west of
the principal Meridian.

The patron of the Archdiocese of Winnipeg is St. Joseph.

Name: MOSAIC
Description:
WHO WE ARE
Mosaic is a community of faith born out of the heart of Los Angeles. Founded by iconoclast and futurist Erwin McManus, Mosaic has been named one of America’s ‘most influential churches.’

McManus has created a culture in Mosaic where anyone can belong before they believe, and Mosaic strives to create a home in the city for anyone who does not have one.

Mosaic is a multi-campus non-denominational church located in Hollywood with seven campuses located from Seattle to Mexico City.

WHAT WE BELIEVE
We believe the church exists to declare the name of Jesus and to build a place called home for the many who do not have one.

OUR MISSION
Our mission is to be a church that lives by faith, is known by love, and is a voice of hope to the world.

OUR CORE VALUES
1. Mission is why the church exists.
2. Love is the context for all mission.
3. Structure must submit to spirit.
4. Relevance to culture is not optional.
5. Creativity is the natural result of spirituality.

Name: LeadHer
Description:
LeadHer has a passion to elevate The Church and it’s God-given role to radically impact the world with the Gospel. We believe that a Christian’s involvement in a church body is essential to growing and developing as a follower of Christ. Our deep passion for the church has cultivated a strong desire to come alongside The Church to join them in challenging believers to become intentional about their impact and ability to change the world and spread The Word.

LeadHer has prayerfully targeted women because they represent the majority of church attendees in today’s typical US congregation in all major denominations. We also direct our chapters towards women because of the relational way that God has designed women to influence and impact others around her. We believe that when you challenge a woman to see her influence and intentionally live to impact the world around her for Christ- her family, her friends, her workplace, her community, and her church are profoundly changed.

Our LeadHer Local chapters are the foundation of our ministry. Through these chapters that meet twice a month, members are united, face to face, with women of different ages, backgrounds, denominations, and walks of life.

The connections that develop in our chapters serve as a catalyst for women to encourage one another to grow in three ways: spiritually, leadership skills,and relationship skills. We believe that growing women in these areas will equip them to live each day with intention and purpose for Christ and thereby impact and elevate The Church in their community and around the world.

Our chapters are free for women to start and for women to join in their community.  We are able to do this because of the monthly financial support of our numerous business, organizations, and individuals who believe in LeadHer’s mission.

Name: Business Link

Business Link’s mission is “Bringing leaders of business and church together for the call.” As a family of churches, Relation Mission, we have a call to be globally fruitful. Our passion is also to help church leaders understand better how they can support business leaders, so that they can work together to plant and strengthen churches to the ends of the earth.

The Office is located at the National Bahá’í Centre and has a presence for public information at the Bahá’í House of Worship in New Delhi.

Name: Divine Shepherd Church
Description:
Welcome to Divine Shepherd Church, Omaha's congregational cornerstone for over 50 years—where tradition meets transformation. We are more than a church; we are a high-energy, mission-based community committed to Love God, Share Jesus, and Serve People.

At Divine Shepherd, we understand that spiritual growth requires more than attendance—it demands accountability, intention, and prioritization. We believe in the power of connection—nourishing our relationship with God and fostering unity among diverse backgrounds, bridging divides within our community.

Our legacy is built on establishing a spiritual foundation for future generations. We prepare our children and loved ones for eternity, investing in a purpose beyond material success. At Divine Shepherd, you'll uncover hope for a better future and experience God's unconditional love.

Wisdom is at the core of our teachings—we delve into the Word, gaining insights beyond human understanding. In an ever-changing world, we offer stability—a God-pleasing foundation that transcends temporary challenges.

Join us as we continue to evolve with the times while staying true to Jesus's core teachings. Whether you're a business executive seeking purpose beyond profit, a parent nurturing spiritual growth in your family, or a community member yearning for deeper connections—Divine Shepherd welcomes you to explore, learn, and grow.

Join us this weekend and discover why Divine Shepherd Church is more than a place of worship—it's a vibrant community united in purpose: Love God, Share Jesus, and Serve People.

Sunday - 8:15 AM (Traditional Service)
Sunday - 10:00 AM (Modern Service)

Name: church of Christ - est. AD33
Description:
In Matthew chapter 16, Jesus promised to build His church on the rock...the foundation that He is the Christ, the Son of the living God.  After Jesus' ascension into heaven in ~AD 33, in Acts chapter 2 the Bible describes the first gospel sermon that Peter delivered to the crowds gathered in Jerusalem.

At the end of Peter's message, as recorded in Acts chapter 2, the crowd was cut to the heart, and asked what they must do to be saved.  In Acts 2:38-40, Peter said:

"Repent, and be baptized every one of you in the name of Jesus Christ for the remission of sins, and ye shall receive the gift of the Holy Ghost. For the promise is unto you, and to your children, and to all that are afar off, even as many as the LORD our God shall call." (KJV-King James Version)

 In Acts chapter 2 and verse 41 is says "Then they that gladly received his word were baptized: and the same day there were added unto them about three thousand souls."  It was at this time that 3,000 people were added to the Lord's church.  Acts 2:47 continues and says... "And the Lord added to the church daily such as should be saved."

It was at this point in history (~AD33) that the Lord (Jesus Christ) began adding people to the church.  Today, those that continue to follow the teachings of Jesus and the disciples as recorded in the New Testament are also added by the Lord to His church.  The church that belongs to Christ was purchased by Christ by His blood on the cross (Acts 20:28).

As it says in Romans 16:16, "All the churches of Christ greet you!"  We greet you today and invite you to worship and to study the Bible with us.

Jesus' church is not a church that belongs to this world.  Jesus himself said that His kingdom does not belong to the world (John 18:36).  

Jesus is the king and head over his church (Colossians 1:18) and God has made Jesus Christ head over all things to the church (Ephesians 1:22-23).

We invite you to worship God and study the Bible with us!

Name: LIFE Theological Seminary, Ikorodu, Lagos
Description:
LIFE Theological Seminary, Nigeria (formerly LIFE Bible College) is named after LIFE Bible College, Los Angeles established in 1923 with its initials: L.I.F.E representing Lighthouse of International Foursquare Evangelism. It was founded in 1955 by the Rev. & Rev. Mrs. Harold Curtis, missionaries of the International Church of the Foursquare Gospel with Headquarters in Los Angeles, California.  
The Curtis had experienced the revival associated with the ministry of Aimee Semple McPherson, the founder of the International Church of the Foursquare Gospel.  Having become Foursquare Ministers, they resolved to assist in the spreading of the gospel and the outpouring of the Holy Spirit to Nigeria and beyond.
From inception, the objective was for the school to be a centre for the practical development of Christian Leaders.  Classes were first held at the present Campus in Ikorodu in 1958.  

Ever since, over 2,000 students from various Christian denominations and countries in Africa have graduated with Certificate, Diploma and Bachelors degrees in different fields of Theology offered by this Seminary.

This Seminary remains indebted to several contributors and missionaries who have sacrificed time; efforts and finances in making this Seminary become forefront in Christian leadership development in Africa.

LIFE Theological Seminary is sponsored by the Foursquare Gospel Church in Nigeria.

Training a committed staff to model Christ likeness
Utilizing a creative, appropriate program for a variety of age groups
Furnishing a controlled environment where campers can make decisions for salvation, sanctification, and service

It is oftentimes affectionately referred to as America’s Catholic Church.

Visited by Pope Benedict XVI, Pope John Paul II, Pope Francis, and Mother Teresa, among others, the Basilica, though distinctly American, rivals the great sanctuaries of Europe and the world.

The Abbey has a school and a world class library. It is the mother house of the English Benedictine Congregation - a federal framework of independent abbeys and priories.

The Abbey, whose architects include Dunn and Hansom, Garner and Sir Giles Gilbert Scott is equidistant between Bath and Wells at the edge of the Mendips. 

Anyone interested in finding out more about a monastic vocation at Downside Abbey can use this page to make contact.

Name: First Christian Church (Disciples of Christ)
Description: This church is part of the religious movement known as “Disciples of Christ” or “Christian Church” born shortly after the American Revolution in 1809. It is the largest Protestant communion founded on American soil and arose as a protest against the sectarianism of the 19th century which had divided and weakened the universal church.  First Christian Church, Wilmington, North Carolina was founded in 1907 by 35 families at a small tabernacle on Fourth St. The congregation met at multiple locations in Wilmington until a church was built at Third and Ann St. in 1947 (now home to the Salvation Army Church). By 1957 the congregation had grown to 500 individuals and additional space was needed. Land was purchased at the current church site at the corner of Oleander and Mimosa Drives. The new sanctuary was dedicated in 1963 and an educational and administrative wing was added in 1972. To meet the needs of the congregation, a second worship service was added in 1997 which is now our contemporary service which meets at 8:45 am. A major renovation of the sanctuary and reception area was completed in 2006 to make it more handicapped accessible and to update the technology available in the facility.

Name: Deeper Christian Life Ministry, Aberdeen, Scotland,  UK
Description:
In Pastor Kumuyi's own words: "God, in the growth of Deeper Life Bible Church, has strategically and prudently used miracles to ‘make all men come to Him' (John 3:26). We take Christian living and holiness seriously."

According to Isaacson, “The history of Deeper Life is very much the personal story of W.F. Kumuyi. He is a gifted man, clear-thinking and humble. He is a dedicated Christian and deserves the title ‘the man of God,' which his followers have given him. 

Pastor Kumuyi's own Christian life developed because he took the Bible so seriously, and so the Deeper Christian Life Ministry has developed in the same way. He spoke clearly and authoritatively to the Nigerian situation. He dealt sensitively with African culture, but also made a firm stand in highlighting the points where local culture was incompatible with the will of God. While stressing the high moral tone which is one of the virtues of most African cultures at their best, he was at pains to specify those aspects of local culture which were unchristian and even evil. In this way, he was not only concerned with some kind of personal holiness, but also genuinely seeking the wholeness and harmony of the community at large, where truth, honesty, and justice should be the predominant characteristics.

Name: St. Nersess Armenian Seminary
Description:
The only seminary of the Armenian Church in the western hemisphere, our primary mission is to educate priests and non-ordained leaders for ministry in the Armenian Church in North America. St. Nersess is uniquely positioned to develop priests who are able to respond effectively to the spiritual and socio-cultural challenges facing the Armenian Church in America. St. Nersess has given 50+ priests, including four bishops and many lay ministers, who are serving the Armenian Church today. The seminary has also educated thousands of youth conference alumni who, cumulatively, have touched the lives of tens of thousands of people. None of this would have been possible without the many generous benefactors who have supported the seminary’s work over the years. 

St. Nersess has a faculty of two professors and seven adjunct instructors covering the areas of liturgical theology, sacred music, spirituality, liturgical practicum, Armenian Church patristics, homiletics (Armenian and English), New Testament and Classical and Modern Armenian. The seminary is staffed by a dean, operations manager, and the following part-time employees: administrative assistant, controller, development director, public relations director, a cook and a librarian. St. Nersess is governed by a board of directors, whose president is the Primate of the Eastern Diocese and whose vice president is the Primate of the Western Diocese.

Description:
We are a place for both winter and summer youth camps where we serve thousands of kids from all over the United States. Name: National Farm Worker Ministry
Description: Nearly two million farm workers work in our orchards and fields, plant nurseries, dairies and feedlots. 85% of our fruits and vegetables are hand harvested; without farm workers our multi-billion dollar agricultural industry would not survive. Yet they labor long hours for low wages and suffer from heat stress and pesticide exposure in the fields. Isolated and excluded from some of the laws protecting other workers, farm workers rely on concerned consumers to help achieve a measure of justice in the fields. NFWM represents consumers, advocates and people of faith and conscience who want to support the Farm Worker Movement.

Name: The Mainstay Foundation - USA
Description: The Mainstay Foundation, a 501(C)3 registered nonprofit organization, is a leading national organization in the Shi’a Muslim American community. The Foundation focuses on research and education, capacity planning and development, and strategic initiatives for the broader Shi’a Muslim American community, providing platforms and resources for collaboration and cooperation between centers and organizations in its national network. The Foundation has published several books on Shi’a doctrine, history and thought. It holds a yearly conference called the Engage Conference, bringing together upwards of fifty (50) Shi’a Muslim centers and organizations from across the continental United States. The Foundation also holds regular visits to the Holy City of Najaf for professionals and students to experience a unique cultural enrichment program hosted by the Islamic Seminary of Najaf. The Foundation similarly hosts leading scholars and professors from the seminary in community tours in the United States, engaging with universities, think tanks, and community centers.

Name: MGOCSM
Description:
Malankara Orthodox Church has witnessed the spiritual progress of lots of young stars through the efforts of Mar Gregorios Orthodox Christian Students Movement (MGOCSM). The laudable initiative for the Senior Leaders and students of our Church, in Madras, in the early part of the 20th century. The organization was founded on the basis of intense yearning for true spiritual progress for the youth along with an ardent spirit of Christian fellowship. The Organization was formed in 1907 as Syrian Student Conference. The first conference of the Movement was held at Madras on the 1st of January 1908.

The Movement had progressed and flourished from 1912 to 1960 with a new vigour as St. Thomas Syrian Student's Movement. The present title of MGOCSM was conferred in the year 1960 and henceforth it has remained the icon and symbol of united Malankara Orthodox Syrian Church. MGOCSM has incorporated within, two student organizations of Malankara Church, namely, St. Thomas Syrian Student's Movement and Mar Gregorios Syrian Student's Association.

Name: First Methodist Church Tulsa
Description:
A Brief History...

In 1886, Tulsa was a rowdy frontier cattle town in Indian Territory when a hardy group of settlers started a Methodist Episcopal Church in 1886. Twenty-one charter members gathered for worship in a downtown open-air brush arbor. Within 35 years, the congregation had built and outgrown two church buildings. In 1921 the congregation moved to its present location. In 1928 the Cathedral Sanctuary building was dedicated.

There would be good times and hard times ahead—oil booms and oil busts, world outreach and world wars, great revivals and the Great Depression. United as a congregation, the people of First Methodist grew stronger and deeper in their faith as they weathered the hard times together. The good times helped energize growth with an actively committed membership under strong Godly leaders.

First Methodist Church is grateful for a rich heritage rooted in a faithful God and His faithful people. Passionately engaged in life transformation in the church, in the community, and around the world, we are excited about the future as we follow Christ where He leads on this faith-filled adventure.

Our Mission:
First Methodist Church of Tulsa wants to see all people become fully committed followers of Jesus Christ.

Our Objectives: Serve local churches that commit to church planting; Serve missionaries that are associated with Baptist Church Planters; Serve newly planted churches that are affiliated with Baptist Church Planters; Serve God's people who have a passion for evangelism, discipleship, and missions. 
The Home office is located in Grafton, OH. Dr. David Little is the President of the Mission.

OUR BELIEFS

The Bible is the divinely inspired Word of God.
In the triune nature of God, the Father, the Son and the Holy Spirit.

Salvation has been provided through Jesus Christ for all men.

In the personal, visible, imminent return of Jesus Christ.
In Water Baptism and the observance of the Lord's Supper.
The Bible teaches that members / believers need to receive ministry not only in church services but in small groups in order to enjoy the teaching, fellowship, and maximum spiritual growth and production God desires them to receive.
That Christians are to obey the command given and lived by Jesus in Matthew 28:19 which reads, "Go and make disciples."

Name: Capitol Ministries
Description:
"Go therefore and make disciples of all the nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and the Son and the Holy Spirit, teaching them to observe all that I commanded you; and lo, I am with you always even to the end of the age." (Matt 28:19-20)

Following the Great Apostle Paul's example, CM is helping fulfill the Great Commission by planting evangelism and discipleship ministries in every stratum of government from city hall and state legislatures to Washington, D.C. and foreign federal capitols.

"...bear My name before the Gentiles and kings and the sons of Israel." (Acts 9:15)

Since 1996 when Capitol Ministries began, we have seen great fruit. 

Ralph Drollinger, President and Founder of Capitol Ministries, teaches a weekly in-depth Bible Study with Members of President Donald Trump's White House Cabinet, a weekly study with Members of the U.S. Senate, and a weekly study with Members of the U.S. House of Representatives. More on our DC Ministry: https://capmin.org/ministries/washington-dc/

Capitol Ministries has planted ministries in 40 states (https://capmin.org/ministries/state-capitols/) and 24 foreign nations on four continents (https://capmin.org/ministries/foreign-capitols/). We purpose to develop 200 ministries in 200 foreign federal capitals around the world. 

In 2017 we launched CivicReach, a ministry that partners with local churches to take the Gospel of Jesus Christ to city council members and county commissioners (https://capmin.org/ministries/civic-reach/).

We invite you to learn more at capmin.org and would love to connect with you on Facebook! fb.com/CapitolMinistries

Resources - Our Bible exploration and study resources are unique. We host a full range of material that is custom-created and intended for a variety of applications, from introducing children to Jesus to deepening the devotional life of long-time Bible readers.

Programs and Services - Churches, Organizations and Ministries all around the country run programs using Scripture Union curriculum. These are supported by regional representatives who provide training, oversight and input to ensure their success.
