Description: Accelerating the Gospel through multi-trade construction, shipping services, and clean water initiatives.

Name: Riverview Church Australia
Description: We are a good news community on mission: loving God, following Jesus and being transformed. Our vision is for everyone to be known in community, everyone formed in Christ and everyone to be a blessing in the city

Name: Zia ul Nabi
Description:
"Experience the Holy Quran like never before! Zia ul Quran Publication has introduced the sacred book in vibrant colors, making it easier to read and understand. This stunning edition features:

Name: Lausanne Europe
Description: Europe has changed. And the challenges and opportunities for Christian mission have changed too. The Lausanne Movement is seeking to respond to this new reality by facilitating a process that, with the help of the Holy Spirit, can make a difference for a generation. We want to give fresh impulse to the proclamation of the gospel in every country of Europe. We want to see biblical truth impacting every sphere of European society. And we want to encourage Christians to influence the realm of ideas which shape the worldviews of today’s Europeans.

Name: Embracing Brokenness Ministries
Description:
We were born into an imperfect world to imperfect parents as imperfect people. We inherited a mess when Adam and Eve bit that fruit. Where perfect fellowship was broken in a heartbeat. But God…. Provided a way. A way to heal the wounds of our past and experience His unconditional love.

At Embracing Brokenness Ministries our mission is to educate and enlighten all who would consider love as the remedy for all. We believe the greatest commandment to love our God with all our heart, soul, mind, body, and strength stands with the second to love our neighbors as ourselves as the most important calling for each and every one of us. We're on a short-term missions trip to bring the Gospel of Christ and the message of healing in Him to all those He brings our way. Through ministry to those in need around the world, through our podcast, blog, vlogs, and books as well as seminars and conferences that focus on our healing journey.

A great deal of emphasis and resources are directed in teaching and encouraging those who have shown a desire to find a new path.  A new way of connecting with the heart of God to find spiritual healing in life.

We must embrace our brokenness knowing we were created for much more. Unique in every way. As wounded healers, we all carry the mantle of love that Christ demonstrated with the Holy Spirit guiding us through the sovereignty of God our Father leading us.

Name: City on a Hill
Description:
“You are the light of the world. A city set on a hill cannot be hidden. Nor do people light a lamp and put it under a basket, but on a stand, and it gives light to all in the house. In the same way, let your light shine before others, so that they may see your good works and give glory to your Father who is in heaven.” — Matthew 5:14–16

City on a Hill is a movement of men and women called to know Jesus and make Jesus known. We seek to live boldly in this generation, emanating gospel light with the words we speak, the decisions we make, and the lives we live.

Our story began in October 2007 with a small team and a big vision to make a difference in Melbourne for the good of all people and the glory of God.

Meeting initially in an apartment before moving to a pub, we now hold 3 services every Sunday in a cinema in the heart of the city. Through the week we are committed to living lives of love, service and generosity.   

Throughout our journey we’ve seen many saved by Jesus while others reconnect with Christ and His church. We have helped care for the poor, pioneer new ministries, train leaders, equip missionaries, and seen many doors opened for the gospel. We are not a building or an authority structure, but ‘a people for God’s own possession that we may declare the excellencies of Him, who called us out of darkness into His marvellous light’ (1 Peter 2:9).

Name: Serviço Pastoral do Migrante - SPM
Narrow Gate Foundation
Description: Narrow Gate is a unique nonprofit that offers a tuition-free discipleship experience for young men—funded in part by our own thriving, purpose-driven businesses that train, employ, and equip people while generating real profit to sustain the mission.  For over 20 years, our tuition-free residential discipleship experience known as Narrow Gate Lodge (www.nglodge.org) has been transforming young men's lives for good.  Simultaneously, we've built Kingdom-businesses that provide ongoing leadership and skills development as a launch-pad for young adults.

Name: Living Word Lutheran High School
Description:
Located in Jackson, WI, Living Word Lutheran High School (LWLHS) mission is to open God’s word to open kids’ worlds.  We are proud to serve over 270 students!

Living Word Lutheran is a family of diverse students, parents, and teachers. It is a place where students come first - their best interests, their success and their plans for the future. Staff and parents collaborate on the academic and spiritual growth of each child, helping students recognize that their value does not come from others, but from who God created them to be.

Our promise is that we will meet each student where they are at and lead them to something bigger, so they can meet the world where it's at, and lead it to something better.

Name: Life Ministry Kenya
Description: Life Ministry is an interdenominational organization that has been serving the Kenyan Church since 1973. We do this through raising leaders of integrity among high school and campus students, professionals and executives, the Church, organizational and political leaders.

Name: Next Level Church | Southwest Florida
Description:
Next Level Church exists to lead people to become fully engaged followers of Jesus. With locations across Southwest Florida, people of all walks of life are welcomed and encouraged to come as they are.

Our hope is to see lost people found, found people free and free people empowered through the love of Jesus and community.

Next Level Church was founded in 2002 by Pastors Matt and Sarah Keller. Since then, it has become one of the fastest growing churches in the US, including being named the 9th fastest growing church by Outreach Magazine in 2013. 

Next Level Church has also been named a best place to work by the Christian Workplace Institute (2014).

Name: Hereford Cathedral
Description: A place of worship for over 1300 years containing some of the finest examples of music and architecture from Norman times to the present day. 13th-century Mappa Mundi & Chained Library exhibition with 1217 Magna Carta, Father Willis organ and world-class choir.  Garden, tower and cathedral tours, Cafe and Shop.  All welcome to worship, including choral services sung by the Cathedral Choir.

Name: Buddhist Churches Of America
Description: The Buddhist Churches of America is a California non-profit religious corporation. Buddhist Churches of America currently has over 60 independent temples and a number of Fellowships and Sanghas with approximately 16,000 members throughout the United States.

Name: St. Patrick's Seminary and University
Description:
St. Patrick’s Seminary & University is located on 40 beautiful acres in Menlo Park in the heart of Silicon Valley. Its expansive park-like grounds, historic chapel, modern classrooms, and extensive library provide an ideal environment for prayer, meditation, and study, within close proximity to major urban centers that provide rich field education opportunities.

For the past 120 years, St. Patrick’s Seminary has successfully prepared men to become Roman Catholic priests in conformity to Christ. The integrated process of human, spiritual, intellectual, and pastoral formation at St. Patrick’s Seminary revolves around our core values of Spiritual Fatherhood, Fidelity, Holiness, Wisdom, Evangelization, Resiliency, and Compassion. Priests formed at St. Patrick’s Seminary become courageous men of reflection and action who live joyous lives as priests. 

The seminary serves Catholic dioceses throughout California, Nevada, the Pacific Northwest, Hawaii, Guam, South Korea, and other Pacific Rim countries. Learn more at our website, www.stpsu.edu. You can also follow St. Patrick’s on Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, and Twitter.

Name: Biblijski institut Zagreb
Description: The Biblical Institute promotes biblical Christianity through education, research and networking.

Name: Youth With A Mission
Description: A large inter-dominational mission movement, one of our values is 'doing new things in new ways'. We want to explore what God is doing in the business as mission movement and to release those called to outwork their ministry through and in business.

Name: The Iona Community
Description:
The Iona Community is an international, ecumenical Christian movement working for justice and peace, the rebuilding of community and the renewal of worship.

We welcome guests and volunteers to Iona Abbey and to Camas. At both residential centres, we offer people from diverse backgrounds and nationalities an experience of living, working and worshipping in community. 

Camas, our adventure centre on Mull, offers a powerful and fun outdoor experience for young people and adults, including school and youth groups. The centre is powered by renewable energy, with an abundant organic garden. 

The MacLeod Centre, our youth and families centre on Iona, is currently closed for refurbishment. 

We resource individuals, churches and community organisations through our global Prayer Circle, our magazine (Coracle), Wild Goose Publications, the Wild Goose Resource Group, and our shop on Iona. 

Through all these strands of work we are transforming lives to change the world.

Iona Community is a Scottish Charity, SC003794, regulated by the Scottish Charity Regulator (OSCR).  Company No: SC096243.

Name: Bridgeway Community Church
Description: Bridgeway Community Church was founded in 1992 by Dr. David Anderson. Today people from our local and international communities come together to worship representing more than 52 different nations. Bridgeway is one church with two unique campuses in Columbia and Owings Mills/ Reisterstown, MD. We offer extensive ministry opportunities that reach into the body of Christ and out to the community. As a strengths-based, non-denominational, creative arts church, we share practical Christ-centered teaching both online and in person. The cultural and racial blend of our congregation has raised interest from leaders around the world as Bridgeway seeks to successfully fulfill its vision: “To become a multicultural body of fully devoted followers of Christ, moving forward in unity and love to reach our community, our culture, and our world for Jesus Christ.”

Name: Port City Community Church
Description: We are a multi-site church in North Carolina with locations in Wilmington, Leland, and New Bern. Our vision is to spur people on to a life with God together for the world.

Name: Beth Tzedec Congregation
Description: Beth Tzedec Congregation is a vibrant multi-generational kehillah, community, that seeks to inspire and enable its members to live meaningful Jewish lives.  We achieve this vision by providing members with a place for prayer, spiritual guidance and growth, observance of life-cycle events, lifelong learning, social and cultural programs and opportunities for tikkun olam, improving the world.

Name: New Beginnings Community Church
Description:
Join us on Sundays!

Redwood City Campus: 9 & 11 AM PT
San Jose Campus: 11 AM PT

Online: 9 AM & 11 AM PT 
https://NBCCbayarea.online.church
YouTube.com/NBCCmessages

New Beginnings Community Church (NBCC) is a group of people on a journey with Jesus. We are a safe place where people who are in different places on that journey can explore, ask questions, and grow in their relationship with God. Whether you’ve never been to church or you are fully engaged in following Jesus, there’s a place for you at NBCC, and everyone is welcome.

NBCC is a uniquely diverse church – in age and stage, culture and class. We believe that the diversity of our church reflects the truth that God really does love all people. Our pastors carry with them a deep commitment to a church that reflects God’s heart for all. NBCC was born out of a desire to join this value with a passion for excellence that embraces big dreams. We are willing to try new things in order to communicate God’s love to those who have given up on church.

Our vision emphasizes 4 key elements:

1) To reach people who feel far from God, which means we meet people where they are at and engage the world with hospitality and generosity;
2) To make disciples, which means we help people to live like Jesus did – with the same God-given purpose, and an extraordinary love and grace for others that can truly change the world;
3) To build families, which means we care about relationships and we help people to thrive by taking intimacy, honesty, and forgiveness seriously within their own families and the church as a family;
4) To transform communities, which means we serve our neighbors and our community to bring greater justice, freedom, and joy

Name: Immaculate Heart of Mary Parish
Description: Seeking to live as disciples of Jesus Christ, we reach out in Christian service to our families, our parish, our community and our world. We value our Catholic beliefs and nurture our continual growth and faith renewal. We strive to be a welcoming, vibrant Christ-centered community of believers that acknowledges and embraces the diversity of our parish family. Our parish life is centered in the celebration of the Word and of the Sacraments, especially the Holy Eucharist. Inspired by the Holy Spirit, we are committed to be stewards of our time, talent and treasure for the sake of building up the Reign of God.

Name: New Generations
Description: New Generations is empowering ordinary people all over the world to create chain reactions of disciples making disciples.

Name: World Community for Christian Meditation | WCCM
Description:
The community is a global and inclusive contemplative family. The roots of the World Community lie in the desert tradition of early Christianity dating back to the 4th century. In 1975 John Main, an Irish Benedictine monk (1926-1982), started the first Christian Meditation Centre in London. The first of the family of weekly meditation groups around the world began to meet then.

At the John Main Seminar in 1991, led by Bede Griffiths OSB, meditators from around the world came together to shape the future direction and organization of the community as a ‘monastery without walls’. They named it The World Community for Christian Meditation because it was not only formed and nurtured by the practice of meditation but existed to share this gift with others. The symbol of the Community - the two birds looking in different directions but resting on the chalice - is a modern version of an ancient way of representing the union of the contemplative and active dimensions of life.

The World Community is now present in 120+ countries. Individuals, weekly groups and centres share the peace and compassion that are the spiritual fruits of meditation. Groups meet in homes, parishes, schools, offices, hospitals, prisons and universities.There are groups for the homeless, for those in recovery from addiction and a special emphasis of the community is to share this gift of meditation with the poor and marginal. Christian Meditation Centres, such as the John Main Centre at Georgetown University, help to share the teaching. There are also online meditation groups. Because meditation is a universal wisdom, contemplative dialogue with other faiths is a priority. The realtionship with the Benedictine monastic family is especially valued and a WCCM Oblate 

The Mission Statement of the World Community is: 
 "To communicate and nurture meditation as passed on through the teaching of John Main in the Christian tradition in the spirit of serving the unity of all"

Name: Rʏғ Rᴇsᴇᴀʀᴄʜ
Description:
RYF Research, which stands for Rekindle Your Faith, is a sacred pursuit to enlighten and kindle the hearts of the Ummah. Our goal is to make the RFY community accessible to everyone, regardless of their background, by presenting the beauty of Islam in a simple and easy-to-understand manner.

We are a community of seekers, dedicated to researching every aspect of Islam, from the Quran to the Hadith to the lifestyle of the Prophet, peace be upon him, and the history of Islam. Our research team is the core of the RYF community, and we document our findings to share with our fellow Muslim brothers and sisters, providing guidance on how to implement them into our daily lives and become better Muslims.

In the Quran, Allah (SWT) says, "Indeed, those who have believed and done righteous deeds - they are the best of creatures" (Quran 98:7). At RYF Research, we are committed to empowering Muslims to be among the best of creatures by offering comprehensive resources and support to deepen their understanding and connection to their faith.

Join us on our sacred journey to Rekindle Your Faith and be among the righteous, dear brothers and sisters.

Name: Santuário de Fátima
Description:
O Santuário de Nossa Senhora do Rosário de Fátima dá expressão ao pedido de Nossa Senhora do Rosário, aludido já em 13 de agosto de 1917 e expressamente indicado na aparição de 13 de outubro desse ano a Lúcia de Jesus, Francisco Marto e Jacinta Marto: «Quero dizer-te que façam aqui uma capela em minha honra, que sou a Senhora do Rosário» (Primeira Memória da Irmã Lúcia). A capelinha foi erguida em 1919 no local das aparições de 1917 na Cova da Iria e, desde então, o espaço do Santuário foi sendo edificado, em resposta ao significativo afluxo de peregrinos.

O Santuário de Fátima é local de peregrinação, que faz memória do seu acontecimento fundante, as aparições de Nossa Senhora aos três pastorinhos. O acolhimento pastoral dos peregrinos é elemento primordial da sua missão.

O Santuário de Fátima custodia a mensagem do acontecimento de Fátima. É sua missão o estudo e a difusão desta mensagem, trabalhada como meio de evangelização em Portugal e no mundo.

O Santuário de Fátima é, por vontade expressa da Sé Apostólica, um Santuário Nacional.

Name: Karimia Association Limited
Description: Karimia Association Ltd is an innovative and dynamic Muslim organisation working across the UK. We have developed centres for worship, education, training and self-development in various locations around the country. The scope and variety of our activities are focused on bringing about a personal, spiritual and moral awakening in the individual.

Name: Community Christian Church, Ft. Lauderdale
Description:
Community Christian Church is a non-denominational, Bible-teaching church located in the greater Ft. Lauderdale area of South Florida.  Our Sunday worship services are 9:30 AM, 11:15 AM, and 6:00 PM.  

"We believe that all people matter to God and therefore they matter to us. Because of this, our doors are open to people from all backgrounds regardless of where they are on their spiritual journey. At Community we are striving to become the kind of church described in the Bible, where there is relevant teaching, heart-felt worship, honest friendships, consistent prayer, and compassionate care for those who are in need. In short, we would like to have the kind of contagious Christianity that can influence and encourage our entire community, one life at a time.

So whether you are a spiritual seeker who's just starting to ask questions about God or a committed Christian who wants to sink the roots of your faith even deeper, you can find a home here at Community Christian Church. You'll find that this is a place where you really do matter to us because you matter to God."

-Scott Eynon, Senior Pastor

Name: Anshe Emet Synagogue
Description:
Anshe Emet Synagogue is a sophisticated, urban congregation with a diverse, multi-generational membership. We take a dynamic approach to halachic matters and offer a variety of prayer services, the core of which is a formal service in the great Rabbinic and Cantorial tradition. We also provide a broad range of programming, which includes thought-provoking educational opportunities, presentations by speakers renowned in the Jewish world and fulfilling social justice projects.
The Anshe Emet Synagogue is one of the oldest Conservative congregations in Chicago. Founded in 1873, the congregation was originally located on Sedgwick Street. We have been at the present site since 1929. Anshe Emet has a long history of being a center for Torah study, cultural activity, and Israel and social justice advocacy.

Name: Remonstrants
Description:
The Remonstrant Church is a liberal type of church, based in the Netherlands. It has in total about 6000 members and friends, divided over 45 congregations: all of these 6000 are grownups, who decided to join the Remonstrants (which is different from other, big churches where everyone that was baptized as a child is registered as member).

Remonstrants place big emphasis on personal faith. What binds them is the Statement of Principle. In addition, many write their own confession of faith on becoming a member .
Remonstrants are by no means always in agreement with one another on questions of faith and social issues. They know that the message of the Gospel can not be separated from true choices in the struggle to live together, on the road to a world with peace and justice.


Some fast facts about us:
- The Remonstrants were the first church in the world to open marriage for same-sex couples. It did so as early as 1986.

- The Remonstrants were one of the first churches in the Netherlands to ordain women as ministers.

- The Remonstrants are open for dialogue with members of other Christian denominations and of other religions. They were among the churches that established the World Council of Churches in 1948.

- In our services, when the Lord's Supper is celebrated, everyone present is invited to take part, whether you are a member of our church, another church or no church at all.

- We defend those whose rights are trampled on by other churches.

Name: EVANGELICAL THEOLOGICAL SOCIETY
Description: EVANGELICAL THEOLOGICAL SOCIETY is a religious institutions company based out of 2825 LEXINGTON ROAD, BOX 927, LOUISVILLE, Kentucky, United States.

Name: Abundant Life Church

Name: Visakha Sri Sarada Peetham
Description:
At the macro-level, Visakha Sri Sarada Peetham is a peetham of traditional Advaita Vedanta of Sri Ādi Śankarāchārya. But, it is much more than a Seat of Advaita Philosophy. It is a Tapo-peetham, a Seat of Asceticism. It is a Jnana-peetham, a Seat of Vedic Wisdom. It is a Siddhi-peetham, a Seat of Spiritual Awakening and Fulfilment.

It is the conviction of Visakha Sri Sarada Peetham that Dharma follows Kaala, or the means of discharge of dharmic responsibilities has to adapt themselves to the current times. The Peetham has taken the task of harmonizing Sanatana Dharama with the changing times upon itself. In specific, the Pīṭhādhipati Sri Sri Sri Swaroopanandendra Saraswati Swamy and the Uttarādhikāri Sri Swatmanandendra Saraswati have been instrumental in promoting and disseminating Hindu Dharma across the country in the form and principle in harmony with the modern times. Sweeping changes are taking place across societies all over the world. Visakha Sri Sarada Peetham has been striving hard to make the Hindu Way of Life and the Hindu Philosophy adapt to these changes, so that Bharateeya Sanatana Dharma remains the life-force of our great nation.

Name: Don Bosco Argentina
Description:
Los Salesianos de Don Bosco son una institución religiosa dedicada a la educación y evangelización de los jóvenes, sobre todo los más pobres y abandonados.

Su tarea se inspira en la vida y obra de San Juan Bosco, sacerdote italiano nacido en 1815 y fallecido en 1888.

De origen campesino y familia humilde, fue sensible a las necesidades de los jóvenes más pobres de Turín, Italia, a quienes les dio una casa que los recibía con cariño, un patio para encontrarse con amigos, una escuela para prepararse para la vida y una parroquia para conocer a Jesús.

La obra de Don Bosco está presente hoy en más de 130 países. En Argentina realiza su misión a través de cien sedes u obras salesianas en veintidós provincias, organizadas en torno a dos regiones: Norte y Sur.

Cada obra salesiana se dedica a la evangelización y la catequesis; la formación de grupos juveniles; la educación y la formación para el trabajo; la atención a los niños, adolescentes y jóvenes en riesgo y la misión entre los pueblos originarios.

Buscamos una vida más digna para todos los jóvenes de Argentina.

Description: TISCA (The Independent Schools Christian Alliance) is an association of teachers and others concerned to promote Christian values in the Independent Schools of the UK and around the world. Description:
The Diocese of Manchester is the Church of England in Bolton, Bury, Leigh, Manchester, Oldham, Rochdale, Rossendale, Salford, Stockport, Tameside and Trafford. It is a place of excitement and vibrancy: a place of extraordinary opportunity for the Church to deliver on its commitment to the establishment of a worshipping, growing and transforming Christian presence at the heart of every community.

Faith, hope and love. The values at the heart of our Church have never been more relevant or contemporary in today’s society. As people become busier, communities become more diverse and life becomes more demanding, it’s more important than ever to reach out and embody our vision.

Name: Archdiocese Of Birmingham
Description:
The Catholic Archdiocese of Birmingham extends from Stoke in the North to the Thames in the South.

It includes seven cities and five counties; an area with over five million people.

It serves a Catholic population of c. 450,000 through a network of 217 parishes and some 240 schools.

Name: Bergen kirkelige fellesråd
Description:
BKF står for Bergen kirkelige fellesråd.

BKF er arbeidsgiver for 160 ansatte i fellesrådet og i Den norske kirkes 25 menigheter i Bergen. Kirkevergen er daglig leder. 

BKF ivaretar administrative og økonomiske oppgaver for menighetene og har ansvar for drift, vedlikehold og utvikling av kirkebyggene og gravplassene i Bergen. Vår visjon er «BKF- speiler Guds himmel i jordnært liv». 

Vi vil være en åpen, frimodig og troverdig organisasjon som legger til rette for en relevant kirke for byens folk - en kirke som skaper gode lokalsamfunn preget av nærhet, omsorg, identitet og kultur, i alle livets faser.

Name: Sacred Heart Southern Missions
Description:
Sacred Heart Southern Missions is a Catholic ministry in the Diocese of Jackson, Mississippi, served by priests and brothers of the Priests of the Sacred Heart, sisters from many different religious communities, and dedicated lay ministers. We are a community of faith working to build up the Catholic Church and make a difference in the lives of people of all faiths who struggle in poverty. We serve in five northern counties of Mississippi and are headquartered in Walls, Mississippi.

Through the financial support of donors throughout the United States, Sacred Heart Southern Missions staffs and funds the programs and work of two Catholic elementary schools providing a proven path out of poverty for more than 550 children, eight Catholic social service offices serving over 3000 families each month, including the only ministry in the area to HIV/AIDS patients and their families. We staff a housing program including Dehon Village with 38 single family homes for low-income families whose rent is subsidized by Sacred Heart Southern Missions. Our Social Services department includes an active volunteer program. Our priests serve six widely scattered parishes in an area that is only two percent Catholic.

Description: Online Madrassa is the leading Quran academy while teaching the Quran online for the last 10+ years. A thousand students around the world completed their online Quran courses with us. Name: JCP Downtown
Description:
Welcome to The Jewish Community Project of Lower Manhattan (often known as JCP Downtown)!

We are a vibrant center for Jewish life in Lower Manhattan. As a community, we build deep connections, explore Jewish identity, and do good downtown.

Jewish centers have been integral to sustaining Jewish life for more than two thousand years, and JCP’s story is no exception. Our organization emerged as a response to the return of Jewish life downtown after September 11, 2001. Since our official founding in 2002, we welcome all who seek a pluralistic experience rooted in the Jewish values of learning, connection, culture, and spirituality, and we engage people of all religious orientations and identities.

From an innovative Early Childhood Center to engaging Hebrew school classes, from Jewish holiday celebrations to adult learning and community service opportunities, we offer compelling experiences for people of all ages and stages of life. Through creativity and community, we celebrate our rich tradition and spark excitement about the role of Judaism in our contemporary lives. 

We can’t wait for you to join our ever-growing community!

Name: Lolland-Falsters Stift
Description:
Lolland-Falsters Stift er Danmarks sydligste stift med Gedser Odde og Gedser Sogn som det sydligste sogn, og med Femø som stiftets nordligste sogn.  

Vi selv ynder at kalde området for Sydhavsøerne eller De Kulinariske Øer på grund af det gode og milde klima, som er perfekt til dyrkning af råvarer,  og som i et stort udvalg kan købes i de mange gårdbutikker og i de lokale supermarkeder. 

Lolland-Falsters Stift blev udskilt fra Fyens Stift i 1804, og det er Danmarks mindste stift. 

Mottoet for Lolland-Falsters Stift er: "Fra fordybelse kommer fornyelse".
Fornyelse kan både være forandring eller bedst mulig bevarelse af det eksisterende. Fordybelse er nødvendigt for at kunne se vejen frem. Hvis ikke fornyelsen har rod i fordybelsen, vil den visne. Og omvendt: En fordybelse, som ikke fører til fornyelse, er gold. Det er som et træ: Det har brug for roden, men roden er ikke noget bevendt uden stammen og grenene.

Name: Yayasan Alfatihah
Description:
Rumah Tahfidz Al Fatihah merupakan Rumah Tahfidz di Kota Semarang dengan program Tahfidz yang menyenangkan untuk anak-anak. Rumah Tahfidz Al Fatihah ini diperuntukkan untuk anak-anak yang belum pernah mengaji, sulit mengaji, dan ketagihan gadget/game. Metode yang digunakan Rumah Tahfidz Al Fatihah adalah menghafal Qur’an dengan metode gerakan, sehingga sangat mungkin untuk mereka yang belum bisa membaca Al Qur’an tetap bisa menghafalnya. 

Alhamdulillah saat ini sudah ada 6 cabang Rumah Tahfidz di Kota Semarang dengan 500san lebih santri penghafal Al-Qur’an. Ada 5 cabang untuk santri non mukim dan 1 cabang khusus untuk santri mukim yang saat ini berjumlah 9 santri. Tidak hanya di Semarang saja, kami juga berniat untuk membuka cabang di berbagai kota besar lainnya, antara lain Bandung, Bogor, Surabaya, dan Pekanbaru karena melihat banyaknya kebutuhan saat ini.

Diharapkan dengan adanya RTA ini bisa membantu orang tua dalam meningkatan minat belajar anak terutama agar anak lebih tertarik dalam belajar Al Qur’an. Terlebih lagi agar anak bisa memanfaatkan gadget dengan baik, yaitu digunakan sebagai media pembelajaran.

Name: Apostolic Assembly
Description: The Apostolic Assembly of the Faith in Christ Jesus is a predominantly Hispanic-American Apostolic (Oneness Pentecostal) Christian church. Founded by 20 churches of Mexican immigrants scattered throughout the American Southwest in 1925, the Apostolic Assembly has grown to be 750 churches in the United States with missionary presence in 27 countries at the present.

Name: Fellowship Chamber
Description:
Uniting and Encouraging
• The Fellowship Chamber brings Christian businesspeople together to meet, share their testimony, hear the testimony of others, gain confidence and pray for each other.
• The Fellowship Chamber is inclusive and calls on its members to reserve judgment, seek humility and love one other.

Equipping
• Through the Fellowship Chamber, members gain practical tools for marketplace success.
• Members learn leadership skills from a biblical perspective.
• One-on-one mentoring and group coaching empowers members with key insight.
• The Fellowship Chamber helps members understand their calling and gifts to better serve God.

Engaging
• Internally, engagement means inspiring each other to lead more fulfilled lives.
• The Fellowship Chamber guides members to serve God and community by volunteering their time and talent in support of local nonprofits and areas of need.
• A portion of membership dues goes to fund worthwhile causes.

Meetings and Events
• At monthly events, such as luncheons and breakfasts, guest speakers deliver knowledge and inspiration on topics that bridge business and faith.
• At weekly networking groups, members create new relationships while exploring specific topics.
• At quarterly fellowship mixers, Christian business owners and professionals generate referrals and strengthen relationships in a relaxed and friendly atmosphere.
• The seminars and courses available through Fellowship Academy help members discover new ways to succeed in business while living out their faith.

Resources
• The Chamber Fellowship provides a membership directory so that we might encourage the growth of each other’s business.
• The Chamber Fellowship publishes a monthly newsletter providing information on issues relevant to the Christian business community and local government.

It is a doorway to action and adventure for young people in 86 nations of the world.
Royal Rangers seeks to develop the total person for Christ-spiritually, physically, mentally, and socially.

The Royal Rangers ministry provides exciting opportunities for boys and girls, in co-educational nations,
to discover a personal relationship with the Lord Jesus Christ. This life-changing ministry is one of the
world’s largest and foremost Christian adventure-based mentoring programs.

The Royal Rangers ministry incorporates both fun and accomplishment in its ministry methods.
It features indoor and outdoor activities including camping, recreation, first aid, crafts, hobby skills,
nature study, sports achievements, awards, trips, Bible study and much more.

These activities are among the resources used by Royal Rangers to build bridges to the heart of youth.
The heartbeat of the ministry is to enable its members to become more successful in every area of their lives.

Name: My Bible Today Podcast
Description:
My Bible Today Podcast is a daily podcast that helps you connect with the Bible in a simple and honest way.

We talk about what the Bible says, why it matters, and how it relates to real life. Whether you’re new to the Bible or have been reading it for years, this podcast is for you.

Each episode is short, friendly, and easy to follow; something you can listen to during your quiet time, on a walk, or while starting your day.

We believe the Bible has something for everyone. And sometimes, all it takes is a few minutes a day to hear what God is saying to you.

Name: Village Church (Canada)
Description:
We exist to see people transformed into fully devoted followers of Jesus.
We live that purpose out by prioritizing three things: gospel, community, and culture. 

Gospel
The gospel offers hope and meaning to our lives. It's about the fact that God loves us so much that he sent his son Jesus to die for our sins and rise from death to transform us. This means our hope is not in what we do for God, but what God, through Christ, has done for us. At Village Church, the gospel informs everything about who we are and what we do. We want to live authentically as imperfect people who have been redeemed by and found purpose in Jesus.

Community
God builds communities of faith by uniting the people he saves and motivating them to love each other and share the gospel. In a world that can be isolating and fragmented, this makes the church a breath of fresh air. It unites people regardless of their gender, age, ethnicity, class, or history. So wherever you're at in life, there's a place for you in Village Church.

Culture
Rather than separate ourselves from the world, we want to be a positive influence in it. Whatever we do as a church, we strive for excellence and want to engage with every level of our culture to see it transformed by Jesus. We want to represent the truths of Christianity to the world in insightful, creative, and gracious ways.

Name: Nueces Mosque
Description:
Donate: nuecesmosque.com/donate 

Join Newsletter: http://eepurl.com/gaAcpX 

Become a Sustainer: nuecesmosque.com/sustainer

Learn about Reconstruction: nuecesmosque.com/reconstruction 

Nueces Mosque is located in the heart of Austin, Texas and serves the diverse population of downtown Austin as well as students from The University of Texas, St. Edward's, and Austin Community College. It is often referred to as the Downtown Mosque or the University Mosque.

Nueces was established in 1977 by the Muslim Students' Association at the University of Texas. It was the first mosque in Austin and initially was named the Islamic Center of Greater Austin (ICGA). The mosque started in a 2000 sq. ft. building without any parking spaces. It was expanded in 1982 with the purchase of an adjacent building that was remodeled into a prayer hall. It was here that Peace Elementary School, the first accredited full-time Islamic School in Austin, was established. It was here that the Muslim community in Austin grew and flourished. From 1982 until 1999, the Muslim community was barely able to manage its activities in those two buildings. By the mercy of Allah (SWT) and the generous support of the local and outside communities, in November 1999, ICGA moved into a larger facility on Manor Road.

Name: Mother of the Americas Institute
Description:
Mother of the Americas Institute (MAI) is a think tank assisting the universal Church and the Church in the United States in the New Evangelization.

MAI serves the new evangelization through theoretical and practical research, publishing, education and training. The results of research are applied through concrete strategies, methods and compelling approaches for expressing the faith. 

Theoretical research in the areas of theology, philosophy, Church history, and pastoral practice are aimed at contributing to new methods and means of expression in the initial proclamation of the Gospel, more effective catechetical formation, and more fruitful pastoral care.  

Practical research in the areas of Church history, the missionary patrimony, experiences of contemporary evangelization apostolates and ecclesial movements, etc. are aimed at developing strategies, methods and means of expressions with contribute in a more immediate manner to the new evangelization. 

Our strategies, methods, and effective manners of expression address the three main emphases of the new evangelization, namely the need for a newness in ardor, methods and expression of the faith.  Education and training focus on strategies, methods and means of expressions to dioceses, parishes, seminaries, apostolates and individuals. - See more at: http://mainstitute.org/

Description:
Haggai International trabaja desde 1969, formando e inspirando hombres y mujeres, líderes influyentes, apasionados por Cristo y por transmitir Su mensaje. 
A través de nuestras Experiencias de Liderazgo Haggai (HLE), exponemos competencias esenciales para dar soluciones que permiten a los líderes cristianos mejorar su nivel de influencia, desarrollando sus actividades con integridad, siendo modelos a seguir con su estilo de vida. 

¿CÓMO LO HACEMOS?
Haggai International selecciona a líderes influyentes y estratégicamente posicionados de Asia, África, Latinoamérica y Medio Oriente; y los entrena para que puedan en su propio entorno presentar, demostrar e inspirar efectivamente el mensaje de Cristo. Name: NEWSONG
Description:
Our vision hasn’t changed in over 20 years. It boils down to two commands: 

“Love God. Love People." 
When they asked Jesus “Who’s my neighbor?” He answered with the story of the Good Samaritan; his neighbor was the MOST UNLOVABLE person in that culture. The outcast. The misfit. 

The “other.” 

As a church, we define neighbor the same way. 

While it may be easier to only spend time with people that are just like us, we are committed to a beautiful experience of multi-cultural diversity where we intentionally “do life” with the “other.” The one that looks and believes differently. The one that may be from a different neighborhood, have a different lifestyle and even financial status. 

We believe that because we are all created in God’s image, there is no longer “us” and “them.” There is only “we.” 

We believe that we are all created by God with immeasurable value, are better when we are unified but not uniform, are desperately in need of a savior, and that we are all responsible to make a difference in the world around us. 

We’d love for you to be a part of the story.

Description:
CBN Europe is part of The Christian Broadcasting Network's global outreach to share the Good News of Jesus to the nations.

Our Mission Statement is to Make the Name of Jesus known to everyone in the UK, Europe and Beyond. This is an audacious statement, but we serve an almighty God who has called us to share the Good News of Jesus to the nations!

The four pillars of CBN’s ministry here are: Media ministry, Prayer ministry, Humanitarian relief (which comprises of Orphan’s Promise and Operation Blessing) and CBN's animated Bible series 'Superbook'.

CBN Europe's media ministry reaches viewers in Europe in 22 languages on over 40 TV stations. Our humanitarian ministry through Operation Blessing and Orphan's Promise impacts thousands of beneficiaries each month through multiple projects, bringing hope to people in need. Operation Blessing Disaster Relief supports people affected by war and natural disasters.

With offices in the United Kingdom, The Netherlands, Germany, Poland, Croatia, Portugal and Ukraine, CBN Europe is strategically positioned to partner with many churches and volunteers to bring hope to a broken world. Together we can make a significant difference!

Name: Heartland Hope Mission
Description:
Heartland Hope Mission is a faith-based 501(c)3 charitable organization. Our mission is to be more than a pantry by providing clients with resources to be food secure & self-sufficient in a hope-filled environment.
Heartland Hope Mission helps working poor families by providing them with life's most basic staples including: a week’s supply of food, clothing, hygiene items, SNAP application assistance, community referrals, diapers & job resources. Because Heartland Hope Mission is a faith based organization, we offer an optional encouraging word & prayer.
When a family is trying to make a decision between food & an electric bill, they are often unable to think past their current situation. Heartland Hope Mission provides food, hygiene items & clothing to help alleviate these immediate pressures. By doing so, we give hope, so these families can consider options to improve their long-term condition. We meet with our clients one-on-one to connect them to resources to become self-sufficient.  Many of the families we serve have never had to ask for assistance.  Our intake process helps identify other resources to help the families work toward self-sufficiency.  We can help families apply for SNAP (food stamps) & approve additional assistance for those in temporary crisis situations.
Heartland Hope Mission works to connect our clients to job opportunities.  Employers are invited to come to Heartland Hope Mission to provide job information, application assistance & first interviews to our clients.  In addition, we have a jobs board that is updated on a weekly basis.  Other agencies also come onsite to provide job & career path assistance. 
Heartland Hope Mission relies on over 6,000 volunteers to serve over 45,000 people today. Our vision is to help create a strong community where all families are hopeful & self-sufficient.  Together we can prevent families from becoming homeless, ensure families have access to food & help families work toward self-sufficiency!

Name: Más Vida
Description:
Nuestro pastor Andrés Spyker nació en Michoacán el año de 1976; sus padres son Juan y Marla Spyker, ellos se establecieron en Morelia junto a su familia en 1984, después de fundar varias iglesias en Lázaro Cárdenas y en la Costa Michoacana. Sus abuelos, Jorge y Leola Spyker estuvieron en México durante los años ’50 y ’60 trabajando con huérfanos y plantando diversas iglesias.

Andrés ha vivido la mayor parte de su vida en Morelia, no obstante a la edad de 19 años fue a estudiar al Instituto Bíblico Elim en Nueva York, en el cual permaneció tres años. A su regreso, se casó con el amor de su vida, Kelly Spyker y actualmente tienen tres hijos: Jared, Lucas y Sofía.

Ministerio

Al volver del instituto Bíblico, empezó a trabajar en la Iglesia Más Vida (en ese entonces llamada Vida Abundante) como administrador y después como Pastor de Jóvenes.

En febrero de 2005, los pastores Andrés y Kelly tomaron el liderazgo de la iglesia, trayendo una visión renovada del propósito de Dios para la ciudad de Morelia, así como una gran pasión por Dios y por las almas.

Hoy en día, Más Vida cuenta con varios campus, el principal se ubica en la ciudad de Morelia y es conocido como Más Vida Cumbres; en Abril de 2013 se abrió el Campus en Guadalajara y una semana después, el Campus Madero que está al poniente de la ciudad de Morelia.

La Iglesia también tiene un campus en la ciudad de Toluca y el 27 de septiembre de 2015 se llevó a cabo el lanzamiento oficial del Campus Ciudad de México; además Más Vida cuenta con varias iglesias misioneras en España: Málaga (1999), Tarifa (1996), Algeciras (2011) y también en Marruecos (1991).

La visión de Andrés, así como de su padre, el Pastor Juan Spyker, es que haya un campus en cada ciudad de la República Mexicana.

For centuries this building, the Cathedral of the Sea, has watched, listened and helped the people of Portsmouth navigate the passage of time. It has witnessed war and peace, famous marriages, been bombed and rebuilt and remains a building of greatness and simple enduring beauty. More than this it is a vibrant and lively community set at the heart of the city and diocese of Portsmouth, responding to the call of God to meet people’s needs and equipping the whole diocese to share the love of God in Jesus Christ. ☩

Description: O Ministério Canaã foi fundado oficialmente no dia 14 de fevereiro de 2000. De forma desprendida, o pastor Jecer Goes deu início ao trabalho em uma sala do antigo Hotel Colonial, na Av. Beira Mar, em Fortaleza. No primeiro culto, ocorrido no dia 16 de fevereiro de 2000, estavam presentes menos de 30 irmãos. Após 30 dias, o trabalho foi transferido para o Colégio Professora Jemina Gois, no bairro Pan Americano. Lá, os cultos foram realizados na quadra da escola. Passados três meses, a igreja passou a ser sediada em um prédio alugado na Av. do Imperador (esquina com a Av. Domingos Olímpio), no Centro da capital cearense. Todos os que faziam parte daquela obra inicial ficaram “orgulhosos”, pois para eles o imóvel era grande e proporcionava melhores condições de realizar um bom trabalho.

Bibelselskabet støtter via indsamlinger også det internationale arbejde med at oversætte og udbrede Bibelen. 

Hendes Majestæt Dronning Margrethe II er protektor for Bibelselskabet.

To this day, Crisis Magazine remains America’s most trusted source for authentic Catholic perspectives on Church and State, arts and culture, science and faith. We have one purpose, and one only: to proclaim Christ’s Kingship over all things, at all times, to all nations.

In this rapidly changing world, St. Luke’s is a refuge to anchor your life on the unchanging truth of God’s perfect, unconditional and never-ending love for all people.

St. Luke's also operates an exemplary school that serves students in preschool through the eighth grade.

Name: Ordem de Malta Portugal

Description:
The Chapel Hill Bible Church is a non-denominational multicultural church in the university community of Chapel Hill. This church has an average weekly attendance of about 1000 for worship with vibrant children's, youth, university, men's, women's, and worship ministries.

We like to define ourselves as the "most relational large church in the Triangle." We GATHER on Sunday mornings to corporately worship and encourage the people of God through expository Bible teaching. We SCATTER to our communities where we live, work, and play to minister and serve one another through over 60 Life Groups around the Research Triangle.

Typical Sunday Attendance: Around 1,000

OUR VISION: To glorify God by being transformed by the gospel: our lives, our cities, and our world.

Name: JCAM - Jesuit Conference of Africa and Madagascar
Description:
Jesuits have been part of the fabric of African society for the last 450 years. In the last half century the order in Africa has transformed from a ministry of foreign missionaries to a truly African body representing the aspirations of an organisation that is bound to supporting a "faith that does justice" as they express their love of God.

Close to 400 ministries under our direction
We have close to 400 ministries in schools, communities and projects in Africa. As a Society we are often associated with evangelism, education and priestly formation but we are also active in taking on some of key structural and spiritual challenges of our time like social injustice, health issues and poverty.

Description: Woodlands operates week-long summer camps for junior, middle school and high school age students, as well as weekend or week-long retreats for all ages throughout the year. Name: Olive Baptist Church
Description:
Existing to develop fully devoted followers of Christ, Olive desires to equip you with the resources, encouragement, and godly community to see you grow in your walk with Christ. Our small groups will give you an opportunity to dig deeper into scripture and get to know other people in similar walks of life.

Olive Baptist Church was founded in 1894 when twelve people from Canoe Baptist Church in Canoe, Alabama came to Pensacola and started the church. Our church was originally found as a wooden structure on the south side of Olive Road, but in 1949, moved to the opposite side of the street.

Today, Olive has grown to an average of 3,000 attendees. Despite its large size, our church still contains the spirit of a smaller church through our numerous Bible studies, small groups, and ministry opportunities.

Experiencing God through worship is one of the most powerful moments in the life of a believer, whether it takes place in the privacy of your own home or with a larger group of believers in a corporate setting. Olive invites you to join us as we strive to create a worship experience that leads people to encounter the greatness and presence of their Creator. Our desire is to have a worship service that leaves you wanting more, inspires you to invite friends, and moves you to a deeper relationship with the Father.

Olive Baptist Church is pastor led, deacon served, committee organized, and church approved. It is also part of the Southern Baptist Convention. All Southern Baptist churches are autonomous but choose to work together to train and send out workers to spread the Gospel on a local, state, national, and international level.

Name: United Church of Bogota
Description: The United Church of Bogotá is a multi-denominational, Bible-based church ministering to the English-speaking community in Bogotá. We are a diverse fellowship of Christians who want to encounter God in worship and experience the impact of His grace on every part of our lives and in our world.

Name: Diocese of La Crosse
Description:
Called by Jesus Christ to “Seek first the kingdom of God;” we are the Diocese of La Crosse, a sacramental people, who worship God, teach the Catholic faith, and serve the needs of the human person.

Established on March 3, 1868, our local Church, the Diocese of La Crosse, was born by decree of Blessed Pope Pius IX. Today, we are proud to serve 950,000 souls, of whom 150,000 are Roman Catholic among 159 parishes. Father René Menard had arrived within our bounds more than 200 years earlier, and after him came other missionaries, settlers, farmers, woodsman, trappers, and riverboat mariners. Since its inception, the Diocese of La Crosse has seen the growth and increase from many nations—Polish, German, Irish, Hmong, and Mexican—added to its native peoples. It has seen the heroic witness of many, including Venerable Frederic Baraga, Venerable Samuel Mazzuchelli, Blessed Solanus Casey, Servant of God Father Joseph Walijewski, and Polonia’s Guatemalan Martyr Blessed James Miller.

The territory of the Diocese is nineteen counties in the west and central part of the State of Wisconsin. The western boundary of the diocese is formed by the Mississippi River. The Wisconsin River forms the southern and eastern boundary of the diocese. The northern boundary of the diocese is the southern boundary of the Diocese of Superior. The majority of the diocesan territory is rural and used for agriculture. There are six urban centers with combined populations close to 630,000.

As we look forward as a diocese, Bishop William Patrick Callahan, our tenth bishop, is giving vision to our mission of building up the Kingdom of God. Called by the love of Christ we recognize the Spirit of God in one another and give witness to the value of human life by mutual respect and honor. In particular, this is represented in everyone’s call to vocation, marriage and family life, sacred worship and a deep desire to live a saintly life and thus meriting heaven.

Name: My Faith Votes
Description:
My Faith Votes is a non-partisan movement that motivates, equips and activates Christians in America to vote in every election, transforming our communities and influencing our nation with biblical truth.

For years, Christians have bought into the lie that faith is a private matter. As a result, more than 25 million Christians who are registered to vote do not vote in Presidential Elections, and as many as 65 million Christians do not vote in local elections.

Imagine 90-million Christians in America being activated to PRAY, THINK, and ACT on their faith. And when informed Christians vote, they bring the influence of their convictions and beliefs to the public square.

Description: Evangelism Explosion is a non-profit Christian ministry that trains people how to share their faith in Christ and how to bring people from unbelief to belief. It utilizes a variety of components including prayer, actual on-the-job training where the experienced lead the inexperienced, and the principle of spiritual multiplication.

Name: Bahá'í Deutschland – Büro für Außenbeziehungen
Description: Das  Büro für Außenbeziehungen der Bahá’í-Gemeinde in Deutschland K.d.ö.R., mit Sitz in Berlin, beteiligt sich auf nationaler Ebene an den vorherrschenden Diskursen der Gesellschaft (Rolle von Religion und Kultur für gesellschaftlichen Zusammenhalt; gesellschaftliche Teilhabe u.a. der Jugend; etc.) und ist außerdem für den Schutz der verfolgten Bahá’í im Iran und Jemen zuständig. Zu beiden Zwecken steht es im Kontakt zur Bundesregierung, Bundestagsabgeordneten, zivilgesellschaftlichen Organisationen, Stiftungen und Journalisten. Es vertritt den Nationalen Geistigen Rat der Bahá’í in Deutschland im Deutschen Institut für Menschenrechte, dem Forum Menschenrechte, der Stiftung der internationalen Wochen gegen Rassismus, dem Deutschen Menschenrechts-Filmpreis sowie dem Bundesnetzwerk Bürgerschaftliches Engagement. Das Büro ist bestrebt, mit und von anderen zu lernen, indem es mit gleichgesinnten Einzelpersonen und Organisationen zum Wohle aller in Deutschland und weltweit lebenden Menschen zusammenarbeitet.

Name: Mount Pisgah Church
Description:
Mount Pisgah Church is a diverse community of believers brought together by the transforming grace and love of Jesus Christ.

A church built on the inerrant truth of the Bible, Mount Pisgah shares the abiding love of God by caring for one another, and reaching into our neighborhoods, our community, and the world with the Good News of salvation.

Led by Pastor Steve Wood, Mount Pisgah  is a church that brings God's Word to people in every situation, from all walks of life, helping them to grow in love and service to our Lord.

Name: Long Beach Rescue Mission & Foundation
Description: Since 1972, the Long Beach Rescue Mission, a 501(c)(3) nonprofit, has opened its doors to thousands of men, women and children. The mission provides emergency food, clothing, shelter and spiritual guidance to the homeless and less fortunate people of the community. The Long Beach Rescue Mission stands dedicated to helping individuals overcome the cycle of homelessness.

Name: Henri Nouwen Society
Description:
Henri Nouwen was a brilliant Dutch-born Catholic priest and pastor, psychologist, professor, and prolific author. He taught at the University of Notre Dame and the Divinity Schools of Yale and Harvard before becoming the pastor at L’Arche Daybreak in Toronto, where he served for the final decade of his life. Nouwen wrote and spoke with honesty and vulnerability about faith, spiritual formation, community, and other themes that still resonate today. He wrote 39 books during his lifetime, with about 1 million copies sold. Today, book sales have surpassed 7 million copies in more than 35 languages, and books drawn from Nouwen’s writings and ideas continue to be published. Although he passed away unexpectedly in 1996, he is regarded as a spiritual master for our times.

The Henri Nouwen Society was established in Canada in 2002. Its core purpose is to share Nouwen’s spiritual vision so that people can be transformed by experiencing themselves as God’s beloved. The spiritual content provided by the Henri Nouwen Society has never been more timely and relevant. In these anxious times, fuelled by the pandemic, war, conflict, climate change, and other concerns, our daily e-meditations (51k subscribers), podcast, and social media platforms have elicited many heartfelt expressions of appreciation and gratitude for Henri Nouwen’s comforting and hope-filled words in these times.

Name: Ahmadiyya Muslim Community Greece
Description: The Ahmadiyya Muslim Community are Muslims who believe in the Messiah, Mirza Ghulam Ahmad (peace be on him) (1835-1908) of Qadian. Mirza Ghulam Ahmad founded the Ahmadiyya Muslim Community in 1889 as a revival movement within Islam, emphasizing its essential teachings of peace, love, justice, and sanctity of life. Today, the Ahmadiyya Muslim Community is the world’s largest Islamic community under one Divinely appointed leader, His Holiness, Mirza Masroor Ahmad (may Allah be his Helper) (b. 1950). The Ahmadiyya Muslim Community spans over 200 nations with membership exceeding tens of millions.

Description: IQRA’ International Educational Foundation designs Islamic studies curricula used throughout the world by Islamic full-time schools, weekend schools, and Muslim homeschools.  IQRA’s curriculum features five Islamic Studies programs: Program of Arabic Studies; Program of Qur’an Studies; Program of Aqidah, Fiqh, and Akhlaq; Program of Sirah and Hadith; and Program of Islamic History and Geography.  Each program’s grade level has Islamic studies textbooks, workbooks, and teacher’s manuals.  Besides Islamic curriculum development, IQRA’ offers teacher training Islamic studies teachers.

Description: KosherKlaf.com is a premier online destination with locations in Woodmere (Five Towns) and Washington Heights. We are dedicated to providing exceptional STaM (Sifrei Torah, Tefillin, Mezuzot, and Megillot) with a commitment to the highest standards of Kashrus and Quality.

Name: Indre Mission
Description:
I Indre Mission vil vi gerne hjælpe hinanden og andre med at trænge til bunds i de store spørgsmål i livet – om meningen med det hele, vores betydning for hinanden, vores værdi, kærligheden og døden. Udgangspunktet er troen på Gud, og målet er at lede mennesker til et møde med Jesus.

Ud over landet inviterer vi alle med i små og store fællesskaber – ofte i missionshuse. Vi tilbyder fællesskab for forskellige aldersgrupper og i forskellige livssituationer. Vi ser missionshusene som kirkens dagligstue, hvor livet som kristen kan udfoldes, og nysgerrige kan møde kristen tro i praksis.

Vi synger, beder, læser i Bibelen, samtaler og lytter til forkyndelse. Vi lever i en tid, hvor det kan være svært at få ro til eftertanke. Det vil vi gerne give anledning til. Og så vil vi opmuntre hinanden til at møde vore medmennesker med kærlighed og omsorg i hverdagen.

Gennem ord og handling vil vi vise, hvem Jesus er og lede mennesker til ham.

Name: Biblical Counseling Center
Description:
The mission of Biblical Counseling Center is “Helping Churches Care for People.” We accomplish this mission by connecting them to the gospel and to the wisdom of the Word while providing compassion care and practical solutions.

Since 1989 we have served churches through helping their people receive compassionate, biblical counseling and by equipping their own people to care through counseling training and resources.

Our online school has trained 1000's of church leaders and volunteers on how to care with confidence, competence and compassion.

Name: Family Christian Center
Description:
It is our vision to build a biblically functioning, Acts 2 community of believers that will help get the gospel into every home in the six-county area. Our definition of success is a changed life. Our mission is to help people find and follow Jesus. 

FCC began in 1987 with the idea that the family should be our first priority. We have experienced consistent growth and now minister to one of the largest congregations in the area.

We utilize modern music styles, video, drama, dance, multimedia, etc. These diverse elements help us reinforce the message we are trying to convey so that those who attend are able to understand and remember these principles as they learn to apply them to their own unique situations.

We are deeply committed to supporting outreach projects in our community, short-term mission trips around the country and to foreign lands, and permanent missionaries in the US and abroad. We allocate a tithe (10%) of every dollar that is given in offerings to our outreach and mission funds. Additionally, several projects are chosen each year for which we raise funds to help support a cause we believe in. 

Recent projects have included digging wells for clean drinking water in 3rd-world countries, building orphanages in Myanmar, purchasing freedom for several children who were trapped in slave trade operations, and providing finances for cleft & palette surgeries for 5 orphans in a remote area of China.

Name: Diocèse de Rennes
Description:
Le diocèse de Rennes, Dol et Saint-Malo s’étend sur le département d'Ille-et-Vilaine. D'une superficie de 6 775 km², il est composé de 7 pays, de 12 doyennés pour 74 paroisses.

Mgr Pierre d'Ornellas occupe le siège épiscopal de Rennes depuis le 26 mars 2007. Il a pour devise épiscopale : « À cause de son grand amour ». En tant qu’évêque du diocèse de Rennes, Dol et Saint-Malo, Mgr Pierre d’Ornellas est aussi Archevêque de la Province ecclésiastique de Rennes, qui regroupe les 4 diocèses de Bretagne et les 5 diocèses des Pays de Loire.

Name: Joodse Gemeente Amsterdam (NIHS)
Description: De Joodse Gemeente Amsterdam bestaat 383 jaar en heeft een rijke historie. Vandaag is het vooral een dynamische kehilla (gemeente), die haar leden in vrijwel alle behoeften van het Jodendom voorziet en een groot scala van activiteiten ontplooit.

Name: Sun Valley Community Church
Description:
Sun Valley first opened its doors in 1990 with a heart to reach people in Arizona who nobody was reaching with the good news that God loves us and that Jesus has provided the way for us to be restored in a relationship with Him.

That is still who we are today.

We are one church that meets in multiple locations across Arizona as well as online with the mission of "Helping People Meet, Know, and Follow Jesus." We believe that we are just getting started and the best is yet to come as we work together to help fulfill the mission that Jesus has given us.

Name: Chestnut Hill Benevolent Association
Description: Founded in 1916 and situated on a 24 acre campus in the Chestnut Hill area of Brookline, MA, Chestnut Hill Benevolent Association’s mission is to  provide a healing refuge and services that minister to the needs of mankind with Christian Science nursing. Its programs include inpatient and outpatient care as well as visiting Christian Science nursing services and a Christian Science nurses training school. Its residential programs range from short term accommodations to interim and permanent housing solutions.

Name: Movimiento Misionero Mundial
Description:
Somos una organización que nació en el corazón de Dios para esta hora final. Nos hemos extendido por la mayor parte del continente americano, abarcando numerosos países de Europa, avanzando en el África, con presencia en varios países del Oriente Medio y Lejano, y creciendo hasta Oceanía. Las actividades que ejercemos radican en la predicación de los Santos Evangelios, enfatizado en la Sana Doctrina Bíblica. Realizamos también campañas de evangelización, estudios bíblicos, formando obreros y estableciendo nuevos templos, además de brindar ayuda a los más necesitados. 

Con 57 años de constitución, hemos llegado a más de 70 países, naciones que han reconocido a Jesucristo como su Señor; alrededor de 8,000 congregaciones doblan sus rodillas ante el soberano Dios y un cuerpo ministerial que sobrepasa los 9,000 obreros, claman día y noche para que la misericordia de Dios se extienda a más naciones.

Nuestro fundador es el Rev. Luis M. Ortiz, las oficinas principales se establecen en Puerto Rico, la Junta Oficial de esta institución tiene la siguiente estructura: Presidente, Vicepresidente, Secretario, Tesorero y Vocales; aquellos que velan y dirigen los principios, objetivos y metas de esta organización. 

En palabras del Rev. Luis M. Ortiz: "La fuerza que nos impulsa y motiva es el Espíritu Santo; la pasión que nos conmueve es la situación de las almas y el motivo que nos inspira es la Gloria de Dios; la fuente que nos somete es el crecimiento de la obra misionera; la urgencia que nos moviliza es la brevedad del tiempo y la realidad que nos alienta es el abundante fruto".

Name: Munshi Singh College
Description: MS College Motihari is situated in Motihari in Bihar state of India. Established in 1945, it is accredited from NAAC, AICTE and it is affiliated to B.R. Ambedkar Bihar University. MS COLLEGE MOTIHARI, Motihari offers 20 courses across 6 streams namely IT, Management, Science, Law, Arts, Commerce & Banking and across 7 degrees like BSc, BCA, BBA, LLB, BA.Hostel facility is not available for its students. Additional campus facilities such as Canteen, Comp. Lab, Counselling, Lib, Medical Fac., Cls. Room, Sports, Seminar Hall, Hostel are also there.

Name: Church of the Saviour
Description: Church of the Saviour exists to see lives radically changed and hearts transformed by the love of Jesus Christ. Whether you’re a first time guest or a long-time member, we want you to feel welcomed into our community.

رواه البخاري و مسلم

Name: Calvin Christian School
Description:
Since 1961 Christian parents have partnered with outstanding teachers to provide God-honoring education at Calvin Christian School. From the sciences to the arts, from the classroom to the playground, every part of the Calvin experience is designed to equip young disciples of Christ for leadership and service.

Today Calvin Christian serves more than 600 students at its K-8 campuses in Blaine and Edina and its high school campus in Fridley. Calvin Christian School is a far-reaching community of believers, serving families from more than 90 churches and about 50 municipalities in the greater Twin Cities.

Name: Compassion Christian Church
Description:
Our mission is to lead people to a life-changing relationship with Jesus.

Join us for worship services at 9:00 and 11 a.m. on Sundays and at 6:45 p.m. on Wednesdays.

Name: Overlake Christian Church
Description: Overlake Christian Church is a place where people are welcomed as they are, not a place for people who have it all together. It's OK to not be OK at OCC. The Overlake family speaks more than 40 languages and comes from more than 45 countries. What unites us is the singular truth that Jesus is the only answer for our lives.

Name: Church of England - Diocese of Derby
Description:
The Diocese of Derby consists of most of Derbyshire and a couple of parishes on the fringes of Staffordshire, serving a population of around one million people, and has more than 300 churches.

It works in communities, schools, prisons and hospitals as well being represented in various other aspects of city and county life.

The diocese has around 220 licensed clergy and 300+ licensed lay ministers (readers).

Name: Valley Family Church
Description: Valley Family Church is a growing church in Southwest Michigan. Our mission is simple: we exist so people get it. Find us in Kalamazoo off the I-94 and Oakland Drive exit. VFC was pioneered in 1991 by Pastors Jeff and Beth Jones, a husband-and-wife team who share a passion for the local church and people. We welcome you to join us and take the next step in your journey of faith. Sundays at 9:30 & 11:30am live or Sunday 9:30am online at valleyfamilychurch.tv.

Name: Virginia Conference of The United Methodist Church
Description: The Virginia Annual Conference services members of 1,200 United Methodist churches and 1,000 clergy in the Commonwealth of Virginia. The conference office is in Glenn Allen, a suburb of Richmond. The conference is the largest in the Methodist connection. Visit our website at www.vaumc.org

Name: Good Shepherd Christian Preschool

Where Preschoolers Learn To Read

'Train up a child in the way he should go, and when he is old he will not depart from it.' Proverbs 22:6

Name: Southland Christian Church
Description: We are courageous followers of Jesus who love God and love people. We are a large community of believers with places of worship covering central Kentucky including Danville, Georgetown, Lexington, Nicholasville, and Richmond. Visit any one of our Christian church services each Sunday or join us online!

Name: Cathedral of Christ the King
Description: The Cathedral of Christ the King is the seat of the Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Atlanta. Founded in 1937, it is the home to almost 6,000 parish families. We serve our parishioners and greater Atlanta through the work of more than 100 ministries. The Cathedral of Christ the King is the seat of the Archbishop of Atlanta, Gregory J. Hartmayer, OFM, and led by our rector, Monsignor Francis McNamee.

Name: Gas Street Church
Description: An anglican resourcing church in the centre of Birmingham, with a vision to be 'Light for the City'

Name: Riverside Church
Description: Our purpose, the very reason we exist, is to make and send disciples who love and live like Jesus.

Name: Evangelical Fellowship of Zimbabwe
Description:
In 1962, a few Evangelical Church leaders came together to form what is now known as the Evangelical Fellowship of Zimbabwe (EFZ). Over the years its membership has grown to over 900 member bodies comprising Denominations, Church Bodies, Ministries and Para-Church Organizations. The Fellowship represents over 4.5 million individual members.

Over the years the EFZ has become a strong, growing and unifying platform for preaching the Gospel, equipping church leaders and facilitating fellowship and joint ministry, meeting the needs of vulnerable communities and being a positive influence in nation-building efforts. Local and International funding partners have played a significant role in assisting the EFZ to fulfil its holistic mandate of being salt and light to the nation; and some of the partners included IOM, Mercy Corps, Higher Life International, Tearfund, Living Water International, World Vision, EU, ISO23, Africare, CCSF and EPOIZ among many others.

Name: Igreja Evangelica Assembleia De Deus
Description: Igreja Evangelica Assembleia De Deus is a religious institutions company based out of Rua do Rosario 1258, Ponta Grossa, Parana, Brazil.

Name: Advent Christian Village
Description:
Since its founding in 1913, Advent Christian Village (ACV) has been devoted to serving others. Originally a home for orphaned children and retired ministers, this vibrant retirement community welcomes people of all backgrounds. Decades of experience have reinforced its commitment to service and given rise to innovative programs and a full range of services for senior adults, families with special needs, and children. The community has developed and grown, adapting its methods to keep pace with the needs of a changing society. Yet, it has remained true to its mission: “to express Christ’s love by providing compassionate care and quality comprehensive services” to those it serves.

Today, ACV is a thriving community of nearly 800 members and 500 employees. The 1,200-acre campus offers scenic landscapes along the Suwannee River within a hometown atmosphere. At ACV, neighbors care about each other. This unique community is enriched by members of all backgrounds, faiths and economic abilities.

No entrance fees and reasonable rates make it a viable option for virtually anyone. Along with a full continuum of care, including independent living, assisted living and skilled nursing care, ACV offers a range of rental options and even home ownership. Members may choose from an array of services, paying only for those they use. Many services are available to the public as well.

This self-contained village has the beneﬁts of a city, complete with a wellness center, quality medical clinic, dining choices, pharmacy, grocery store and market center. There is plenty to do, with engaging activities for on- and off-campus members and a variety of volunteer opportunities.

Many people say they feel something special when they visit ACV for the first time. This is truly a warm and engaging community. We strive for quality in all that we do and measure ourselves by our mission, striving every day to live out that attitude of compassion.

Name: Bryn Mawr Presbyterian Church
Description:
BMPC is a large congregation of the Presbyterian Church (USA) with a wide variety of programs and ministries for all ages. Our campus on the Main Line, just west of Philadelphia, is busy seven days a week as a place to enjoy community and find perspective. Here, we are encouraged to ask questions as we learn and think about the Christian faith, and we are given space to open the Bible and discover greater meaning in our daily lives. On Sundays and throughout the week, we find support and enrichment, strengthen existing relationships, build new ones, and find hands-on ways to help others.
 
We come together as a community of faith to worship, when we put all else aside to glorify God. Our beautiful Sanctuary is filled with glorious stained glass windows, a magnificent pipe organ, the music of choirs, and the Word of God. Whether you join us for worship there or in the intimacy of our Chapel, it is our hope that you will experience God's presence.
 
Though our Sunday morning worship style and space is traditional, you'll discover that our theology is open-minded and inclusive. Called to embody Jesus' hospitality, we affirm the worth of all, regardless of race, ethnic origin, sexual orientation, or worldly condition. Our sense is that we are more fully God's people when we recognize the gift of diversity, including diverse paths to God. Grounded in worship and inspired to serve, the BMPC community offers reassurance and hope in a continually changing world.

Name: Muslim Society of Jersey Shore (Masjid Bilal)
Description:
Mission:
Masjid Bilal (Muslim Society of Jersey Shore, Inc.) is a 501(c)3 non-profit religious organization.
An Islamic Trust (Waqf) Affiliated with North American Islamic Trust Inc. (NAIT)
Serving Toms River TWP, Ocean, Burlington, Atlantic, Cape May Counties, and Jersey Shore Communities.
Committed to the Islamic way of life-based on the Qur’an and life example (Sunnah) of Prophet Muhammad (Peace and Blessings Be Upon Him and all prophets Since Adam), expressed through member activities in areas such as education, Sports, and civic involvement.

Our hope is that the misconceptions people might have carried for a long time will begin to fade away as we seek to become a community of grace, passionately pursuing life and mission with Jesus. We long for people to be drawn back to Jesus, seeing him and the life he offers in a fresh, new way. 

We invite you to come and experience the heart-felt worship, warm fellowship, and straightforward biblical teaching Fellowship Greenville has to offer. We don't claim to have a corner on the market when it comes to good churches, but we do feel that we are different from other churches you might visit in the Greenville area. Our vision is to see God transform us into a community of grace living out the purpose and personality of Jesus in our world. We want to gather as a body and then go out into the world, allowing God's grace to flow through us.
