Name: Daily Intercession
Description:
Welcome to Daily Intercession People, a global faith-based community dedicated to the power of collective prayer, worship, & the word. 

Our mission is to unite believers from all corners of the world, fostering a network of support and spiritual growth through daily intercession.

Our Purpose:
At Daily Intercession, we believe in the transformative power of prayer, worship, & the word. We provide a platform for individuals to share their prayer needs and intercede for others, creating a ripple effect of faith, hope, and love. Our community is committed to lifting up those in need, supporting the less privileged, and promoting unity within the body of Christ.

What We Offer:
- Daily Prayer Prompts: Start each day with guided prayers to inspire and uplift.
- Testimonies of Faith: Share and celebrate stories of answered prayers within our community.
- Virtual Prayer Meetings: Join us for regular online gatherings to pray, worship, and share word with fellow believers.
- Devotional Content: Access a rich library of devotionals, worship music, and inspirational messages.
- Volunteer Opportunities: Engage in various activities, from leading prayer, praise, & word groups to community outreach.

Get Involved:
Whether you're looking to strengthen your prayer life, find spiritual support, or volunteer your time and talents, Daily Intercession welcomes you. Together, we can make a meaningful impact through the power of prayer, worship, and word.

Prayer requests:
Send in your prayer requests here:- https://forms.gle/7REr1B28adwVrQ4H7

Connect with Us:
Join our LinkedIn community to stay updated on our latest initiatives, events, and volunteer opportunities. Let's unite in faith, support one another, and witness the incredible things God can do through collective intercession.

Daily Intercession – Where Every Prayer Matters.

Description: St. James Cathedral is a vibrant and historic Episcopal church in the heart of downtown Chicago, welcoming you as you are, without conditions or expectations. Name: Union Gospel Mission of Salem
Description:
Union Gospel Mission of Salem was founded in 1953 by a group of Christian businessmen with a heart for the homeless…

Our Union Gospel Mission seeks to restore dignity, hope, and renewal by sharing the love and grace of Jesus Christ with those in need. Through an array of transformative services (such as the New Life Fellowship) and comprehensive human care, we reach out to hurting and vulnerable neighbors to help them discover new life in Christ.

Name: Racers For Christ
Description:
Team RFC is a group of people whose love of the Lord is so strong that we include him in our everyday activities... even on race day or at the car shows. Our motto is "Bringing Inspiration to the World of Motorsports." To do so we provide chapel services, pastoral care, outreach and Christian fellowship.

We participate at races, car shows, cruise-ins and tractor pulls. You can see our staff at drag strips, circle tracks, road courses and the lakes too. One might see us at a meeting with wild customs, radical street rods or those restoration projects seeking to duplicate (or improve upon) showroom condition. But through it all we remember that "Cars are our Passion, People are our Purpose."

The Christian Motorsports International, Inc. family of ministries conducts non-denominational chapel services at local and professional racing events nationwide, providing chapel services, invocations, and a pastoral presence for more than 2000 racing events each racing year -- including drag racing, open wheel and motorcycle racing, boat and stock car racing, off-road and airplane racing, and hot rod events.

For many people involved in motorsports, either as a profession or as a hobby, consistent participation in a local church body is difficult.  For some, Team RFC provides the pastoral support not unlike that which they would find from a home church, including: communion, weddings, vow renewals, baptisms, baby dedications, hospital visits, sick calls, memorial services, and funerals.

Name: St. Michael the Archangel Catholic Church
Description:
St. Michael is a Roman Catholic community called by God to discipleship through worship, education, and service. We strive to be a place that exudes the joy, peace, and mercy of Christ. As a thriving parish with over 5,000 registered families in Cary, North Carolina, we are dedicated to creating an environment where all feel welcome and embraced.

At the heart of our mission is a commitment to grow in faith and share God's love. Our beautiful church, named after St. Michael the Archangel, serves as a spiritual sanctuary where you can encounter Christ and connect with others. Whether you’re visiting for the first time or returning after a while, we extend a warm welcome to all.

We offer a wide variety of programs for all ages, including Mass, Adoration, Bible Studies, and Faith Formation, as well as service opportunities to give back to our community. With over 60 ministries, there’s something for everyone to get involved in and grow spiritually. We are also proud to provide a preschool and school on campus and have a vibrant Hispanic Ministry offering bilingual worship opportunities.

Though we are a large parish, we maintain a close-knit, family-like atmosphere where everyone is valued. St. Michael is a place where people come together to worship, learn, and serve, united in faith and love.

We invite you to join us at St. Michael—your spiritual home. Explore our website, attend Mass, and take the next step in your journey of faith.

Name: The Bayou Church
Description:
The Bayou Church is all about helping people connect in Christ-centered relationships that lead to a real, full and lasting life—the kind of life that is only found in Christ. It starts with a relationship with God through Jesus and grows through our relationships with each other. These relationships are actually producing real life change— we’re discovering, a little more every day, the life God made us for.

We invite you to join us this Sunday. Service times at 9:30 & 11:00 am. There's something for every age of your family!

Name: Missioners of Christ
Description:
Missioners of Christ is a Catholic Christian Community centered in prayer and united by covenant that evangelizes, disciples and sends forth youth and young adults on domestic and foreign missions to fulfill the Great Commission of Christ.

Our “four pillars” shape the essence of our formation and spirituality; we are Eucharistic; understanding the Eucharist to be “the source and summit of Christian life” (CCC 1324), Charismatic; believing in and calling upon the Gifts of the Holy Spirit, which are “at the service of charity which builds up the Church” (CCC 2003), Marian; holding a special devotion to Our Lady, which is “intrinsic to Christian worship” (CCC 971), recognizing Mary as a “preeminent and… wholly unique member of the Church” (CCC 967), and Magisterial; being submitted to the teaching authority of the Catholic Church, whose “authority in this matter is exercised in the name of Jesus Christ” (CCC 85).

Our Covenant defines our commitments to each other and collectively to God, in how we live out our daily experience as a Missioner. This Covenant serves as a general rule of life, which allows us to be in solidarity with the greater Missioner community, whether we are in proximity to other members or not. The Covenant also reinforces and emphasizes aspects of the Catholic Christian life which are already expectations of the Church for its faithful, such as frequenting the Sacraments, stewardship, prayer and fasting, and charity to one’s neighbor.

Description:
Hare Krishna
Come and experience the Nectar of Life at the Hare Krishna Temple, Singing, Dancing, Philosophy and a great Free vegetarian feast.

Every Sunday 6:00 pm

WhatsApp - 8087032032

===============
Mission Statement
1. To systematically propagate spiritual knowledge to society at large and to educate all people in the techniques of spiritual life in order to check the imbalance of values in life and to achieve real unity and peace in the world.
2. To propagate a consciousness of Krishna (God), as it is revealed in the great scriptures of India, Bhagavad-gita, and Srimad-Bhagavatam.
3. To bring the members of the Society together with each other and nearer to Krishna, the prime entity, thus developing the idea within the members, and humanity at large, that each soul is part and parcel of the quality of Godhead (Krishna).
4. To teach and encourage the sankirtan movement, congregational chanting of the holy name of God, as revealed in the teachings of Lord Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu.
5. To erect for the members and for society at large a holy place of transcendental pastimes dedicated to the personality of Krishna.
6. To bring the members closer together for the purpose of teaching a simpler, more natural way of life. 
7. With a view towards achieving the aforementioned purposes, to publish and distribute periodicals, magazines, books and other writings.

Name: Tewkesbury Abbey
Description:
We would like to welcome you to enjoy the peace and beauty of our Abbey.
Consecrated in 1121, the church of the Benedictine Abbey of Tewkesbury survived the monastic dissolution of 1540 thanks for the generosity of the townspeople, who bought it from King Henry VIII for £453. It remains the spiritual focus of parish life today.
The Abbey is of cathedral proportions and is one of the largest parish churches in the country. There has been a church on this site for over 1,200 years and, in fact, we will be celebrating our 900th anniversary in 2021. Exciting plans are already underway to ensure that the Abbey will be fit for purpose for 2021 and for many years to come.
We are supported only by the people who worship here and by our many visitors, whose generosity enables us to keep this beautiful building, and the work we do here, alive and growing.
Please do come and visit us soon!

Name: Greenhills Christian Fellowship
Description:
Greenhills Christian Fellowship, also known as GCF, is a church at the very heart of Ortigas Center, Pasig City - one of the fastest-growing commercial centers in the Philippines today. The GCF family of churches has an over-all constituency of over 7,000 worshippers. GCF was born out of a New Testament heritage, assisting in the spreading of the gospel through evangelism, discipleship, and in the multiplication of churches

MISSION

To make disciples of Jesus Christ in the Philippines and beyond (Matthew 28:18-20)

VISION

Lives and communities transformed through Jesus Christ (2 Cor. 5:17, Acts 2:42-47)

CORE VALUES

1. Centrality of God's Word (2 Tim. 3:16-17)
2. Discipleship and shepherding through small groups (Mark 3:14, Acts 2:46-47)
3. Vibrant worship and fervent prayer (Acts 2:42-47)
4. Excellence in life and ministry (1 Cor. 14:12, 2 Cor. 8:7)
5. Intentional evangelism and church planting (Mat. 28:16-20, Acts 1:8)
6. Leadership development (Mark 3:14, 2 Tim. 2:2)

Name: Words of Hope
Description:
Our Mission: We equip indigenous Christian leaders to share the hope of the gospel in the heart languages of unreached people.

In countries where the Good News of Jesus Christ is illegal or persecution is commonplace, partnering with indigenous leaders to proclaim the gospel through a variety of communication channels is the most effective means of beginning to reach the population with words of salvation and encouragement.

For 80 years, Words of Hope has harnessed the power of the spoken and written word to bring Christian truth to ears that need to hear it. The first messages of hope were sent out exclusively on radio. Today, words of gospel hope are shared in over 80 heart languages in collaboration with local leaders.

Name: Middle Collegiate Church
Description:
Middle Collegiate Church is a celebrating, culturally diverse, inclusive and growing community of faith where all people are welcomed just as they are as they come through the door. As a teaching congregation that celebrates the arts, our ministries include rich and meaningful worship, care and education that nurture the mind, body and spirit, social action which embraces the global community, and participation in an interfaith dialogue for the purpose of justice and reconciliation. The Rev. Jacqui Lewis, Ph.D. is Senior Minister of Middle Collegiate Church.

Established in 1628, the Collegiate Church is an ecumenical church affiliated with the Reformed Church in America, the National and World Council of Churches, and the New York City Council of Churches.

Name: Indianapolis Muslim Community Association Inc. (IMCA)
Description: The Indianapolis Community Association is a 501c3 religions nonprofit established in 1977. Its subsidiaries include Masjid Al Fajr (mosque), MTI School of Knowledge (K-12 school), Al Ilm (Sunday school), a food pantry, and burial program. IMCA is the most diverse Muslim Community in central Indiana.

Name: National Shrine of Saint Elizabeth Ann Seton / Seton Heritage Ministries
Description: As the home of the first American-born saint, our mission is to share the life and legacy of Saint Elizabeth Ann Seton as a source of inspiration for all people. At our site, we have two historic home that she lived in, a museum and visitor center that tell her story, and a Basilica where her remains are entombed. We offer daily tours, as well as Mass Sunday through Friday and Confession Monday through Friday.

Name: YJP Atlanta
Description:
Young Jewish Professionals (YJP) Intown, Atlanta serves as a hub for social and educational activities for any Jew that moves — serving the hundreds of young Jewish adults living and working in Atlanta.

Meet, network, study, and socialize with other young Jewish professionals in their 20’s and 30’s in an inclusive, congenial, stylish, and relaxed setting with a spirited vibe.

Name: The North Georgia Conference of The United Methodist Church
Description:
Comprised of approximately 400 churches,  1,000 clergy members, and 220,000 lay members, The North Georgia Conference of The United Methodist Church seeks to empower our United Methodist connection to actively engage and transform communities through the love and grace of Jesus Christ. 

The United Methodist Church is a world-wide denomination committed to open hearts, open doors, and open minds through active engagement with our world. John Wesley and the early Methodists placed primary emphasis on Christian living, on putting faith and love into action. This emphasis has continued to be a hallmark of United Methodism today.

Name: Word of Life Christian Center
Description: Word of Life Christian Center in Rock Island, IL

Name: White Memorial Presbyterian Church
Description:
White Memorial Presbyterian Church is a congregation of the Presbyterian Church (USA) and is a member of the New Hope Presbytery. We affirm that we are a part of the universal fellowship of Christian churches, which includes all those who acknowledge the one God, profess faith in Jesus Christ as Lord and Savior, and trust the redeeming power of the Holy Spirit.

At White Memorial we worship, embrace and serve in order that we might lend our voices to the proclamation of Jesus Christ and his love. Our most ardent hope is to be a community dedicated to the proclamation of God's good news and serving our neighbors.

Name: Daily Injera
Description: Daily Injera is a Christian ministry which delivers a verse from The Holy Bible each day.

Name: Interfaith Council of Metropolitan Washington
Description:
Created in 1978, the InterFaith Conference of Metropolitan Washington (IFC) brings together eleven historic faith communities to promote dialogue, understanding and a sense of community and to work cooperatively for justice throughout the DC region.

IFC members currently include the Baha’i, Buddhist, Hindu, Islamic, Jain, Jewish, Latter-day Saints, Protestant, Roman Catholic, Sikh and Zoroastrian faith communities.  We work towards creating a just community through coalition-building, education, training and advocacy; by uniting diverse faith leaders to speak and act together; by publishing community resources; and by holding public events including concerts, award ceremonies, lectures, and public dialogues.

Name: Church Ministries International
Description:
Church Ministries International serves as a catalyst for urban churches toward strategic planning, partnerships, leadership training, resourcing and other ministries that address the goal of evangelizing and discipling entire nations.

CMI is an evangelical, interdenominational, non-profit 501(c)(3) organization dedicated to equipping urban churches in Latin America evangelize their city and nation. We work with teams of missionaries and pastors of various evangelical churches who have the vision and commitment to carry on a concentrated program of evangelism, discipleship and church planting. We assist them with program strategy, facilities and other necessary tools, helping to make missionaries and pastors many times more effective. Since 1973, using the same methodology, CMI has seen thousands of people make decisions for Christ and many large churches established.

Although initiated by The LeTourneau Foundation, the ministry soon outgrew those resources. To meet the need, Roy LeTourneau and Kenn Opperman (a former missionary to Peru) co-founded an organization to meet the new challenge. Originally called Church Planting International, the name was later changed to LeTourneau Ministries International. Today, the work initiated by LMI is carried on under the banner of Church Ministries International.

Name: Yoga For Modern Age
Description:
Vraja Bihari Das, also known by his legal name Venugopal Acharya, is a highly respected spiritual teacher, author, and full-time monk at the Shri Shri Radha Gopinath Mandir, ISKCON Chowpatty, where he has been serving for the past two and a half decades. He holds a Masters’s degree in Monetary Policy and International Finance, as well as a Majors degree in Economics.

He has a wealth of experience in the corporate world, having worked with multinational companies such as KPMG Pete Marwick and ICICI Bank after completing his MBA in Finance. He is also a member of the Temple Care Committee and congregation development at ISKCON Chowpatty, where he provides counselling to young men in the monastery and trains a satellite community of over 100+ families in various parts of Mumbai.

Vraja Bihari Das is well-known for his deep study of Vedic and Contemporary History, and for presenting this knowledge in a relevant and relatable way through life-enhancing seminars for corporates and students, both physically and online. He is particularly passionate about teaching Bhakti-Yoga, a form of yoga that involves investing emotions and prayers.

With over 1000+ articles on his website (www.yogaformodernage.com) and hundreds of talks on his YouTube channel, he continues to guide and inspire people across borders through his social media presence. He has authored 5 books including ‘Cricket in a Monastery’. One of his books, “Mind Your Mind – Three Principles for Happy Living,” is a culmination of his daily study, writing, contemplation, and prayers over the last twenty years. In this book, he shares different tools to help people feel loved and improve their relationships with themselves and with those who matter to them. Additionally, his recent release “Lessons from the Road” shares insights from his deep study of Vedic scriptures like The Srimad Bhagavatam & Bhagavad Gita.

He is also a seasoned international speaker, having travelled and spoken in the USA, Australia, UK, Germany

Name: Atheists In Kenya Society
Description: The Atheists In Kenya Society is Kenya's first and only registered atheist organization. (Societies Act CAP 108)

Name: Cathedral of Faith
Description:
CATHEDRAL OF FAITH

We are located in the heart of Silicon Valley. We are a multi-cultural, multi-generational, multi-sensory, non-denominational Church that has grown in every decade since the 1960’s. In 2013, we have a total of 7 other sites in the Bay area. Outreach Magazine listed Cathedral of Faith as on the top 100 Churches in the Nation.

Name: Temple Israel of New Rochelle
Description:
We are a community that adds meaning and purpose to modern lives through an inclusive approach to Judaism. We live our Jewish values in our worship, celebration, lifelong learning and Tikkun Olam—the repair of the world.

Our membership is a microcosm of southern Westchester County: dynamic, diverse, mult-igenerational, and forward thinking. Our members come primarily from lower Westchester and southern Connecticut, including the towns of Eastchester, Greenburgh, Greenwich, Harrison, Hartsdale, Larchmont, Mt. Vernon, New Rochelle, Pelham, Rye, Scarsdale, White Plains, and Yonkers … and beyond.

We welcome singles and families of many compositions, including interfaith, LGBT, seniors, and people with disabilities. Our hearing impaired members often enjoy sign language interpretation at Friday night services. Our Synagogue is proud to have passed its 100th anniversary, reflecting a heritage that includes a significant number of 2nd and 3rd generation families. Over the past few years, our community has seen an increase in the number of new families joining us, many with children who attend our early childhood program and religious school.

Learn more about membership at Temple Israel by contacting the Executive Director, at (914) 235-1800  or visit www.tinr.org and click on "Join Us"

Name: inChrist
Description: A Christian Organization creating a social media application to stay connected with the common people of the community, strictly devotional

Name: Global Impact NG
Description:
We are not just a religious institution. We are building an enduring institutional brand focused on human transformation; propagate it on a global scale; challenge the usual.

Our mission is thus;
We Reach...
We Build...
We Serve...
We are Global Impact.

Name: Preaching Coach
Description: Preaching Coach offers you personal coaching and mentoring time and resources to communicate with clarity, competence and confidence!

Name: Bay Area Christian Church
Description:
At the Bay Area Christian Church, we are passionate about making God known and following Jesus’s example of doing good.

We are one church with seven campuses across the Bay Area, and we are inspired by the powerful example of the Christians in the Bible whose faith in God brought healing and change to the world around them. Their impact came from being deeply rooted in the Word of God, doing good for people wherever they went, and building deeply interconnected and loving relationships with one another. 

These principles have guided us to create a God-focused, life-changing, and non-denominational spiritual community that we hope will bring light to a world that so often needs it.

Name: The Religion Teacher
Description:
The Religion Teacher provides practical resources and training for religious educators in Catholic schools and parish education programs. 

Religion teachers, theology teachers, and catechists are always hungry for new ideas and helpful resources. This website was created to provide teachers with the resources, tips, strategies and tools to become excellent religious educators. Other school subjects have countless resources in textbooks and on the web while religion and catechesis seem to lack quality educational materials. At the same time, the majority of religion teachers and catechists do not have a formal background in education. Teaching about God is challenging but can be a rewarding experience when the teachers are given the best resources and training available. Here you will find everything from the basics to advanced teaching techniques. Most importantly, this blog is an opportunity for you to share your ideas and experiences as a teacher and catechist.

Name: Family Church
Description: A network of neighborhood churches reaching south Florida with the gospel of Jesus Christ.

Name: Episcopal Diocese of Chicago
Description:
Christ-centered, inclusive, sustained by prayer, and committed to service, the Diocese of Chicago is committed to “Leading with Love.”

We welcome skeptics, seekers and believers into a community of some 30,000 baptized members, nearly 400 clergy and 114 congregations spread across 21 counties in northern and west central Illinois.

Rooted in the church’s Anglo-Catholic tradition, we are strengthened by a diversity of cultural, liturgical and theological expression. Our diocese is known for its emphasis on leadership and congregational development.

Our commitment to social witness and community service is expressed through the work of Episcopal Charities, and a variety of diocesan and parish-based initiatives.

We are proud to be one of the 111 domestic and overseas dioceses comprising The Episcopal Church, one of the 42 member churches, or provinces, of the worldwide Anglican Communion.

Name: Hales Corners Lutheran Church & School
Description: Lutheran church of the Missouri Synod sharing the Good News of Jesus Christ.  We are saved from our sins by the death and resurrection of Jesus, the unlimited grace of God the Father and the continued presence of the Holy Spirit.  With this in mind, we accept God's gift of salvation and will strive to take as many with us to heaven that we can.  Building missional maturity in all who come this way is our main focus.

Name: Rivers Crossing Church
Description: Our mission is simple. We exist to give people an opportunity to be fully engaged followers of Christ. No matter where you are on your journey, we desire to see you move towards the extraordinary life that God has planned for you.

Name: Kirchlicher Dienst in der Arbeitswelt der Nordkirche
Description:
Der KDA der Nordkirche ist der Fachdienst für die Bereiche Wirtschaft und Arbeitswelt der Evangelisch-Lutherischen Kirche in Norddeutschland (Nordkirche). Wir sind Vermittler zwischen Kirche und Arbeitswelt. Auf der Basis des christlichen Menschenbildes engagiert sich der KDA für die menschengerechte Gestaltung von Arbeit und für nachhaltiges Wirtschaften. Umgekehrt bringen wir Erfahrungen und Kenntnisse aus der Arbeitswelt und der Welt der Wirtschaft in die Kirche ein.

Wir bearbeiten und diskutieren arbeitsmarktpolitische, sozialethische und ökonomische Fragestellungen. Der KDA fördert Diskurse über die „Arbeit der Zukunft“ sowie humane und gerechte Arbeits- und Wirtschaftsweisen. Wir stehen im Dialog mit Gewerkschaften und Politik, Handels- und Handwerkskammern und Verantwortlichen in Unternehmen und Arbeitgeberverbänden. Der KDA nimmt Partei für die Menschen – gleichgültig, ob sie Führungskraft, spezialisiert, arbeitssuchend, gering qualifiziert oder Unternehmer sind.

Wir kooperieren bei unseren Angeboten und Veranstaltungen mit NGOs, mit Kirchengemeinden, Kirchenkreisen und anderen kirchlichen Institutionen wie beispielsweise Brot für die Welt, der Evangelischen Akademie der Nordkirche oder dem Frauenwerk der Nordkirche. Der KDA ist auf dem gesamten Gebiet der Evangelisch-Lutherischen Kirche in Norddeutschland präsent – in der Metropolregion Hamburg genauso wie in den Flächenstaaten Schleswig-Holstein und Mecklenburg-Vorpommern. In allen drei Bundesländern gibt es Regionsverantwortliche, die vor Ort Kontakt- und Netzwerkarbeit betreiben. Die überregionale thematische Arbeit wird durch Fachreferentinnen und Fachreferenten begleitet.

Auf Bundesebene arbeitet der KDA der Nordkirche mit den KDAs anderer Landeskirchen in regelmäßigen Ausschüssen zusammen. Der Bundes-KDA ist dabei Teil des Evangelischen Verbands Kirche Wirtschaft Arbeitswelt (KWA).

Name: TFHNY | The Father's House
Description: We are a church that believes in Jesus. A church with a vision to see people Love God, Love People and Influence the World. We are a multi-generational, multi-cultural and multi-site church located in Rochester, NY. Senior Pastors Pierre and Marlize du Plessis.

Name: MSC Canada
Description:
At MSC Canada, our team works tirelessly to diminish the obstacles that sending churches and going workers face as they seek to follow the pattern outlined in Acts 13. We have been doing this for over 85 years. Today we serve over 400 workers in more than 60 countries worldwide with programs designed specifically to address the barriers facing sending churches and going workers.

Financial – MSC handles over 20 million dollars in annual donations securely and in compliance with all relevant regulations governing Canadian charity law. We do not take any fees from funds given to workers or projects.

Logistical – MSC assists sending churches and commended workers in assessing and managing risk, navigating emergencies and crises, dealing with various governmental and immigration agencies, and facilitating communication with support partners.

Missional – MSC enables workers’ cross-cultural ministry through orientation training, on-demand video learning, strategic retreats and ongoing WorkerCare field visits provided by former mission workers who can relate to the complex realities of modern missions.

Name: One Gospel Network
Description: A relational network of churches who love Jesus and are empowered by the Spirit to multiply fruit for the glory of God

Name: Muslim Students Association UMN
Description: The Muslim Students Association is a nonprofit student-driven organization committed to serving the campus and community-at-large with a two-part mission: promoting a complete understanding of Islam and building community. We seek to cater to the needs of Muslim students and faculty on the University campus by providing critical Islamic services alongside a multitude of programs and events. Additionally, we aim to foster dialogue with the greater campus community to advocate for unity and be more aware and understanding of all faiths.

Name: Valley Beth Shalom
Description:
Valley Beth Shalom is a synagogue community committed to the quest for a Jewish life that is Godly, meaningful, and purposeful.

OUR MISSION - We transform lives through:

Torah - Jewish Learning

Avodah - Jewish Worship, Celebration, and Commemoration

Gemilut Hasadim - Ethical Action and Social Justice

Havurah - Jewish Fellowship, Community, and Belonging

These basic principles sustain our synagogue as a participatory, activist, inclusive community whose members feel connected to one another, to their Jewish past, to the Jewish future, to Israel, to Jewish communities and throughout the world.

Name: Wilson University | Apostolic Higher Education
Description: Wilson University is an Apostolic Pentecostal institution of higher education that offers degrees and certificate programs.  Through our affiliation agreement with William Jessup University, Wilson University is able to offer students accredited degrees at the associates, bachelors, and masters level.

Name: Voice of Hope
Description:
Mission
Voice of Hope Ministries exists to extend the love of Jesus Christ by nurturing and training children through Biblical teaching, Christian character models, life skills coaching and educational support.
Our Long-Term Goal
Over the past 35 years we have reached thousands of children. Now we seek to serve thousands more by expanding the ministry to 20 school-based sites over the next 10 years.

Name: Ablaze Malawi
Description: ABLAZE is a Christian organisation which has a vision of seeing the whole be set on fire for God and on fire for change. We tend to actualize this by utilizing media and technology.

Name: The Cross, Greek Christian Ministries
Description: The Cross is a student lead ministry that focuses on fraternity and sorority life at the University of Tennessee and soon to be at Vanderbilt University as well. The Cross introduces Greek students to Jesus and helps them take their next step in a relationship with him through outreach, service, leadership, and discipleship.

Name: Compassionate Hope
Description: Compassionate Hope is a global charitable organization providing hope and a future to victims and potential victims of human trafficking and religious persecution in Southeast Asia.

Name: Allianz-Mission e.V.
Description:
As a Christian missions organization, we work in 41 countries worldwide. It is important for us to accompany our employees well and competently so that we can fulfill our mission: Getting people to be moved to engage in God’s world and to change the world. 
We want people to get a taste of what it is like when God's kingdom becomes visible: Places where people experience justice, children get a chance at education, hardship is fought - so that people can live in dignity.

Name: The District Church
Description:
From a small group “seeking the peace of the city” at Pastor Amy and Aaron Graham’s home in 2010, God birthed a movement in Washington, D.C. that is now The District Church. Together, that small group discerned God’s calling to plant a Christ-centered, culture-defining church for transplants and natives.
We are a church committed to impacting our city for Christ, one neighborhood at a time.

Name: Ovdei Hashem
Description: The Ovdei Hashem provides convenient and reliable Minyanim for people working in the Newark area.

Name: Bistum Regensburg
Description:
739 erhob der heilige Bonifatius Regensburg zum Bistum. Im Laufe der Geschichte des Bistums wirkten hier die Heiligen Wolfgang, Emmeram, Erhard und Albertus Magnus als Bischöfe. Weitere bedeutende Bischöfe waren Johann Michael Sailer, Georg Michael Wittmann, Michael Buchberger und der ehemalige Präfekt der Glaubenskongregation, Gerhard Kardinal Müller.

Seit dem 26. Januar 2013 ist Dr. Rudolf Voderholzer Bischof der Diözese.

Name: IMPACT Netwerk
Description: IMPACT is een open netwerk voor en door jonge christen professionals (globaal 22 - 35 jr) die elkaar via events en netwerken ontmoeten op actuele relevante thema's. Elke verbinding is er één(!) om elkaar te stimuleren in werk, relaties en samenleving herkenbaar te leven naar het voorbeeld van Jezus.

Name: Warm Beach Camp & Conference Center
Description: Warm Beach Christian Camps and Conference Center (WBC) provides year-round conference, camp and retreat facilities for church, school, and non-profit groups; offers programs for adults, families, and youth; and presents the Northwest holiday tradition, The Lights of Christmas. WBC is conveniently located both one hour north of Seattle, and south of Bellingham.

Name: Grace Community
Description: Grace Community of Tyler is a not-for-profit and non-denominational Christian organization with church campuses, schools, and early education in East Texas.

Name: Patriarcado de Lisboa
Description: O Patriarcado de Lisboa é uma diocese da Igreja Católica em Portugal

Name: Movimiento Avanzada Católica
Description: Avanzada Católica es un movimiento nacido en el Perú, dependiente de Pro Ecclesia Sancta y reconocido por la autoridad eclesiástica. Desde hace más de 25 años nos dedicamos a la formación de jóvenes y familias en valores cristianos, y al desarrollo de proyectos de desarrollo y labor social en zonas marginales y rurales de nuestro país. Actualmente contamos con presencia en el Perú (Lima, Arequipa, Trujillo, Chiclayo, Piura y Huaraz), EEUU (California y Minnesota) y España (Madrid y Alicante). Y tenemos más de 3000 miembros participando activamente en su formación y proyectos sociales.

Name: Family Community Church
Description:
Welcome to Family Community Church, a growing church where you will find something for everyone! Practical, down-to-earth messages, uplifting music, youth & children's classes, plus programs for couples, singles, women, parents, seniors, and more!

Weekend Services:

Saturdays @ 5:00 PM 
With childcare for 18 mos - Kindergarten

Sundays @ 9:00 AM
With classes for nursery through Grade 5

Sundays @ 11:00 AM
With classes for nursery through High School

Name: Herzl-Ner Tamid
Description: We are a synagogue whose operations are consistent with all non-profit organizations. We welcome employees from all backgrounds and experience. We seek employees who enjoy working as a team and partnering with community members in a diverse environment.

Name: Glendale United Methodist Church - Nashville
Description:
We at Glendale UMC want you to know that no matter: Where you’ve come from or where you are going; what you believe or what you may doubt; what you are feeling or just not feeling; what you have or don’t have; and no matter the color of your skin, who you love of how you identify  – all of who you are is welcomed into this community of faith by a God who loves you and knows you by name.

Check us out for worship this Sunday at 10am!

We are inclusive + affirming + reconciling.

We are an official Reconciling Congregation. This means that we are a safe place for all of God’s children to grow deeper (or begin) their faith journey –  no matter who you love or how you identify. We are intentional in connecting with the LGBTQ+ community through Pride and other community events and actively support the work of Nashville LaunchPad, The Oasis Center and other life changing (+ saving) organizations.

What is worship + the community like?

People check out Glendale for the first time every week in worship @ 10am. You will experience a laid back atmosphere, a message of hope + love, upbeat music and people with diverse faith journeys – old and young, raised in the church, new to church, those from other denominations and faith traditions to some who question it all – you name it. All are welcome at Glendale. #YallMeansAll

What if I'm not a Christian?

Not a problem. We welcome everyone. Our faith is a journey – each person at their own place as we grow in this life together. Come just as you are to Glendale – no exceptions and no exclusions.

Glendale United Methodist Church - Nashville | 900 Glendale Lane | Nashville, TN 37204
615.297.6233 | contact@glendaleumc.org

Name: Think Catholic
Description: Think Catholic's mission is to fufill the desire of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ, that "all men might be saved and come to the knowledge of the Truth" (1 Tim. 2:4). In order to to accomplish this task, Think Catholic serves Catholic Parishes and Diocese as their trusted Evangelization Agency. Our comprehensive services span--strategic planning, consulting, on-site workshops, web design, search engine optimization (SEO), geo-targeted advertising, graphic design, content creation, social media management, and more....

Name: Lake Geneva Ministries
Description:
Lake Geneva Ministries exists to provide a place set apart for campers and guests to have life-changing experiences.

Our Core Values

TEAM:  Winning Together
EXCELLENCE: Doing our very best
CONNECTION: To God and one another
SAFETY: Spiritual, Emotional, and Physical
ADVENTURE: Work Hard, Play Hard, Explore and Discover

Name: Valley Church
Description:
Core Values: 
We major on the majors.
We help relationships thrive.
We love our community.
We pursue boundless generosity.

Weekend Worship:
Online - Saturday at 5:30 pm  |  Sunday at 8:15, 9:30 & 11 am
Auditorium  |  Sunday at 8, 9:30, and 11 am
Event Hall  |  Sunday at 10:15 am
