                    Memorization and Much more )
		6, Learn Quran According to your Preference.
                7, Learn Spoken & Quranic Arabic
		8, Special Adult course ( 2 years Fiqh.ul.islam Short course )

Name: University Christian Outreach
Description: University Christian Outreach is a strategic response to the Great Commission of Jesus Christ: changing campuses to change the world. We build life-giving campus communities to reach university students and call them to respond to the Gospel by putting their faith in Jesus and receiving new life in the Holy Spirit. We form students into mature, lifelong, missionary disciples and send them into the world to build Christian communities and call all nations to rise and live.

Name: Northridge Church, Rochester, NY
Description: Northridge Church exists to make more and better followers of Jesus by creating environments for people to belong, believe in Jesus, and become more like Him. Through our mission, we long to saturate Rochester with the gospel and are committed to spreading the message of Jesus’ love and grace to every corner of our communities and world.

Name: Holy Cross Lutheran Academy
Description: Holy Cross Lutheran Academy is a PK-12 grade college preparatory school located in Sanford Florida.

Name: Igreja Evangelica Assembleia De Deus Em Pernambuco
Catholics Come Home
Description:
Catholics Come Home, Inc. is an independent, non-profit Catholic apostolate that creates effective and compassionate media messages (evangomercials) and broadcasts them nationally and internationally, in order to inspire, educate and evangelize inactive Catholics and others, and invite them to live a deeper faith in Jesus Christ, in accord with the Magisterium of the Roman Catholic Church.

Catholics Come Home is guided by a 30 person Advisory Board, including Cardinal Seán O'Malley, Bishop Thomas Olmsted, Bishop Michael Sheridan, Bishop Jaime Soto, Bishop James Conley, and other highly respected theologians and Catholic business executives. 

Our evangomercialsTM and Web sites are Bringing Hundreds of Thousands Home. 
Catholics Come Home employs mass media outreach effectively to create a highly successful Catholic evangelism effort never before seen…and the results are astonishing:

“Six months after the CatholicsComeHome.org media campaign ended, a comprehensive analysis was conducted, which revealed the average increase in Mass attendance (returned Catholics, new converts) was 12 percent, even though population growth in the Diocese of Phoenix was flat during that period. This equates to an increase of as many as 92,000 souls who came home!” –Ryan Hanning, Director of Adult Evangelization, Diocese of Phoenix

*View our famous and powerful evangomercials, including "Epic" and "Movie" at CatholicsComeHome.org.

Name: Grace Christian Academy
Description:
We are a Junior Kindergarten (age 4) through 8th grade private, Christian school, located in Fairfax County, just minutes from the District of Columbia. GCA was established in 1973 and serves a diverse student body. Grace Christian Academy is a part of the Wisconsin Evangelical Lutheran Synod (WELS) - one of the largest private school systems in the country.

At Grace Christian Academy, we emphasize Christ in everything and provide our scholars with a superior education, laying the foundation for success in this life and the next.

We want our scholars to make a Christ-guided impact in this world.
We strive for excellence in Christian education and community outreach.

At Grace Christian Academy, we believe that Jesus Christ is our Lord and Savior and that he commanded us to share the good news with all people (Matthew 28:19) and train children in the way they should go so that when they are old they do not depart from it (Proverbs 22:6).

We achieve our purpose by integrating God's Word into every aspect of our curriculum, creating a culture where scholars and staff see Christ in everything and follow his example while providing opportunities for scholars to grow in all areas of life.

Our school offers a rigorous academic program to prepare scholars for high school. It includes the core subjects recognized by the public education system and courses in Bible history and doctrine. GCA is blessed with a diverse student body and committed to a low student-to-faculty ratio which gives scholars individual instruction and attention. We also provide co-curricular activities, such as athletics, fine arts, exploratory classes, and service projects to prepare students for all areas of life. Our graduates go on to thrive in both private and public schools, including Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) programs.

Name: Life Matters Worldwide
Description: Life Matters Worldwide is a pro-life organization based in the United States.

Name: Timberlake Church
Description: Our mission is to help people find and follow Jesus. We have an unwavering focus on seeing people discover the hope and joy that comes with having a relationship with Jesus Christ. Locations in Redmond, Duvall, and Castle Rock, WA.

Name: Katoomba Christian Convention
Description:
KCC's passion is to see people transformed by Jesus Christ, through the powerful preaching of the Bible.

We have events throughout the year catering for all ages, from toddlers through to great-grandparents. Bible talks from all conventions are available for purchase from the online shop.

Our conference centres at Katoomba provide quality accommodation, catering and meeting facilities for churches, school, community and corporate groups.

Browse the conventions and links below.

Name: Oblates of St. Francis de Sales
Description: The mission of the Congregation is to grow as a religious community in loving union with God and with each other, to share our charism with the People of God, and affirm them in "living Jesus" as the needs of the Church dictate. Following the example of our founder, Father Louis Brisson, and in accordance with the spirit of St. Francis de Sales, we Oblates place ourselves under the guidance of the Holy See in the pursuit of this mission.

Name: Servant of the Lord
Description: A podcast for God's children to learn and grow.

Name: Biblical Counselling UK
Description: We’re dedicated to helping people, whatever their struggle, receive the support they need. We’re convinced that the local church is the best place to turn to in your time of need. So, we focus on equipping and supporting local churches to get alongside people in the struggles of life.

Name: Belfast Bible College
Description:
The college is a vibrant and diverse Christian community, where staff and students live and learn together as they seek to grow as disciples and serve God with their lives.

BBC students are from different places, different churches, with different callings, and yet are united by a love for God and His people. Belfast Bible College is a unique learning environment where study, discipleship, personal transformation and practical application go hand in hand…

Name: Gloria Dei Lutheran Church
Description: Building Communities - Transforming Lives

Name: Candlewood Church
Description:
For over 25 years, Candlewood Church Omaha, a non-denominational, Bible-based, Christian church, has been fulfilling its mission of loving God and others, building strong families, helping people realize their potential, caring for our community, and communicating the Gospel of Jesus Christ. 

Candlewood has vibrant ministries on the University of Nebraska-Omaha (UNO) campus. We’ve counted over 400 salvations and 500 baptisms. With God’s help, we have made a tremendous impact in our community and around the world by dedicating time to evangelism, community service, and missions.

We believe God wants to do more.

Name: Emmanuel Lutheran Church
Description: vibrant; growing; and connected

Name: Congregation of Christian Brothers
Description:
We are a loose network of organisations, groups and individuals across the world seeking to continue the spirit of Edmund Rice. As a young man, Edmund, with a group of friends, immersed in the world of work and family life, formed a little Gospel community active in the city of Waterford. They shared prayer and spiritual reading, and they combined this with active outreach to the destitute, street children, orphans, prisoners, slaves and immigrants.

Edmund Rice Mission focuses on the liberation of people and communities. Edmund Rice, inspired by Jesus, responded to the marginalised of his time. Today, living out of a new consciousness of the interconnection of all life, groups of people inspired by the story of Edmund's life form small communities that journey and converse together, stepping outside their comfort zones in respectful service. They seek to make a difference to the lives of marginalised people through compassionate presence and seeking mutual transformation. Edmund Rice Mission promotes human rights and fullness of life for all, especially those made poor. 

Our work is shaped by the  UN Sustainable Development Goals expressed in development projects in more than 15 countries.

Name: C3 Church Sydney
Description:
C3 Church is a global movement of 450 churches in 64 countries, with over 100,000 church members throughout the world. 

Established by Senior Pastors Phil and Chris Pringle in 1980 on Sydney's northern beaches, C3 Church has since expanded into an international family of churches with a passion to see people know Christ, make disciples and build the church. 

C3 Church Sydney includes churches at Oxford Falls, Silverwater, Rozelle and Penrith and with 6 weekly extension services across Sydney.

Name: Episcopal Diocese of West Texas
Description:
We are a diocese of 87 congregations spread over 60 counties in South Central Texas. Visit our website (dwtx.org) to find an Episcopal church or school near you, read the latest news, find employment opportunities, and join our ongoing ministry.

We are the West Texas branch of the Jesus Movement. All are welcome here.

Name: Diocese of Westminster Education Service
Description:
The Education Service, together with colleagues in the Property Department, supports 207 schools to ensure that they are successful and act in accordance within the Trust Deed of the Diocese.

We offer advice and direction on a broad range of educational matters such as, Religious Education and curriculum issues, Catholic Life of a school, staffing and recruitment, governance, admissions and capital and academy developments. In order to do this, the team work closely with Parishes, Local Authorities, the Department for Education, NGA and the National College for Teaching and Leadership.

Strong partnerships are key and the team value the support of the Catholic Education Service. Catholic schools and colleges have a significant part to play in the mission of the Church. Our Diocesan schools make a strong contribution to education in London, Hertfordshire and Surrey. They exist so that the Gospel of Jesus Christ is proclaimed to future generations.

In the words of Cardinal Nichols, “Being Catholic means being open to the whole and being dedicated to the unity of the whole. Being Catholic means finding ways of expressing and deepening that 'universality', that Catholic character, and being ready always to embrace the other.”

Name: Ignatius House Jesuit Retreat Center
Description:
Ignatius House Jesuit Retreat Center is a Catholic non-profit in Atlanta that offers silent retreats, non-profit meeting space, and spiritual direction for people of multiple faiths. Retreats are offered as half-day, single-day, weekend, week-long or 30-day experiences. We welcome inquiries about customizing a retreat for personal goals or a gathering for private groups.

Located north of I-285 in Sandy Springs, Georgia, Ignatius House is a destination for all seasons. The 20-acre facility is surrounded by indigenous forest overlooking the Chattahoochee River. Expansive grounds include 1.5 miles of trail, a waterfall, natural creeks, and quiet resting places. Nearly 5,000 square feet of meeting space is available for non-profit groups that need to recharge for important missions ahead.

Given the frenzied pace of modern life, it is increasingly hard to escape daily routines and noise for sacred, rejuvenating self-reflection. Spiritual retreats at Ignatius House offer space and solitude to focus who your Creator made you to be – not just your best self, but your truest self. 

Ignatius House is operated by the Atlanta Jesuit Community, part of the global Society of Jesus. Central to the work of Ignatius House are these precepts:
-	walking with the excluded
-	care for our common home
-	spiritual growth

Learn more at http://www.ignatiushouse.org

Name: Grand Rapids First
Description:
Grand Rapids First exists to bring people to Christ and equip them to be like Him — both locally and globally.

Located in Wyoming, Michigan, we are a diverse, Spirit-empowered church passionate about reaching people from all walks of life with the love and hope of Jesus. Through dynamic worship services, relevant teaching, and life-giving ministries, our heart is to see lives transformed and generations impacted.

We offer ministries for every age and stage of life — from kids and students to adults and seniors — providing opportunities for spiritual growth, authentic community, and meaningful service.

Beyond our walls, Grand Rapids First is deeply committed to outreach, partnering with local schools, nonprofits, and global missions to meet tangible needs and share the Gospel in practical ways.

Led by Pastors Sam and Brenda Rijfkogel since 2005, Grand Rapids First is part of the Assemblies of God and embraces a mission to serve our city and the world with compassion and excellence.

Whether you are exploring faith, seeking community, or looking for a church home, we invite you to join us and be part of what God is doing through Grand Rapids First.

Name: Tirikala
Description:
Tirikala bridges ancient knowledge systems with modern guidance, providing structured, clear, and transformative healing practices. Our mission is to empower individuals by fostering mind, body, and soul wellness through authentic spiritual practices that are relevant for today’s world. We combine tradition with innovation, ensuring every intervention delivers clarity, continuity, and measurable impact.

Mission:
To preserve and modernize ancient spiritual wisdom, offering practices that enhance holistic well-being while remaining accessible, authentic, and actionable.

Vision:
To be a trusted center for spiritual wellness, where tradition meets modern guidance to inspire clarity, growth, and balance in every individual’s life.

Values / Core Principles:

Authenticity: Preserving the purity of ancient practices
Clarity: Structured guidance for meaningful transformation
Continuity: Sustainable practices that endure across generations
Impact: Positive, measurable change for mind, body, and soul

Specialties / Key Offerings:

Spiritual Consultations & Guidance
Healing Practices & Rituals
Nadi Astrology Insights & Remedies
Online Homam and Puja Services
Personalized Wellness Programs
Spiritual Pilgrimage Tour & Travels
Workshops & Community Engagement

Name: Boston Church
Description: We are a nondenominational church in the Greater Boston area with a vision to reach the next generation with the gospel.

Name: Bible2School
Description:
Did you know that public school students can attend a Bible program DURING school hours? That’s right, and it's allowed by law and can happen in any state! 

Bible2School, a public school outreach ministry, equips communities to provide free Bible classes to children in 2nd, 3rd, 4th, and 5th grade DURING their public school day — typically over lunch and recess. Children walk or are bused to a nearby church where volunteers engage them in lively worship, interactive Bible lessons, hands-on activities, and spiritual connection. 

With over 45 years of experience implementing successful programs, Bible2School has the proven tools and resources to help Christian adults bring weekly Bible classes to elementary students in various communities across the nation. Through a Bible2School Membership, Christian adults have the amazing opportunity to make a significant impact in their community by teaching truth and inspiring hope to public school children. Bible2School's vision is that every public school in the nation would have a dynamic Bible program taught during their school day as allowed by national released time court rulings.

Name: Valley Baptist Church
Description: A place you can call home.  Valley Baptist Church is a multicampus church for all people who want to know God better by learning to serve Him.

Name: Temple Shalom
Description:
Temple Shalom is a place for meaningful worship, meaningful learning, and meaningful gathering. Our temple welcomes the beautiful diversity of families and individuals as we journey together seeking to enrich our lives through the three historic aspects of synagogue life – a house of prayer for meaningful worship, a house of study for meaningful learning, and a house of connection for meaningful gathering.

We pride ourselves on being a warm and welcoming community for multifaith families and embrace our interfaith families in all aspects of synagogue life. We want your family members from other faith backgrounds to feel comfortable and engaged. Everything we do at Temple Shalom is open to all our members and many events are open to the community.

Name: creedle gGmbH
Description:
DIE DIGITALE REFORMATION!
creedle inspiriert, vernetzt und ermutigt

creedle erreicht Menschen mit der Botschaft von Jesus Christus und bringt Christen mit christlichen Angeboten zusammen, damit mehr Christen ihre Berufung leben können. creedle bringt Christen zusammen und mobilisiert sie, damit alle Menschen von Jesus und seiner Botschaft hören.

Was ist das Ziel  von creedle?  Wir wollen mit creedle gemeinsam Jesus digital sichtbar machen. Alle Menschen sollen wertvolle und wahre Inhalte zu Jesus finden können. Und zwar auf die Art, dass Menschen zunächst gute Inhalte im Netz finden können und durch creedle mit anderen Christen und Gemeinden vernetzt werden.

Wer steht hinter creedle?
creedle ist eine gGmbH, die konfessionsübergreifend, rechtlich unabhängig von Kirche und christlichen Werken und vor allem Christuszentriert umgesetzt wird. Dabei sind bereits zahlreiche christliche Werke als Unterstützer und Partner angegliedert.

Name: The Foundation of the  Roman Catholic Diocese of Raleigh
Description: Mission to promote and grow existing endowment funds and create long term planned gift vehicles for the benefit of all Catholic ministries of the Diocese of Raleigh.

Name: Northland Christian Education System
Description:
NCES partners with Christian families and churches to provide a God-centered, discipleship-based school with academic excellence and a biblical worldview, equipping students to serve and influence our world for Christ. 

From pre-school to 12th grade our students learn how the story of God's redemption is woven in and through learning. We cultivate an educational environment inside and outside of the classroom which prepares student to learn, love, and lead the world to Christ.

Name: Temple Israel of the City of New York
Description: A Reform Jewish community in the Upper East Side of New York City.

Name: Boca Raton Community Church
Description:
Welcome to Boca Raton Community Church, where together we worship Christ, grow in understanding His Word, care for one another, and extend compassion to our local community and around the world.

Our aim is to show that Jesus Christ does make a difference, one life at a time. If there is any way we can serve you or your family in your walk of faith or if you have any questions, do not hesitate to contact us.

Name: Islamic Sciences & Culture Academy
Description:
What We Do
The Islamic Sciences and Culture Academy affiliated to Islamic Propagation Office of Qom Seminary which started its activities under the title of Islamic Studies and Research Center in 1984, was established with the definitive license of Ministry of Science, Research and Technology.
The Academy is a seminary-affiliated, research and scientific institution which deals with explicating and expanding the belief, perception and Islamic values and also cogitation and development of Islamic sciences and knowledge, through playing an active mediating role among seminaries according to the religious needs of people and the Islamic Republic of Iran. 

Goals
Explicating pure knowledge of Islam and refining religious culture from dogmatism and eclecticism.
The systematic explication of Islam and presenting efficient individual and social models appropriate for the needs of contemporary world through development, cogitation and empowerment of Islamic sciences.
Provision of scientific support for systematization and efficiency of religious government and Islamic Revolution
کلمات کليدي

Name: Catholic Sports
Description: CATHOLIC SPORTS IS A DENVER-BASED NATIONALLY-GROWING RECREATIONAL SPORTS OUTREACH FOR 18-39 YEAR OLDS. OUR MISSION IS TO BUILD AUTHENTIC COMMUNITY THROUGH RECREATIONAL SPORTS. “AUTHENTIC COMMUNITY” EMBODIES OUR CORE VALUES: HOSPITABLE OUTREACH, CATHOLIC ORTHODOXY AND VULNERABLE INTIMACY. WE EXERCISE THESE VALUES PRIMARILY THROUGH SPORTS LEAGUES & BIBLE STUDIES.

Name: Chicago Tabernacle
Description: A Place of Becoming

Name: The Student Christian Movement
Description:
SCM is a community of students, past and present, and it is community that is at the heart of who we are and how we do things. Wherever students find us and however they engage, they are our purpose; supporting them to be, and, where they are open to it, to grow in their lives and faith. 

We are committed to an inclusive, progressive and radical approach to the Christian faith, and this is evident from a grassroots level through to the board of trustees. Our members are also who lead us, appointing their representatives to the board of Trustees and General Council, at whose meetings the vision and strategy of SCM are set. Throughout our work we seek feedback from students and listen to their voices as we move ever onwards.

Our membership is made up of an incredible diversity of people. Some gather in local communities on their uni campus or at church, others regionally through informal networks, and some won’t have a local community, so their place of connection is through our national, especially online, work. Wherever someone connects with SCM, our values are present and our aims are worked towards. For some SCM is a place to ask hard questions and be challenged by others who are wrestling with faith and ethics. For others, it is a safe Christian space to express and settle in their queer identity. And for all, it is a place where they can simply be.

SCM is more than just a gathering place. We are also committed to putting faith into action and are a movement for today, which means campaigning on current issues of concern such as the refugee crisis, the arms trade and food injustice, and modelling LGBTQ+ celebration and pushing the church to do the same. It means giving a platform to new and established theologians who encourage us and challenge us to grow in our faith.  As we put our faith into action we often see our members becoming more confident as leaders, growing as everyday theologians, developing as activists and flourishing in their discipleship.

Name: Mission Training International
Description:
Mission Training International’s vision is to see cross-cultural messengers of the Gospel of Jesus Christ experience effectiveness, endurance and personal vitality. 

Doing cross-cultural ministry is difficult and requires more than a sense of calling. It requires a broad set of skills and a healthy approach to life and ministry. A large majority of cross-cultural workers aren’t adequately prepared for their calling, which leads to frustration, burn-out, or more destructive patterns.  This is where MTI can help.  For 60 years, MTI has been providing relevant, practical, in-residence training on the make-or-break issues that help make cross-cultural messengers of the Gospel more effective.

Name: SIBKL
Description: SIBKL, short for Sidang Injil Borneo (Borneo Evangelical Missions) is a Christ-centered, Bible-based, Spirit-empowered church in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia. We have our roots in East Malaysia and since 1994, we have grown from fifteen people to over 4000 in two main locations and three church plants. We are also actively engaged in the digital space on major social media platforms with more than 16k subscribers on our YouTube Channel providing weekly online content. Our vision is to be inspired by God to build a strong, excellent and dynamic church that will influence the nations and impact generations. For more info about us, visit sibkl.org.my

Name: Kulture Shapers Gathering (KSG)
Description:
Kulture Shapers Gathering (KSG) is a bi-monthly physical meeting under Worshippers InGathering Ministries International (WIMI), held in Abuja every second Saturday and last Thursday of the month.

Led by Rejoice Azuka Uwuseba and Rukky Judah Uwuseba, KSG exists to raise voices that shape culture through Kingdom values, leadership, and influence. It is a movement of believers, creatives, and reformers who gather to learn, align, and deploy divine strategies that impact spheres such as media, governance, education, business, and family.

Through worship, teaching, and impartation, KSG equips individuals to live intentionally, lead effectively, and influence culture for the glory of God.

Name: SFU Muslim Students Association
Description:
The Muslim Student Association of Simon Fraser University has been active for many years, and continues to be involved in providing a space for Muslims to pray, learn, meet others and get involved in the community.

The Muslim Students Association (MSA) is a volunteer organization run by Muslim students on campus. The MSA is one of the oldest clubs at SFU, founded during the early 70’s. The main purpose of the MSA is to raise awareness of Islam on campus and to provide related services and activities for both Muslim and non-Muslim students.
Services that the SFU MSA provides include:

    Daily Prayer Room (Musallah)
    Wudu facilities
    Jum’aah Prayers
    Lectures/Halaqas
    Sisters’ Speak
    Quran Classes
    Socials & Potlucks
    Humanitarian Fundraisers
    Ramadan Iftaars
    Themed Workshops
    Guest Lectures
    Muslim Advocacy & Dawah Work
    … and much more!

The MSA is located in the MutilFaith Centre (AQ 3200) at SFU Burnaby Campus.
For general inquiries, please email us at sfumsa@hotmail.com.

Name: Mobberly Baptist Church
Description: MBC seeks to lead all people into a life changing, ever growing relationship with Jesus Christ.

Name: Lord Krishna's Bhagavad Gita
Description: This page has been created to share the divine words of wisdom of Lord Krishna as spoken through the Bhagavad Gita

Name: FrikirkeNet
Description: FrikirkeNet er et netværk af flere end 120 frikirker, organisationer og institutioner. Kirkerne har baggrund i blandt andre Baptistkirken, Apostolsk Kirke, Missionsforbundet, Mosaik, Vineyard og Hillsong - dertil kommer en række uafhængige frikirker.

Name: Christian Student Fellowship (UKCSF) at the University of Kentucky
Description:
CSF is a non-denominational campus ministry at the University of Kentucky. CSF isn’t a place. CSF isn’t a program. CSF is everywhere on campus—although we do have a building where we sometimes hang out. It’s a million different places and a million different people. It’s downtown serving the homeless. Climbing at the Johnson Center. A conversation about Jesus ’til 2am. It’s wherever people are interacting with each other and with God.

CSF is mainly staffed by recent UK grads who take the first year or two post-graduation to give back to the UK community by working full time at CSF. A few long-term staff help guide and invest into these younger staff members who in turn pour into our student leaders as they reach out to campus with the good news and good life of Jesus.

Name: Mlekhet Hoshev | StaM Institute
Description:
Mlekhet Hoshev is a formal institute, devoted to spreading STaM; Sefrei Torah, Tefillin, Mezuzot, Megilot, and other Tashmishei Kdusha, to all Jews in Israel and abroad.
The institute is based in Petach Tikva, Israel, employs dozens of certified scribes (sofrim) and proofreaders (Magihim), Yirei Shamayim, and dedicated to this sacred work. All our sofrim and Magihim received their qualifications and certifications through the 𝐌𝐢𝐬𝐡𝐦𝐞𝐫𝐞𝐭 𝐒𝐓𝐚𝐌, 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐘𝐚𝐝 𝐑𝐚𝐟𝐚𝐞𝐥 𝐢𝐧𝐬𝐭𝐢𝐭𝐮𝐭𝐞, 𝐁𝐧𝐞𝐢-𝐁𝐫𝐚𝐤.
At Mlekhet Hoshev, we prioritize quality and non-compromising sanctity, dedication to traditional Sofrut, along with up-to-date knowledge and the finest raw materials. Our goal is to provide our fellow Jews with meticulously sanctified items, and affordable.
One of our expertise in restoring and repairing old Torah scrolls has led to meaningful achievements, including saving dozens of Torah scrolls from Gniza, including those saved from the Holocaust, Russia, Ukraine, and Morocco.

We are known as experts and a trustworthy choice recommended by 𝐜𝐡𝐢𝐞𝐟 𝐫𝐚𝐛𝐛𝐢𝐬, 𝐀𝐬𝐡𝐤𝐞𝐧𝐚𝐳𝐢 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐒𝐞𝐩𝐡𝐚𝐫𝐝𝐢, selected as an 𝐨𝐟𝐟𝐢𝐜𝐢𝐚𝐥 𝐬𝐮𝐩𝐩𝐥𝐢𝐞𝐫 of government institutions, Ministry of Defense, and many leading companies.

The institute is recommended by widely respected Chief Rabbis:

Name: Comunión Ecuménica Mundial
Description:
Es una entidad religiosa independiente de las Iglesias consideradas tradicionales o históricas y existe para la unidad fraterna de ministros iglesias y fieles que buscan y aman a Dios de corazón, trasciende a todos los carismas de fe y respeta las expresiones de fe de cada carisma, comunidad, congregación o iglesia, es ecuménica trascendiendo al concepto forjado desde principios del siglo XX por que acepta en amor a las personas sin importar su carisma de fe, para aclarar esto es necesario entender la raíz y significado original de la palabra ecuménico que procede del griego antiguo «οἰκουμένη» (oikoumenē, aunque se pronuncia (en griego moderno) ikuméni) significa “tierra habitada” es decir todo lugar en el que se encuentran seres humanos , de esta manera la Comunión Ecuménica es fraternidad de todos los seres humanos y por ser mundial esta fraternidad no se circunscribe a un solo lugar geográfico, más bien es universal.

A diferencia de los movimientos ecuménicos como la octava por la unidad de la iglesia (de origen Anglicano ), el ecumenismo Romano, la iglesia de Taizé o la iglesia de la Unificación; la Comunión Ecuménica Mundial recibe y acoge todos los carismas de fe, por ejemplo ortodoxos, episcopales, anglicanos, coptas, luteranos, metodistas, … sin cambiar la identidad del carisma en expresión de fe, ni en el rito religioso; está dirigida por el Patriarca de la Comunión Ecuménica Mundial (C.E.M ) y los cancilleres que conforman el cenáculo, además forman parte de la C.E.M: El Registro Ecuménico Mundial de Sacramentos y Órdenes Sagradas, La Conferencia Episcopal Ecuménica, el sistema de medios RUJOT, y el Instituto Teológico Filosófico Ecuménico.

Name: Kashidarshana

Description: Casas is a welcoming multi-generational church in Oro Valley, AZ that places high value on belonging before you believe. As a Christ-centered church, we believe life is a precious gift from God and that His desire is for each of us to experience the acceptance and freedom in Him. In doing so, we hope to become an expression of love to our community and the entire world. There is a place for everyone here at Casas. Welcome home.

Name: Worldlight Revival Ministries, International
Description:
Worldlight Revival Ministries is a revival movement birthed by the Holy Spirit. The main purpose of WRM is to bring revival across the world in this end time. This will be done by letting the world know that Jesus is the light of the world and without him, we will all be in total darkness. "Then spake Jesus again unto them, saying, I am the light of the world: he that followeth me shall not walk in darkness, but shall have the light of life" (John 8.12 KJV). The light that the world needs is Jesus Christ and that is how the name Worldlight came about. 

We strongly believe in the Word of God that with the word everything is possible, and know that everything exist because of the word and for that reason, the word has power to do what God sent it for. 

We also believe in the done work on the cross, that only through the death of Christ can one be saved, by accepting Christ as his Lord and personal savior. We know that Christ has bought us with his precious blood, and on the cross he has finished the work by given us his blood (life) and body. We believe that through the blood and his name we have overcome the enemy. 

We believe in the power and the demonstration of the Holy Spirit. We believe in ministry of the Water/Word for our cleansing, the blood for our sanctification, and anointing/Spirit for our empowerment. Revival cannot take place without the Holy Ghost, therefore Worldlight continue to seek the rain of the Spirit through prayer and the word. When people finally experience Gods revival power, they will experience the light of God around them.
Worldlight Revival Ministries is a revival movement birthed by the Holy Spirit for the purpose of bringing a revival across the world in this end time. 

Our Mission
Worldlight Revival Ministries is assigned to raise people, especially the young men and women whom God will use to bring revival unto this world through the power of prayer and the demonstration of the Word of God.

Name: The Quran Learn
Description:
Online Quran Platform
We offer online Quran and Arabic language classes for children and adults of all genders. Quran, and Islamic studies with male and female tutors. Upon completion of the course, students will receive a certificate. Provides online classes for The Quran Learn Institute, Arabic language, Quran, and Islamic studies. They offer a 3 Day free trial for those who want to start their learning journey.

Name: America's Keswick
Description:
America’s Keswick is a ministry that teaches and models Biblical Christian Living through Addiction Recovery for men and women, Training and Resources, Christian Conferences, Concerts, and Events, and by providing group retreat rental facilities and service to other like-minded organizations. 

The purpose of America’s Keswick is to model and teach Biblical living in Christ Jesus, to provide an environment in which Biblical life transformation takes place and to foster active personal involvement in the local church.

Name: Bruderhof
Description:
The Bruderhof is an intentional Christian community of more than 2,700 people living in twenty-three settlements on four continents. 

We are a fellowship of families and singles, practicing radical discipleship in the spirit of the first church in Jerusalem. We gladly renounce private property and share everything in common. Our vocation is a life of service to God, each other, and you.

None of us owns anything personally, and our communal property belongs not to us as a group but to the cause of Christ. Anyone who has decided to become a member freely gives all property, earnings, and inheritances to the church community. In turn, all necessities such as food, housing, and health care are provided for. Members generally work for and in the community, but none of us receives a paycheck, stipend, or allowance. In our homes and daily lives, we try to live frugally and give generously, to avoid excess, and to remain unfettered by materialism. In these practical ways we seek to witness that under the stewardship of the church, everything we have is available to anybody in need.

Name: Heavenya

We hope to preserve the Christian community in the realm of technology for upcoming generations. We are creating one place for believers in Christ to join in fellowship with healthy relationships, community, experiences, support, and connection.

We provide education, networking, and support for Christians working in the health and human service fields, with an interdisciplinary approach and aligned with biblical integration.

Name: Church at the Cross
Description:
We live in a lost and dying world which continues to pursue its failing strategies for meaning and fulfillment in life. While people often look good on the outside, their lives are sometimes more accurately characterized by self-indulgence, guilt, lust, confusion and despair. Seeking his own remedies, man continues his downward spiral of separation from God. Man’s only hope is the person of Jesus Christ. For men to turn to Christ, they must be told about Him and to see His reality displayed in the lives of His followers. As the body of Christ, the church is the visible earthly representation of who He is. Every church has the purpose of leading people to Christ, maturing them in the faith and then encouraging them to go out into all the world and let them know He loves them and desires to save them as well. Therefore, the purpose of the Church AT The Cross is to lead all people to:

Know Christ, Grow in Christ and Go for Christ!

Description: Word of Life Church has both a North and South location in Wichita, Kansas. Our Mission is to Pursue Jesus, Love People, and Transform the World. Several ministries are available for your family including Life Prep Academy (K-12), Christ-centered childcare centers, healing ministry through Word of Life Counseling Center, and a worldwide outreach through Vision 2020. We are a diverse come as you are church being built by His unconditional Love and His uncompromising Word. Our sincere prayer is for the entire body of Christ to operate as one so that the world may believe (John 17:21). We would be honored if you would consider visiting with us this Sunday! Name: Q Christian Fellowship
Description:
We are a diverse community with varied backgrounds, cultures, theologies, and denominations, drawn together through our love of Christ and our belief that every person is a beloved child of God.

We recognize that the church has not and does not always make it easy for those who identify as lesbian, gay, transgender, bisexual, same gender loving, queer, intersex, gender fluid or questioning to reconcile these aspects of themselves with their Christian identity. We are witnesses to both the harm that faith communities can inflict and the hope and healing that is possible through Christ.

By gathering together, embracing our differences, and celebrating our shared trust in God’s love in Christ, we seek to point toward the full inclusivity to which God is calling us. Just as Jesus led by example in his life on earth, we are dedicated to living out a transformative example of fellowship in the world.

Wherever you are on your journey, whether you are examining your own identity or walking alongside others, you have found a place of welcome. We are grateful that God has brought you here, and we look forward to sharing the path forward together.

Name: Igreja Assembleia De Deus - Ministerio De Madureira
The Insititute of Spritual learning
Description: ISL is a modern institute set-up to accommodate the need for a better understanding in matters pertaining to the spiritual self. The objective of every human-being on this small planet is to better understand themselves. We have accumulated, over the past 4000 years, a complete unabridged version of the knowledge of life. ISL has begun the task of organising this information into clearly understandable sections of human reasoning. By not excluding any ideas developed by the human mind, and by including all possible paradigms, ISL has been able to correlate and index the main similarities between all forms for understanding. The Institute of Spiritual Learning has for many years, been developing a clearer understanding of the needs of its students. And without their dedication the development of our school would not have been possible.

Name: The Coptic Orthodox Church Centre UK
Description:
Home to the Cathedral of St George, the Media and Communications Office for the Coptic Orthodox Church in the UK, and the Coptic Youth Mission Office, the Centre is a hub of activity hosting conferences and retreats, and providing the facilities and resources used by a variety of ministries that serve the UK.

His Grace Bishop Angaelos, General Bishop of The Coptic Orthodox Church in the United Kingdom is the resident Bishop at the Centre. Full biography available via http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bishop_Angaelos
Follow His Grace @BishopAngaelos on twitter, or BishopAngaelos.org, Youtube.com/BishopAngaelos. 

Media and Communications Office:                CopticMediaUK.com 
Coptic Youth Mission:                                     CopticYouthMission.com 

Church History:

Today, the Coptic Orthodox Church has the largest Christian presence in the Middle East, with approximately 10-15 million members in Egypt, representing about 15 per cent of the population. Coptic Orthodox Christians vibrantly, actively and faithfully fill their churches and monasteries, living as productive and faithful members of their communities. The Church has also experienced rapid growth in the lands of immigration over the past 30 years, in the United Kingdom, Europe, North America and Australasia. There are now over 15 dioceses and 500 parishes outside Egypt, and the communities living abroad are not considered to be a diaspora, as 90 per cent of Coptic Christians still live in their native Egypt.

Name: His Men Ministries
Description:
The VISION of His Men Ministries is to radically change and enhance a multitude of mens’ lives, families, communities, and careers through the transformation of men into “His Men,” predestined by God as adopted, blameless, blessed, chosen, enlightened, forgiven, holy, loved, mighty, redeemed, righteous, and sealed by the blood and finished work of Jesus Christ, His death, burial, resurrection, and ascension into heaven, where He sits today at the right hand of the Father, interceding on the behalf of all those under His authority and Lordship that call upon His precious and perfect name.

The MISSION of His Men Ministries and its Founder is to serve as a modern-day Barnabas, a dependable and consistent encourager and teacher of God’s Word through various methods, including career development and coaching, corporate chaplain services, daily scripture lessons, domestic mission trips, motivational speaking, on-demand prayer, the sharing of relationship and parental wisdom, and weekly devotional teachings.

The personal mission statement of His Men Ministries' Founder is to co-create a world of constant love and charity for one another (1 Peter 4:8; 1 Corinthians 13:4-8) through encouraging and coaching all God's children to intentionally seek authentic salvation, growth, and change through prayer, through the truth of God's Word, and through the teaching of God's Son and our Savior, Jesus Christ, in order that we all produce and share the fruits of the Holy Spirit (Galatians 5:22-23) and experience the peace of God that surpasses all understanding (Philippians 4:6-7).

Name: Living Hope Baptist Church
Description: Living Hope Baptist Church is a missional church filled with imperfect people who have found that doing life together under God's grace is the way to go. We seek to connect with God and care for people as a community of faith. We do this by helping people Get to God, Grow in Christ and Give to Others.

Name: Atlanta Dream Center Church
Description:
A CHURCH FOR EVERYONE.
Our church's vision is to simply be a church for everyone. It doesn't matter the background, the race, the age, the social status nor does it matter what happened in your life, we want to be a church that anyone can come and leave changed because they met Jesus.

Our Story:
Our story begins when Pastor Tommy was only 15 years old. In 2002, Tommy's parents, Pastor Paul and Patty Palmer visited Atlanta, Georgia, on a family trip. During their visit, they encountered a homeless man sleeping on the steps of a locked church.

They knocked on the door of the building and discovered that the staff was inside attending to church business, little interested in the man sleeping on their steps. At that moment, the Atlanta Dream Center Church was born. The Palmers, along with nine of their eleven children, left a comfortable life in California and moved across the country to establish the church in the center of what was, at the time, one of Atlanta’s most crime ravaged communities.

Before Sunday services were established, the church reached the city’s most down trodden neighborhoods by knocking on the doors of local residents, offering simple acts of service, such as mowing lawns. This became the church’s first outreach, known as Adopt-a-Block. The premise was simple: show people the love of Jesus by serving them well. The outreach transformed the community within a matter of years.

(It didn’t go unnoticed. Pastor Paul was given the Phoenix Award, Atlanta’s highest honor for citizens, by Mayor Kasim Reed in 2015 to acknowledge the impact of the Atlanta Dream Center Church.) The church quickly became known for having open arms (and doors) to those most overlooked by society. The homeless were welcomed in and helped, the neglected were accepted and loved, the outcast found a home.

Today, we host the a place of worship for everyone. Including you. Join us 10am every sunday and 1pm online on our church center app.

Name: Marketplace Leaders
Description:
Marketplace Leaders exists to help men and women fulfill God’s call on their lives by providing a FREE devotional that is received by thousands of workplace believers all over the world and by training business leaders to see their work as a catalyst for change through training events and other ministry events.

The links on our homepage connect you to our many services. Whether you simply want to signup to receive our free daily email devotional, TGIF Today God Is First, which goes around the world to over a quarter of a million people daily, or need mentoring in your workplace call, or want the latest in workplace resources, Marketplace Leaders has a full range of products and services to equip you to “co-labor with Christ” to see the transformation of your workplace, city, and nation for Him.

Our Goals:

•To break down the wall of secular versus sacred.
•To bring revival to the workplace.
•To help business people see their work as their calling and ministry.
•To engage more workplace Christians in the "work of the ministry."
•To see the power of God manifested in the workplace.

Name: The Revelation Notes Daily Devotional
Description:
The Revelation Notes Daily: 

A daily devotional that delivers God’s prophetic word to inspire and equip you spiritually. It’s for everyone—professionals, business leaders, students, and youths—bringing deeper revelations of God’s word for your daily journey. Follow this page and connect to God’s wisdom today!

Description: Chabad at Pitt is a Jewish home away from home for University of Pittsburgh students who wish to belong to a Jewish community, meet other students and learn about Judaism.

Name: Fearless Family of Churches
Description: At the Fearless Family of Churches, every person will have a deepening relationship with God and with others.  We believe God wants us to join with His Son Jesus to fix what is broken in lives and neighborhoods.  This journey begins as we learn to think the way thought, love the way Jesus loved, and serve the way Jesus served.  Doing this we live the full life that God intends for us to enjoy together.

Name: Living Word Church
Description:
Since Ridgecrest Living Word Church’s inception in January of 2006, Dr. Barry Lee Weddle and Pastor Nancy Weddle have helped people discover God’s best for their life through strong Bible based faith teaching, modern worship, diversity, and a warm and friendly atmosphere. Living Word is changing the way people see church and encounter God.  

Many have overcome addictions, marriages have been restored, and most have experienced increase in their personal finances.  We are a church that ignites passion for the things of God and a place of integrity that exhibits godly character, and strong Biblical convictions.

This is not a church. This is a movement. This is an apostolic movement that is raising up sons. What the earth needs is not another preacher with a message or another singer with a song, but it needs to see a son with an inheritance. We have got to shift from a church mindset to a kingdom mindset and with a kingdom mindset we get an inheritance – we raise up sons. It’s an apostolic movement.

We’re a family that has decided to go from belief to behavior with ONE FOCUS! We are a unique family, submitted and committed to evolving into a people of exceptional character and integrity. We are not seeking a congregation but a generation!

Name: Episcopal Diocese Of Washington
Description: The Episcopal Diocese Of Washington comprises the 88 churches, 20 schools and 6 campus ministries in the District of Columbia and its surrounding Maryland counties (Montgomery, Prince George, Charles and St. Mary's counties).

Name: Pathway Church
Description: At Pathway, we believe no one is too far away from God or unable to take one simple step toward Him. An extraordinary life in Jesus is available to everyone if they are only willing to take that one step. Whether you’re broken or think you have it all together, unchurched or lifetime follower of Christ, you're welcome here.

Name: Københavns Domkirke - Vor Frue Kirke
Description:
The Cathedral of Copenhagen is named Church of Our Lady (in Danish: Vor Frue Kirke). It is situated centrally in Copenhagen, next to the main building of the University of Copenhagen.
The construction of the original Collegiate Church of St. Mary began no later than 1187, and in 1209 it was consecrated. In 1314 a fire destroyed the limestone cathedral so completely that it was rebuilt. A school was established early, and in 1479 parts of the church school were chartered to become the University of Copenhagen. 
In September 1807 the cathedral fell victim to the bombardment of Copenhagen by the British Navy. 
The cornerstone of the current building was laid in 1817 and the work completed by Whitsunday 1829. 
Danmarks Radio broadcasts a morning act of worship from the cathedral every day except Sundays from 8.05 to 8.25 Danish time (live on P2 and DR2), and makes a recording of the latest transmission available online: https://www.dr.dk/radio/p2/morgenandagten and via podcast itpc://podcast.dr.dk/p2/rssfeed/Morgenandagten.xml 
The building measures 83 m in length and 33 m in width. The interior of the nave is 60 m long and over 25 m from floor to ceiling. With all galleries open, the church can seat more than 1100 people. The tower is 60 m high.

Name: Islamic Society of Boston Cultural Center
The Scottish Episcopal Church
Description:
The Scottish Episcopal Church is diverse in its tradition, outlook and culture.  Such diversity enriches the Church and is expressed in warm relationships of mutual respect for one another.

Firmly rooted in the life of Scotland and part of its rich history, the Scottish Episcopal Church is also deeply committed to its membership of the world-wide Anglican Communion, which is a family of over 70 million Christians in more than 160 countries.

The Scottish Episcopal Church is known as a Province of the Anglican Communion.  Within the Province there are seven areas (known as Dioceses) which cover mainland Scotland and the Islands. Each Diocese is overseen by a Bishop.

There are over 350 Scottish Episcopal Churches across Scotland which offer a range of liturgical worship and music.

The Scottish Episcopal Church is governed by a representative body knows as the General Synod (comprising of a representation of clergy and lay people from each of the seven dioceses. The General Synod meets once a year for three days to discuss and vote on a range of matters and issues and to determine policy and priority for the work of the Church throughout the year.

Description: Palm Beach Synagogue is a religious institutions company based out of 120 N County Rd, Palm Beach, FL, United States.

Name: St. Thomas Aquinas Church
Description: Saint Thomas Aquinas Church serves the needs of Catholics at the University of Virginia, whether students, faculty or staff, and we are located right on the edge of the beautiful Grounds of the University of Virginia, within easy walking distance of the dormitories.

Name: Sacred Heart of Jesus
Description: Sacred Heart of Jesus Catholic Church is located in Broussard, Louisiana. We're a worship community that has been around since the 1800’s. Our mission is to provide the spaces and tools necessary for all our parishioners to have a personal encounter and relationship with Christ. To help us facilitate this quest we have organized a method to develop a deeper connection with Christ that we call the Parish Life Cycle. We’d love the opportunity to meet you in person, give you a tour of our campus and share with you all that the we are blessed to offer.

Name: Tenth Church
Description:
Tenth Church is a place where people of all different backgrounds can discover Christ, a community of spiritual transformation that seeks social justice for all. 

Our community includes immigrants, students, and young families; artists, entrepreneurs, athletes, and professionals; the urban poor and people on the margins of society. The Canadian Broadcasting Corporation (CBC) has described Tenth as “a resting place for people from all walks of life.”

You are welcome to join us as we walk with God for others and gather on Sunday:

East Van | 10am | 4015 Fraser St

Evening | 6 pm | 11 W 10th Ave

West Side | 5pm | 2733 W 41st Ave

Mt Pleasant | 9 + 11am | 11 W 10th Ave

UBC Pt Grey | 10am | 2260 W Mall

Name: Clear Bible
Description: Clear is accelerating the faithful translation of Scripture until all people can access God’s Word in their own language.  Clear serves Great Commission organizations by giving Bible translators visibility into the meaning and structure of the original Hebrew and Greek, which empowers translators to check their work as they go, improves their drafting, increases accuracy, reduces external dependencies, and shortens the overall project time.

Name: Theologische Fakultät Paderborn
Description:
Die Theologische Fakultät Paderborn ist eine staatlich anerkannte Hochschule in Trägerschaft des Erzbischöflichen Stuhls Paderborn.
Als älteste Hochschule Westfalens wurde sie am 10. September 1614 gegründet. Heute verfügt sie über 15 Professuren und weitere Lehrbeauftragte, die in Philosophie und in den verschiedenen Disziplinen der Katholischen Theologie forschen und lehren.

Name: Catholic Diocese of Townsville
Description: The Diocese of Townsville was established in 1930.  It covers an area of 434,400 square kilometres, extending from Townsville on the Coast, to the Whitsunday and Burdekin regions in the South and North to Ingham and Halifax, south-west to Winton, and west to the border with the Northern Territory, encompassing Mount Isa and several small, isolated western towns and east to Palm Island.

Name: Madison Mission SDA Church
Description:
Without a regular place of worship, the early Madison Mission took on the characteristics of a nomad church as the congregation met at different venues from week to week. At least twenty different locations were utilized, including Mount Sano State Park and several school auditoriaum. As the church shifted to different locations each week, a building committee worked hard in the background and in 1998 secured 17 acres of prime property in Madison, Alabama. While preparation and construction took place, the church was blessed to have a temporary home at the Pentecostal Lighthouse Church of God in Christ in Huntsville.

On Wednesday, February 23, 2000, the Madison Mission Family Life Center was officially opened, and the jubilant members moved into the magnificent 14,000 square feet facility. The five-day opening exercises set the tone for the future as thousands of attendees celebrated with worship, drama, concerts, sports, and community activities. Each week, up to two-thousand worshipers gather to present offerings and sing praises to the Almighty God. Joining the weekly worshipers are the tens of thousands of viewers who tune in to the weekly The Mission is Possible telecast, and the faithful Internet congregation who tune in to the live services each week from as far away as Europe and China.

Name: The Cathedral of St. Philip
Description: The Cathedral of St. Philip serves the world with the grace of Jesus Christ. We are one of the largest parishes in The Episcopal Church, part of the worldwide Anglican Communion of Churches. We are also a cathedral, serving the Episcopal Diocese of Atlanta.

Name: National Association of Congregational Christian Churches
Description:
The National Association of Congregational Christian Churches (NACCC) is an association of over 300 Congregational Christian churches.

The NACCC was founded to help Congregational Christian churches maintain their autonomy while benefitting from the experiences, resources, and insights of other churches. The NACCC works to connect our member churches and foster an open dialogue about best practices, common issues, and effective solutions, helping each participating church find meaningful ways to grow and encourage faith among their congregations.

Our MISSION is to nurture fellowship among Congregational Christian Churches and to support ministries of the local church in its community and to the world, all in the name of Christ.

Our VISION is vital and healthy Congregational Churches, sharing the love of Jesus the Christ.

Name: St. Charles Borromeo
Description: Catholic Community of St. Charles Borromeo

Name: Wisdom Ways Center for Spirituality
Description:
Mission  |  Wisdom Ways creates open, accepting spaces that advance the spiritual growth, authenticity and fullness of life for all people. Our resources and offerings aim to help support movement into meaningful and impactful action to advance personal and communal well-being  – and you won’t be doing it alone.

Vision  |  Moving people from deep inner listening into courageous action to co-create a better world.

Values  |  In a spirit of wisdom, We honor the inherent dignity, particularity, and wholeness of each human person. We believe that each of us can call forth healing, growth, integration and expanded freedom for ourselves and each other. We listen to and support the creative expression of spiritual wisdom across varied religious and secular ways of being. We commit ourselves to inclusivity, accessibility and liberating justice on behalf of personal transformation and the transformation of the world.

Goals: 
• Developing communities of mutual support and belonging for people as we search for meaning amidst everyday life experiences.
• Elevating voices of historically oppressed persons, finding shared wisdom and understanding while exploring distinct spiritual paths and modes of expression.
• Co-creating a viable and sustainable structure for Wisdom Ways to thrive now and in the future.

Wisdom Ways believes that an inherent part of that mission is ensuring the equality and inclusion of all persons, mindful of gender identity and expression, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, class, disability, immigration status, religious beliefs, age, or any other diverse backgrounds. Acknowledging our purpose as a programming and story-sharing organization, we continually strive to offer a diverse community that features the voices of historically oppressed persons, elevating the shared wisdom of their experiences as a vital element of our mission and society. We are committed to the inclusion of all persons.

Name: 231 Collective
Description:
Laugh more. Worry less. Skit Guys Media delivers family-friendly, faith-filled entertainment. Entertainment with purpose.

The Skit Guys is a 501(c)3 nonprofit organization. Donations to our ministry help us continue to create relevant content for churches, families, and people all over the world that draws them closer to Jesus and His Truth (and makes them laugh more!).

Description: City to City Australia exists to help leaders build gospel movements in Australian cities. The vision of City to City Australia is to join with God to see major city centres in Australia worshipping Jesus Christ as Lord so that God will renew them spiritually, socially and culturally to the glory of God the Father.

Name: Wilshire Baptist Church
Description: Wilshire is a Christian community of believers who meet weekly for worship and spiritual growth. We're a safe place to wrestle with life's questions and find your role in the mission of God to the world. Our mission is to build a community of faith shaped by the Spirit of Jesus Christ. We are affiliated with the Cooperative Baptist Fellowship and the Baptist General Convention of Texas.

Name: UCEI
Description:
The Union of the Italian Jewish Communities (UCEI) is the body representing Italian Jews before the State and its institutions, both at national and international level.
UCEI preserves Italian Jews’ traditions and their religious, social and cultural life; it coordinates and supports activities organised by each of the Jewish communities, which are Judaism’s traditional institutions in our country; it promotes the preservation and enhancement of the Jewish cultural heritage; it launches and develops platforms and its own means to share and communicate; it organises events aimed at spreading knowledge on Jewish culture, Holocaust remembrance, and the Jewish people’s history; it engages in interreligious dialogue through initiatives to support encounter and exchange of ideas with other faiths; it fosters relationships with the State of Israel and promotes the spread of knowledge on the State of Israel in Italy; it interacts with other countries’ Jewish communities and institutions, both at European and international level.
The relationships between the State and the Union are regulated by Law No. 101 of 1989, based on the Agreement signed on 27th February 1987, as provided under Article 8 of the Italian Constitution.
The overall running of UCEI is regulated by the Statute of Italian Judaism. Under the guidance of its President, UCEI fulfils its functions through the following bodies: the Committee, consisting of 9 members; the Council, composed of 52 representatives of the different communities; the Rabbinical Council; the Rabbinical Assembly; the Board of Arbitrators; the Secretary General.
In Italy, the Agreement officially recognises 21 Jewish Communities, namely Ancona, Bologna, Casale Monferrato, Ferrara, Florence, Genoa, Leghorn, Mantua, Merano, Milan, Modena, Naples, Padua, Parma, Pisa, Rome, Turin, Trieste, Venice, Vercelli, Verona.
All of the Communities are represented and protected, individually and as a whole, by the Union of the Italian Jewish Communities

Name: Sephardic Jewish Brotherhood of America
Description:
The Sephardic Jewish Brotherhood of America is the largest Sephardic benevolent organization of its kind in the United States. Founded in 1916 by Jewish immigrants from Ottoman lands, the Brotherhood has worked to serve the most needy in our community and create a home for Sephardic Jews from around the world. 

The Brotherhood has the unique mission to support Sephardic individuals, families, and communities deriving their lineage from the Ladino-speaking Jewish communities of Greece, Turkey, and the Balkans. Unlike the Yiddish speaking Ashkenazi Jews of Eastern Europe and the Arabic speaking Jews of the Middle East, these Sephardic Jews date their history back to Spain, where they developed the language of Ladino (Judeo-Espanyol), a mix of biblical Hebrew, old Catalan Spanish, and elements of Greek, Turkish, and Arabic. After the Spanish Inquisition of 1492, these communities carried their language, culture, liturgy, and traditions with them to the Ottoman lands of the Balkans. Centuries later, many of these Jews immigrated to the United States at the turn of the 20th Century.

Today, the Brotherhood is proud to serve nearly 1,000 families from across the United States and continues to support the Sephardic community in the spirit laid out by our society’s founders. In addition to providing burial services for its members, the Brotherhood has expanded its programs to support the future generations of our community and reinvent what it means to be Sephardic in the 21st Century. New initiatives include a Sephardic Birthright Israel Trip, the annual Greek Jewish Festival in Lower Manhattan, and the Greek Jewish & Sephardic Young Professionals Network, which have all been created to ensure we meet the needs of our newest generation of members.

Name: Temple Beth Israel
Description:
Since our founding in 1930, we have been enriching the lives of our congregants through prayer, study, and celebration.

We believe in a living Judaism that respects our history while simultaneously engaging with the opportunities and challenges of the modern world.  Guided by the principles of egalitarianism and inclusion, we form a welcoming congregation that strives to create a deep, lasting connection to Jewish life.

Temple Beth Israel offers a wide variety of programs, classes, services, lifecycle events, festivals, choirs, and clubs.  We invite you to join us here on Facebook, in our digital home at www.tbi.org.au, and at our community centre.

Every week, we celebrate Shabbat with on both Friday evening and Saturday morning.
Kabbalat Shabbat (Friday evening) begins at 6.15pm.
Shabbat Shacharit (Saturday morning) begins at 10am.

Name: Christ Journey Church
Description: Christ Journey is a Christian church in Miami serving the city through its food pantry, ongoing community projects in parks, schools, beaches and more. We are a family church where kids can make friends and learn about Jesus in a fun way. Our worship services are dynamic, multicultural and engaging. We offer Spanish and Portuguese translation.

Name: Nations Church
Description:
Nations Church is a diverse, vibrant and passionate community. We are a multi-cultural, multi-generational body of people passionate about reaching and influencing our communities with the life-changing story of Jesus.

We are known for our declarative and powerful worship, our enthusiasm for each other and our eager acceptance of everyone we meet. Nations Church maintains two main drives of influence; our community programs and our Church discipleship. Our foundation however is always Jesus; we are His stories, we are His church.

Name: Sisters of Mercy of the Americas, NyPPaW
Description: New York, Penn and the Phillipines

Name: SFOI
Description:
Sports Fan Outreach Int'l goal is for sports fans attending major sporting events like the Super Bowl, Final Four, Rugby World Cup, Olympics, etc. to become Christians. The vision to reach this goal is mobilizing teams of evangelists to preach the Gospel to the fans in person, minister to the fans as they respond, and refer them to a Bible teaching church for discipleship.

SFOI has established a program for men who want to enter full-time evangelistic work called the George Whitefield Program so the Gospel can be heard, responded to and Lord willing believed in more places more often: http://sfoi.org/GeorgeWhitefieldProgram/

Name: Worldwide Discipleship Association
Description: WDA supports the Church worldwide by offering training systems and support that helps Christians grow to maturity in Christ.  Built around a paradigm derived from Christ's training approach, we feature seminars, printed resources, and consulting help that "meets people where they are, and helps them take the next step."

Name: Salt Company
Description:
Salt Co originated in DuPage County, in the western suburbs of the USA. An adult leader called forth student leaders in several high schools in the towns of Wheaton, West Chicago and Glen Ellyn and they met regularly to inspire each other to be the ‘salt of the earth’ in their high schools. As a result of this regular gathering, thousands of high school students and eventually middle school students started to gather in homes and in super groups together to talk about important issues of Life. The movement spread to the Western Great Lakes Region and eventually culminated in the DC/LA national movement of the largest youth ministry in the world called Youth for Christ, that was the precursor to the Billy Graham association. The name DC/LA stood for the two largest convention centers in Washington D.C. and Los Angeles that were filled up every 3 years for the last 25 years, with student leaders who convened to be trained on how to turn their schools and communities upside down for good.

The new Salt Co. is a reconstitution and reformation of the original organization , re-tooled into a high tech/high touch approach that can touch and transform North America and then, the world.

Name: Pontifical Council for Culture
Description:
Il Pontificio Consiglio della Cultura è il Dicastero della Curia Romana, che coadiuva il Sommo Pontefice nell'esercizio del Suo supremo Ufficio pastorale per il bene e il servizio della Chiesa universale e delle Chiese particolari, in ciò che concerne l'incontro tra il messaggio salvifico del Vangelo e le culture, lo studio dei gravi fenomeni di frattura fra Vangelo e culture, di indifferenza religiosa e di non credenza; le relazioni della Chiesa e della Santa Sede con il mondo della cultura, promovendo in particolare il dialogo con le varie culture del nostro tempo, affinché la civiltà dell'uomo si apra sempre di più al Vangelo, e i cultori delle scienze, delle lettere e delle arti si sentano riconosciuti dalla Chiesa come persone al servizio del vero, del buono e del bello.
Il Pontificio Consiglio della Cultura, inoltre, segue e coordina l'attività delle Pontificie Accademie e ha contatti periodici, per una reciproca collaborazione, con la Pontificia Commissione per i Beni Culturali della Chiesa.

Church POWER House Certification

Name: Men As Christ
Description: A Christian Faith Group that shares the Word of God with 30,000+ men & women in 160+ countries. If you've never had a relationship with Jesus Christ (or, if you've drifted a bit from Him), please consider opening your heart up to Him and accepting Him as your King and Savior. By seeking Him, He will meet you and love you where you are; and, your life will never be the same. To learn more and possibly join us and our weekly blog, please check out our website or send Gregg a message.  God bless!!!

Name: Linden Christian School
Description:
Linden Christian School is a dynamic independent school inspired by faith, motivated by excellence and anchored by meaningful relationships. Our exceptional teaching staff boasts a rich variety of talents, skills and interests. With their guidance, Linden students grow to be insightful thinkers and discerning decision makers. We give them the tools, knowledge and opportunities to succeed; we expose them to extracurricular options so that they can develop new skills; we provide expert instruction and a lively, caring community.

We also seek to impart the values that will serve as a foundation for a purposeful, meaningful life – helping students grow to be devoted followers of Christ and compassionate community members.

Name: Bethel Leaders Network
Description:
We are a unique expression of our Bethel Church family, carrying the same heart and vision: to see the global expansion of God's Kingdom through His manifest presence.  Our mission is to provide a space where leaders from around the world can connect, be equipped, and feel empowered to live out Kingdom culture and see Heaven transform their homes, spheres and regions.

Our dream is that the BLN family will spark reformation within the church, release freedom into every sphere of society, and create apostolic places and spaces that empower every believer to walk boldly in their God-given uniqueness. Together, we will take the Kingdom further than we ever thought possible.

Name: Christ Church Cathedral, Houston, TX
Description:
Since its founding in 1839, Christ Church has been proclaiming in word and deed the love of God that was made known in the person of Jesus Christ. At its inception, the church was a small parish situated in the middle of a tiny, swampy settlement town.

Today, Christ Church Cathedral sits at the heart of the country's fourth largest city, a beacon of faith and charity to a thriving metropolitan center. Between then and now we have survived several destructive fires, been inspired by numerous great leaders, received designation as the cathedral of the Diocese of Texas, and completed a magnificent construction and renovation project to meet needs of the 21st century.

As a cathedral parish, we are more than just the church home to thousands of Houstonians. We are the "seat" of the Bishop of Texas. We are a place of hospitality and worship for all Episcopalians in the Diocese of Texas. We are an ecumenical center of learning and formation for Christians of all backgrounds and many non-Christians. We are the parent of over a dozen outreach organizations meeting the needs of countless people in need around our community. We are all this and more. We are Christ Church Cathedral.

Name: First Free Church
Description: First Free is a vibrant church home for many people…people with diverse backgrounds, varying needs, and even differing perspectives.  We want First Free Church to be a place where you can come as you are, get your deepest questions answered, and even feel safe to struggle in your spiritual journey.  Even though life is challenging we believe that there is hope and meaningful life-change available through a relationship with Jesus Christ.

Name: SJI
Description:
SJI is committed to:
Mobilising - Helping people to find their place in God’s plans.
Mission – Assisting local churches as they communicate the Gospel relevantly and powerfully.
Justice – Committed to a holistic Gospel that challenges injustice and encourages the church to serve the poor and the marginalised.

Name: The Windsor Village Church Family
Description: Love! Bless! Teach!

Name: East Indonesia Union Confrence of Seventh-day Adventist Church (UKIKT)
Description:
Over the years, our church has agreed upon key statements that summarize the principal teachings Seventh-day Adventists understand from the Bible. These statements are made collectively by a group of scholars studying and prayerfully searching the Bible with the help of the Holy Spirit.

Currently, Adventists hold 28 fundamental beliefs that can be organized into six catagories—the doctrines of God, man, salvation, the church, the Christian life and last day events. In each teaching, God is the architect, who in wisdom, grace and infinite love, is restoring a relationship with humanity that will last for eternity.

Name: Sanctus Church
Description:
Our Mission:
To glorify God by reaching and enabling people of all ages and nations to become fully devoted followers of Jesus Christ.

Our Vision:
To become a missional church of 10,000 meeting the physical, emotional and spiritual needs of people in Jesus' name.

Name: New Life Community Church, Chicago
Description: New Life is a non-denominational Christian church. We currently meet at 14 locations in and around the city of Chicago. Over 4000 people gather each Sunday at one of our 27 weekend worship services. We are passionate about helping people reconnect with God and seeing lives transformed. On Sundays we meet for worship and teaching in larger gatherings. During the week we meet in small groups in homes or other meeting places. Our heart is to be all over the city for the good of the city.

Name: Trinity Baptist Church
Description:
We believe BIG.
We serve BIG.
We give BIG.

Trinity Baptist Church is an autonomous body of believers who have been saved and eternally secured by grace alone through faith alone in the Lord Jesus Christ, baptized as believers by immersion, and joined together by the Holy Spirit, with Jesus Himself as the Head of the church.

Name: UNIKAHIDHA
Description:
Unit Aktivitas Kerohanian Hindu Dharma atau  disingkat UNIKAHIDHA merupakan wadah untuk menaungi mahasiswa/mahasiswi  beragama Hindu di Universitas Brawijaya.
