Name: Young Catholic Professionals - San Diego
Description:
YCP was founded in 2010 to help young professionals connect with one another and maintain their Catholic faith while pursuing demanding careers. The San Diego chapter of YCP was founded in August 2016.

Our mission is to encourage young adult professionals to Work in Witness for Christ.

YCP brings young adults together for speaker events, panel discussions, and networking in order to foster Catholic identity, to encourage community, and to inspire a call to action.

Name: Marilyn Hickey Ministries
Description:
Marilyn & Sarah Ministries has made a dramatic impact on many countries worldwide; from disaster relief efforts in Haiti, Indonesia, and Pakistan to providing food for the hungry in Mexico, Costa Rica, Russia, and Manila.

Marilyn has expressed great desire in uniting people together and where there is need, there is a call to respond. With a true humanitarian’s heart, Dr. Hickey has helped millions of individuals overcome setbacks.

Dr. Hickey is also a highly sought after international public speaker and her lectures on healing and the Bible have broken records in several countries for the largest public meeting ever held in that nation’s history.

Marilyn and Sarah are frequently invited to speak within the U.S. and around the world. Their teaching has been impactful on individuals, cultures, and countries.

At least twice each year, Marilyn and Sarah take a large group of travelers to visit an international country, learn the culture, and explore its history. Recent trips include China, Greece, Turkey, Albania, Morocco, Israel, Egypt, and Italy.

Name: South Central Conference of Seventh-Day Adventist
Description: Located in Nashville, Tennessee, the South Central Conference, is headquarters for the ministry of Seventh-day Adventists in the states of Kentucky, Tennessee, Alabama, Mississippi and the portion of Florida lying west of the Apalachicola River. This conference comprises more than 29,000 members in 146 congregations. We are one of eight conferences located in the Southern Union Conference of Seventh-day Adventists.

Name: Evangel Business
Description:
At the Evangel University Business and Economics Department, our programs are personal and practical, led by faculty with real-world experience and the right expertise to guide you. With an Evangel business degree, you will be equipped to serve as a leader in public and private businesses, government, and non-profit organizations.

Core to our business program is the development of a Christian worldview, refining character so that you may represent Christ well in the marketplace as an ethical leader.

Our rigorous degrees, accredited by the Accreditation Council for Business Schools and Programs (ACBSP), provide the business training and skills you need to excel in your vocational calling. With hands-on experience dealing with real business challenges – and opportunities in investment, business, marketing, and service – you will learn what it takes to be successful in the business world.

Name: Black MillennialCafe
Description: The Black Millennial Cafe is a resource for persons and institutions interested in statistical information on black millennials and faith. Millennials are persons born between 1981-1996. The BMC was created to educate, train and coach institutions engaging Black millennials, using her extensive and unmatched research.  Founding curator of the Black Millennial Cafe, Rev. Dr. Brianna K. Parker desires to provide information to those who are most interested in legitimate, authentic, productive engagement of millennials.

Name: Uri L'Tzedek
Description: Uri L'Tzedek is an Orthodox social justice organization guided by Torah values and dedicated to combating suffering and oppression.  Through community based education, leadership development and action, Uri L'Tzedek creates discourse, inspires leaders, and empowers the Jewish community towards creating a more just world.

Name: Mennonite Church Eastern Canada
Description: MCEC is a diverse community transformed by the love of God, inspired by the hope we find in Jesus and called to action by the power of the Holy Spirit.

Name: SCORE International
Description: SCORE specializes in short-term mission trips that partner with long-term sustainable ministries in twelve different countries: Argentina, Brazil, Colombia, Costa Rica, Cuba, Dominican Republic, Haiti, Honduras, Panama, South Africa, Zambia, and across the United States.

Name: Comunidade Vida Plena
Description:
A Comunidade Vida Plena se importa com Deus, com o próximo e com o mundo. Somos uma igreja que busca ser relevante na sociedade e que não se limita em fazer a diferença apenas dentro do templo, pois acreditamos que o Reino de Deus deve expandir e sair das quatros paredes.

Temos ministérios que impactam e espalham o amor de Jesus para aqueles que ainda não o conhecem e grupos de crescimento que levam uma palavra de vida para as casas, proporcionando relacionamento mais próximo entre os membros da casa.

Name: Love New Canadians
Description: Love New Canadians is a ministry organization that equips local churches with the means to serve new Canadians in their neighbourhoods and to be intentionally welcoming for immigrants. As intercultural ministries seek to build strong relationships with new immigrants, Love New Canadians makes a point to facilitate the understanding of and create settlement programming for new Canadians. Our hope is that, through our intuitive and adaptable curriculum, new immigrants will be able to adapt quickly to Canada and connect with mature believers who will show them the love of Christ. We equip local churches with the means to begin a "Bridges" class, which provides a gentle and engaging introduction to the life and teachings of Jesus.

Name: Transform Our World
Description:
Transform Our World is built and hosted at Harvest Evangelism Inc. which is the umbrella organization for the International Transformation Network, an alliance of marketplace and ministry individuals working together to transform our world for the better.

The transformation movement is different because it is spiritual without being religious. Even though faith in God is its central tenet, there is no religious flavor to it. In fact, more than in houses of worship, it is taking place in the marketplace.

It is revolutionary without being rebellious.

It is contagious without being infectious.

It is empowering without being overpowering.

It is personal while at the same time touching cities and nations.

It is both death-defying and life-giving, because it is being facilitated by people who are overcoming systemic evil by the blood shed on the cross by the Holy One, people who have exchanged their lives for His life.

https://www.facebook.com/transformourworld

Name: Soul Survivor Watford
Description:
We are a church family in Watford, Hertfordshire that is seeking to love God, love people, and love the world around us. 

We’re an Anglican church which means we’re part of the Church of England but we’re very relaxed and informal. We meet in two warehouses next to each other on an industrial estate in North Watford and hold three services each Sunday at 9.15am, 11.30am and 6.30pm. We also livestream our 11.30am service each Sunday so you can join us any time you like without even leaving your house! 

If you want to get in touch or find out more visit our website!

Name: Worldview Academy
Description: Worldview Academy is a nation-wide ministry which trains Christian students to think and live in accord with a biblical worldview.

Name: Asociación Santa Cruz
Description:

To achieve it, we support children and young people trough integral formation in service, character righteousness, faith and love to our country, Colombia.

Name: Grace Community Church
Description:
Grace Community Church is a church for people who don't go to church in Arlington, VA. We enjoy partnering with other organizations to serve the community and to create meaningful events and services that are relevant to your everyday life. 

Expect friendly people, hot coffee, and great music. Whether you've never been to church before or this is your first time back in a long time, come and see what Grace is all about.

Name: Institute for Faith & Life
Description: Affiliated with Lewis University

Name: Newman Center–St. Thomas Aquinas Church
Description:
The Newman Center – St. Thomas Aquinas Church has served the spiritual, social, and leadership formation needs of Catholic students attending the University of Nebraska–Lincoln for over a century.

Having outgrown the original facility, the beautiful, new Newman Center - St. Thomas Aquinas Church was constructed in 2015 on the corner of 16th and Q streets on campus. Since expansion, our existing ministries have thrived and many new ones have begun!

Name: CityBridge Community Church
Description:
Connecting a broken city and broken people to the Good News of Jesus Christ. We are a community transformed by the Gospel of Jesus and devoted to following Him.

In the summer of 1998, a group of friends met to consider what it would look like to prayerfully pursue a life of faithfulness to Scriptures as they devoted their lives to Christ together in Dallas. 

By the end of 1999, this core group had grown to 150 people who covenanted together to serve Christ and His purposes. These friends’ faithful obedience sparked change in Dallas County as Jesus converted people, healed marriages, and equipped thousands of people for the work of ministry. They couldn’t keep that hope contained only in Dallas, so a steadfast group of members uprooted their lives to plant a Plano campus of Watermark. 

The Lord moved mightily in and through our body over the past 6 years. Our Watermark Plano campus was mature enough in its leadership, membership, and financial sustainability to be its own independent church! We are confident this next step will allow us to more faithfully and effectively raise up fully devoted followers of Christ in Collin County and beyond. After 6 years of "being the church" in Collin County, Watermark Plano launched as CityBridge Community Church.

Though we changed our name, our mission is unchanged: We call all people to full devotion to Jesus Christ. We strive to create a safe place with a high call.

Name: CenterPoint Church
Description:
Whether you go to church, consider yourself a Christian, or you're just curious about God, we invite you to come and experience CenterPoint. We are a gathering of people from all different walks of life, from Brooklyn to Montauk, that are searching for more from life then what society teaches us we should expect. We are spiritually minded and understand that there has to be more in this world then acquiring things, getting married, having kids and then one day, dying. This pursuit has led us to the Bible. We find that it has the answers that we are searching for and that through God's wisdom given to us in its pages we can be better people and find understanding for the spiritual yearning of our soul. This yearning has led us to discover that Jesus is more then a religious figure, but that he is in fact the answer that we have been searching for.

Everyone is welcome to come out and be part of our Sunday gathering. Whether you have gone to church your whole life or this will be your first time, we would love to have you. Oh, and don't worry about dressing up, I never do. On behalf of the entire CenterPoint Family, I welcome you.

- Brian McMillan (Pastor)

Name: Institut du Bon Pasteur
Description: The Institute of the Good Sheperd ("Institut du Bon Pasteur", in french, his official internal language) is a roman catholic society of apostolic life, composed of priests, deacons, and seminarians. Name: Stonecreek Church
Description: Stonecreek Church exists to lead people to pursue life in Jesus as their greatest passion. Stonecreek is determined to communicate effectively God’s message of freedom, liberty and belonging, to a culture in desperate need of hope. We understand our times, and — by design — we focus our energies and resources to effectively present a relevant message.

Description: world Overcomers is a 10,000 member church led by Pastor Andy Thompson and is based out of Raleigh/Durham,NC.

Name: Jubilee
Description:
Onze missie: Gods sterke hand te zijn naar de mensen om ons heen. 

Als mensen binnenlopen bij Jubilee ervaren ze precies dat: God in al Zijn kracht en liefde strekt Zijn handen naar je uit. Mensen komen tot herstel, genezing, vinden nieuwe hoop, nieuw leven en een nieuw begin. 
We geloven dat God iedere gelovige wil vullen met de kracht van de Heilige Geest, zodat jij ook Gods sterke hand kunt zijn voor de mensen om je heen. 

 
Onze visie: Impact, daar waar jij bent

De waarheid vanuit de Bijbel en de kracht van de Heilige Geest maken je vrij van alle negatieve gedachten en geven je een nieuwe blik op jouw identiteit en je bestemming in Jezus Christus. 
Deze grote verandering heeft impact op jouw omgeving - daar waar jij bent. Jij wordt een licht dat schijnt in de duisternis. Jij wordt het zout, de smaakmaker van jouw omgeving. 
Hoe bereiken we onze visie?
- We koppelen je aan God
- We maken je sterk
- We helpen je te wandelen in je bestemming

Jubilee is gestart op 15 september 2016 in Oosterhout.

Name: St. Timothy's Episcopal Church
Description: Welcome Good People. We are an inclusive, vibrant, Christian community grounded in the living tradition of the Episcopal Church in the United States.  St. Timothy's is located in the heart of Desoto County, MS and has been proudly serving this area for 40 years.

Name: Bear Creek Camp
Description:
Bear Creek Camp is a ministry of the Southeastern and Northeastern Pennsylvania Synods of the Evangelical Lutheran Church in America (ELCA). Located on nearly 3,000 acres in the beauty of the Pocono Mountains, Bear Creek Camp offers year-round opportunities, including summer residential youth camp, nature day camp, environmental education programs, weekend camp retreats and outside group rentals.

Mission
Bear Creek Camp’s core purpose is to help individuals explore, celebrate and grow in God’s creation. Bear Creek Camp staff and volunteers support that mission of experiential ministry by guiding campers to:

Have fun
Share faith
Build character
Embrace adventure, and
Cultivate community.

Name: Dominican Sisters of San Rafael
Description:
We are the Dominican Sisters of San Rafael, a congregation of Catholic sisters (sometimes referred to as nuns). We strive to be faithful to the vision of St. Dominic, to our charism to preach the Gospel with our lives, as well as our words, and to our mission of bringing the Gospel to bear with depth and compassion on the critical issues of our times.

Since 1850 the Dominican Sisters of San Rafael have been a vital part of the history and development of California, its people, and its church. We have established convents, schools, and hospitals throughout California and Nevada. We are committed to the Mission of Dominic. 

St. Dominic left a legacy of breadth of vision, extraordinary compassion, incredible zeal, firm hope and spontaneous joy. As living examples of St. Dominic's faith, we embrace diversity of expression and encourage each sister to discover and develop her God given gifts and talents for the sake of mission.  You’ll find us in health care facilities, schools, social service agencies, retreat centers and parishes. 

We celebrate our calling as preachers and we welcome other women to join us in community and ministry. 

If you would like to visit or already know that you are interested in a religious vocation, contact our vocations office at:
415-257-4939 / vocations@sanrafaelop.org

Name: Love Your Neighbor
Description: For over 30 years, Love Your Neighbor has been serving the Hudsonville, Jenison and Grandville area has been mobilizing area churches with the intent of supporting the church in its Biblical mandate to care for the poor in our midst. Through our partnership with local churches and their dedicated volunteers, we are able to provide many of the basic necessities to those coming to us for assistance. We coordinate over 50 different ministries and offer a Life Skills Program that empowers individuals toward authentic life transformation.

The Same Gift Ministries is a Christian Ministry focused on bringing God's (YHWH) Holy Protection to this generation.

Name: International Christian Assembly (ICA)
Description:
International Christian Assembly (ICA) is an international church in Hong Kong. ICA was founded in 1973 by Reverend Otis Keener who was a missionary affiliated with the Assemblies of God Division of Foreign Missions in the USA. The leaders of the mission board gave him permission to move to Hong Kong and pioneer ICA which was the first of its kind. There are now many International Christian Assemblies in other major cities worldwide.

From its first meetings in the former Furama Hotel (now the AIG Tower) in Central, Hong Kong followed by those in a number of locations which included the Hong Kong Exhibition & Convention Centre, ICA is now permanently situated in North Point having purchased and converted an old theater building in 1996 which is adequate to house the 1,000-member congregation and meeting rooms. Apart from the Main Church building, ICA’s building facilities also include ministry centers in North Point, Wan Chai and Chai Wan.

ICA is Pentecostal in belief. Their motto is “Reaching our generations, releasing into the nations”

Name: Atlanta Unity Mosque
Description: El-Tawhid Juma Circle (eTJC) Unity Mosques operate under the concept of radical Tawhid. The eTJC Unity Mosque family of congregations is founded on the certainty that all human beings, without exception, are equal to one another socially and ritually and in potential Divine agency. All are welcome at eTJC Unity Mosques spaces for prayer, community, reflection, and discussion which affirm and include all. ETJC Unity Mosques are places of healing for everyone ETJC Unity Mosques are safe spaces for everyone to worship, commune, and just be. We celebrate pluralism and diversity enshrined in 49:13 of the Quran. ETJC Unity Mosques extend an intentional welcome to people of all races, classes, abilities, health (including HIV) status, sexual orientations, gender identities, sex, ages, family and relationship statuses, and religions. ETJC Unity Mosque's beliefs and practises are based on the understanding that all persons are equal agents of Allah in all aspects of ritual practice. Everyone is welcome, even encouraged to take a turn in each aspect of Juma services, including but not limited to making the call to prayer, giving the sermon, and leading the ritual Friday prayer. We stand for radical tawhid. Absolute Oneness. Absolute Equality, like the teeth of a comb, shoulder to shoulder against injustice and tyranny, including tyranny from the pulpit. That is our Islam. Nothing else.

Name: Our Lady of Good Counsel Church
Description: We are a parish (Marysville, PA) in the Roman Catholic Diocese of Harrisburg with a mission church (Saint Bernadette) located in Duncannon, PA.  Founding pastors were Bishop Lohmueler and Cardinal Keeler.  Originally a mission of Our Lady of Lourdes, Enola, OLGC was named a parish in 1954.

Name: The Vine Church
Description:
The Vine Church is a Vineyard church located at 2616 Peddlers Village Rd. Suite 2 in Goshen, Indiana. The Vine has services on Sundays at 9:15AM and 11AM EST with a mission to love, serve, and reach our community and the city of Goshen. We are members of the Association of Vineyard Churches. https://vineyardusa.org/ Visit https://www.thevinegoshen.org to learn more about us.

In the Summer of 2014, The Vine began with monthly gatherings at our pastor Travis and Jennica’s home, to cast vision and invite friends to join in planting a new Vineyard church in Goshen, Indiana. Six months and many living-room-worship-services later in January 2015, The Vine began meeting for Sunday morning services in Goshen. We were continually amazed at the growth God brought us as we had to move into larger facilities three times in our first two years as a church family. By God’s grace, we purchased our current facility in the Summer of 2019, and began the year long process of transforming an empty warehouse that had once served as a grocery store and indoor soccer complex into our new church home. From the very beginning, and as our church grows, the Vine remains committed to keeping relationships at the heart of what we do. We believe that it is through relationship with God first, then with others, that we can create a thriving community of healthy Jesus-followers that reach the city of Goshen.

Name: The Temple Emanu-El Streicker Center
Description:
Community has been at the heart of Judaism since the days when Abraham gathered a small band of men and women around him to worship a single God. Over the centuries, the shape of our communities morphed from desert tribes to shtetls, from magnificent synagogues like ours to the State of Israel.

But the power of community has never diminished. Community celebrates our joys and comforts us in our sadness. It grounds us in our values as we face an often-confounding world. It provides us the strength to cope when we lack the strength to do so alone.

Community is a constant reminder that we belong.

The Temple Emanu-El Streicker Center is proud to continue the tradition of community-building, even with new ways of doing so. Since March 2020, our virtual community, over 100,000 strong, has shared laughter with Harvey Fierstein, sung along with Idan Raichel, studied the Book of Ruth with Dr. Avivah Zornberg, explored angels and devils with Tony Kushner, delighted in the kitchen with Michael Solomonov and discovered how much we have in common with the Shtisel family.

As we face the challenge of simultaneously coping with a global pandemic and forging pathways to a more just society, and as we face a critical election, our fall programs will continue to offer you enriching opportunities to discover new books, meet Pulitzer Prize–winning authors, explore the latest in biblical archaeology, enjoy the music of the Orchestra of St. Luke’s and see how much Jews and Christians actually have in common.

Name: Eco-Adventist Initiative
Description:
The Eco-Adventist Initiative (EAI) is a ministry focused on growing environmentally-conscious Seventh-day Adventist members, churches, and institutions across the globe.

EAI's philosophy:
1) We were created to be stewards of the earth. The wellbeing of the natural world is not more important than human beings, yet it still matters. There is strong Biblical evidence that we were created to live in harmony with all life around us – people, animals, and planet – and that Creation care should be something we all strive for because God gave us the responsibility to look after it (Genesis 2:15).

2) Creation testifies of God's glory. Whereas the world which human beings create brings imbalance and reflects sin, the works God has created reveals His character and majesty (Romans 1:20). To glorify the Lord, we must value and preserve not just human souls, but the rest of His Creation as well.

3) Humanity does not exist in a bubble, so neither should our Christian ministry. Known as the "One Health" concept, the wellbeing of people depends on the wellbeing of the animals and natural environment around us. Even the Adventist health message declares the benefits of nature to human wellbeing. To truly practice this health message, it must be a "one health" message, seeing the wellbeing of humans, animals, and planet as one package.

Name: CLASFON NATIONAL
Description:
At CLASFON, we're more than just a student organization – we're a dynamic community of aspiring legal professionals driven by faith, integrity, and a commitment to excellence. 

As the National Student Body of the Christian Law Students' Fellowship of Nigeria (CLASFON), we are dedicated to nurturing the next generation of leaders who will shape the legal landscape with a profound sense of purpose and a heart rooted in Christian values.

Name: Fraternité Sacerdotale Saint-Pierre - FSSP District de France
Description: La Fraternité Sacerdotale Saint-Pierre est une société cléricale de vie apostolique et de droit pontifical. Fondée en 1988, cette communauté de prêtres au service de l’Église travaille à une double mission : la formation et la sanctification des prêtres, et leur action pastorale sur le terrain. Présente dans une quarantaine de diocèses en France, la Fraternité Saint-Pierre sert l’Église en vivant de la liturgie selon la forme extraordinaire du rite romain.

Name: ALIVE Kenya
Description:
Young people in Kenya want to evangelize and change Africa by starting from within the continent. This is the local chapter of ALIVE which started in USA and has the following message:

In every generation, there is a call for committed and dedicated young people with the courage to do ordinary things extraordinarily well. Through out the corridors of time, many faithful servants have responded to this call. From Enoch, Joseph, Daniel, Nehemiah, the apostles, and down to our Adventist pioneers; this higher calling to lead by principle and conviction challenges every member of our generation to cease settling for mediocrity and become an agent of positive change.

At the time when it seems like all hope is gone, Africa needs to know that though stricken with wars, famine, HIV/AIDS, malaria and other diseases, heaven has not given up! As Adventists, we believe the Great Controversy- the war between Christ and the fallen angels- is the causative issue behind all problems facing Africa and the world. Therefore, Africa’s problem which is spiritual, demands a spiritual response.

It has been our privilege to witness the birth of an African Seventh-day Adventist youth-led movement whose mission is to make a difference in this generation by providing that spiritual response.

From village to village and country to country, you and I can bring the gospel as outlined in the three angels message of Revelation 14 to a continent in dire need of hope. We have the answer to the cries of our continent and our people.

Name: Metro Life Church
Description:
Building a community of people committed to loving God, growing together, and sharing Jesus Christ. Non-denominational church in Casselberry, FL. Join us Sundays @ 10am.

Since 1985 we've seen lives being transformed through the gospel. Living out the great commission through local missions as well as global missions. We want to  connect faith and work while equipping you for the calling God has on your life.

Name: HSA - UWC
Description:
FFWPU is comprised of families from around the world striving to establish a world of peace and unity among all peoples, races, and religions as envisioned by Rev. and Mrs. Sun Myung Moon and to embody the ideal of true love as taught in the Divine Principle, the core teachings of the Unification Movement.

FFWPU champions three ideals: family, peace, and unification.

Our mission statement is:

“To guide America back to God through the teachings and Marriage Blessing of True Parents.”

With God as our Heavenly Parent, we all share the same equal and divine value. We seek to equip individuals with the knowledge needed to develop a personal relationship with God and with each other based on that premise.

As our parent, God longs for us to experience the full range of relationships in our lives. It is through the experience of love as children to our parents, as brothers and sisters, as husbands and wives, and as parents to our children that we inherit God’s heart as our own and are then able to embrace the world with God’s love and create one family under God.

By working together with affiliate organizations, partners in the professional world, and inter-religious organizations, we seek to collaborate and find practical ways to improve humanity’s relationship with the environment, each other, and improve our understanding of the cosmos, ultimately bringing about a world of peace and prosperity. Acting on God’s behalf with intentional acts of love, we can expand the culture of heart in the world.

Name: Crossroads Bible Church
Description:
Crossroads Bible Church is a faithful, loving and influential family of Christ-followers in the Eastside of Seattle, who are committed to loving God, loving people, and reaching the world for Christ.  

We are committed to strong Biblical teaching and equipping people for a fruitful walk with Christ.  We are passionate about worshiping God, connecting in authentic community, serving in our areas of gifting and reaching out to our neighbors, both near and far.

Description: Cornerstone Church is a welcoming, non-denominational Christian church with locations across West Michigan, including the Greater Grand Rapids areas of Caledonia, Heritage Hill, and South Wyoming. We offer contemporary worship services where people of all ages and backgrounds can grow in faith, find community, and serve others. With Sunday services, livestream options, and ministries for kids, students, and adults, Cornerstone is a place to explore and strengthen your faith journey. Visit us this Sunday or check out upcoming events to experience how we’re “Helping People Know Jesus and Make Him Known.”

Name: Voice of Hope Ministries
Description: Voice of Hope Ministries, Inc., founded in 1982, is a Christian community center founded on principles of self-help, spiritual enrichment and urban renewal. Voice of Hope Ministries exists to provide children living in the neighborhoods of West Dallas with strong character models, education support, life skills, and family support services needed to become productive Christian citizens.

Name: Forum for Leadership and Reconciliation
Description: Forum for Leadership and Reconciliation (Forum) is a not-for-profit 501(c)(3) organization created in 2011 to support two existing projects: Renewing Our Minds (ROM) founded in 1999 and Economics, Diplomacy and Integrity (EDI) started in 2006. Forum continues to support those projects and creates projects that promote the development of leaders committed to reconciliation, peace-building and integrity. We base our principles on the life of Jesus as Forum continues to pursue its mission through relational education, training, mentorship and coaching, as we go out to serve and care for the communities we live. Striving to be Ambassadors for a Better World.

Name: SNJM U.S.-Ontario Province
Description:
The Sisters of the Holy Names of Jesus and Mary U.S.-Ontario Province is an international congregation of Catholic Sisters with offices located in Oregon, Washington, California and Windsor, Canada.

The Sisters of the Holy Names of Jesus and Mary (SNJM) have a long history of educating young women and other ministry work dating back to the founding of the order in 1843. Their story in the United States begins more  than 150 years ago when 12 SNJM Sisters from Montreal disembarked from a steamer ship in Portland, Oregon. They arrived on October 21, 1859, at the request of Portland Archbishop Francis Norbert Blanchet, to establish schools in the Northwest territory.

These dedicated Sisters, only a few of whom were fluent in English when they arrived, quickly set to work and on November 6, just a few short weeks after their arrival, opened St. Mary’s Academy to the girls of the city.

In 2006, the U.S.-Ontario Province was formed, uniting the five predominantly English-speaking provinces of the Congregation.

Name: Catholic Diocese of Amarillo
Description:
Welcome to the Catholic Diocese of Amarillo! We serve the 26 counties of the Texas Panhandle, supporting over 30,000 Catholics in their journey of faith. With 49 parishes and 14 missions, we’re a diverse, welcoming community grounded in prayer, service, and the sacraments.

Led by Bishop Patrick J. Zurek, our mission is simple: to bring Christ to others and help people grow in faith — whether through parish life, youth ministry, Catholic schools, or outreach to those in need.

Here on LinkedIn, we share diocesan news, upcoming events, job openings, and ways to get involved. Whether you’re a parishioner, ministry leader, or just curious about our work, we’d love to connect with you.

Let’s build up the Church together — right here in the heart of the Panhandle.

Name: The Promise Pastors Network - Kenya
Description:
Welcome to the Network of Pastors, a vibrant and supportive community for pastors, clergy, and ministry leaders across the globe. Our mission is to connect, inspire, and empower those dedicated to leading congregations and serving communities.

About Us: The Network of Pastors is a professional group designed to foster collaboration, share best practices, and provide resources for spiritual and leadership growth. We believe that by coming together, we can strengthen our ministries and make a greater impact.

Our Objectives:

Connection: Build meaningful relationships with fellow pastors and ministry leaders.

Support: Offer a space for encouragement, prayer, and mutual support.
Education: Share insights, resources, and training materials to enhance pastoral skills.

Collaboration: Facilitate partnerships and collaborative initiatives that benefit our communities.

Inspiration: Provide a platform for sharing testimonies, success stories, and innovative ministry ideas.

Why Join Us:
Networking Opportunities: Connect with like-minded leaders and expand your professional network.

Resource Sharing: Access a wealth of resources including sermon materials, church management tools, and spiritual growth content.

Professional Development: Participate in webinars, workshops, and conferences focused on leadership and ministry development.

Community Support: Engage in discussions, seek advice, and receive prayer support from fellow members.

Collaborative Projects: Get involved in joint initiatives and outreach programs that serve our communities.

Join us today and become a part of a growing network dedicated to nurturing faith, fostering leadership, and advancing the mission of the church. Together, we can achieve more and support each other in our spiritual and leadership journeys.

Name: Engedi Church
Description:

Name: Henry Center for Theological Understanding
Description:
The Carl F. H. Henry Center for Theological Understanding seeks to bridge the gap between the academy and the church by cultivating resources and communities that promote Christian wisdom. We provide a unique opportunity for ministers, professionals, and academics in various fields to work collaboratively with seminary faculty engaged in biblical and theological reflection. Our work is aimed at promoting gospel-centered thinking and living for the glory of God, the good of his church, and the life of the world.

Linktree: linktr.ee/henrycenter

Name: The Mount Church
Description: We are committed to making a difference to people. We can’t keep to ourselves what God is doing in us. It’s time For One More!

Name: Catholic Student Center at USF
Description: The Catholic Student Union at the University of South Florida is a mission of the Diocese of St. Petersburg ministering to students as they navigate through college life and explore Catholicism while building friendships and growing in their faith. Campus ministry includes retreats, bible studies, faith formation, and recreational activities based on worship, evangelization, discipleship, service, and student leadership. The mission also includes a permanent community of parishioners including faculty, staff, alumni, and local residents who assist the mission and attend mass at the chapel. All are welcome to join in the Celebration of the Eucharist and continue to explore their Catholic faith journey.

Name: St Mark Catholic Church
Description:
We the members of St. Mark Catholic Church, a Faith Community called as one by Christ, to serve and witness in His name, seek to celebrate our faith through service and evangelization to each other, our families and our community.

St. Mark Catholic Church in Huntersville, North Carolina is a young and growing parish. Located in northern Mecklenburg County, we are a growing family with a diverse and committed community.  Each day we strive to be a faith community that receives its strength and mandate for the Gospel we live and the faith we celebrate.

Name: The Summit Church
Description:
The Summit Church exists to Receive and Share the Love of Jesus! No matter where you are in life, we are here to help you take your next step towards the God who made you and loves you. We meet at multiple locations throughout the Triad area of North Carolina and beyond the Triad via our online campus.

The Summit Church is non-denominational and not governed by any other organization or association. We are distinctly Christian in our beliefs and doctrine, holding to the Bible as our guide. People who attend The Summit come from a variety of denominational backgrounds and many have never been to church before. We don’t want a denominational name or link to be a deterrent to any person who wants to check us out. We will partner with other organizations and associations from time to time for the purpose of outreach, mission projects, and church planting – both locally and globally.

Name: Cave Global Network
Description:
Cave Global Network is a community dedicated to fostering spiritual growth, leadership, and purpose-driven living. We provide resources, insights, and support for individuals on their journey of faith, empowering them to navigate life’s challenges with wisdom and resilience. Through devotionals, leadership training, and community connections, Cave Global Network seeks to inspire, uplift, and equip people around the world to live out their calling with integrity and impact. Join us as we grow together, rooted in faith and committed to making a positive difference. #CaveGlobalNetwork

For more information visit cavedevotionals.com

Name: EHills Church
Description: At EHills, we are For Real, For Aurora, and For Better

Name: Saints Peter and Paul Parish
Description: A Roman Catholic Church located in Naperville, Illinois in the Diocese of Joliet, IL

Name: Grace United Church of Christ - Sauk Village,IL
Description:
Grace United Church of Christ is a new church start planted by Covenant United Church of Christ and nurtured by the Illinois Conference and the Eastern Association. We stand on the site of the former, United Church of Christ in Sauk Village and pay homage to those Saints of God. We ask God’s blessings to be upon them and thank them for leaving us this sacred place to continue the work of our Lord and Savior Jesus the Christ.

It is our desire to serve God and God’s people by spreading the Gospel message through the preached word, Bible Study and Music Ministry; provide activities and services that enrich the Body, Mind and Soul; create an environment for cultivating leaders for ministry and service; and provide a safe haven for children of all ages to grow and prosper in the ways of the Lord.

We invite you to join us as we shape this vision to empower God’s people for action in their lives, community and the world.

Yours Because He Lives,
Rev. Mel

Name: Christ's Church - Mason
Description:
Christ's Church
Every week, hundreds of adults, youth, and children come to Christ’s Church to worship together. Everyone is welcome no matter your background or where you are on your faith journey.

We are committed to serving in the local community, nationwide, and around the world. To learn more about missions, contact Pastor Brad Wilson, brad.wilson@ourchristschurch.com.

We provide opportunities to engage through Sunday and weekday small groups. They are focused on applying God’s Word to their lives. Contact groups@ourchristschurch.com .

Check out our website, www.ourchristschurch.com, for more information and videos of our sermons and other teaching moments.

Name: The National Presbyterian Church
Description:
Leading People to Become Faithful Followers of Jesus Christ Together in God’s World.
The National Presbyterian Church (NPC) is committed to discipleship, to community, to service, and, most importantly, to a deep and abiding faith in Christ. Sunday services held weekly at 8:15, 9:15 and 11 a.m.

Name: Oneida Baptist Institute
Description: Oneida Baptist is a Christian boarding and day school for students, Christian or non-Christian, in grades 6-12 with a mission to provide a structured living, learning and working environment where each student is challenged to grow mentally, physically, socially and spiritually.

Name: Reality LA
Description: Reality LA is a church of broken people being made new by Jesus and we long to see that renewal spread throughout Los Angeles. Our city is filled with injustice, loneliness, and millions of people who are far from God. But we believe Jesus changes everything. So we’re seeking the renewal of Los Angeles through the good news of Jesus. We long to see lives transformed, the lonely brought into family, and the City of Angels further resemble the City of God.

Name: Country Club Christian Church
Description: Country Club Christian Church is a part of the Christian Church (Disciple of Christ) denomination located in the historic Country Club District of Kansas City, Missouri.

Name: Maharshi Sadafaldeo Vihangam Yog Sansthan
Description:
Situated on the banks of Holy Ganges, and very close to the Triveni Sangam (Tri-junction) of rivers Ganga and Yamuna, Jhunsi Ashram is the centre of excellence, and the operational international headquarters of Sadguru Sadafaldeo Vihangam Yoga Sansthan. Rich in spiritual energies, surrounded by lush greenery and echoed by the sound of chirping birds and river waves.
This 25-acre campus is a paradise for spiritual seekers.The temple and the meditation cave A 4-floored beautiful temple, overlooking the Sangam of Ganga and Yamuna. It is built over the meditation cave of Sadguru Shri Sadafaldeo Ji Maharaj. The cave is still available for the seekers of advanced meditation. A centre of very high spiritual energies, the practitioners very easily experience high spiritual events. Above the cave are 3 floors, also suitable for meditation, having beautiful sculptures of Sadguru Sadafaldeo Ji Maharaj.
There are 2 main guest houses, capable of housing over 200 people comfortably in AC rooms, AC dormitory or air cooled rooms. The state of art canteen serves pure and vegetarian food, apt for a spiritual practitioner.
Jhunsi ashram is well equiped with library, where enlightening and informative books are available. It houses scriptures of Vedas, Indian literature, books from philosophers etc.

Name: Derech Institute for Torah Studies (Ohr Somayach)
Description:
Welcome to Derech, where no two students are alike, and where a young man with limited Jewish background learns with a student who grew up in a yeshivishe family, or with a modern orthodox yeshiva day school graduate. 

The common denominator is that each individual wants to be a mensch, to grow in his Yiddishkheit, and to become closer to Hashem.

Derech’s approach is based on the verse, “Chanoch lanaar al pi darko” (Mishlei 22:6, “Educate a child according to his unique qualities”). Every student is treated as an individual, and no singular educational approach works best with every individual. 

There are many legitimate ways to serve Hashem. Some of our graduates continue learning in kollel while others join the Israeli army. Most return to North America, Europe, South Africa or Australia for university or vocational school. We work with students to help them identify their strengths and talents, nurture these in the course of their studies, and try to give them skills to implement them in the future so that they can become productive members of their Jewish communities.

Derech is the post high school "Gap Year" program of Ohr Somayach.

Name: The City Church - Luzira
Description:
This is a place where we continue to be witnesses of the kingdom of God at hand. Celebrating life and sharing love... the love of Christ as we commune with God, commune with fellow Christians and connect to the outside world!
Welcome to The City Church. A place you come and feel welcome, Stay and keep growing, then go and reach out to others.

Name: Ethical Society of St. Louis
Description: The Ethical Society of St. Louis is a Humanist congregation, a place where people come together to explore the biggest questions of life without reference to scripture, religion, or God. We meet on Sunday mornings at 11 a.m. for Platform (our equivalent of a church service), and have many other programs throughout the week. We welcome Humanists, agnostics and atheists, as well as people from various religious backgrounds. We come together to celebrate our journey through life and affirm our ability to live ethical lives without traditional religious beliefs.

Name: Eastern Mennonite Missions
Description:
Today, the Western church seems to be caught in an irresolvable tension between being appealing while also being a disciplined and discipling community. While we affirm the church's desire to be warm and welcoming, we also desire a church community capable of confronting principalities and powers; a community that serves as an effective witness to God’s vision to transform all things.

This vision, and our own experiences with Christ’s transforming love, carry us into three tiers of work, as expressed in our mission statement:

Christ’s transforming love compels us to
     cross cultures
     engage the world
     and make disciples of Jesus

Name: B'nai Aviv Synagogue - The Conservative Synagogue of Southwest Broward
Description:
Welcome To B'nai Aviv!

B'nai Aviv is located in the beautiful Florida city of Weston, in Southwest Broward County. Our synagogue is a member of the The United Synagogue of Conservative Judaism. Founded in 1988, B’nai Aviv has become a true extension of our synagogue families and is now one of the most prominent synagogues in South Florida.

Our Early Childhood Education Center (ECEC) is accredited by the National Association for the Education of Young Children and offers our children a healthy social and educational experience under the care of a qualified staff. Our award winning Religious School, Kindergarten through 7th grade, is the largest Conservative supplementary school in South Florida, providing a concentration in Hebrew Language, Torah studies, Israel, mitzvot and more. 

Our Adult Education program offers learning for adults of all ages, with classes ranging from Torah trope and Hebrew to Jewish history and contemporary events. Our youth programs are extensive as well, and B’nai Aviv is proud that we have one of the finest youth programs in the country. Our Chalutzim, Kadima and USY programs are not only the largest groups in Broward County, but are prominent both in the South Florida region and in National and International venues as well. 

B'nai Aviv also hosts the community Judaica High School, where students from 7th through 12th grades have the opportunities for advanced learning and even accruing early college credits.

We also offer Men’s Club, Sisterhood, Hazak (for seniors), Social Action, Athletics, Bar/Bat Mitzvah training, Social events, Parent/Teachers organizations, celebration of holidays, and so much more!

Our membership consists of young families, seniors, and singles, and whether you are already a member or someone looking for a Jewish house of worship to call home, everyone is welcome at B'nai Aviv. We invite you to come and visit the only full-service Conservative synagogue in Southwest Broward County

Name: NY State Licensed Clinical Social Worker
The National Shrine of Our Lady of Champion
Description: The National Shrine of Our Lady of Champion is the first and only Church-approved Marian Apparition site in the United States. The Blessed Mother appeared to Adele Brise, a young Belgian immigrant, giving her a message to pray for conversion and to gather the children to teach them what they should know for salvation.  The Shrine is located a few minutes northeast of Green Bay, WI and is open 365 days per year.  To learn more, visit championshrine.org

Name: Saint Paul's Baptist Church
Description:
We are one church in multiple locations.  

Our vision is to touch the world with love, communicate the positive power of Christ to our generation by finding needs and meeting them, finding hurts and healing them, finding problems and solving them.  

We exist to empower people to grow into the persons that God created them to be by celebrating God’s goodness in worship, connecting with each other in small groups, caring for each other and the world and contributing to the transformation of the world through gifts based ministry in the name of Christ.

Name: World Sport Ministries
Description:
World Sport Ministries (WSM) is a Christian sports ministry that is called to Partner, Plant and Proclaim.
We Partner with churches, organisations and individuals in the UK and internationally.
We Plant Community Sports Teams(CSTs) by training leaders and creating resources to share.
We Proclaim the gospel of Jesus Christ by equipping CSTs to stage projects and processes that reach communities through sport.

Currently, there are over 80 teams in 12 different nations.

Name: Comunidad Bet Hilel
Description: Somos un grupo de hombres y mujeres como vos, que nos proponemos, cada día, encontrar nuevos caminos para unirnos a nuestras raíces, a nuestra historia y a nuestros hermanos en Israel.

Name: Christ Church Cranbrook
Description: Founded in 1928, Christ Church Cranbrook (CCC) occupies a unique position in the greater Detroit area, providing a large dynamic, thriving, inclusive and welcoming parish in the Episcopal Diocese of Michigan. The parish was founded by remarkable acts of generosity and philanthropy and continues its deep and abiding commitment to share the Gospel and promote positive social change through its many ministries and partnerships in Detroit and Pontiac. Celebrating its 90th anniversary this year, CCC aims to gather Christian people from diverse backgrounds around God’s table in communion through its fourfold vision: Meeting Jesus, Finding Joy, Sharing Beauty, and Serving Others.

Name: Dunwoody Baptist Church
Description: DBC is a place where we are passionately becoming more like Jesus and are committed to transforming our homes, church, community and the world. This happens when we love God, love people, make disciples and make a difference.

Name: Williamsburg Community Chapel
Description: Williamsburg Community Chapel is an interdenominational family of faith called to make disciples of Jesus Christ by: meeting people where they are on their spiritual journey, getting people into God’s Word and God’s Word into people, and preparing believers for works of service to foster unity and maturity in the Body of Christ.

Name: St. Mary Armenian Apostolic Church
Description: Toronto’s largest Armenian Apostolic Church serving over 30,000 Armenians

Name: Triad Baptist Church
Description:
Founded in 1983, Triad Baptist Church is a family of more than 1,400 with a mission to guide individuals toward Christ both in the Piedmont Triad area and in more than a dozen countries. From our wooded, 90 acre campus in the heart of Kernersville, we seek to honor Christ by worshiping our living God, connecting with people, serving our community, and reaching the world with the gospel of Jesus Christ. Visitors and members have described us as “friendly” and “Biblically sound!"

The many ministries directed by Triad have a united purpose. Whether it be our youth and children’s ministries, including the pre K-12th grade academy; worldwide missions outreach; our ministries for moms, for those grieving, for those with cancer; our Sunday evening seminars on practical topics such as managing finances, strengthening marriages, and parenting; the Connect Groups, which offer small-group support, fellowship, and teaching; or if it is any of our other many ministries; our goal is to build stronger relationships with God through our church and to make a difference in Kernersville and beyond.

Stronger lives, stronger families, and stronger communities … it’s a never-ending journey of faith.

Name: Washington City Church of the Brethren
Description: We live out our faith in community. That community begins in the congregation, but extends also to the district, and to the church as a whole. In other words, the life and work of the Church of the Brethren begins within hundreds of congregations but reaches around the world.

Name: United Methodists of Greater New Jersey
Description:
We equip spiritual leaders to make disciples and grow vital congregations to transform the world.

The Greater New Jersey Conference of the United Methodist Church (GNJ) is the regional body and central office for professional staff and ministry for the more than 530 congregations in New Jersey and parts of New York and Pennsylvania.

There are more than 50,000 people who worship with us each week. GNJ is one of 57 annual conferences in the United States and 76 additional annual conferences internationally. There are more than 12 million United Methodists throughout the world.

Name: Kenneth Copeland Ministries - Europe
Description: The mission of Kenneth Copeland Ministries is to teach Christians worldwide who they are in Christ Jesus and how to live a victorious life in their covenant rights and privileges. The fulfillment of that mission takes place when those believers become rooted and grounded enough in God’s Word to reach out and teach others these same principles.

Name: Muslim Student Association at UVA
Description: The MSA strives to be a welcoming, inclusive, and diverse space for  our brothers and sisters on Grounds. We hope to maintain a community of support for our members through various social, religious, and cultural events, alumni networks, and volunteer opportunities. We aim to strengthen community ties and provide a library of social, religious, and academic resources while simultaneously promoting the presence of Muslim students at the university.

Name: Kildonan Community Church
Description: Creating communities where people feel comfortable talking about God Jesus and the Holy Spirit.

Name: Hominem Instituciones Religiosas
Description: HOMINEM es un proyecto que nace con el objetivo de atender las necesidades de instituciones religiosas con la prestación de servicios sociales dirigidos a la asistencia sociosanitaria para personas mayores.

Name: Christos Commission Covenant Conglomerate Corporation (C5Fold)
Description:
Christos (Commission) Covenant Conglomerate
Headquartered around the Kingdom & @ the Chesapeake Bay
(Hampton Roads, Richmond, D.C., Baltimore & Delaware City!)

Name: Eagle's Landing First Baptist Church
Description:
Our mission is to help people know Christ personally and live out their faith daily. We’re passionate about creating spaces where faith comes alive, families are strengthened, and people discover their purpose.

We believe in excellence, generosity, and authenticity, bringing our best, celebrating together, and honoring God in all we do.

To learn more about our church, upcoming opportunities, and how you can be part of what God is doing, visit https://eagleslanding.org/jobs/

Name: Rebuilt Parish
Description:
Rebuilt Parish is a Catholic parish renewal movement that is boldly impacting parish leaders, providing them the process and tools they need to refocus on reaching the un-churched, creating a clear discipleship path to grow their church, and revitalizing their parish culture.

Beginning within our personal journey at our own home parish, we have developed a model to establish an engaged and growing local Catholic community. This growth continues in real-time as we develop new resources and tools to fulfill the mission God gave us: go make disciples.

Our Mission: Making Disciples by Helping Parishes Make Disciples

Name: Hindu Heritage Restoration Foundation
Description:
Preserve. Protect. Revive.

The Hindu Heritage Restoration Foundation (HHRF) is a Section 8 company dedicated to restoring India’s sacred heritage with scale, integrity, and devotion.

We work across five core verticals:

Temple Restoration – Identifying, reviving, and maintaining neglected and forgotten temples.

Scripture Digitization – Preserving ancient Hindu texts through large-scale digitization and public access.

Gaushala & Orphanage Support – Sustaining dharmic institutions that care for the vulnerable and voiceless.

Girl Child Education – Funding and facilitating education for underprivileged Hindu girls.

Cremation of the Unclaimed – Offering dignity in death through proper last rites for abandoned souls.

All initiatives currently run by Karora Charitable Trust (KCT) and its allied trusts will now be consolidated under HHRF for better governance, transparency, and impact.

Our vision:
To build a ₹100 Cr corpus that enables sustainable and impactful dharmic work across Bharat.

Join us in reviving the roots of Sanatana Dharma temple by temple, text by text, life by life.

Name: Guywire Ministry
Description:
Guywire Ministry is a ministry dedicated to introducing men to God and supporting men in developing meaningful connection to God and other guys. 

Based at North River Community Church, Guywire Ministry offers a number of growth and fellowship opportunities that are open to all men anywhere and from any church.  A number of these opportunities are portable and allow men to participate no matter where they live.

Guywire Ministry is dedicated to men's ministry anywhere.  That is why we work with other churches and ministries to promote and support the development of men's ministry efforts.

Name: Reformed Forum
Description: Reformed Forum is an organization committed to providing Reformed Christian theological resources to pastors, scholars, and anyone who desires to grow in their understanding of Scripture and the theology that faithfully summarizes its teachings.

Name: Jannah Institute
Description:
Jannah Institute is a global educational platform founded by Dr. Sh. Haifaa Younis, dedicated to reviving hearts through Qur’anic knowledge and spiritual growth—especially for women.

With a focus on accessible, authentic, and engaging Islamic education, we offer programs in Qur’an memorization, tajwīd, tafsīr, Fiqh, and purification of the heart. Through both online and onsite classes, we serve thousands of women, youth, and families around the world.

Our mission is simple: bring people closer to Allah ﷻ by making sacred knowledge not only attainable but transformative. Whether you're beginning your journey or seeking to deepen your connection, Jannah Institute is a place to grow—spiritually, intellectually, and emotionally.

Name: KwaZulu Mission
Description:
KwaZulu Mission exists to further the administration of God's word throughout KwaZulu Natal in four main areas:

1. To share the true gospel of Jesus the Messiah.
2. To teach the word in context, in light of its background.
3. To correct prevalent false teaching.
4. To equip other believers to rightly divide the word of truth.

There are four main ways we seek to fulfil this vision:

1. To engage in and to partner with various evangelism and outreach efforts.
2. To collect and create Bible teaching materials in English, IsiZulu, Afrikaans & IsiXhosa.
3. To offer, host & participate in Bible workshops, courses & conferences.
4. To build & maintain relationships with like minded ministers, ministries & Churches.

Name: Promissio.DK
Description:
THE GOSPEL OVER BORDERS - TOGETHER WITH OUR PARTNERS
We will, with heart, mouth and hands, be God's tools for people to have a life-changing encounter with Jesus Christ! We collaborate with Lutheran churches and Christian organizations in Ethiopia and Liberia and send employees to work.

Name: Experience Life
Description: Experience Life is a dynamic new church in Lubbock, TX where the motto is "No Perfect People Allowed."  Since being founded in 2007, our church has grown from 30 to over 3,000, and we have seen over 1,000 first time commitments to our Lord, Jesus Christ. In 2009 Experience Life was named the eighth fastest growing church in the country by Outreach magazine.

Name: Berean Christian Church
Description: Non-denominational Christian church in metro-Atlanta with multiple locations and ministries for every age and stage.  Believers committed to Worship, the Word and Witnessing

Name: Dar Hidayah
Description:
Dar Hidayah – Guidance Begins Here

At Dar Hidayah, we believe authentic Islamic knowledge should be accessible to everyone, everywhere.

 Based in Pakistan and serving students worldwide, Dar Hidayah Online Islamic Institute connects learners with qualified male and female scholars (Ulama) who teach Qur’an, Tafsir, Hadith, and Islamic Studies.

We provide:
 Online, interactive classes
 Courses in both Urdu and English
 Flexible learning for all ages and backgrounds
 A safe and authentic platform for men and women

 Our mission is simple: to spread the light of Qur’an and Sunnah globally, using modern technology to reach the Ummah wherever they are.

 Whether you’re a professional seeking deeper understanding, a parent guiding your family, or a student beginning your journey — your guidance begins here, at Dar Hidayah.

 We also welcome collaborations with Islamic institutes, educators, and organizations worldwide.

“The best among you are those who learn the Qur’an and teach it.” — Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Name: North Pacific Union Conference of Seventh-day Adventists
Description: North Pacific Union Conference is the Northwest regional headquarters for the work of the Seventh-day Adventist Church throughout the northwestern United States: Alaska, Idaho, Montana, Oregon and Washington. This includes more than 102,000 members, 535 churches, and 135 elementary and secondary schools.

Name: Communauté du Chemin Neuf
Description:
Couples et célibataires consacrés, femmes et hommes, originaires de différents pays et de différentes cultures, nous avons choisi l’aventure de la vie communautaire à la suite du Christ.
https://bento.me/cheminneuf

Name: Missions of Hope
Description:
"So that the Word of God may run and be glorified." 
                                                                   -- II Thesalonians 3:1

Missions of Hope exists to commission and send missionaries and to keep missionaries sent. 

As a missionary cover organization for domestic and international missionaries, we work in collaboration with churches, dioceses, religious congregations, and lay movements to create and support missionary endeavors. While we focus on long-term missions we believe that there is great value in offering young adults the ability to experience the missionary lifestyle through short-term missionary endeavors in hopes that they will be inspired to give their lives to Christ in service to Him and the proclamation of the Gospel. 

Missionary cooperation, called for by Vatican II, emphasizes the importance of lay people and organizations working together to make world missions more effective and more efficient. Missionaries join Missions of Hope and adhere to our Code of Christian Conduct, entering the mission field in total freedom to serve the Body of Christ in the areas where they can be of greatest use. Missions of Hope gives missionaries the support they need to serve without apprehension. We also provide the organizations that we collaborate with the access to young, energetic, and faithful missionaries who are able to support themselves and maintain their autonomy.
