Name: Mount Angel Abbey
Description:
The Benedictine monks of Mount Angel Abbey founded their community in 1882, having come from the Abbey of Engelberg in Switzerland. Situated on a hilltop overlooking Oregon’s Willamette Valley, the monks of Mount Angel weave together a place of prayer, hospitality, education, and reflection deeply rooted in the Rule of St. Benedict. Responding to God’s call to holiness and preferring nothing whatever to Christ, we dedicate ourselves to a life of prayer and work. We strive to support one another in community, to serve God, the Church, and the larger society. We do this as we celebrate the Holy Eucharist together, pray the Liturgy of the Hours five times daily in choir, and devote ourselves to work, reading, and silence.

The principal works of Mount Angel Abbey are: Mount Angel Seminary, the Saint Benedict Guesthouse & Retreat Center, the Mount Angel Abbey Library, the Abbey Bookstore & Coffeehouse, and the Benedictine Brewery.

Name: American Baptist Women's Ministries
Description:
American Baptist Women's Ministries is a diverse community of American Baptist women and girls engaged in ministry in Christ's name. With local, area, region/state, and national levels of ministry, AB Women's Ministries creates a community of passionately faithful, mission-minded women and girls engaged in worship, service, and friendship.

Mission
AB Women’s Ministries is a Christ-centered ministry with a commitment to encourage and empower women and girls to serve God.

Vision
Every American Baptist church shall have a vital women's ministry program that empowers women and girls to become and develop as God's person, build God's faith community, and serve God's world.

Purpose
As servant leaders, we serve American Baptist churches by encouraging existing vital ministries with women and girls and empowering churches to build such ministries.

Name: WikiReligions
Description:
Our mission at WikiReligions is to help you sightsee the practices of your own reliance, appreciate your neighbor’s opinions, and acquaint yourself with the world’s major religions, irrespective of your own religious experience.

Our aim is to great a strong knowledge database of all the world religions that will familiarize us more with one another and to the whole world. It is a one single platform that covers study books, devotionals, prayers and reference material.

Name: Rishikesh School of Yoga
Description:
Rishikesh School of Yoga is the oldest school located in the city popularly known as, "Yoga Capital of World". In the 1990s, we were the only school in this holy city but our traditions are thousands of years old.

We provide yoga teacher training courses based on the ancient styles including; 200 Hour Yoga Teacher Traininig Course, 300 Hour Yoga Teacher Training Course and 500 Hour Yoga Teacher Training Course registered with Yoga Alliance, USA.

Besides Yoga Courses, we also offer spiritual courses like yoga retreats, meditation, prana sadhana and much more. We are the original and oldest yoga school in Rishikesh. We keep the tradition alive.

For people who want to develop a connection with their best self, their true self, we share not only the secrets of yoga but give you a traditional Brahman/ashram environment. This helps you achieve the goal of yoga to experience transcendental consciousness and unite with the Supreme self. Here is how we do it.

Name: The Refuge
Description:
The Refuge is a Christian Ministry based in Columbus and Lancaster, Ohio offering free drug and alcohol rehab.

Our 13-month Men's Residential Addiction Recovery Ministry has helped thousands of men who were struggling with addiction to transform their lives. You can learn more at TheRefugeOhio.org 

Founded in 1999 as a simple residential ministry in rural Vinton County, Ohio, The Refuge continues to grow and expand to serve not only the hundreds of men who come to our 5 locations across central Ohio, but their families, churches, and communities as well. The Refuge has had the honor of helping more than 5,000 people enter a brand new life - free from addiction and on their way to becoming better citizens, sons, husbands, and fathers. 

The Refuge has seen tremendous growth as a hub of discipleship and transformation, and we trust God to continue to use us to “...bring Good News to the poor...to proclaim that captives will be released, that the blind will see, that the oppressed will be set free…”

Name: Dar Al-Hijrah Islamic Center
Description: Since the inception of Dar Al-Hijrah Islamic Center in 1983 CE / 1403 AH. The needs of the Muslim Community and that of its interfaith neighbors have been paralleled by, and reflected in, Dar Al-Hijrah’s growth. For approximately eight years a house, which remains to this day on the premises, served as the original masjid. Located along Route 7 in Falls Church, VA, the site of the masjid was chosen due to its accessibility and close proximity to the major suburban regions that constitute the Washington, D.C. Metropolitan area. Dar Al-Hijrah has a strong community from which to establish itself with approximately 40,000 Muslims residing in the area that the center serves. Construction of the current facility began in 1986. Despite seemingly insurmountable obstacles, the facility was opened on March 2, 1991 / Sha`ban 15, 1411. Dar Al-Hijrah Islamic Center is a non-profit organization registered in the State of Virginia with a federal tax I.D. number 31-1256417.Dar Al-Hijrah Islamic Center has been affiliated with the Muslim American Society (MAS) since 1999. The Muslim American Society is a religious, educational, cultural, charitable, and social grass roots organization. It is a pioneer in its embodiment of principles and thinking of an Islamic reform movement that uplifts the individual, family, and society.

Name: MISSION BEHIND BARS AND BEYOND INC
Description: MB3 is an ecumenical, 501 (c) 3 nonprofit organization whose mission is to educate every community across the Commonwealth of Kentucky to welcome, support, mentor, and equip returning citizens with the tools to successfully re-enter society.

Name: EABENSON MINISTRIES
Description:
EABENSON MINISTRIES  is reaching more people than ever before through innovative ministries and global outreach projects.

Subscribe now to the EABENSON MINISTRIES channel to receive new messages of hope and encouragement. Click  to subscribe.

EABENSON MINISTRIES is committed to helping people from all walks of life experience JESUS CHRIST the saviour of the world through the WORD, sermons, teachings, preaching, and a whole lot of  original programs. 

Dr EABENSON JAMES and Dr SHAMIN EABENSON along with the entire EABENSON MINISTRIES family are so thankful for the opportunity to share the greatest message of all with individuals, families and communities all over the world.

Name: First Presbyterian Church of Columbia, SC
Description:
“In keeping with the Great Commission, First Presbyterian Church of Columbia aspires to be a dynamic Christian community within the heart of South Carolina, centered in the worship of God and bearing witness to the Gospel of Jesus Christ our Lord.

"As Christ's disciples we strive to build vibrant lives, strong families, and a fellowship in which members care for and love one another and together seek the well-being of our city, state, and the world.

“By the power of the Holy Spirit we seek to develop a vital preaching and teaching ministry, aimed at drawing men and women to Christ; healing, comforting and nurturing them in faith; equipping them for service, and deploying them effectively into all walks of life.”

Name: Midweek in Mayfair
Description:
We open the Bible to explain the person and work of Jesus Christ, for people of any and every background.

We apply the gospel to help those who follow Christ to connect their faith with their work. 

We help Christians to encourage each other as they seek to live out their faith at work and engage with the ethical challenges of their profession.

Name: Chabad at Texas A&M University - Rohr Jewish Center
Description: Rohr Chabad Jewish Student and Community Center

Name: St James's Piccadilly
Description:
We offer daily services and a cultural programme of talks, events and concerts. We seek to be a welcoming space for people to reflect, create and debate. St James's also runs an number of programmes in the community supporting those in need as well as initiatives addressing climate crisis.

St James's Piccadilly is one of the few churches designed by Sir Christopher Wren outside of the City of London. A Grade 1 listed building, it is home to a number of notable art works, both historic and contemporary; Grinling Gibbons to Che Lovelace. 

The peaceful garden and courtyard (which offers a street food market on most weekdays) are enjoyed by thousands of Londoners and visitors every year, a green space in the bustle of the West End.

Name: Victory Life Church
Description: Victory Life Church is a religious institutions company based out of United States.

Name: Da'watul Islam UK & Eire
Description:
About Da'watul Islam UK & Eire
We are one of the foremost Muslim organisations in the United Kingdom and have branches in major towns and cities nationwide.
We are a founding member and affiliate of The Muslim Council of Britain.
Since our establishment in 1978, we have worked with all sections of society to develop and deliver programmes that deal with education, community cohesion, and respect of other faiths and cultures.
Although the focus of our organisation’s work is primarily on the British Muslim community, it is not to the exclusion of others; all communities are benefiting from our wide ranging partnership programmes.
Our Vision:
To be an exemplary Islamic organisation contributing to the betterment of British Muslims and society at large.
Our Mission:
To be an organisation providing an effective platform to propagate Islam as a balanced and comprehensive way of life; support Muslim communities; develop educational, social, and outreach programs; and foster good relations with other religious communities, governmental and voluntary organisations.
To inform and influence public policy and services in order to achieve better outcomes for British Muslims, their families and communities, and work in cooperation with other like minded organisations to achieve this.

Surely Man is in loss
Except those who have Faith and do righteous deeds, and (join together) in the mutual teaching of Truth, and of Patience and Constancy.

Name: Baptist Global Response
Description:
Baptist Global Response (BGR) is a disaster relief and community development organization with a heart for helping people in desperate need. We seek to demonstrate the love of God so that people in need have the opportunity to experience the full and meaningful lives God created them to enjoy. Our desire is to see people live their lives with hope and peace, inspired to raise their families in confidence, build their communities with dignity and share this life with others.

BGR and its partners respond to people with critical needs, whether those needs arise from chronic conditions—like extreme poverty, contaminated water, or endemic hunger—or acute crises—such as natural disaster, personal trauma, or social upheaval.

Name: Evangelischer Kirchenverband Köln und Region
Description: Evangelisch in Köln und Region – das ist gelebte Kirche von Menschen für Menschen in Gemeinden, Diakonie, Kultur, Bildung oder auch Angeboten wie Jugendarbeit, Beratungsstelle, Krankenhaus-, Notfall-, Gehörlosen- und Telefonseelsorge.

Name: Shepherd of the Hills Lutheran Church
Description: Shepherd of the Hills is a joy-filled gathering of God’s people who desire to grow more deeply as disciples of Jesus so that we may faithfully follow him in witness and service. Whether you’re looking for a church home to nurture your life in Christ, or you’re simply “passing through” the St. Louis area looking for a place to worship, we invite you to join us on Sunday mornings at 9:00am, to experience the heart and soul of Christian community.

Name: Global Harvest Church
Description:
At Global Harvest Church, our vision is to become a multi-channel megachurch of interconnected branches, reaching into all continents and shaping the culture of nations and generations.
We help individuals discover and accomplish their God-given purpose through life-transforming experiences with Christ.

Name: St. Albert the Great University Parish at Michigan Tech
Description: St. Al's preaches the Gospel at Michigan Tech, serving the students, faculty, and staff.  We seek to foster encounter and deep relationship with Jesus Christ, and His Body, the Church.

Name: Bible Davids Ministries
Description: In over 3 decades of dynamic global ministry, Bible Davids Ministries has impacted millions of lives with the Gospel of Jesus Christ through Book & Magazine Publishing, Church Planting, Daily Television and YouTube Broadcast, Healing and Miracle Conferences, Massive Gospel Campaigns, Miracle Prayer Lines, Mentorship and Leadership Programs and Humanitarian Efforts. Bible Davids Ministries is a Holy Ghost Movement that is bringing deliverance, healing, activations of believers and a mega harvest of souls into the Kingdom of God

Name: Light Upon Light
Description:
Light Upon Light is a UK -based Islamic conference that has its roots in London and is committed to inspiring and uplifting thousands of attendees through interactive and inspirational events. Our humble beginnings started with a single event in London, which featured distinguished guest speakers such as Mufti Menk. 

Today, propelled by popular demand, we have expanded to hold events on a national scale, reaching a broad audience of individuals seeking spiritual enlightenment and community connection. Our journey of 
growth and expansion is a testament to the will of Allah and the unwavering support of our community.

Our events are designed to be engaging and interactive, fostering an environment of active learning and personal development. We believe in offering a platform that supports diverse voices and perspectives. Over the years, we’ve been proud to host some of the most esteemed scholars, artists, and sports personalities in the Muslim community. Their contributions have made our events rich with insights and have furthered our mission to inspire and enlighten our attendees, contributing to a deeper understanding of the faith we share and cherish.

We offer a range of packages for individuals and organizations who want to sponsor our events and services and support our mission. By sponsoring us, you will not only help us achieve our goals, but also gain exposure, recognition, and goodwill for your brand.

Name: Destiny Worship Center Destin FL
Description:
BRIDGING DENOMINATIONS
Our philosophy is to build on our heritage, not abandon it. At Destiny Worship Center, we embrace the term “interdenominational.” People from all denominations can feel welcome at our church. We desire for every person to accept that they belong, first and foremost, regardless of the church background, if any, they may have. As God touches their lives, they will then believe in Him and behave according to His Word. We do not believe God is looking for reasons to exclude people. His nature is to include people. The best common ground we all have is that we believe in Jesus, believe in what He did for us, and love Him with everything we have.


MAKING DISCIPLES
Our vision is to help you fulfill your God-given destiny. To accomplish this vision, our senior leadership has developed a Growth Track. Every person who comes to Destiny Worship Center is welcome to engage in the Growth Track.


BUILDING FAMILIES
Building families remains one of the top priorities at Destiny Worship Center. We believe that a strong church is measured by the strength of the families that form it.  At Destiny Worship Center, we offer a number of ministries geared towards strengthening families and the relationships between families.


TRANSFORMING COMMUNITIES
Transforming communities is the reason we exist.  We transform our communities by exercising goodness, generosity, and love to those outside of the church through local and global missions.

Name: Southwestern Union of Seventh-day Adventists
Description: The Southwestern Union is the headquarters of the Seventh-day Adventist Church in the Southwest, serving a five-state area, which includes Texas, New Mexico, Oklahoma, Arkansas, and Louisiana.

Name: Young Catholic Professionals - Orange County
Description:
Young Catholic Professionals is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit on a mission to challenge, train and inspire young adults working in various professions to

Work in Witness for Christ.
YCP brings together young adults and seasoned business professionals in a variety of events to foster Catholic identity, encourage community and inspire action.

Name: Christian Service Brigade
Description:
CSB Ministries exists to build godly men of today and tomorrow. Operating as Christian Service Brigade they provide discipleship tools to the local church to equip men to train other men, young men and boys to passionately follow Christ.

Christian Service Brigade was founded in 1937 in Wheaton, IL. For over 75 years they have answered the call of 2 Timothy 2:2, "And the things you have learned from me, entrust these to faithful men who will be able to teach others also."

Name: Iglesia Manantial De Vida
Description: Iglesia Manantial De Vida is a Religious Institutions company located in 3223 U St, Omaha, Nebraska, United States.

Name: Intriciti
Description:
Our Passion

To share the gospel of Jesus Christ to business leaders through our five practices, to effect change, through the integration of faith and business.

Our Vision

To inspire individuals and organizations, both local and global, to lead others through the integration of faith and business by sharing our best practices and of equal importance - our failures.

Our Values

Our five core values drive us towards our passion. We value authentic faith, innovation, excellence, servant leadership and strategic partnership.

Our Objectives

Changes Lives
Best Practices
Expansion of Intriciti

Name: İsmailağa Camii İlim ve Hizmet Vakfı
Description: Mahmûd Efendi Kuddise Sirruhû tarafından kurulan, İsmailağa Cemaatinin çatı kuruluşudur. Kuruluş Türkiye çapında çeşitli faaliyetlerde bulunmakla birlikte yurtdışında amaçları doğrultusunda çeşitli kişi ve kuruluşlar ile temas hâlindedir.

Name: Palatine Institute
Description:
The Mission of the Palatine Institute for Christian Study and Culture is to help Christians develop a cultural expression of Biblical faith in everyday life. 
We provide resources to educate, encourage, and equip individuals, families, and organizations to apply a Biblical worldview to all aspects of life. In doing so, the Palatine Institute seeks to “repair the ruins” of our civilization from the roots up—helping families display the Glory of God first at home and then to the entire world.

Name: Instituto de Missões Noroeste
Description: O Instituto de Missões Noroeste promove reavivamente a reforma por meio do voluntariado missionário em projetos sociais e evangelísticos integrados no Amazonas e lugares desafiadores ao redor do mundo.

Name: Eastbrook Church
Description:
Eastbrook Church is a vibrant, diverse, multiethnic church located on Milwaukee's North side. We are non-denominational and known for our global missions work, relevant teaching, creativity, diverse worship expressions, and welcoming environment. 

Our mission as a church is to proclaim and embody the love of Jesus Christ in the city of Milwaukee and in the world. Since 1979, Eastbrook has grown to become a diverse church with an emphasis on local and international outreach.

Services on Saturdays at 5 pm  |  Sundays at 8, 9:30 & 11 am

Name: Saint Augustine Academy Ventura
Description:
St. Augustine Academy is a private, independently operated Roman Catholic school situated in Ventura, California. It is located within the Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Los Angeles.

The school's patron Saint is Augustine of Hippo. The school offers a classical liberal arts curriculum. The liberal arts are divided into the trivium consisting of grammar, logic and rhetoric and the quadrivium consisting of arithmetic, geometry, music and astronomy. St. Augustineâs has been named to the Catholic Education Honor Roll for the eighth consecutive year. The school is accredited with the Western Association of Schools and Colleges, WASC, as well as the Western Catholic Educational Association, WCEA.

Mission Statement
In fidelity to the truths of our Holy Catholic Church, the Saint Augustine Academy mission is to assist parents in their duty of fostering within their children growth in the theological, intellectual, and moral virtues.

Some Facts
Faculty is 27% male and 73% female with an average of 8 years at the school.

68% have Master degree or higher.

91% of St. Augustine graduates move on to attend universities like Thomas Aquinas College, Christendom College, University of Dallas, UC Berkley, Franciscan University of Steubenville, West Point, United States Naval Academy, Air Force Academy, Wyoming Catholic College and various community colleges

High School students averaged 54 hours of volunteer service to the community in 2014.

Total cumulative service hours totaled over 3,426.

Name: The Chapel, Buffalo NY
Description:
The Chapel is a non-denominational church with three locations in the Western New York/Buffalo area. 

Our mission is to see every man, woman, and child have repeated opportunities to hear and see the Gospel of Jesus Christ.

Name: Fellowship Alliance Chapel
Description:
Our Vision

We want people to know God's call and follow it.

Our Mission

We want to help people connect to God, to His people, and to His work.

Our Core Values

·         Lost people matter to God. He wants them found. (Luke 19:10)

·         Prayer is the primary work of the people of God. (Philippians 4:6, 7)

·         Everything we have belongs to God. We are only stewards. (I Chronicles 29:14)

·         Knowing and obeying God’s Word is fundamental to all true success. (Joshua 1:8)

·         Without the empowerment of the Holy Spirit, we can accomplish nothing. (I Corinthians 2:4, 5)

Name: Burke Community Church
Description: A non-denominational church with a family feel committed to knowing Jesus Christ and making Him known in Burke, Northern Virginia, Washington D.C., and around the world. Welcome to Burke Community Church!

Name: The Summit Lighthouse
Description:
Welcome to the official LinkedIn page of The Summit Lighthouse 

We are a non-profit organization founded in 1958 by Mark Prophet, dedicated to the application, publishing and expansion of the Teachings of the Ascended Masters.  

Our headquarters and worldwide communities are passionate about sustaining the balance of Light for the earth, serving humanity, and being a force of inspiration for those who are striving to do God’s Will in today’s challenging world.

Our headquarters is located in the Rocky Mountains near Gardiner, Montana. We hold four quarterly conferences and other events attended by people from around the world seeking a higher calling.   We are adjacent to Yellowstone National Park with abundant wildlife and wilderness.  Our spiritual retreats provide the perfect setting for studying, learning, meditating, decreeing, praying and practicing yoga.

If you are a Keeper of the Flame interested in taking a sabbatical, you can go to: http://www.summitlighthouse.org/aboutus/volunteers/ 

To consider a gift or donation to further the mission of The Summit Lighthouse and Church Universal and Triumphant, you can go to: http://www.summitlighthouse.org/give/

Name: Stichting Dominicanenklooster Zwolle
Description: Het Dominicanenklooster Zwolle is een plaats om in een open ontmoeting met stad en regio, met elkaar in de Dominicaanse traditie het leven en het samenleven te verdiepen, te vernieuwen en te vieren.

Name: Trinity Baptist Church of San Antonio
Description: Trinity Baptist Church is a caring family of believers communicating and applying the life-changing message of Christ’s unconditional love since 1949.

Name: Impact Campus Ministries
Description: Our campus ministries are built on the premise that true success is developing “intimacy with God” (a personal, real, dynamic relationship with God through faith in Jesus). We do this by pursuing it ourselves, modeling it, and purposefully teaching it to university students across America. When we Impact the U, we Impact the World.

Name: Ecclesia Assen
Description:
Ecclesia Assen, een bloeiende christelijke gemeenschap in Assen waarbij huisgemeenten de basis vormen. Bij ons draait alles om groeien in Christus en Hem centraal stellen in ons leven. 

Een huisgemeente is een actieve familie van gelovigen die samen komen in de huizen en samen willen groeien naar het beeld van Jezus. Zo'n huisgemeente noemen we een gezin, en samen met de andere huisgemeenten vormen we een grote familie; de gemeente. 

We zijn een groeiende familie die elkaar ondersteunt en toerust om op te staan voor Zijn Koninkrijk en gerechtigheid.  Gods wil is dat Zijn gemeente wandelt in Zijn Koninkrijk hier op aarde. De gemeente is Zijn handen en voeten om het evangelie te verkondigen en daar kracht bij te zetten. Wij geloven dat wonderen en tekenen hierop zullen volgen.

“Want het Koninkrijk van God bestaat niet in woorden, maar in kracht."
1 Korinthe 4:20

Ook organiseren we diensten, geven we onderwijs en tal van andere activiteiten waar je van harte welkom bent. Dit alles om zoveel mogelijk mensen te bereiken en hen te wijzen op Jezus.

Name: Our Lady of Good Counsel Retreat House
Description: As a Catholic ministry in the Diocese of Lincoln, our Mission is to provide a sanctuary and experience which leads people to encounter Jesus Christ. Through inspiring retreats and warm hospitality, we seek to transform and renew the spiritual lives of all desiring the opportunity to listen to the Holy Spirit and know the Father's love, peace and healing.

Name: St. John's United Church of Christ
Description: Promote the love of God and neighbor.

Name: One Earth Sangha
Description:
One Earth Sangha is expressing a Buddhist response to the ecological crisis. Through our website, social media channels and sangha networks, we offer Dharma (Buddhist teachings), provide trainings and offer resources to individuals and sanghas who wish to respond with wisdom and compassion to the call of livingkind and of our one and only home.

Join our mailing list at www.OneEarthSangha.org/join

Name: West Michigan Youth for Christ
Description: YFC reaches young people everywhere, working together with the local church and other like-minded partners to raise up lifelong followers of Jesus who lead by their godliness in lifestyle, devotion to prayer and the Word of God, passion for sharing the love of Christ and commitment to social involvement.

Name: Temple Beth Sholom (Miami Beach)
Description:
We are the largest and oldest reform synagogue on Miami Beach, with 1100 member households. Along with our numbers comes a strong sense of community. 

We emphasize the importance of individuals and strive to integrate new members into the Temple community. We want our members to feel comfortable in the Temple. 

We are a Reform congregation, a member of the Union for Reform Judaism, in the mainstream of liberal Judaism.

Name: Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
Description: Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate is a religious institutions company based out of 323 Oblate Dr, San Antonio, Tx 78216.

Name: Providence Baptist Chapel, Bedford
Description: Bible believing Reformed Baptist Church seeking to reach the community of Bedford and beyond. We follow closely in the footsteps of John Bunyan, one of Bedford's greatest sons and the author of the second most printed book ever written "The Pilgrim's Progress".

Name: Catholic Rural Life
Description:
About:
Applying the teachings of Jesus Christ for the betterment of rural America.

Mission:
Catholic Rural Life’s mission is to apply the teachings of Jesus Christ for the social, economic, and spiritual development of rural America with responsibility for the care of God’s creation. By providing spiritual, educational, and advocacy assistance, CRL helps rural people shape their own destinies and lead lives of dignity.

Organization Overview:
Catholic Rural Life was founded on November 11, 1923 on the third day of a gathering of bishops, priests, lay women and men in the St. Louis University Library who shared concerns about Catholic rural life and determined that it was time to form a permanent organization (then known as the National Catholic Rural Life Conference). Father Edwin V. O'Hara, Director of the National Catholic Welfare Conference's Rural Life Bureau, was the man who called this group of people together. He was the spirit behind this conference and became the energetic guide of the new organization during its first decade.

After a stint as Chaplain in World War I, Father O'Hara was asked to conduct a study of rural Catholic education. This study confirmed his belief that the Catholic Church was weak in rural areas. He found that the rural Church was under served in terms of priests, churches, hospitals and Catholic schools -- part of a pattern of overall neglect in which Church authorities gave little attention to the social, economic or religious problems of rural Catholics. Father O'Hara made recommendations to the bishops to remedy the problem.

By November 1923, he had generated enough interest in Catholic rural life to call for the first Catholic Rural Life Conference - the one held at St. Louis University. When the organization of the National Catholic Rural Life Conference came into being, he became its executive secretary and brought his energetic abilities with him.

Website:  www.catholicrurallife.org

Name: The Treasure Box Singapore
Description:
The Treasure Box Singapore is a local non-denominational Christian company, founded on the belief that spiritually healthy and whole families are central to God’s plan to bring His Kingdom on earth, and that the key to growing such families is in discovering the treasures of knowing and experiencing God together as a family through worship, prayer and studying His Word.

We believe it is time for family altars that have been broken down to be rebuilt, and where there were none, for these altars to be built unto the Lord. To that end, our mission is to inspire, train, and resource parents with the tools they need to lead their family in seeking God together, so that we will see a generation of God-fearing families rise up to impact their communities, their nation, and the nations of the world.

Name: Fabric Minneapolis
Description:
Fabric was developed in 2006 (originally named Jacob's Well) and began weekly gatherings that fall as a new way of being church. This is a community that believes that God matters, and that we need to come together to learn, grow and serve the larger world. We value better questions more than conversation stopping answers. We are not interested in conformity or certainty, but seek unity in diversity and shared hope and purpose. We want to be church, not have one.  We also know that God, faith and church need to be relevant, honest, thinking and casual and enjoy experimenting with how to be that.

Fabric is a self-sustaining community that is seeking to develop more neighborhood-based congregations in the Twin Cities, and to teach what it learns to others who can use those principles to create churches in their own communities.

Fabric is a 're-invented' church, exploring new modes and models of being church and is proud to be part of the ELCA (Evangelical Lutheran Church in America).

You can also find us on Facebook as "Fabric Minneapolis" (www.facebook.com/FabricMPLS)

Name: Christian Churches United of the Tri-County Area
Description: Christian Churches United is an education management company based out of 413 S 19th St, Harrisburg, Pennsylvania, United States.

Name: GPIB (Gereja Protestan di Indonesia bagian Barat)
Description:
GPIB (Gereja Protestan di Indonesia bagian Barat) adalah bagian dari GPI (Gereja Protestan Indonesia) yang dulunya bernama Indische Kerk.

Teologi Gereja ini didasarkan pada ajaran Reformasi dari Yohanes Calvin, seorang Reformator Prancis yang belakangan pindah ke Jenewa dan memimpin gereja di sana.

GPIB didirikan pada 31 Oktober 1948 yang pada waktu itu bernama De Protestantse Kerk in Westelijk Indonesie berdasarkan Tata-Gereja dan Peraturan-Gereja yang dipersembahkan oleh proto-Sinode kepada Badan Pekerja Am (Algemene Moderamen) Gereja Protestan Indonesia.

Name: مجمع البحوث الإسلامية بالأزهر الشريف_AIRA
Description: مجمع البحوث الإسلامية هو الهيئة العليا للبحوث الإسلامية بالأزهر الشريف، وتقوم بالدراسة في كل ما يتصل بهذه البحوث، وتعمل على تجديد الثقافة الإسلامية وتجريدها من الفضول والشوائب، وتجليتها في جوهرها الأصيل الخالص، وتوسيع نطاق العلم بها لكل مستوى وفي كل بيئة، وبيان الرأي فيما يَجِدُّ من مشكلات مذهبية أو اجتماعية تتعلق بالعقيدة، وحمل تبعة الدعوة إلى سبيل الله بالحكمة والموعظة الحسنة.

Description: MSU is an organization that provides Muslims with a strong sense of unity through events, speakers and programs built to engage and develop communities both inside and outside of the organization. Whether through annual events, weekly gatherings or daily prayers, our purpose is found through our ability to unite individuals regardless of their differences.

Name: First Baptist Church of Eugene
Description: Reaching the community

Name: Franciscan Sisters of Little Falls
Description:
We live the Gospel of Jesus. 

In the example of Francis and Clare of Assisi, we offer peace and joy to our world and walk side by side with persons who are poor. We are committed to promoting human rights and dignity for all, building community wherever we live, and healing the sources of Mother Earth’s wounds.

Name: High Impact Teens: Ken Turner Ministries
Description:
Ken Turner has been in full-time youth ministry since 1987.

After twenty-one years of full-time youth ministry Ken Turner established a ministry in 2008 with a key focus on reaching high-risk youth in the community of Indianapolis, Indiana. The result was an immediate invitation to develop key programs in the Marion County Juvenile Detention Center. Approximately 2000 youth are detained each year in the MCJDC. They key programs our ministry has developed are:

The Man Up study for teen boys who are “fatherless.” 80-90% of the young men that participated in the weekly study were “fatherless” and many others were being raised by a “disengaged father” so they expressed a great need to understand God’s plan and purpose for them as young men.

The Mentor Outreach Team for troubled youth. Recruiting, training and managing a team of adults in the community to become engaged as mentors to troubled youth.

High Impact Parenting is our outreach to parents of teens. Our parent outreach provides training and support for parents of teens and pre-teens from a Biblical foundation.

Name: Catholic High School of Pointe Coupee
Description:
Our Mission

The mission of Catholic Interparochial School of Pointe Coupee is to provide a religious, academic, and disciplined Catholic education whereby students develop their talents in service to school, church, and society.

The Shared Values of Catholic of Pointe Coupee

    * Each child is accepted for his own inherent spiritual, physical, and intellectual abilities and potential for growth.
    * A spirit of mutual respect among teachers, students, and family fosters the good of all within the community.
    * Teachers are aware of the responsibility and privilege, which rests upon the school in being entrusted with the education of children.
    * Faculty and staff seek to gain guidance, strength, and wisdom from their common interest and personal dedication to the education of all students.
    * The school designs school liturgies, prayers, course offerings, and extra curricular activities to train students to think critically, make appropriate judgments, value justice, and prepare for the future.
    * The school cultivates a growing appreciation of individual heritage and promotes harmony among students of different talents and backgrounds.
    * Students develop awareness of the obligations and privileges of belonging to a democratic society.
    * High expectations for student learning guide curriculum development and teaching strategies.

Name: St. John Catholic Student Center
Description:
St. John The Evangelist Catholic Student Center has the sole responsibility and focus for providing pastoral care and Catholic faith formation to approximately 25,000 students on the Oklahoma State University campus in Stillwater, OK. 

Students come to college seeking happiness and often find it in worldly desires, which leaves them empty and searching for more. St. John exists to offer students the path to true happiness, Jesus Christ. Authentic friendships, marriages and vocations come from the community offered here. There is such joy radiating through the lives of the students and we want you to share in that same joy.

Come visit us on the corner of Miller and Knoblock, you'll be glad you did.

Name: Pentecostal Youth Fellowship of America
Description: Over the past 40 years, Pentecostal Youth Fellowship of America (PYFA), a non-profit Christian organization has sought to encourage and empower youths for the ministry and leadership roles of tomorrow. Founded in 1981 by a handful of youth leaders who had a vision to create an organization to bring the youth of our Indian community together, it has grown into a ministry that now serves over 40 churches in the greater New York region. PYFA serves more than 2,500 youth and adults in their spiritual journey to reach their God given potential through retreats, missions, talent development, conferences, and athletics.  In addition, it provides a venue for fellowship among believers. This organization has helped many young people develop into mighty men and women of God who are being used by the Lord in various capacities in His vineyard and have dedicated their lives to His service. With the guidance of God and the direction of his laborers, PYFA continues to inspire and challenge youths for the advancement of the Kingdom of God. PYFA is built on the foundation of the leadership of past officials.

Name: The Catholic Cemeteries Association
Description: The Catholic Cemeteries Association is a nonprofit corporation of the Roman Catholic Diocese of Pittsburgh. Its mission is to administer the Corporal Works of Mercy of burying the dead and to care for their resting places. In the provision of this special ministry, the Association seeks to console the bereaved and to preserve, promote and protect the Church's teachings on death and resurrection, as well as prayer, liturgy and devotions for the dead.

Name: Areopagos Norge
Description:
Areopagos vil bidra til at flere mennesker i vår tid kan erfare fred, mening og sammenheng i tilværelsen

Har du noen gang lengtet etter noe, uten å helt vite hva? Vi tror det er en lengsel i oss alle, som forener oss på tvers av kultur, tro og livssyn. I en verden preget av rastløshet, polarisering og uro, stråler det allikevel håp.

Hva gjør vi? Vi ønsker å forene det som ofte adskilles: kropp, sjel og ånd; fordypelse og handling; tro og tanke. Ut fra en dyp refleksjon og med vår mer enn 100-års erfaring fra både Øst og Vest, eksperimenterer vi med nye praksiser når det gjelder tro og spiritualitet. 

Vårt bidrag er å skape møteplasser og dele kompetanse, som bidrar til mer dialog og trospraksis i denne verden. Vi skaper rom, der målet ikke er å endre den andre, men hvor endring likevel kan finne sted. Vi gir språk og plass til åndelige erfaringer og lengsler. Dette gjør vi gjennom store og små arrangementer, partnersamarbeid, podkaster og artikler. 

Vi arbeider i Norge, Danmark og Asia. Vi er Areopagos og du er velkommen med på reisen. 

La oss sammen søke skjønnhet, sannhet og godhet.

Name: WE ARE ONE CHURCH
Description: Vi är en kyrka i hjärtat av Stockholm. Vi tror på Gud, vi tror på Jesus och vi tror på dig! Vi har Gudstjänster varje fredag kl 19, söndag kl 10 & kl 18 som du kan se via vår livestream på www.waoplay.com.

Name: Sound Vision Foundation
Description: Sound Vision's mission is to cultivate harmony among Muslims and their neighbors through art, media, strategic communication, and education. Its the producer of the children's program Adam's World; offers Colors of Islam classes; NextGen Training and operates nation's only Muslim Network TV on Galaxy 19 Satellite, Apple TV, Amazon Fire TV, Roku.

Name: SVIVAH
Description:
SVIVAH is a multigenerational, diverse, powerful community of Jewish women* who deserve to be inspired, supported, connected, and celebrated. In Hebrew, svivah means “to surround her”, and that is precisely our goal. SVIVAH is a women-designed, women-run, women-centered community dedicated to exploring, designing, and discovering a nourishing, inspiring, and connected Jewish communal experience for women. 
The purpose of “community” is to make us stronger, nurture our values, show us where we belong, inspire and enable us to achieve more than we can alone. Women are strengthened by each other; the Jewish community will be better for our strength. 

We aim to be inclusive, open-minded, and respectful of every path every woman is on. And we are glorious in our diversity. We are a beautiful sisterhood -- may our differences intrigue us and help us grow, while our commonalities unite us and help us flourish.

*As always, SVIVAH defines "Jewish woman" as anyone wishing to be included in a circle of Jewish women. If you want to be here, we want you to be here. Welcome.

Name: United Methodist Church - Common Cup Youth Min.
Description: Cooperative Youth Ministry - 4 churches in SE Portland, OR

Name: Eagles Nest Church
Description:
Eagles Nest Church specializes in developing and equipping leaders for the work of the ministry and the marketplace.  Our motto is “Preparing You To Soar!”  We believe that you were born to be an Eagle!  You were created to soar!  You were ordained to do great things for God!  As a church, we want to help you become all that God created you to be. 

The Kingdom Agenda
Eagles Nest Church functions on a Kingdom Agenda, which is defined as the visible demonstration of the comprehensive rule of God over every area of life.  This rule is reflected through the four divinely ordained spheres of the individual, the family, the church and society.

 At Eagles Nest Church, we will teach our members the principles of the Kingdom of God so they learn to function under the authority of the Kingdom in every dimension of life.  Through the proper blending of God’s Word with good works, the goal of our church is to visibly model God’s Kingdom on earth as it is in heaven.  Simply put, we are called to help people Soar To Their God-Given Destiny!

Vision Statement
To have transformed lives that reflect the values of the Kingdom of God.

The Mission
To disciple the members of Eagles Nest Church so that they are equipped to impact individuals, families, churches and communities from a Kingdom perspective.

Mission Statement
Changing Lives, So We Can Change The World!

Name: Baptist General Association of Virginia
Description:
BGAV is a missionary movement unleashing the power of the resurrected Christ through the local church for the renewal of all things.

BGAV is a cooperative missions and ministry organization that consists of over 1,300 autonomous churches in the Commonwealth of Virginia, as well as churches from several other states and some outside the US.

Name: Our Lady of the Assumption Catholic Parish
Description:
Our Lady of the Assumption Catholic Parish in Ventura, CA 
We, as faithful followers of Jesus Christ, create, nurture, and maintain a community of love through worship, reconciliation, healing, formation, education and service.
Through Word and Eucharist, we seek the wisdom and guidance of the Holy Spirit, embracing the love of our Creator in each thought, word and action. As humble servants of God, we remain faithful to the teachings of the Holy, Roman Catholic Church, waiting in joyful hope for our Lord's coming.

Name: Arden Wood
Description: For over eighty years Arden Wood's mission has been to provide a haven for full reliance on Christian Science healing. Christian Science nursing and nurses training are at the heart of this mission. A Christian Science Visiting Nurse Service brings inspired care to homes in the greater San Francisco Bay area.  Additionally, we offer a sheltered care program, residential services and spiritual renewal (quiet study) guest accommodations, with access to extensive Bible reference and study resources.

Name: Christ Church Spitalfields
Description:
Welcome to Christ Church Spitalfields

We are a vibrant and friendly church in London’s East End, with a vision to Follow Jesus, Build Life Together and Renew East London. Our passion is for more and more people to get to know Jesus and his love for us. 

Contact us at:
hello@spitalfields.church

Name: Igreja Nova Aliança
Description:
A Igreja Nova Aliança (INA) nasceu em 1963, na cidade de Londrina (PR), com a chegada dos pastores Samuel e Lygia, vindos do Estado de São Paulo. 
Em obediência a um direcionamento de Deus, a INA se estabeleceu por meio da pregação da Palavra em locais públicos como praças, campos de futebol, auditórios de teatro e tendas de lona. Muitos milagres e sinais acompanharam o crescimento da igreja, que desde o início se alicerçou nas Escrituras, no mover do Espírito Santo e na adoração extravagante. 
Dando continuidade a esse mover, em 1998 houve a transição do pastorado para os atuais pastores Davi e Monica de Sousa, os quais foram responsáveis pela implantação da visão de células. A partir de então, a igreja local experimentou uma rápida expansão com o aumento considerável de membros, a construção de um novo templo e a abertura de novas igrejas no Brasil e também no exterior. 
Seguindo essa visão e na busca por estratégias que disseminem a Palavra de Deus no meio da igreja, cremos que Deus tem grandes planos para você, que deseja conhecê-lo e se aprofundar ainda mais no conhecimento de Cristo.
Além disso, para você que deseja se envolver nesta Casa, conheça também nossa Integração, um curso presencial de 4 semanas por meio do qual você e sua família poderão conhecer um pouco mais sobre a nossa visão, prática e valores. Invista em seu crescimento e intimidade com nosso Criador. Faça-nos uma visita. Vamos juntos nessa jornada!

Name: Beyneynu - בינינו
Description:
Beyneynu is a non-profit organization that monitors missionary activity in Israel and works with government and community leaders to create awareness of the challenges facing the public and to facilitate the establishment of proper boundaries in their partnerships with faith-based organizations.

Objectives
· IDENTIFICATION - Identify and monitor known missionary leaders and organizations operating in Israel
· RESEARCH - Explore the challenges and complexities of Israel’s alliances with fundamentalist, Evangelical and Messianic Christian groups
· COMMUNITY AWARENESS – Produce and promote presentations, public lectures, and conferences directed towards educating Jewish leadership and organizations
· SOCIAL AND MAINSTREAM MEDIA – Generate video productions, media articles, reports and public awareness alerts and conduct interviews and social media discussions
· LEGISLATION – Consult with legal sources and advise lawmakers regarding the many challenges, including “religious freedoms”
· ADVOCACY - Initiate legal and civil campaigns and petitions to respond to missionary advances
· EDUCATION – Provide lectures and education opportunities to educate future leaders in the counter-missionary field.
· BUILDING BRIDGES - Advance a non-theological dialogue of respect and boundaries with other faith communities, encouraging honesty and transparency
· DEVELOPING PROTOCOLS - Formulate community guidelines and standards to help guide those engaged in interfaith endeavors

Name: Ommrudraksha
Description:
This firm has been arranging workshops for awareness on Rudraksha. We have networks in Bangalore, Bhubaneshwar, Delhi. We specialize in best quality Rudraksha. We make different wearable items. All items are energized in accordance with the rituals.

We supply Rudraksha beads and malas (Certified and Energized), other related products, meditation items. Our product items mostly for Healing and Self Empowerment.

Name: विचार क्रांति अभियान
Description:
विचार क्रांति अभियान लोगों की सोच के परिष्कार और जन मनोविज्ञान की सकारात्मक दिशा का अनूठा आंदोलन है।

"विचार क्रांति" का अनूठा प्रयोग - विचारों का क्रमिक शोधन, दृष्टिकोण का धर्मी परिवर्तन और आध्यात्मिकता के दिव्य क्षेत्रों तक मानव मनोविज्ञान का उदात्त रूपांतरण इस मिशन की नींव है। मिशन के संस्थापक पं. श्रीराम शर्मा आचार्य ने व्यापक विषयों पर लगभग 3000 ज्ञानवर्धक पुस्तकें लिखीं। सोचा परिवर्तन आंदोलन में शामिल हैं:

धार्मिक ज्ञान का प्रसार

जन जागृति अभियान 

नए युग का आगमन

विचारों में अपार शक्ति होती है। हालाँकि हममें से अधिकांश के लिए विचार केवल किसी की कल्पना की उपज हैं। आमतौर पर लोग विचारों को खाली समय का मनोरंजन समझते हैं, लेकिन इसका एक ही कारण है कि उन्होंने विचारों की शक्ति पर कभी ध्यान ही नहीं दिया।

 

वास्तव में यह सारा संसार विचारों की रचना है। भौतिक जगत के स्थूल भौतिक पदार्थ और कुछ नहीं बल्कि विचारों की सूक्ष्म शक्ति की रचना है। शास्त्र इस संपूर्ण ब्रह्मांड का वर्णन सृष्टिकर्ता की अनेकों के रूप में प्रकट होने की इच्छा के रूप में करते हैं। "एकोहं बहुस्यामि"। यदि हम उस तक नहीं भी जाते हैं, तो भी हम इस विकसित भौतिक संसार के रूप में अपने चारों ओर प्रकट विचारों की शक्ति को देख सकते हैं। सभी भौतिक विकास, कला, उपकरण, तकनीकी नवाचार, जीवन-परिवर्तनकारी साहित्य उनके रचनाकारों के मन में उत्पन्न विचारों का परिणाम हैं। पहले उनके विचारों में ऐसी रचना की सम्भावना उत्पन्न होती है, फिर उसी विचार पर चिंतन और जाँच अन्ततः सृष्टि के रूप में प्रकट होती है।

Name: Hillel at Baruch
Description:
Baruch College is the flagship business college of the City University of New York and the largest business school in the United States. Hillel at Baruch provides infrastructure and support services to Jewish undergraduate and graduate students at Baruch College, helping them explore and embrace their cultural and religious heritage and create a vibrant Jewish campus community.

Hillel seeks to engage every Jewish university student, from those with strong Jewish backgrounds to those with none at all. Students may take advantage of Hillel’s leadership opportunities, travel experiences, Israel education and advocacy, Jewish spiritual and intellectual enrichment, holiday services, volunteer and charitable projects, and more. By creating warm, supportive Jewish communities, Hillel encourages emerging adults to take ownership of their Jewish experience and define “Jewish” their own way. Success is gauged not only by the number of attendees at an event, but also by the depth and breadth of meaningful relationships and experiences.

Hillel at Baruch is always looking for talented and passionate professionals to help enhance our community. If you are a student at Baruch College seeking an internship, or you are a professional seeking to advance in the Jewish non-profit world, please contact us to inquire about open positions.

Name: Faith at Work
Description: Prepare for an exhilarating experience as the Faith at Work Summit embarks on its mission to unite leaders across the diverse spectrum of the faith and work movement. This summit is not just an ordinary gathering; it's a dynamic national event designed for collaborative learning and inspired by the shared goal of serving Jesus more faithfully and effectively in the marketplace.

Name: Roman Catholic Diocese of Burlington
Description: The Roman Catholic Diocese of Burlington serves 110,000 Catholics located across all 14 counties in the State of Vermont, under the leadership of The Most Reverend John J. McDermott. There are 36 active priests, 44 permanent deacons and 15 religious ministering in 68 parishes across the state. The diocese includes 12 Catholic schools and an educational system with an estimated 2,500 students. Through Vermont Catholic Charities’ social services and homes for the aged, the Diocese of Burlington assisted more than 3,000 Vermonters last year.

Name: Irving Bible Church
Description:
We want to lead a movement of gospel transformation as people meet Jesus, learn to trust and follow him, and are sent out into their neighborhoods as missionary disciples.

Our dream is that God will use the work and witness of our people to transform the Dallas-Fort Worth area such that consumerism gives way to compassion, individualism gives way to community, and striving for ourselves gives way to blessing our neighbors. 

This is the journey to which we believe God has called us. This is the shape of our future. May we be captured by the uncommon love of Jesus and compelled to join his work in the world.

Name: University Christian Ministries
Description:
University Christian Ministries exists to make, mature, and mobilize Christ followers on post-secondary campuses across British Columbia. We want to see the campus, the marketplace, and the world transformed through reconciliation with Jesus Christ. We believe that if we reach the campus, we can reach the world.

We've cultivated authentic hospitable communities on campuses around British Columbia since 1980, offering post-secondary students a place for holistic worship through genuine welcome, invitation, and inclusion. Our community culture is based on students leading students, alongside a staff team working to serve the needs of the campus.

We enjoy the local oversight of the BC and Yukon Pentecostal Assemblies of Canada while welcoming people of all backgrounds. Our ministry is connected to more than 50 campus ministries across Canada through the Serve Campus Network.

Name: Kingdom Visionaries Network
Description:
KVN is an interdenominational nondenominational ministry. This means that we do not fall under any denominations, yet we accept all denominations. We are called to raise consecrated vessels unto God who’d be instruments for kingdom emergence. We exist to bridge gaps, fill up crevices, solidify foundations, bring balance, and equip the Ekklesia - the Bride of Christ.

Our community exists to serve as a safe haven and firelighter for God's sons and daughters throughout all spheres of influence. We value family and at KVN, sons of God, apostles, priests, and kings, all gather to fellowship with the King of kings.

Do you have any questions, do send an email to info@kvni.org.

Name: The Church in the 21st Century Center
Description: The Church in the 21st Century (C21) Center at Boston College is a catalyst and resource for the renewal of the Catholic Church in the United States.

Name: All Shores Wesleyan Church
Description:
All Shores Wesleyan Church (formerly Spring Lake Wesleyan Church) is a multi-site Wesleyan church with campuses in Spring Lake, Michigan and Coopersville, Michigan. The mission of All Shores is to be a community that is "radically loving, growing together in Christ."

All Shores is a church that seeks to remove the barriers people have to experiencing the grace of God, bringing justice where there is suffering, hope where there is despair, truth where there are lies, and the Kingdom of Christ where there is darkness. Ultimately, this pursuit lives out the words of Jesus that people will know us by our love. This is the kind of love that meets people in the realities and mess of life.

All Shores believes that grace is the catalyst to spiritual growth. The love of Jesus accepts people where they are and the Holy Spirit inspires them to become like Christ. We are on a path towards maturity and devotion to Jesus and desire to grow as a follower of Jesus in community with other believers, as He designed. We don’t just go to church, we are the Church.

Name: Pope Francis Forums
Description: Ask questions, find answers, discuss Catholicism and debate Vatican issues and the Catholic church.

Name: Kingdom Learners
Description:
Our goal is to awaken the body of Christ so all believers can put their faith into action. We do so by providing "Daily Word" videos to encourage and equip fellow believers, we offer free "Courses" to deepen the faith of all Christ followers, and we assist God's fellow-workers in starting their own "Home Church" so they can begin making disciples, who make disciples. 

DAILY WORD: Each day we will release a daily teaching video to strengthen and encourage the body of Christ. Visit our site and sign up today to have these teachings sent to your inbox.

COURSES: Visit our site to check out our free courses to deepen your faith, to become doers of God's word and not just hearers, and to grow your relationship with the Father.

HOME CHURCH: We want to help to Create a safe space for believers to gather and grow in their relationships with one another, and the Lord. Contact us at info@kingdomlearners.com to get started today.

Name: Kane Street Synagogue
Description: Vibrant synagogue in Cobble Hill, Brooklyn.

Name: Crossing Cultures International
Description:
The mission of Crossing Cultures International is to glorify God by equipping Christ followers globally with comprehensive training for effective ministry.

We will impact the world, led by the Holy Spirit, to equip Christlike servants who equip Christlike servants who passionately and effectively serve in local church ministry, church planting, and cross cultural missions.

Name: Evansville Catholic Diocese
Description: We, the Catholics of the Diocese of Evansville, are committed to continuing the mission of Jesus Christ in the world today by joyfully living the gospel, ministering to all in justice and charity, and inviting people into a personal encounter with the living God through Word, Sacrament and Service.

Name: The Gospel
Description:
The Gospel is the writing and speaking ministry of Lance Dixon that is devoted to the exhortation of the true gospel by expounding on the scriptures that explain its mysterious truths. The message of a holy and merciful God saving His elect through the death, burial, and resurrection of His Son Jesus Christ.

*Lance serves as chaplain with the US Army Reserve


*Doctrine is informed by the 1689 Baptist Confession of Faith

Name: Faith Fellowship Ministries World Outreach Center
Description: Pastor David T. Demola is the pastor and founder of Faith Fellowship Ministries World Outreach Center and also the founder and president of Covenant Ministries International. Pastor Demola and three others began a home Bible study which rapidly grew into what is now known as Faith Fellowship Ministries World Outreach Center, a growing congregation of over 6,000 families in fellowship. Pastor Demola is spreading the true light of God’s love throughout the world through his weekly international cable telecast, The Teaching Ministry of David T. Demola; and the annual International Pastors and Ministers Conference - IPMC. Additionally, Pastor Demola has an extensive collection of DVD’s, audio cassettes, video tapes, books and various other forms of literature in circulation throughout the world. Pastor Demola has served as the Mid-Atlantic Regional Director and Trustee of the International Convention of Faith Ministries, and is listed in Who’s Who in the American Religion. Having served in the ministry for over 47 years as of this writing, Pastor Demola is known internationally for his expansive vision, his apostolic and exhortation anointing, and his dynamic presentation of the Word of God. Pastor Demola is not only passionate about bringing salvation to the lost, but also making disciples by rooting people in the Word of God and teaching them of their rights and privileges as God’s children.

Name: CUPAV
Description:
O CUPAV - Centro Universitário Padre António Vieira - é um espaço para estudantes universitários. Estuda-se, conhece-se gente e cresce-se. O nosso slogan é: "Aqui encontras coisas que as aulas não dão": o encontro com Deus, o autoconhecimento, a comunidade e o voluntariado. É um espaço católico aberto a todos.

Na prática, o que encontras é uma casa, um programa de atividades e muitos grupos diferentes que se reúnem sobretudo à noite (grupos de crescimento no fé, de voluntariado, de reflexão, etc).

O CUPAV pertence à Companhia de Jesus e é fruto da colaboração entre jesuítas e estudantes, em torno de um grupo de 11 animadores que estão prontos para te mostrar tudo!

O nosso "patrono" é o Padre António Vieira, jesuíta, missionário, pregador, homem de Deus com os pés assentes no mundo concreto. E é exatamente isso que pretendemos para nós.

Aparece!

His Eminence Metropolitan Gerasimos leads over 150,000 Greek Orthodox faithful in Alaska, Arizona, California, Hawaii, Nevada, Oregon, and Washington. Under the direction of His Eminence, the Metropolis supports the ministries and work of local parishes and, through the life of the parish, extends its support to both clergy and laity. Today, the Metropolis has developed thirteen distinct Ministries which support the unique needs of its parishes and provides spiritual and educational programs for the preservation and perpetuation of our Orthodox faith and Hellenic culture, as well as social and outreach activities to support local and international communities.

Name: Stichting In de Rechte Straat
Description: In Europa leven miljoenen mensen in geestelijke duisternis, zonder God en Zijn Woord. Stichting IRS ondersteunt kleine protestantse gemeenten en evangelisatieacties in (voormalig) rooms-katholieke gebieden. Ook in gebieden waar voorheen het rooms-katholicisme sterk vertegenwoordigd was, zet de ontkerkelijking steeds meer door.  Stichting In de Rechte Straat (IRS) bestaat sinds 1960 en weet zich geroepen om in deze gebieden de rijkdom van het Evangelie te delen. IRS staat voluit in de lijn van de Reformatie en verlangt ernaar de bevrijdende boodschap te delen van redding door het geloof in Jezus Christus als enige Middelaar en Zaligmaker.

Name: Christ Inside Ministries Inc
Description: Christ Inside Ministries believes the Bible is the authoritative Word of God.  Jesus Christ is God the Son.  On earth, Jesus was completely human but at the same time completely God.He is the only man ever to have lived a sinless life so that he could be a substitution for us in satisfying God's demand for perfection.  He died on the cross for all mankind and rose from the dead on the third day according to the scriptures, ascended to the right hand of the Father, and will return again in power and glory.

Name: Sihanada International Meditation Community
Description:
Founded and Headed by Ven. Borelle Seeladassana, it is an Institute that teaches Meditation, under the guidance of Lord Buddha’s Teachings, up to attainment of Nibbāna.

Methodology is based on: 
Tipitaka, Commentarial Work written by Bhadanta Acariya Buddhagosa and Sub- Commentarial work. Main Commentary that is used is Visuddhimagga (“Path of Purification”)

The main teacher has meditated under the guidance of several teachers in Sri Lanka and Myanmar. While he found all the meditation experiences valuable, he believes that the teachings in the Visuddhimagga provide a clear and practical path of meditation that can be studied by the modern-day yogi. The Visuddhimagga uses the Pali texts (Tipitaka) and the ancient Sinhalese manual known as the Maha Atthakatha to take the yogi through the seven stages of Purification (referred to in the Rathavinita Sutta).

The Seven Stages of Purification are: 

1. Virtue
2. Concentration
3. Purification of View
4. Purification by Overcoming Doubt
5. Purification by Knowledge and Vision of What is the Path and What is Not the Path 
6. Purification by Knowledge and Vision of the Way
7. Purification by Knowledge and Vision

The institute plans to teach suitable students that may comprise of Buddhist monks, Buddhist nuns and laypersons. Our belief is that each student is different and needs individual attention to help guide them through and avoid the obstacles and missteps that can occur while meditating. We choose students carefully with the main focus being – their inherent desire to practice Lord Buddha’s teachings correctly and a desire to attain Nibbāna.

Further, it is important to note that all these meditation techniques will be based on the Teachings of the Buddha found in the Tipitaka, Visuddhimagga and accompanying Commentarial and Sub-Commentarial work.

Our mission is to humbly teach a pure meditation technique in Sri Lanka and re-establish Sri Lanka as a meditation stronghold in the Theravada Buddhist world.

Description: WEST GEORGIA CHRISTIAN CAMPUS FELLOWSHIP is a religious institutions company based out of PO BOX 10041, CARROLLTON, Georgia, United States.

Description: New Covenant is striving to be a church where you can find your place within God's family and discover God's purpose for your life. Since it's inception in 1988, we have been preaching the Word of God without compromise and invite you to join us every Sunday at 10:30am.

Name: The Knights of the Kingdom
Description:
The Knights of The Kingdom is a non-denominational Christian network focused on mobilizing skilled teams to empower the local church and parachurch organizations by serving ministry needs, conducting training, and augmenting special projects at no cost to the receiving ministry.  
Through this work, opportunities are created for members of The Order to serve the King with their gifts and abilities.  Additionally, the global body of believers is strengthened by collaborating with other denominations to see Jesus glorified. 
The primary goal will always be presenting and/or supporting the presentation of the Gospel.

Name: The Faculty Office
Description:
The Faculty Office has its origins in the Ecclesiastical Licences Act 1533. This Act transferred to the Archbishop of Canterbury the power to grant “all maner licences, dispensacions, faculties, composicions, delegacies, rescriptes, instrumentes or wrytynges have byn accustomed to be had at the see of Rome”. The Act created a new court – the Court of Faculties – and provided for the appointment of a Judge to preside over it.  This jurisdiction was to be administered by the Archbishop’s “comissarye” assisted by a “clerke”. The issue of Special Marriage Licences and the appointment of Notaries Public were, before the Reformation, functions carried out by the Pope or the Papal Legates. For this reason, the functions under the Act are sometimes referred to as the “legatine powers”.  The powers are more constitutional than ecclesiastical in the modern sense of that word.

Today the Faculty Office is presided over by the Master of the Faculties (as the “comissarye” is now styled) and its day to day administration is the responsibility of the Registrar (a post currently held jointly). Enquiries about Notaries and Special Licences should be sent to the Registrar of the Faculty Office rather than to the Archbishop. The functions of the Faculty Office extend to both England and Wales (and, in the case of Notaries, further afield).  Those functions are now threefold: the issue of Special Marriage Licences, the regulation of the Notarial profession and the legal work for the awarding by the Archbishop of “Lambeth” Degrees.

Name: BANTAMA SDA CHURCH, KUMASI, GHANA
Description:
Bantama Seventh-Day Adventist Church was established in April 1973 by the Central Ghana Conference of SDA after a six (6) week Evangelism campaign which was held at the Ghana National Cultural Centre in Kumasi, the Garden City of West Africa. The 1973 Evangelism campaign which was led by Pastor M.A. Bediako was funded by a devoted American Evangelist and Philanthropist ,one Pastor Ford Harrison who donated Two Thousand U.S Dollars ($ 2,000.00) through the late Elder R.A. Kissi of Nkawkaw.

The following ministers actively participated in the 1973 evangelism campaign:

1. Pastor David Colheart, a young Australian student missionary who was on his way to Australia after his religious assignment in the then Upper Volta now Burkina Faso.
2. Pastor Antobam from Oda District.
3. Pastor Charles Amoah, a former Amakom Church Pastor.
4. Pastor M.E Ampate, the former Kumasi District Pastor.
5. Pastor S.Y. Ampomah of Ntonso Church, and
6. Pastor V.C.B. Debrah, the former Lay Activity Director of Central Ghana Conference.

There are a great number of individuals who have also contributed immensely towards the success of the church and whose names for the purpose of this work could not appear on the record. We hope and believe that God knows them all. Their reward is in heaven.

Name: Modern Catholic Family
Description:
At Modern Catholic Family, our goal is to blend timeless Catholic traditions with your everyday realities, sparking a deeper faith practice within your home. 

Our podcasts bring you a variety of Catholic voices - devoted families, esteemed Catholic leaders, and spiritual mentors - all dedicated to strengthening their faith and yours through shared stories, experiences, and practical life.

Join a community of Catholic families who come together to grow and strengthen their faith through shared experiences, stories and support.

Name: Unitarian Universalist Congregation at Shelter Rock (UUCSR)
Description:
UUCSR is a welcoming and affirming congregation that celebrates all belief systems, identities, and journeys. It is a framework for exploring spiritual diversity in community.

The pillars of the faith are justice, equity, and compassion. 
Climate Justice • Racial Justice • Social Justice • LGBTQIA+ Justice • Mental Health

Unitarian Universalism is a liberal religion committed to deeds, not creeds. UU congregations affirm and promote seven Principles, including the inherent dignity and worth of every person. The Unitarian Universalist Congregation at Shelter Rock is one of more than 1,000 UU congregations in the United States that make up the Unitarian Universalist Association.

Name: Main Campus Christian Union (MCCU)
Description:
MCCU is the first inter-denominational Christian movement and was established in 1957 as the royal Technical College Christian Union with a meager membership of 30 students in the then Royal Technical College. Its aim was to provide relevant and student-oriented spiritual nourishment that the students could identify with. With this small number of membership, the Christian Union (C.U) used to hold its services in the common rooms at the halls of residence, currently referred to as rooms. The first CU chairman was Rtd. Archbishop Dr. David Gitari. Many great people have walked the path behind him; Bishops, Apostles, Pastors, Politicians of influence, Academicians, great career men and many other professionals.

As years went by, the student enrolment significantly increased. Consequently, the C.U. membership increased considerably and hence common rooms could no longer hold the numbers. Today, MCCU holds all its services in Taifa Hall.

Main Campus Christian Union (MCCU) was registered on June 10th 1975, the same year it was issued with a registration certificate. As the recognized body of protestant believers in the University of Nairobi, MCCU has been more than united family in Christ. Step by step, the union has grown in extending Godly comfort and love of Christ to many. The programs that exist in MCCU today have a rich spiritual background. Sunday services and Friday fellowships began many years back and continue to run until today.

MCCU has been the mother to many Christian unions of the University of Nairobi. The University of Nairobi administration has supported MCCU all through. Through the office of the dean of students and the university chaplaincy, MCCU has been able to get help, communicate, voice concerns and also listen from the university fraternity for the support. MCCU has held annual missions since 1989. In all truth MCCU is a union to thank God about. Of its existence, its impact, its mandate and its widespread reach, there have been no limit.

Name: Church at the Crossing
Description: We are a Christian community that seeks to serve God as we embrace both the great commission, "Go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit" (Matthew 28:19 NIV) and the greatest commandment, "Love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your strength and with all your mind and Love your neighbor as yourself." (Luke 10:27 NIV) Jesus modeled this time and time again: even loving His neighbor on the cross as they were being crucified, “Jesus answered him, ‘Truly I tell you, today you will be with me in paradise.’” (Luke 23:43). Even in His final moments before death, Jesus pursues one more person and initiates one more relationship. We have simplified these commandments into our mission: Go. Love. One More.

Name: Loudoun County Public Schools
Description:
Crossroads is a community that intentionally celebrates and extends God’s unlimited welcome and unconditional love to people both inside and outside the walls of a church building.  

Our desire is that everyone - whether they enter our doors or not - would discover the sacredness of their own story, celebrate their spirituality, and fully enjoy an everyday life with God. 

We are equally passionate about and committed to two forms that help us pursue our vision: one is the ministries centered here at Crossroads Drive, and the other we call “Imagine”. Through Imagine we meet in restaurants, bars, coffee shops, homes, yoga studios and in nature with people who have no interest in coming to a church building. We call this a “Both/And” church and through these two forms we are empowered to offer God’s radical grace and love in ways that enable people both inside and outside the walls to change the world one person, one family, and one community at a time.  

Crossroads United Methodist Church is in Ashburn, VA.

Name: Mishpoche

Name: The Stewards' Trust
Description:
The Stewards' Trust is a community - some would even say a 'family' - of all ages and stages of life, from different church traditions across the UK, who are committed to following Jesus. 

We are passionate about Christian discipleship, mentoring and leadership development.

Disciples, helping to make disciples has the power to transform lives, homes, organisations, communities, and ultimately the world!

Name: Brightmoor Christian Church
Description:
Brightmoor Christian Church is a Religious Institution located in 40800 W 13 Mile Rd, Novi, Michigan, United States.  Brightmoor a member of the Assemblies of God USA.

Currently the Assemblies of God USA and Assemblies of God organizations around the world make up the world's largest Pentecostal denomination with some 65 million members and adherents. But numbers fail to tell the real story, and statistics can never meet human needs. People who need help in coming to know God, have problems in their home, or are troubled by any of a multitude of other things that keep people from being happy and fulfilled, need someone who cares. And that's the message of our Fellowship. Because Jesus cares for people, the Assemblies of God is people who care about each other.

Name: The Russian Orthodox Church of the Archangel Michael
Description:
We are a parish of The Russian Orthodox Church Outside Russia (ROCOR) 
Our local Russian Orthodox community has a history going back to the establishment of a Russian Orthodox mission in Blacktown in 1952.
We strive to be a devout comminty with a warm welcome to visitors and new parishioners. We usually have a light lunch after the Liturgy on Sundays. All those who attended the Liturgy are welcome to join us in the Parish Hall.
Our services are in both Church Slavonic and English, although currently predominantly in Church Savonic. The sermons are in English.

Name: Keluarga Mahasiswa Buddhis Universitas Gadjah Mada

Kamadhis UGM adalah organisasi yang bersifat kemahasiswaan di bidang kerohanian, mencakup sikap dan kepedulian mahasiswa buddhis UGM terhadap dinamika kampus UGM, dinamika kehidupan sosial (bermasyarakat, berbangsa, dan bernegara), dan dinamika kehidupan individual sebagai wujud implementasi Buddha Dharma.

Name: Atelier voor Aandacht
Description:
Het Atelier voor Aandacht is een plek voor meditatie en bezinning in het centrum van Tilburg. Wij:
- ‘werken’ aan aandacht en mindfulness
- ‘maken’ tijd en ruimte voor vertraging en verdieping
- ‘oefenen’ met stilte ín de storm

Traditioneel associëren we meditatie en mindfulness vaak met stiltecentra in de bossen, maar onze filosofie is dat de uitdaging juist is om te leren dealen met de vele prikkels en de drukte die ons in dit hedendaagse leven zo in beslag kunnen nemen. Het doel is rust en focus te vinden temidden van de hectiek. Daarom zijn wij – Ad de Wolf en Karin Doms – begin dit jaar gestart met het Atelier voor Aandacht: een centrum voor meditatie en bezinning midden in het hart van de Tilburgse binnenstad. 

Dagelijks kan je bij ons vrij binnenlopen voor (begeleide) meditatie sessies. Maar ook kun je een afspraak maken voor (zakelijke) groepsbijeenkomsten of individuele sessies voor verdieping en vertraging, om zo meer aandacht te kunnen geven aan elkaar of aan een specifiek inhoudelijk thema.

Name: Notre Dame Sisters
Description:
MISSION
We accept from God the call to reach out to people with unmet needs, to nurture in them a conviction of God’s love that they may recognize their own gifts in and through the limitations and pain.

VISION
As we, the Notre Dame Sisters, continue to respond to the call of Christ, we commit ourselves to building a world Christian community of love and support for each person by meeting unmet needs. In the spirit of Christ and our founders, we will alleviate the pains of alienation and eradicate its causes by enabling persons to be reconciled to God, themselves and others through a lifestyle of prayer, simplicity, hospitality, service and risk.

CHARISM
We, the Notre Dame Sisters, are called to be women of faith and prayer, Living like Mary in dependence on God. Rooted in community, we are sent to share our gifts in joyful, loving service to others.
