Description: Temple Beth Tikvah, a Reform congregation embracing our Jewish tradition, has enriched the spiritual lives of our members as well as the broader Jewish community since 1987.  To our members, Temple Beth Tikvah is truly a “House of Hope” and a place they can join with others to experience the richness of Jewish life.

Name: Christ Covenant Church
Description:
OUR PURPOSE
Christ Covenant Church is a reformed congregation of the Presbyterian Church in America committed to making disciples who worship God, grow in community, love others and serve the world.

OUR MISSION
Christ Covenant Church seeks to develop disciples of Jesus Christ by helping them move toward spiritual maturity.

    Worship God
    We will enable people to encounter the living God in the context of His worshipping community and to hear the proclamation of the Gospel in its full counsel.

    Grow in Community
    We will encourage those who have come to saving faith to become members of Christ Covenant Church and enfold them into a class or small group in order to learn and apply God's Word to their lives.

    Love Others
    We will equip members for deeper and more practical levels of discipleship as they learn to love Christ and others more deeply and to live out the Gospel in every arena of life.

    Serve the World
    We will engage members in the advance of the Kingdom of God, both near and far, by sharing the Gospel in the power of the Holy Spirit with lost persons and by reflecting the beauty and holiness of Christ to lost cultures.

Name: Islamic Studies Research
Description:
“ISR & IFR – Islamic Studies & Islamic Finance Research Library: 

India’s First Integrated Islamic Library—Where Every Page Revives the Heart, Every Prayer Restores the Soul, and Every Woman and Child Finds Their Rightful Place in the House of Allah.”

“ISR & IFR Library – The First Door to Knowledge, Dignity, and Deen for Every Heart in Mumbai.”

Embark on a journey with us through the captivating world of Islamic Studies. Drawing from the Quran and the rich traditions of Hadith, we delve into the profound teachings of Islam. As we explore the beauty of the Quran, we're reminded of its invitation to humanity, encouraging reflection, understanding, and enlightenment. Join us as we uncover the wisdom of Islam, guided by verses such as:

1. "Invite to the way of your Lord with wisdom and good instruction, and argue with them in a way that is best. Indeed, your Lord is most knowing of who has strayed from His way, and He is most knowing of who is [rightly] guided." (Quran 16:125)

2. "Say, 'This is my way; I invite to Allah with insight, I and those who follow me. And exalted is Allah; and I am not of those who associate others with Him.'" (Quran 12:108)

Our exploration also encompasses the teachings of Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) as recorded in the Hadith collections of Bukhari and Muslim, providing further insight and guidance for inviting people to the path of Islam. Join us as we deepen our understanding and share the timeless wisdom of Islam. #IslamicStudies #Quran #Hadith

Name: EvanTell, Inc
Description:
EvanTell empowers Christians worldwide to share their faith through training, outreaches, tools, and resources that present the gospel in a clear, biblically accurate, and encouraging way. We have been sharing the gospel and training Christians around the world to do the same for more than 50 years. 

Through in-person and online training as well as print and digital resources, we equip individuals, churches, academic institutions, pregnancy resource centers, mission organizations, and ministries to confidently and clearly share their faith.

EvanTell is an international ministry with evangelism resources in 59 languages. Our ministry partners are in more than 15 countries, and our resources are used around the world wherever the gospel is declared.

EvanTell exists to advance the Great Commission by sharing the Gospel of Grace, clearly & simply, and equipping & encouraging the Global Church to do the same!

Name: Sisters of St. Dominic of Amityville, NY
Description: Amityville Dominican sisters are called to be signs of joy and hope through prayer and the search for truth, and by ministering to God's people. The Sisters' sponsored ministries are Opening Word and Dominican Village.

Name: St. Anne Catholic Church
Description: Roman Catholic Church in Gilbert AZ

Name: Diocese of Kalamazoo
Description: Serving the need of the Catholic Parishes and Schools of Kalamazoo.

Name: First Presbyterian Church of Atlanta
Description: Founded in 1848, First Presbyterian Church of Atlanta is a thriving 2,000 member congregation llocated at the corner of Peacthree and 16th Streets in the heart of Midtown next to the High Museum of Art. We are richly diverse, but come together for dynamic worship with inspiring music, outstanding Christian education, landmark community outreach and global missions, community fellowship, and pastoral care and counseling. Come join us!

Name: V.D.S.D.A - Vishnu Darshan Sevashram
Description:
Vishnu Darshan is a proudly Indian brand dedicated to reviving the rich heritage of traditional pooja practices through premium quality dhoop cups, sambrani, and natural cow dung-based incense products. We specialize in handcrafted, eco-friendly fragrances that bring divine energy, purity, and calm to homes, temples, and spiritual centers.

As one of the youngest-led ventures in the spiritual wellness space, we are driven by the mission to blend tradition with innovation. Our flagship products like guggal-loban sambrani cups, cow dung pizza box cups, and scented dhoop cups (Gulab, Mogra, Chandan, Lavender) are trusted by thousands across India.

 What Makes Vishnu Darshan Unique:
100% Natural & Handmade Ingredients

Sacred Cow Dung Sambrani Cups

Temple-Tested & Devotee-Approved

Bulk, Retail & Custom Branding Solutions

Available Online & Offline across India

Whether you’re a wholesaler, a spiritual center, or a devotee at home, Vishnu Darshan ensures authentic spiritual fragrance for every aarti, pooja, and meditation.

Name: Müllers
Description:
We are all about Strengthening the Church to meet the needs of the vulnerable by inspiring faith, nurturing family, resourcing ministry and transformation communities.

Müllers is the working name of The George Müller Charitable Trust.

Name: Christ Community Church
Description: Making disciples of Jesus for Kingdom Impact.

Name: Sunday Breakfast Mission, Inc
Description: Founded in 1893, Sunday Breakfast Mission served the homeless, addicted and impoverished through Christ-centered programs to meet their spiritual, social and physical needs. Our primary goal is to restore people to a right relationship with God, their family and society.

Name: Associação Freis Carmelitas Mensageiros
Description:
O Instituto Religioso Freis Carmelitas Mensageiros do Espírito Santo tem como missão contemplar e evangelizar à luz do Espírito Santo, frutificando em boas obras e saindo a serviço dos irmãos onde e quando for necessário.

Por isso, foi fundada a "Associação Freis Carmelitas Mensageiros", que tem como missão ser a fonte dos instrumentos de evangelização necessários para que a Palavra de Deus chegue a todos que precisem. Loja virtual, editora de músicas, editora de livros, eventos e mídias sociais são os canais.

Name: Ocean Grove Camp Meeting Association
Description:
A HERITAGE OF WORSHIP IN A SEASIDE SETTING

Ocean Grove is the longest-active camp meeting site in the United States, and since its founding in 1869 as an outgrowth of the nation-wide camp meeting movement, it has developed into a diversified and dynamic year-round community. Ocean Grove’s rich history of Christian witness spans nearly a century-and-a-half, yet the organization’s core mission remains: to be the seaside community where all generations can know and grow in Jesus.  Ocean Grove accomplishes this mission by providing opportunities for spiritual birth, growth, and renewal through worship, education, cultural, and recreational activities, all in a beautiful, seaside setting - God's square mile on the Jersey shore.

Name: Catholic Church - Fribourg
Description: The Catholic Church that is present in the canton of Fribourg

Name: Igreja Batista Agua Viva
Description: A Igreja Batista de Água Viva (IBAViva) é uma comunidade cristã evangélica batista, fundada em 2º de novembro de 1990 por um grupo de cristãos moradores de Vinhedo . Está filiada à Convenção Batista Brasileira e à Convenção Batista do Estado de São Paulo.

Name: Cape Christian
Description: A family-oriented, inter-denominational contemporary church located in Cape Coral, Florida. There are five weekend worship opportunities: Saturday- 4:00, 5:30pm and Sunday - 9:00, 10:30 & 12:00.  We are also online at www.live.CapeChristian.com

Name: Bay Area Community Church
Description: At Bay Area, our vision is to help change the world by advancing the gospel in you, your neighbors, and the nations.

Name: The Park Forum
Description:
As the Park is to the City, so the Word is to Life. We exist to promote Bible engagement in the urban church on a daily basis by creating short and smart materials – for individuals and small groups. 

Our Daily Urban Devotional provides a reflection, in 400 words or less, on one of two daily readings taken from the M’Cheyne Reading Plan. 

Join us today on TheParkForum.org.

Name: St. Andrew's United Methodist Church
Description: St. Andrew's United Methodist Church is in the business of making connections: connecting people to God and each other.

Name: Conseil des Institutions Théologiques d'Afrique Francophone
Description:
Le CITAF constitue un vaste réseau d’écoles, d’instituts et de facultés répartis dans 17 pays d’Afrique francophone.
Il s’engage dans la recherche de l’excellence académique au service de la formation biblique et théologique, pour équiper et renforcer les institutions de toute l’Afrique francophone.

Name: Wilderness Torah
Description:
MISSION
Wilderness Torah promotes healing, belonging, and connection. We awaken and celebrate earth-based Jewish traditions through inclusive and intergenerational village-building.

VISION
A vibrant, resilient world, where humanity remembers how to care for each other and our precious earth.

Name: Pastoral Transitions
Description: Serving church leaders as they care for the outgoing pastor and their family.

Name: United Methodist Chruch
Description: The people of The United Methodist Church are putting our faith in action by making disciples of Jesus Christ for the transformation of the world, which is our church’s mission.

Name: UNIVERSAL LIFE CHURCH ONLINE SEMINARY
Description:
The Universal Life Church Monastery, also known as the "ULC", has been serving our online church community for nearly 40 years.

Our fundamental tasks are to promote freedom of religion and to "do that which is right" - core tenets that underscore everything we do. The ULC appeals to a worldwide audience through our online church services and our overarching belief that "we are all children of the same universe."

Over 20 million ministers worldwide have become ordained with the Universal Life Church. The ULC is a non-denominational organization open to people of all faiths, offering everyone the religious freedom to become an ordained minister and practice their beliefs. Those who get ordained with the Universal Life Church are empowered to take control of their own spiritual lives and do good in their own worlds. As a minister, you have the authority to perform weddings, funerals, baptisms, or even start your own ministry.

Name: St. Pius V Catholic Church and Elementary School
Description: St. Pius V is a Catholic parish in Chicago's Pilsen community. As a church we are committed to promoting the message of Christ through our personal, spiritual and social development in a community that respects race, religion and social condition. Please visit us at StPiusVParish.org to find out about all of the services we offer.

Name: Igreja do Recreio
Description: Igreja do Recreio tem como missão trazer pessoas a Jesus, edificar os cristãos e juntos adorarmos a Deus, servindo a Cristo na comunidade do Recreio e no mundo. Sendo assim, acreditamos que através do Amor, Fé e Serviço, seremos um referencial de igreja evangelizadora, engajada, acolhedora, consagrada e que por meio da capacitação de discípulos faremos diferença neste mundo.

Name: MAVEN
Description:
MAVEN is youth-focused. We are passionate about young people. Youth. Students. Junior highers. High schoolers. College students. The next generation. Because of our love for youth, we are also passionate about the people who disciple the next generation. Parents. Grandparents. Pastors. Youth leaders. Organizational leaders. Educators. We want to come alongside them to partner, guide, encourage, teach, train and equip them to raise up the next generation for the cause of Christ.

Gender identity. Sexual orientation. Pornography. Casual sex. Consumerism. Affluence. Substance abuse. Violence. Technology. Social media. Entertainment. Racial tension. Pluralism. Secularism. Atheism.

Are they ready for these conversations? Are we? The typical evangelical kid in our typical evangelical church is not. Neither are their parents or leaders. Rather than bemoan the situation and give in to despair, the church needs to redouble her efforts in discipling the next generation. And we need to do it in culturally-savvy and relevant ways, while never compromising the truth of Christ.

We must repackage and re-present the truth to media-saturated students who have been raised on screens and images. We must wrap the truth in culturally savvy ways that grab the attention of their distracted minds, gain a hearing and woo them back to Christ. We need a new brand—a new movement—to reach that kind of kid. That’s why we need MAVEN.

Name: Church of the Holy Spirit
Description:
Church of the Holy Spirit is a lively, family church that gathers in the Kirstenhof area of Cape Town and is networked across surrounding Cape Town communities.
It is part of the Anglican parish of St John the Evangelist, Wynberg.
The church is networked around a central hub and includes missional communities reaching various areas of need and people groups.

Name: Temple Beth El
Description: TBE prides itself on being a warm and caring community that provides a spiritual home for Jews of all ages and backgrounds, including interfaith families. Temple Beth-El offers vibrant, meaningful worship services including daily morning and evening minyan, as well as programming in conjunction with the Jewish holidays.

Name: The Canadian Islamic Centre (Al Rashid Mosque)
Description:
We started as the first Mosque in Canada (in 1938) and have grown into one of the leading charitable Muslim organizations in the country, with an array of development initiatives in the Edmonton Muslim community. 

Our most visible initiatives include the Edmonton Islamic Academy, the Al Rashid Educational Fund (AREF), the ARCA Banquet Facility, the Al Rashid Mosque and the Quran Institute.

Name: Kentucky Tennessee Conference (@kytnsda)
Description: One of eight conferences that make up the Southern Union of the Seventh-day Adventist Church. We are a ministry dedicated to the preparation of a people grounded in Scripture and ready for the soon return of Jesus Christ. We depend on the Holy Spirit’s guidance to remain true to and further the Three Angel’s Message of Revelation 14.

Name: Young Missionary Alliance
Description:
The  Young  Missionary  Alliance  (YMA)  is  a faith-based  organization  that  serves  as  a missional  platform  for  young  professionals who are  filled  with  the  passion  for evangelizing  and  providing  practical assistance  directed  towards  community building. We aim to recruit,  train,  equip  and commission  young  people  in  order  to  see God's  love  manifested  in  communities  we work  with. 

Our Vision:
To see the manifestation of God’s love in PNG Communities

Our Mission:
Our Mission is to disciple and train young professionals to be missionaries, social justice champions and community builders.

Our Values:

 Missio Dei: "Missio Dei" is translated to as "Missions of God" reflects us asa faith-based organization. We aspire to know God and tomake him known.  This gives YMA it's missional identity. 

 Community: We fully recognize the eternal fellowship of the Triune Godand we aspire to see this manifested in the organization andthe communities that we work with. 

 Intellectual & Professional Integrity: We recognize that God has graced us to work within the professional and academic spaces and we strive to use our academic skills,qualifications and professionalism to serve God. 

  Stewardship: We believe in stewarding our gifts, talents, education andresources and directing that into Missio dei

Our Objectives:

 To recruit, train  and commission students and young professionals to do God’s mission. 

To build the capacity of our members through our programs.

 To form partnerships with Christian churches, the private sector and the government in a collaborative effort to provide practical assistance in the communities of Papua New Guinea. 

 To provide Christian pastoral care for the purpose of spiritual growth and development. 

 To encourage community empowerment through capacity building, personal integration and social welfare.

Name: Kingdom Business Association
Description: The Kingdom Business Association is a relational association of business people who want to walk in integrity and fulfill their business calling in a remarkable way. KBA is under the umbrella of MorningStar Ministries in Fort Mill, SC.

Name: Area Youth Ministry
Description: We’re a group of UC Berkeley students and alumni who love God and have a passion for youth ministry. We started this non-profit organization to bless our communities and help high school students through the ups and downs of life. We are not a church. We don’t meet on Sundays. We’re committed to creating a space for youth to reason about truth, connect with like-minded friends, and become agents of God’s love in their homes, their churches & communities.

Name: Landesstelle der KLJB Bayern (Katholische Landjugendbewegung)
Description: 25.000 Mitglieder der KLJB Bayern bewegen das Land - Bayerns größter Landjugendverband mit rund 900 Ortsgruppen in sieben Diözesanverbänden.

Name: Wesleyan Holiness Church UK
Description: The Wesleyan Holiness Church in the UK is a church that is on the move. Moving from Maintenance to Mission. Putting discipleship at the centre; engaging with and reaching out to local communities.

Name: Joyful Way Incorporated
Description:
Joyful Way Incorporated traces its origin to Cape Coast, one of the historic cities in the Central Region of Ghana. In the year 1972, a passion for Christian music among a number of Senior High School students led to the formation of two groups known at the time as the “Noise of Joy” and the “Evangels”. Composed of students from three renowned schools in Cape Coast – Adisadel College, Mfantsipim School and Wesley Girls High School – the two groups eventually merged to form the JOYFUL WAY SINGERS, and is now known as JOYFUL WAY INCORPORATED. Music evangelism became popular through the pioneering work of this group.

Travelling throughout the country, we ministered everywhere we could find a soul to receive the gospel message accompanied by the music that rolled off our lead guitars, tambourines, drums, bass guitars and keyboards. As the founding members proceeded from high school to the universities and other higher institutions of learning in Ghana, they formed nucleus groups and continued to operate in their new environments. Out of the nucleus groups, three branches were formally established in 1978 in Cape Coast, Kumasi and Accra. Accra became the National Headquarters. In that same year, the group was registered under the Trustees Incorporated Act.

Today, Joyful Way Incorporated has an active ministering membership of about 150 young people. All members of the ministry are volunteers. They are professionals from various industries and students from various public and private universities in Ghana. They contribute their talents, energies, time and money to advance the Lord’s kingdom through this group. Throughout these many years, many others have served in this ministry who are no longer active ministers with the group. We call them associates and they number several hundreds and can be found all over the world. With their support through prayer, counsel, network and finances the present generation of Joyful Way Incorporated continues to make a joyful noise

Name: Global Radio Outreach
Description: Global Radio Outreach is a religious institutions company based out of 7704 24th Ave NW, Seattle, Washington, United States.

Name: Journey Church
Description: We exist to see lives transformed to live and love like Jesus.

Name: Evangelische Heimstiftung Pfalz
Description: Wir, die EVH, sind einer der großen diakonischen Träger der Pfalz, mit über 1200 Mitarbeitenden an 13 Standorten der Kinder- und Jugendhilfe, der Suchtkrankenhilfe und Rehabilitation, der Eingliederungshilfe und Arbeit und Inklusion von Menschen mit Beeinträchtigung.

Name: Camp Wojtyla
Description:
Mission:
Through an authentic encounter with creation, Camp Wojtyla aims to awaken in young people a full relationship with God, shaping their relationships with themselves, others, and creation.

Camp Wojtyla is a Catholic adventure program which was launched by FOCUS (The Fellowship of Catholic University Students) and holds week-long camps for middle school and high school students.  Camp Wojtyla also serves college students who form the counselor staff and participate in the leadership training program for two months during the summer.  Camp Wojtyla takes students into the heart of the outdoors to teach about the Catholic faith through things like mountaineering, rock climbing, rafting, and outdoor cooking and is dedicated to evangelizing youth through the beauty of the sacraments and of creation.

Name: Avenue941
Description:
Avenue941 is an academically-focused, spiritually-driven after school program for K-12 students. We exist to empower churches to open their doors throughout the week to support the next generation by achieving academic progress, building confidence, growing spiritually and improving behavior. 

In spring of 2023, 81% of our students made significant academic improvement, 84% grew in confidence, 75% had improved behaviors and 66% made spiritual growth.

We are a proud member of the Manatee Chamber of Commerce.

Name: Wilderness Trek
Description:
At Wilderness Trek, we use the outdoors and high adventure as a spiritual catalyst in order to help teens, young adults, families, groups, and leaders grow into who God calls them to be. We introduce people from all across the country and walks of life to the wonders of God’s creation in order to help them deepen their faith and connect with the things that matter most.

We are passionate about the outdoors and believe there is a wonder working power in God’s creation. It is our goal to get as many people as possible into the raw stillness and solitude of the stunning Rocky Mountains, away from the noise and busyness of the world, to have life and eternity changing experiences.

Name: D. James Kennedy Ministries
Description:
D. James Kennedy Ministries (DJKM), formerly known as Truth In Action Ministries, is a national Christian media outreach that educates, motivates, and activates Christians to understand God's truth, apply it to their daily lives, and transform American culture for Christ. The mission of DJKM is to introduce people to Christ, nurture and encourage Christians, and reform cultures.

Our Mission. 
- Introducing People to Jesus Christ
        Preaching and teaching God's Word through media and "in ways yet undreamed of"
- Nurturing and Encouraging Christians
        Assisting Christians and churches in spiritual growth and training them to transform the culture.
- Reforming Cultures
        Encouraging religious liberty by proclaiming the Lordship of Christ and America's Christian heritage, and lovingly applying principles to all cultures and spheres of life

Our Vision. 
D. James Kennedy Ministries seeks to communicate through media, and in “ways yet undreamed of,” the Gospel, the Lordship of Christ, and a biblically informed view of the world, so that “Jesus Christ be known throughout all of the Earth and that everywhere He might be trusted, loved, and served.”

Core Values:
- Communicating biblical truths with love and without compromise
- Transforming the culture for Christ
- Equipping and mobilizing the Church to engage the culture
- Upholding the standards of passion, excellence, and integrity in all we do


DJKM relies on the grace of God and the voluntary gifts of friends to support its media outreach. It adheres to the highest financial standards and is a member in good standing of the Evangelical Council for Financial Accountability.

Name: Hindu YUVA USA
Description:
The Hindu civilization has an unbroken chain of several thousands of years of history, culture, and heritage. The Hindu thought is a knowledge base which has been collected since the ancient Vedic period, and is timeless and applicable to all of humanity. Despite its huge following, rich history, and numerous contributions to the human race, the Hindu way of life, or Dharma, is perhaps the least understood among the major religions today. 

Hindu Dharma provides a beacon of hope in today’s challenging and dynamic world situation. The great Hindu monk Swami Vivekananda came to Chicago as a representative of Hinduism at the World Parliament of Religions on September 11th, 1893. His famous speech carries more meaning and weight than ever before.

Young Hindu Americans carry a great deal of responsibility to share this culture, preserve its meaning, and practice its universal values as the inheritors of this way of life. Hindu YUVA derives its inspiration from this ancient knowledge and understands the mammoth task lying in front of the young Hindu Americans. Hindu YUVA aims to provide a platform to preserve, practice, promote, and protect Hindu Dharma by bringing together Hindu college students and young professionals across the United States.

Name: Park Street Church
Description:
We are a community of believers who, for over two centuries, have organized our church life around the Christian gospel. We believe the good news that God loves all of us and has a plan to save us from our sins, making us whole persons through faith in his son, Jesus Christ. Our purpose is to be a people of God who glorify him and do his work in Boston and around the world. We take seriously Christ’s commandment to make disciples and the imperative to express his love through works of service. To that end, we are involved in a number of ministries, including Arts, Christian Education, Disabilities (Enable Boston), Families, Internationals (FOCUS), Medical (Boston Healthcare Fellowship), College and University students, Young Adults, and others. Additionally, we devote significant time, prayer and resources to global outreach. We support staff and associate missionaries, short-term group trips, and global professionals.

Join us for worship on Sundays at 8:30 and 11:00 AM or 4:00 PM or anytime at www.parkstreet.org.

Name: Society of Adventist Communicators
Description:
The Society of Adventist Communicators is an organization for the socialization, continuing education, recognition, and nurture of Seventh-day Adventists whose careers are in fields of communication.

This professional development organization began in 1988 as the Southern Society of Adventist Communicators (SSAC). After 11 years, a vote was cast in 1999 to invite all Adventist communicators in the United States - thus the Society of Adventist Communicators (SAC) was born. “By expanding the organization to communicators across the nation, it has provided resources which has enabled SAC to grow and also meet the needs of the Adventist communicators who are employed by non-Adventist organizations,” said Olson Perry, former treasurer/secretary of SAC and former communication director of the Southern Union Conference. “This has also raised the level of fellowship and training among our members,” he added. The first national convention was held in 2000 at the Seventh-day Adventist World Headquarters building in Silver Spring, Maryland.

Name: Eaton Hope Center
Description: EHC is a church in the process of a relaunch. More info coming soon. We also have a campus in Greenville, called Greenville Worship Center, and are expecting soon to launch Ohio Life In Focus, a community project designed to offer court ordered classes in Anger Management, alcohol and chemical addiction / abuse, and more.

Name: BUDDHISM
Description: Meditation is a major corner stone to the Diamond Way Buddhist practice. Our meditation brings a calmness and new awareness of oneself although this is when our practice of working with mind is just beginning. Through our meditation we work on developing additional qualities such as wisdom and compassion for all sentient beings. It is this which gives us a true inner happiness and with regular meditation these positive personal developments become part of our everyday lives.

Name: The C3 Church
Description: The C3 Church is an evangelical, charismatic Christian church based in the UK. With locations in Bury St Edmunds, Cambridge, Colchester and online!

Name: Andrew Osakwe Ministries International
Description:
Andrew Osakwe Ministries International (AOMI) is a global ministry designed for spreading the message of the new covenant and God's kingdom across Nigeria, Africa and around the globe, using every available media, and in every known language. It was founded by Dr. Andy Osakwe with the aim of mobilizing the body of Christ to rise up to its full stature, take over the "kingdoms of this world" and prepare itself for the return of our Lord Jesus Christ.

Our vision is to see the body of Christ fully awakened to the realities of the new covenant and God’s kingdom, operating in its full stature, thus fulfilling its purpose of discipling the nations of the earth.

Our mission is to reach the world with the gospel of the kingdom and perfecting the saints for the work of the ministry in preparation for Christ’s return.

The main goal of AOMI is to achieve a global coverage with its programmes, events and publications, thereby saturate the world with the Gospel of the Kingdom, so that the knowledge of God’s glory will cover the whole earth as the waters cover the sea (Habakkuk 2:14). By doing so, people will get saved, discover who they really are in Christ and be empowered to live victorious Christian live

Name: Moravian Theological Seminary
Description:
Moravian Theological Seminary offers graduate and continuing education programs that equip persons with the knowledge, skills, and being to engage in vocations in ministry, chaplaincy, counseling, spiritual direction and other settings. Rooted in the Moravian heritage of ecumenism and service, we welcome students preparing to serve in diverse socioeconomic, intercultural, and interfaith contexts.

Each of our Degree and Certificate Programs, from a Master of Arts in Chaplaincy to a Certificate in Spiritual Direction, provide you with a strong foundation for growth and success in a range of vocations and ministries—as a counselor, church leader, chaplain, community organizer, and more.

Whether pursuing a Master of Divinity or a Master of Arts in Clinical Counseling, our small classes led by dedicated faculty engage you in deep examination and reflection. And our Core Curriculum, courses taken by all degree students, ensures that your Moravian Theological Seminary education  includes a diversity of perspectives, enriches conversations throughout each program, and supports the spirit of community Moravian Seminary is famous for.

Name: Episcopal Diocese Of Virginia
Description:
Diocese Of Virginia Episcopal is a religious institution based out of 110 W Franklin St, Richmond, Virginia, United States.

The Diocese of Virginia was organized in May 1785 and was one of the nine dioceses represented at the first General Convention in September 1785 which brought together the Protestant Episcopal Church in the United States of America. Today, the Diocese of Virginia is the largest in the Episcopal Church and includes 38 counties in central and northern Virginia.

Name: Birmingham Metro Baptist Association
Description:
Birmingham Metro Baptist Association is a voluntary cooperative effort of 180 local churches, who lead mission and service opportunities in response to recognized needs in the community. Churches working together can go far beyond what any individual church could do alone. 

The mission of carrying the gospel of Jesus Christ throughout our community is as important today as it was when our local churches first gathered in 1833 as the Canaan Association of United Baptists. These first churches came together from other associations that did not believe in supporting missionaries. Many of our current churches were started through mission work of BMBA.

BMBA provides year-round opportunities for church members to bear a Christian witness to their neighbors through block parties and outreach, to those in need through feeding and housing, and to the ends of the earth through mission trips and disaster relief work.

Member churches can receive support through leadership training, legal and financial consultation, church planting and planning of group events.

Let’s celebrate our history and look to our future with excitement as we watch how God works through our local association of churches.

Name: Cornerstone Church
Description:
Cornerstone Church is a mid-sized contemporary United Methodist Church in Auburn Alabama.

Cornerstone Church is made up of ordinary people from all walks of life who understand they need to continually rely on God. We aren’t perfect, but we seek a God who cares and desires to be active in our daily life. We believe church is for everyone; it should be fun, exciting, powerful and relevant.

The story of Cornerstone United Methodist Church is short, yet filled with great accomplishments occurring at a pace that can only be attributed to God and the people He has gathered to serve together in this young church.

Cornerstone came to life in 1999 when Rusty Hutson and his family moved to Auburn to plant a church for the Alabama-West Florida Conference of the United Methodist Church. The first meeting was held at the Auburn Wesley Foundation with a small group of 35 who learned that the church would be named Cornerstone United Methodist Church. They began with weekly Wednesday night meetings to plan, pray and grasp God’s vision

Name: Nova Igreja
Description:
Somos uma igreja contemporânea, descontraída, desprendida de tradições, mas muito comprometida com a Palavra de Deus porque temos consciência do quanto fomos perdoados e somos amados por Ele. 

Na Nova, revelamos Jesus ao mundo de uma maneira alegre, acolhedora e libertadora.

Andamos com base na nova identidade que recebemos de Deus, nos deixando moldar por Ele de dentro para fora, sem acusação ou pressão para seguirmos algum padrão criado por pessoas. Vivemos sempre confiantes na bondade do nosso Pai, sabendo que ela não depende de performance ou mérito humano. 

Nossa crença e jeito de ser estão claros em nossas pregações, nas músicas e artes em geral, nos nossos ministérios, eventos, programas e relacionamentos. Nós trabalhamos para que o Reino de Deus seja cada vez mais expandido e é por isso que usamos todos os recursos que o século XXI nos oferece.

Name: The Episcopal Church of the Ascension
Description:
Ascension began as the vision of the vestry of St. John's Episcopal Church, now the Cathedral of the Diocese of East Tennessee, to plant a mission church in rural West Knoxville. West Knoxville Fellowship was formed and its members began evening meetings in parishioners' homes. Through the gifts of land by the Van Deventer family and a hugely successful member canvass of our founding families, the Church of the Ascension was granted full parish status in 1957 with a congregation numbering around 225.

The church prospered and grew as people moved west from Downtown Knoxville. In 1986 a major addition to the nave was completed increasing its seating capacity to 650 people. The Rev. David M. Potts was called to be the first rector in 1957. Over the ensuing years, we were served by 5 other rectors, 17 assisting clergy and our church has grown to well over 1000 members.

The hallmark of Ascension, both past and present, is strong lay leadership, a highly engaged congregation, a high level of giving and a talented, dedicated staff. Throughout its history Ascension has thrived and pressed forward in its mission: to enable ourselves and others to experience God in Christ, to seek and serve Him in others, to grow towards Christ and to proclaim Him to the world.

Name: Govardhan Math
Description:
The GovardhanaPeetha was established in 486 BC in the Karthika month of the Hindu Calendar. Sri NischalandaSaraswatiMaharaj is the 145th Shankaracharya of this peetha. Adi Shankaracharya initiated a practice that the head pontiffs of each of the four peethas would be considered his image. The lineage of the Shankaracharyas of the four peethas has continued incessantly so far. The role of Shankaracharyas of the four peethas in protecting and growing the Hindu sanatana dharma is very significant. Even today the four peethas of Shankaracharyas is glorifying the unity of the nation. Adi Shankaracharya was a famous and worldwide spokesperson of sanatana dharma. By providing an existential platform for vedic culture he placed a strong foundation for ages to come. The sanatana dharma is eternal and is auspicious for the whole world.

 

In keeping with the traditions of Adi Shankaracharya, the pontiff of Puri, JagadguruShankaracharya Sri NishchalandaSaraswatiMaharaj, being merciful to everyone, has started institutions such as Dharma SanghaParishad, Aditya Vahini and AnandVahini to protect the country, its values and integrity. Under the aegis of these institutions the supreme sanatana dharma is being propagated and transmitted all the time.

Name: Westchester Reform Temple
Description:
Welcome to Westchester Reform Temple.
Your home. You’re home.

Among the more than 900 Westchester Reform Temple (WRT) member households, you’ll find the embodiment of Reform Jewish diversity in America today. Within our ever-evolving Reform traditions, we aim to satisfy all appetites for Jewish soul food—both spiritual and physical—through meaningful expressions of prayer, song, ritual, study, and community involvement.

We strive to realize this through our five pillars:
Avodah → Spirituality
Chavurah → Community
Talmud Torah → Learning
Tikkun Olam → Repairing the World
Clal Yisrael → The Jewish People

Our History
Founded in 1953, in 1954 world famous architect Marcel Breuer designed WRT’s original building, with a sanctuary in the shape of the Star of David. Continuing in the tradition of creating meaningful spaces for worship and learning, WRT completed a renovation in 2008 to honor the past and look to the future of our community: creating a new “green” sanctuary and social hall, and transforming the original sanctuary spaces into religious school classrooms and a Beit Midrash—a place of study.

Our Synagogue
To meet the needs of our diverse, multigenerational community, WRT’s architecture and landscape provide spaces for contemplation, study, and celebration, creating a welcoming and inclusive atmosphere to those with special needs and a congregation with varied expressions of Jewish identity.

Designed with warm materials and natural light, the sanctuary creates a worship space that is sacred and intimate for a wide variety of services and life-cycle events. Just outside its doors is the Center for Jewish Life (CJL), an additional space for worship, gatherings, catered events, and educational and social activities.

Name: Our Lady of Mount Carmel Parish
المجلس العلمي الأعلى
Description:
بموجب الظهير الشريف رقم 1.80.270 المؤرخ في 3 جمادى الأخر 1401(8 ابريل 1981) تم إحداث المجلس العلمي الأعلى، ونص الفصل الاول من الظهير المذكور على أنه يتولى جلالة ملك المغرب أمير المؤمنين رئاسته. 

يتألف المجلس العلمي الأعلى من أعضاءه السادة العلماء، ويقوم بمهمة كاتب عام لهذا المجلس السيد محمد يسف الذي تم تعيينه بتاريخ 26 مارس 2000.

وقد حدد الظهير الشريف رقم 1.03.300 المؤرخ في 2 ربيع الأول 1435(22 أبريل 2004) المهام المناطة بالمجلس العلمي الأعلى، فيما يلي:  

دراسة القضايا التي تعرضها عليه جلالتنا الشريفة (أمير المؤمنين جلالة الملك)؛
إعداد برنامج عمل سنوي يتضمن الأنشطة المقترح القيام بها من قبل المجالس العلمية المحلية؛
الإشراف على عمل المجالس العلمية المحلية وتنسيق أنشطتها؛
إصدار التوجيهات والتوصيات الرامية إلى ترشيد عمل المجالس العلمية المحلية وتفعيل دورها في تأطير الحياة الدينية للمواطنين والمواطنات المغاربة من المسلمين؛
إعداد النظام الداخلي للهيئة العلمية المكلفة بالإفتاء والمصادقة عليه؛
إحالة طلبات الإفتاء في القضايا المعروضة عليه إلى الهيئة المكلفة بالإفتاء قصد دراستها وإصدار فتاوى في شأنها؛
إقامة علاقات تعاون علمي مع الهيئات والمنظمات الإسلامية ذات الاهتمام المشترك على الصعيد الوطني والدولي.

Name: Conferencia del Episcopado Mexicano A. R.
Description:
Jesús fundó su Iglesia dotándola de una estructura, eligiendo a los Doce Apóstoles con Pedro como su Cabeza (cfr. Mc 3,14-16). Los Obispos, sucesores de los Apóstoles, constituyen un Colegio, cuya cabeza es el Papa, sucesor de san Pedro[1]. Este Colegio ejerce sus funciones de muchas maneras, una de ellas es a través de las Conferencias Episcopales que existen en las distintas naciones[2].

La Conferencia Episcopal, institución de carácter permanente, es la asamblea de los Obispos de una nación o territorio determinado, que ejercen unidos algunas funciones pastorales respecto de los fieles de su territorio, para promover el mayor bien que la Iglesia proporciona a toda la gente[3]. Su principal órgano ejecutivo es el Consejo de Presidencia.

La Conferencia del Episcopado Mexicano (CEM) es el organismo colegial de los Obispos mexicanos que, sin disminuir la responsabilidad de cada obispo en el ámbito de su propia diócesis, favorece la promoción y la tutela de la fe y las costumbres, la traducción de los libros litúrgicos, la promoción y la formación de las vocaciones sacerdotales, la elaboración de materiales para la catequesis, la promoción y la tutela de las universidades católicas y de otras instituciones educativas, el compromiso ecuménico, las relaciones con las autoridades civiles, la defensa de la vida humana, de la paz, de los derechos humanos, la promoción de la justicia social y el uso de los medios de comunicación social para la evangelización[4].

[1] Cfr. Código de Derecho Canónico, c. 330.
[2] Ibíd., c. 336.
[3] Ibíd., c. 447.
[4] Estatutos, arts. 1 y 2.
Location: Del. Gustavo A. Madero, Ciudad de México

Description:
English and Polish version

Fundation "Bread of Life" is a nonprofit, non-denominational Christian organization based in Długa Goślina near Poznan with offices in Poznan and Kalisz. It runs without interruption since 2001 and is based on the teachings of Scripture and Christian ethics.

Our mission is to assist, support and activate people who, for various reasons, are on the fringes of society and because of their life situation are unable to return to the society and normal life. 

Currently we operate in Poznań, Kalisz and Długa Goślina, with plans to expand  to other cities.

Bread of Life is a non-profit organzation and its activities are made possible by support from companies, institutions and people of goodwill.

Name: Congregation Beth Israel, Austin
Description:
AT CONGREGATION BETH ISRAEL WE ARE...

JOYFUL
Shabbat services at CBI are full of joyful melodies, the beautiful voice of our Cantorial Soloist, vibrant sounds of acoustic guitar and piano.

The Child Development Center & Preschool at Congregation Beth Israel (cdc@cbi) is now one of the most sought after & highly accredited early childhood programs in Austin. Each Friday, our halls are filled with little voices welcoming Shabbat with song and the sweet smells of fresh-baked Challah. 

DIVERSE
We have a history of making a difference. Not only were we the first Jewish congregation in Austin, we were the first synagogue in Austin with a female rabbi, with a female cantor, with a gay rabbi, and with a gay cantor.

One of the newest and most active groups in our community is our LGBTQ+ group, Ga'avah (Hebrew for "pride"). 
We envision a safe environment where LGBTQ people, inclusive of all our intersecting identities, are fully liberated within our congregation.

We pioneered the Austin Jewish Community's MLK Social Justice Weekend (2016) and more recently have started Chavuraction Circles at CBI. The goal of the Chavruaction Circles is to build an anti-racist culture in our congregation by gathering for anti racist learning and action rooted in our shared Jewish values.

INCLUSIVE
We are intentional in our efforts to make CBI a joyful places for all worshippers. Open Door Shabbat, our sensory friendly, "come as you are", music and movement driven service, is for people with ALL abilities to come and share their Ruach (Hebrew for "spirit") and joy.

Interfaith families are embraced here! About 1/3 of our members identify as being part of an interfaith household. We welcome your questions about Judaism and offer casual programming where new Jews, lifelong Jews, Jews by association, and Jewish adjacent people can all choose to participate at level that is comfortable to them.

Name: New Life Church (Everett, WA)
Description:
New Life Church is part of a larger family of churches called "Foursquare." Foursquare is a Pentecostal denomination that resulted from the dynamic evangelistic ministry of the founder, Aimee Semple McPherson. In 1915, God called her to “preach the Word.” Aimee began traveling, holding crusades in the Eastern United States where many people were saved and healed. She was a powerful communicator and God used her in mighty ways. The first Foursquare church was opened on January 1, 1923. 

Aimee traveled and preached the Foursquare Gospel, which teaches: Jesus Christ The Savior, The Baptizer with The Holy Spirit, The Healer, and The Soon and Coming King. As Aimee continued to train and send out preachers and missionaries, the need for a connection and organization became apparent. Today the International Church of the Foursquare Gospel spans the world, with more than 1,700 churches in the United States and more than 66,000 churches globally in 140 countries.

On July 11, 1940, Aimee Semple McPherson held a crusade at the Everett Civic Auditorium to a packed crowd. As a result of this gathering, Everett Foursquare Church was planted. The church met in a small building on 25th & Wetmore in North Everett. In 1949, Maynard Haaland became Senior Pastor of the Everett Foursquare Church. He served faithfully as Senior Pastor for over 20 years.

In 1964 the newly named “New Life Center,” purchased and renovated the abandoned Roosevelt School on Madison & Highland. Under the leadership of Pastors – Tom Ferguson, Randy Remington, and Nate Poetzl, the church experienced explosive growth and dynamic ministry to the community and the world.

In 2011, Jim Romack and family came to serve as Lead Pastors of “New Life,” where Jim as a young man had served on staff as the Youth Pastor.

In New Life’s 70+ years of history, thousands have come to know Jesus as their Lord and Savior, several churches have been planted, and global outreach has touched multiple countries.

Name: Sisters of the Holy Family of Nazareth - Holy Family Province
Description:
We are an international congregation of vowed religious women dedicated to spreading the Kingdom of God’s love, particularly within families. 

Today, there are some 1,000 sisters serving in 16 countries: Australia, Belarus, Croatia, Chile, France, Ghana, Great Britain, Italy, Israel, Kazakhstan, Philippines, Poland, Russia, Spain, Ukraine, USA; they are joined by over 800 lay associates.

Holy Family Province, the U.S. province of the Sisters of the Holy Family of Nazareth, has some 165 sisters serving in Connecticut, Illinois, New York, Ohio, Pennsylvania, and Texas. The Provincialate is located in Des Plaines, IL, a Chicago suburb. We share in Jesus’ mission of spreading the Kingdom of God’s love. Name: Highlands Community Church
Description:
As part of an effort to reach children in this community, and the vision of a widow woman to start a Bible camp for children in the area, God led Wally and Inez Wilson to Renton and to eventually start what is now Highlands Community Church. Today, over 70 years later, God continues to use Highlands to help people find and follow Jesus.   

Highlands Community Church had its humble beginnings in 1943 when local Protestant churches sought to provide services for 10,000 Boeing Corporation employees living in a temporary war housing project. In 1946, six people met with a young pastor who had served three summers at a Bible camp ministering to the children of the temporary community. That young pastor, Wallace Wilson, was asked to establish a permanent church in the area. On the third Sunday of September of that year, they gathered for their first service at the Renton Highlands Housing Administration building. 

The Lord continued to bless the congregation. Pastor Jim Amandus was confirmed to be Pastor Wilson's successor by the Elder Board and congregation, and faithfully served as Lead Pastor beginning in January of 1989. Over the years, the congregation grew significantly in numbers as well as in community and worldwide impact.

In late 2009, Highlands became one church meeting in two locations, Renton and Kent. In 2018, Pastor Jim retired after nearly 30 years of service, and Jesse Campbell became our new Lead Pastor. We have been blessed by the continued faithfulness of God and are seeing incredible growth and excitement around the ministries of Highlands. By faith, we believe that Highlands' greatest days of kingdom impact lie ahead, as we bring the hope and healing of the gospel to our community and across the globe!

Name: Fraternità Cristiana Opera Padre Marella
Description:
Fraternità Cristiana Opera di Padre Marella Città dei Ragazzi è un Ente ecclesiastico civilmente riconosciuto; ha la forma giuridica di Associazione riconosciuta, iscritta nel luglio 2001 nel Registro delle Persone Giuridiche presso la Prefettura di Bologna.

Nel febbraio 2024 l’Associazione ha iscritto un Ramo E.T.S. al Registro Unico Nazionale del Terzo Settore (‘RUNTS’), per partecipare ai benefici ed allo spirito della Riforma del Terzo Settore di cui al D. Lgs. n. 117 / 2017 e succ. mod.

Accogliere, rispettare e ascoltare i più fragili. Farsi voce di chi non ha voce. Rifiutare e denunciare qualsiasi ingiustizia e discriminazione. Questa è la nostra vocazione. 

L’impegno sociale dell’Opera è possibile grazie ai nostri numerosi e instancabili volontari, che operano nelle province di Bologna e di Ravenna. Gestiamo e conduciamo centri di accoglienza, case-famiglia e comunità terapeutiche per rispondere alle diverse tipologie di esclusione sociale presenti sul nostro territorio.

Oggi, nelle diverse case dell’Opera, accogliamo e ospitiamo più di 200 persone, che assistiamo e supportiamo con costanza, accompagnandole nel reinserimento sociale. Oltre a queste, grazie ai nostri volontari, offriamo quotidianamente altre forme di assistenza a circa 150 persone: pasti presso le nostre strutture, vestiti, borse spesa, aiuti economici. 

Le comunità dell’Opera Padre Marella, che comprendono 7 strutture di accoglienza e 2 case famiglia, rappresentano un rifugio di solidarietà e sostegno per chi vive situazioni di fragilità.

Ogni comunità è un luogo dove si sperimenta la condivisione e la cura reciproca, in linea con i valori trasmessi da Padre Marella e Padre Gabriele Digani.

Attraverso il lavoro di volontari e operatori, offriamo un ambiente accogliente, capace di rispondere ai bisogni materiali e spirituali di chi si trova in difficoltà, favorendo il recupero della dignità e della speranza.

Description: Temple Beth Shalom is a warm, vibrant and diverse community of individuals and families. Through worship, celebration, lifelong learning and service to others, we seek to inspire spirituality, nurture personal growth, promote social justice, deepen Jewish experiences and enrich the life of each member.

Name: Federation of Jewish Communities in the Czech Republic
Description:
The Federation of Jewish Communities in the Czech Republic (FJCCR) is a Czech Jewish umbrella organization representing Jewish Communities and other independent Jewish institutions across the Czech Republic, in Israel and throughout the world. 
The FJCCR is a member of the European Jewish Congress and the World Jewish Congress.  FJCCR maintains a long term partnership with the American Jewish Joint Distribution Committee. 
FJCCR coordinates the activities of the Jewish communities’ especially in religious, social, educational and cultural aspects. FJCCR works with Czech government institutions, state administration, local authorities, or other Czech institutions. FJCCR also functions and cooperates with foreign institutions and organizations.
FJCCR actively monitors the environment against all manifestations of anti-Semitism, racism, xenophobia, fascism or any other discrimination. Federation protects the memory of Shoah victims. FJCCR preserves and maintains the Jewish monuments in the Czech Republic, unless they do not belong to the local Jewish Communities or supported entities.

Name: LEADERSHIP TRANSFORMATIONS
Description:

Transformation takes place over time, but the internal condition of the leaders' soul must be right. The greatest transformation comes during times when we quiet ourselves long enough to listen attentively to God, to one another and to what’s stirring in our own hearts. 

At LTI, we seek to increase the "attentiveness quotient" within individuals and teams — helping them to become more aware of God's presence and direction as he forms them more into the likeness of His son Jesus, gives them discernment for the path ahead and renews their strength as only he can. In all that we offer, our mission is to cultivate vibrant spirituality and attentive discernment in Christian leaders and their teams.

- Pathways: A weekly e-newsletter, full of resources and practices for the care of your soul
- Podcast: A new episode of The Discerning Leader podcast airs each week 
- Resources:  Formation resources, books, devotionals and retreat guides, found at www.spiritualformationstore.com
- Soul Care Retreats: Online and in-person retreats that bring renewal
- Spiritual Formation Workshops: Online and in-person teaching to equip leaders around various formation topics
- One on one: Mentoring, coaching, Sabbatical planning, team discernment, and spiritual direction
- Assessments: Church Health Assessment Tool, Team Health Assessment and Enneagram 
- Abide: A 1-year certificate in spiritual formation
- Emmaus: A 2-year certificate program in formational leadership

We have experience of equipping churches, planters and pioneers to multiply. We’re continually adding to our knowledge from practitioners so we can share best practice and training, underpinned by theological resources.

Our resources equip leaders as they develop and lead growing churches. We offer learning, coaching and consultancy with some of the most experienced people in church growth, helping churches to connect with their tradition of growth and reframe this for a contemporary context.

Name: STIRRED-UP INC.
Description:
Stirred Up is a non-profit,  organization working with adolescent boys and girls from underprivileged backgrounds in inner-city Atlanta.  Through the personal involvement of the staff of Stirred Up, these adolescents receive a foundation of hope through God's family life, educational assistance to help rescue them from the corruption of their environment and mentoring in the development of a God-centered relationship through sports or other areas where they can excel.  We see lives transformed as we bring hope to these children who lack opportunities to reach beyond their circumstances.

Within each and every child is one primary need—to be loved.  Inner-city youth so desperately needs to be loved.  They will never develop a sense of self-worth, will never explore the possibilities that the future might hold for them unless they know that someone loves and supports them.  The forces threatening to extinguish the flames of hope in these young lives include single parent households, lack of healthy male role models, poverty, teenage and out-of-wedlock pregnancies, alcohol and drug use and high drop-out rates within the school system.  With the odds stacked against them, they have little reason to look to the future with much hope.  Stirred Up mission is to walk with these youth and to show them they are loved, they are worthy and have a reason to hope for the future.

Our mission at the UCC is to help UT students come to know Jesus, the Truth who sets us free. We want to help them grow as disciples through prayer and intellectual formation, so that they can help others to find freedom and life in Jesus and his Church. 

In a world that offers so many cheap and fleeting pleasures, we want to help our students encounter the beauty of God that every heart longs for. We want to offer the UT campus an encounter with God, whom St. Augustine called the “Beauty ever ancient, ever new.”

And in our deeply wounded and divided culture, we want our community to be a place where people encounter the goodness of God—through authentic friendships, selfless service to those on the margins of our society, and the kindness that welcomes everyone and invites them to join us in discovering the freedom of the sons and daughters of God.

Name: St Cyril's Coptic Orthodox Theological College
Description:
St Cyril's Theological College is an accredited member institution of the Sydney College of Divinity and strives to deliver the highest quality of Orthodox theological education in the Coptic tradition. We offer a full range of internationally recognised degrees at both undergraduate and postgraduate levels. Our courses are delivered by a dedicated, scholarly faculty, in frequent collaboration with leading scholars in Orthodox theology from around the world.

However, while we certainly strive for academic excellence at St Cyril’s, we also believe that theology is not purely about academia. Rather, good theology is impossible without a lived encounter with the living God. Theology is inseparable from spirituality, as Evagrius of Pontus famously wrote: “The one who prays is a theologian; the one who is a theologian, prays.” 

St Cyril of Alexandria, our patron saint, wrote early in the fifth century that "the meeting ground between God and man is Flesh and Christ." To truly study theology is to step onto this meeting ground, to encounter Christ Himself, God made flesh, in heart, mind, soul and body.

For this reason every member of our staff and faculty, every subject in our degrees, and our entire theological program, is directed to a singular aim: meeting the incarnate God. 

We strive to humbly realise this aim within a rigorous educational framework, in harmony with our modern Australian context, without ever losing sight of the spiritual dimension of authentic Orthodox theology and education.
