Name: The Center for Jewish Life at Beth David
Description:
There is no greater cause than making a positive impact on the lives of others; that is the mission of The Center for Jewish Life at Beth David. Central Miami’s growing population has put before us the task of creating a multi-use space to engage our community, educate our children, and provide an inclusive home for those who yearn for a place to belong and strengthen their faith.

Our vision will create new and better facilities for prayer, play, and events to better serve the community. By building it together, The Center for Jewish Life will be a spiritual, educational, and recreational space that will engage and strengthen our community for years to come. Together, we aspire to not only reach the campaign’s goal to build The Center for Jewish Life, but also to continue raising funds to create a sizable endowment to ensure future sustainability and growth.

Name: Fox Valley Christian Action
Description:
Our passion is to see the power of the Gospel transform lives of families living in our local public and subsidized housing communities.  We accomplish this through residential summer camp, school-year teen programming, and after school kids clubs.

Fox Valley Christian Action is non-profit organization governed by a board of directors. We are supported by faith through charitable giving. We are members of the Evangelical Council of Financial Accountability.

Name: Fullerton Free (EvFree Fullerton)
Description:
Empowered by the Holy Spirit, Fullerton Free is a loving community,
united in sacrifice, and living like Christ for the glory of God.

Name: Dhammakaya Foundation
Description: peace to inner peace for every people.

Name: Christians in Action Missions International
Description:
Christians in Action is an international, interdenominational, and trans-cultural evangelical missionary society with ministry in 21 countries over 5 continents. Its 300 ministry personnel have the goal of planting indigenous national churches by evangelism and discipleship.

OUR VALUES

Prayer
  - Jesus advised his disciples that the harvest was plentiful but that the laborers were few. He, therefore, instructed them to ask the Lord of the Harvest to send forth laborers into the harvest
  - Join with us as we daily pray for the Lord of the harvest to send forth laborers into His global harvest.

Evangelism
  - Evangelism is the Heartbeat of Missions
  - Jesus' final commandment was The Great Commission.

Outreach & Relief
  - CinA specializes in person-to-person witnessing, yet remains flexible concerning the use of specific outreach methods
  - By providing community support to impoverished people, we can open the door to effective evangelism & church planting.

Church Planting
  - The aim of our missionary outreach is to establish self-running, interdependent churches under national leadership
  - When churches are established, they provide a base for new believers to grow in Christ, find their own place of service, & then carry on the mission of evangelism.

Missionary Training
  - CinA offers a specialized training program designed to equip someone for effective missionary service
  - CinA offers a Missionary Candidate course for those who have already received training or ministry experience elsewhere
  - CinA encourages further education & training to enhance a missionary's ministry skills.

Preparing Nationals for Leadership
  - We emphasize the training of national leaders through personal discipling & using local existing training institutions.

Name: Bibles for America
Description: Giving away a Bible you can understand and Christian books

Name: Evangel
Description:
Evangel exists to build the Church in the Northern Great Plains Region through lives transformed by a Jesus-centered message, Spirit-empowered experiences, and authentic relationships before Jesus returns.

Evangel is one big family that meets every week to worship Jesus, reconnect over coffee, and enjoy life together. It’s important to celebrate the victories and hold each other up in the struggles, and that’s why a relationship with Jesus is best lived out in community!

Name: Diocese of Colorado Springs
Description: Created in January 1984, the Diocese of Colorado Springs serves a territory that spans 10 central and eastern counties of Colorado.  The Diocese serves more than 176,000 Catholics in 39 parishes and missions.  The Diocese and its parishes provide religious education and formation to more then 9,000 students. To keep up with the needs of the Diocese's growing population, its social outreach continues to expand.  Last year, Catholic Charities of Colorado Springs assisted over 240,000 individuals and families.

Name: Navigators UK
Description:
The Navigators are an organisation that helps people explore questions about life and faith from a Christian perspective.  Our motto is "Helping people to know Christ and to make him known".

We have been operating in the UK for over 50 years and are part of an international association working in over 100 countries around the world.

Name: Zoroastrian Youth of North America (ZYNA)
Description:
Zoroastrian Youth of North America (ZYNA) is a committee of FEZANA (Federation of Zoroastrian Associations of North America) dedicated to representing and empowering the Zoroastrian youth across North America. We foster youth involvement through religious education, leadership development, and by cultivating a strong social community.

ZYNA organizes meaningful events for our youth to engage in, including retreats, virtual webinars and leadership opportunities that promote both connection and learning within the Zoroastrian faith.

This LinkedIn page serves as your central hub for community news, event updates, and networking opportunities within both the North American Zoroastrian and the global Zoroastrian youth community.

Name: Episcopal Diocese of Atlanta
Description:
The Diocese of Atlanta is comprised of 120 Episcopal worshiping communities throughout Middle and North Georgia with some 50,000 members. We are the ninth largest of the 110 dioceses of The Episcopal Church. We are a Christian community blessed with vibrancy and variety.

Our diocesan bishop is the Rt. Rev. Robert C. Wright, who was ordained and consecrated on October 13, 2012. The Rt. Rev. Keith B. Whitmore has served as assistant bishop since April 2008. In 2014, The Rt. Rev. Don A. Wimberly joined the bishop visitation schedule for congregations in the Diocese of Atlanta.The Offices of the Bishop are at the Cathedral of St. Philip in Atlanta, Georgia.

We have more than 200 canonically resident clergy, a large number of non-parochial clergy and an abundant supply of lay ministers.

The Diocese of Atlanta was created in 1907 from the Diocese of Georgia. Our parishes thrive in the metropolitan areas of Atlanta, Columbus and Macon, as well as in smaller cities, towns and rural communities in the mountains of North Georgia and the lower elevations of Middle Georgia. The oldest church in the diocese is Christ Church in Macon, which was organized in 1825. The newest parish is St. Benedict's in Smyrna, established in 2008.

As part of The Episcopal Church, the Diocese of Atlanta is connected to the worldwide Anglican Communion of 70 million people in 38 provinces. We engage in special companion relationships with several dioceses, in Africa and and South America.

If you have any questions, please don't hesitate to drop us a note. Please write to communications@episcopalatlanta.org, and someone will get back to you promptly.

Name: St Paul's Theological College Malaysia
Description:
St Paul’s Theological College Malaysia (SPTC), is a young, vibrant theological college in the centre of Kuala Lumpur. We are an affiliate of St Mellitus College, one of the most exciting and innovative places to study theology and to train for ministry in the UK.

SPTC is dedicated to helping Christians explore their faith deeply, combining academic excellence, a focus on missional leadership and the flexibility to fit around busy lifestyles. We work within a spirit of generous orthodoxy, welcoming all kinds of Christians and denominations, working together to be effective in engaging in mission and evangelism.

VISIT OUR OPEN HOUSE!
Location: Kuala Lumpur, Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur

Name: Lakeview Church
Description: Joining God in the Renewal of All Things. Sundays 10:30 AM

Name: Alliance of Baptists
Description: The Alliance of Baptists began in 1987 as a prophetic voice in Baptist life. Today, we are a faith community comprised of male and female laity and clergy, people of diverse sexual orientations, gender identities, theological beliefs, and ministry practices. We are about 4,500 individual members and roughly 140 congregations knit together by love for one another and God, combining progressive inquiry, contemplative prayer and prophetic action to bring about justice and healing in a changing world.

Name: MAG+S
Description:
MAG+S es el lugar donde poder hacerte las preguntas más importantes de tu vida.

Nos une nuestra fe en Jesús, al que vemos como un modelo que traspasa los tiempos y las ideologías. En este mundo lleno de ofertas, nosotros elegimos el Evangelio. A contracorriente, nos mueve el modo de actuar de Jesús y aunque parezca una locura, seguimos soñando con un mundo mejor, más justo y más unido. Le seguimos al estilo ignaciano, “como un amigo habla a otro amigo”.

Bienvenido a un lugar donde poner con Dios tus deseos y propósitos y así aprender a elegir lo que más conduce a dar sentido a nuestra vida.

Name: Favor International
Description: Our purpose is to multiply and accelerate indigenous leaders transforming their nations. By any means resulting from prayer, we serve and train indigenous leaders who, through "discipleship factories," make disciples who make disciples of Jesus Christ. Together, we see God end wars and align communities, regions, and nations with the purposes of God in all aspects of life.

Name: Catholic Theological Society of America
Description:
The CTSA is the principal association of Catholic theologians in North America. With a membership of more than 1300, the CTSA is the largest professional society of theologians in the world. Active members of the Society hold the doctorate in theology or a related discipline. The purpose of the CTSA is to promote theological research in the Roman Catholic tradition that is attentive to contemporary problems faced by the Church and the world.

Since its founding in 1946, the CTSA meets at its annual convention where its members engage each other in dialogue by presenting their ongoing research and receiving the constructive criticism of their colleagues. Much of this research is published in the Proceedings of the Catholic Theological Society of America. The annual convention also enables the many CTSA members who are teachers to share their best pedagogical practices. At the convention Business Meeting, the attending members elect a future President of the Society and new representatives to the Board of Directors. Also, at each convention, the Society honors one of its members for a lifetime of distinguished theological achievement through the conferral of the John Courtney Murray Award, named for one of its early Board members who was a major theologian in the American Catholic Church.

Name: University Catholic Center at UCLA
Description: The University Catholic Center is a ministry of the Paulist Fathers serving at UCLA since 1919. For over 100 years, the University Catholic Center has served the spiritual needs of countless UCLA students, faculty and staff. Education happens both inside and outside the classroom, and the UCC is committed to helping you grow in all dimensions of your life. We want you to know that we are here to assist you in any way we can. This is your Catholic home at UCLA!

Name: Faber Institute
Description:
The Faber Institute is committed to “awakening souls” in an age when the existence of the soul, and of its particular capacities and formidable powers, is overlooked. We do this by putting people in touch with sources of depth and vision within themselves through which they gain a discerning capacity to recognize the highest good of persons, and its counterfeits.

The works of the Institute are the “works of soul”:
- teaching spiritual and theological doctrine in accessible ways which inspire personal conversion
- preaching the Gospel and opening the riches of the Scriptures
- giving the Spiritual Exercises of St. Ignatius through Retreats
- giving spiritual direction to individuals
- doing the priestly work of the Sacraments
- giving counsel to the doubtful
- creating bonds of trust and giving help to all Christian denominations
- teaching people how to deliberate well and to make good decisions in Christ
- and perhaps most of all, how to practice the art of friendship in the way that Christ Himself demonstrated in his earthly life as possible, and necessary, for us

Name: Cornerstone Christian Church of God
Description: Our vision is to bring restoration and transformation to all by teaching, preaching and demonstrating the gospel of Jesus Christ.

Name: Creation Today
Description: Creation Today (CT) is a leading Christian-apologetics ministry, defending the literal interpretation of the Genesis creation account from the theory of evolution. We desire to spread the Gospel of Jesus Christ through the science of God’s creation. Today, CT is an international ministry with some of the most requested resources on the topics of creation, apologetics and evangelism.

Name: Central Presbyterian Church
Description: Central Presbyterian Church is a religious institutions company based out of United States.

Name: Our Lady of Good Counsel Church and School- PLYMOUTH, MI
Description: Our vision is to offer every person in our community a life-changing encounter with Jesus.  Fr. John Riccardo is our pastor.  The OLGC School is K-8.

Name: 24-7 Prayer NL
Description:
Gebed versterkt mensen, verfrist kerken en verandert de maatschappij. Daarom leert 24-7 Prayer NL mensen God te ontmoeten, zodat biddende gemeenschappen ontstaan die Jezus zichtbaar maken in de maatschappij. 

Wat is 24-7 Prayer?

De 24-7 beweging is begonnen doordat een gebedsbijeenkomst van studenten zich plotseling verspreidde over meer dan 12.000 locaties en bijna iedere denominatie in de wereld: van de katholieke kerk in Oostenrijk tot het Leger des Heils in Australie en tot diverse vervolgde geloofsgemeenschappen in het Midden Oosten.

Vandaag de dag is 24-7 Prayer een familie van kerken & geloofsgemeenschappen maar we zijn ook een start-up dat innovatief gebedsmateriaal levert. Daarnaast is 24-7 Prayer een kweekvijver voor nieuwe projecten en NGO’s zoals 24-7 Ibiza, de 24-7 academy en Prayer Spaces in Schools.

Maar het hart van 24-7 is het gebed wat non-stop blijft doorgaan over de gehele wereld. Toen jij sliep vannacht ontmoette iemand ergens in de wereld Jezus in een 24-7 Prayer Room. Op het moment dat jij dit leest is iemand bezig met het zoeken naar manieren zijn gebeden op een praktische manier uit te leven onder de mensen die dat hard nodig hebben.

Voor meer informatie over ons verwijzen we je graag naar onze website.

Name: Metro Church DC
Description: God has uniquely wired Metro Church to live out the great commission (Mathew 28:18-20) by embracing three principles that govern the decisions we make as a church. Metro Church is a God-First church that embodies an Accepting Atmosphere with Relevant Environments.

Name: Living Water Community Church
Description: We desire to know Jesus as our Savior, Sanctifier, Healer, and Coming King and to make him known Here, There, Everywhere

Name: Seventh-day Adventist Church - Northern Peruvian Union
Description:
Seventh-day Adventists, with more than 17 million members worldwide, are a Protestant Christian church organized in 1863 in the United States. Its origin comes shortly after the movement led by William Miller that emphasized the need for a greater emphasis in preaching on the brief return of Jesus Christ to this world.

The headquarters of the Seventh-day Adventist Church in northern Peru is responsible for administrative coordination in 5 mission fields in the regions of Lima, Ancash, La Libertad, San Martín, Lambayeque, Cajamarca, Amazonas, Piura and Tumbes, with more of 250 thousand members.

Name: Cornerstone Christian Church of God
Description: Our vision is to bring restoration and transformation to all by teaching, preaching and demonstrating the gospel of Jesus Christ.

Name: Safinah Institute
Description: We are an organisation that is founded on Quranic principles. We serve in the spirit of excellence with integrity and compassion for our community, our nation and the world. We serve the lost and the broken with utmost dignity and respect.

Name: The Mustard Seed Street Church
Description:
The Mustard Seed Street Church has been essential in fighting hunger and restoring faith to a large portion of people living in poverty, as well as the working poor, in greater Victoria since 1975. The Church ministries are nearly 100 percent community funded and include Vancouver Island’s largest Food Bank, a Family Center, Hospitality programs, and addictions recovery at Hope Farm Healing Centre as well as more traditional Church services.


Our Vision:
    As followers of Christ, we envision a compassionate community where the cycles of poverty have been broken and lives restored.

Our Mission:
    Our mission is to build community and maintain relationships, providing essential and transformative services to those in need.

Our Values:

Christianity: knowing that love comes from Christ and redemption is the way of God’s Kingdom, we serve and lead as a response to that love, shared with our neighbours, a love that pervades everything that we do and are.

Integrity: knowing that trust is key in all relationships, we act ethically, lead with transparency and are accountable.

Collaboration: knowing that we are a part of a system of care for those we serve, we lead and partner with others to achieve our vision, honouring the work of others and bringing our best to those we serve with.

Empowerment: knowing that we are capable of transforming our community for the better, we support those we serve in doing their part, invite our staff and volunteers to create solutions, be adaptable and take action while respecting and inviting the capability of everyone.

Safety: knowing that we are entrusted with precious lives and for the health and wholeness of those we serve, we commit to prevent the occurrence or risk of injury, danger or loss for all in our community.

Name: Meatball and Falafel: The Podcast
Description: a

Name: Holy land Christian Ecumenical Foundation
Description:
HCEF is committed to the continued presence and well-being of Arab Christians in the Holy Land and to developing the bonds of solidarity between them and Christians elsewhere.

HCEF lives its mission by witnessing the teaching of our Savior Jesus Christ.

We educate people around the world about the presence of Arab Christians in the Holy Land and remind them of their moral obligation to the Mother Church.

We build community between Christians throughout the world and Arab Christians of the Holy Land through pilgrimages, publications, and communication.

We develop bonds of solidarity through church-to-church, family-to-family, and person-to-person partnership programs in order to bring Christians together in community for future cooperation.

We promote peace, justice, and reconciliation in the Holy Land by removing the causes of human suffering and replacing despair with hope, fear with security, and humiliation with dignity.

We undertake specific projects designed to advance better living conditions for Arab Christians in the Holy Land. Projects focus on creating employment, advancing education, building and renovating houses, and developing the healthy growth of children.

Name: Scott Dawson Evangelistic Association
Description: SDEA exists to share the Gospel and is home to a variety of events and ministry programs -- Strength to Stand, UNITE, Safe at Home, the STS Institute, and more.

Name: Hillcrest Christian School (Thousand Oaks)
Description: Hillcrest Christian School, located in beautiful Ventura County, Thousand Oaks, California, offers four distinct accredited programs: preschool, elementary school, middle school and high school. In addition we have an English Language Development program for foreign students, and our Hillcrest Academy  to support homeschooling families. Each of our schools is dedicated to serving the Christian community by providing high quality Christian education and training for the development of well-rounded students who will impact this world for the Lord Jesus Christ through Biblical thought and action.

Name: Hampton Roads Leadership Prayer Breakfast
Description: The Hampton Roads Leadership Prayer Breakfast is a nonprofit that has been gathering Christian leaders throughout Hampton Roads from every sphere of society to pray for God’s blessing, healing, protection, mercy, and wisdom for America for the past forty-five years.

Name: Lifeline Chaplaincy
Description: Our mission at Lifeline Chaplaincy is to provide compassionate support to the seriously ill, their families and caregivers, and to be an educational resource for crisis ministry. We provide care in Houston, Dallas, Fort Worth, Austin, Temple, and Waco hospitals.

Name: St Matthew Church & School
Description: K-8 catholic school and church

Name: Diocèse de Bayonne, Lescar et Oloron
Description:
Le diocèse de Bayonne, Lescar et Oloron est un diocèse de l'Église catholique en France. 
Il couvre l'ensemble du Pays-Basque et du Béarn (Pyrénées-Atlantiques, 64)
Mgr Marc Aillet en est l'évêque depuis 2008.


contact: communication@diocese64.org

Name: Archangel Michael Coptic Orthodox Church
Description: Archangel Michael Coptic Orthodox Church in Orange County is one of the original ten Coptic Orthodox Churches established in Southern California by the effort of His Grace Bishop Dioscorus. Archangel Michael Coptic Orthodox Church was first established in 1981.

Name: Nishmat - The Jeanie Schottenstein Center for Advanced Torah Study for Women
Description: Nishmat was founded in 1990 by Rabbanit Chana Henkin to open the gates of higher Torah learning to women. Now situated in its beautiful campus in Jerusalem's Pat neighborhood, south of Katamon, Nishmat has become a world center for women's scholarship, leadership, and social responsibility, and a world leader in paving a new path for women in Jewish life.  Ten years after its founding, Nishmat created the new religious role of Yoatzot Halacha, women halachic advisors, opening the way for women in religious leadership. At the same time, the school established what has become an acclaimed advancement program for Ethiopian-Israelis, coupling Jewish studies with an intensive program moving talented young Ethiopian women from the social margins to the mainstream of Israeli society.

Name: Church Of God In Christ
Description: The Church of God in Christ, Inc. (COGIC) is a Christian organization in the Holiness-Pentecostal tradition. It is the largest Pentecostal denomination in the United States. The membership is predominantly African-American with millions of adherents. The Church has congregations in 112 countries around the world.

Name: Salvation Army LA Metro
Description: The Salvation Army LA Metro Office is here to serve the community of Los Angeles through shelter, homeless services, family support, children's and youth centers, Spiritual care and so much more. We are champion's of Hope for All!

Name: KINGSWAY INTERNATIONAL CHRISTIAN CENTRE
Description: Kingsway International Christian Centre is a church where everyone can receive the love, acceptance, help, hope, forgiveness and encouragement of Jesus Christ.

Name: The Church of England Birmingham
Description:
Birmingham is the UK’s second city with the youngest urban population in one of the most lively and diverse regions. 
The Church of England Birmingham, founded in 1905, is one of the 41 diocese of the Church of England and covers nearly 300 square miles, including parishes in Birmingham and parts of Solihull, Sandwell, Warwickshire and Worcestershire. We have 187 churches and worship centres with an average Sunday
attendance of 17,000 from a population of 1.5 million. In these churches are ministers and churchgoers representing a wide scope of tradition, ethnicity, age and background.

Name: Grace Presbytery
Description:
Comprised of 133 congregations with approximately 25,000 members in 55 counties of North, Northeast, and Central Texas, Grace Presbytery is part of the Presbyterian Church (USA) and Synod of the Sun. The presbytery is responsible for the government of the church throughout its district, and for assisting and supporting the witness of congregations to the sovereign activity of God in the world, so that all congregations become communities of faith, hope, love, and witness.

Grace Presbytery claims God’s mission as our calling: to proclaim the gospel of Jesus Christ who saves, redeems, and reconciles the world to God through ministries of witness, justice, and mercy. Grace Presbytery prepares and supports leaders to build vital congregations that reach seekers and those in need, nurture disciples, and send apostles of our Lord into the world.

Name: The Roman Catholic Diocese of Baton Rouge
Description: The personnel of the administrative departments of the Diocese of Baton Rouge collaborate with the Bishop and his work of evangelizing, catechizing, leading worship and giving pastoral care to the people of the larger community, and support the parishes of the diocese in accomplishing their ministry.

Name: Diözese Regensburg
Description:
739 erhob der heilige Bonifatius Regensburg zum Bistum. Im Laufe der Geschichte des Bistums wirkten hier die Heiligen Wolfgang, Emmeram, Erhard und Albertus Magnus als Bischöfe. Weitere bedeutende Bischöfe waren Johann Michael Sailer, Georg Michael Wittmann, Michael Buchberger und der ehemalige Präfekt der Glaubenskongregation, Gerhard Kardinal Müller.

Seit dem 26. Januar 2013 ist Dr. Rudolf Voderholzer Bischof der Diözese.

Name: Embracing Unity
Description:
Equipping Christians to talk race, build church unity and put things right in our communities.  

We have a small group experience, that we are willing to share.

We have a Facebook page where like minded people can share their successes and challenges: https://www.facebook.com/groups/embracingunity 

And we have an online academy where individuals can work through material we've organized into modules that introduce you to God's plan for racial reconciliation.  While this material is best consumed by proceeding through the organized modules, individual essays and videos can be shared on social media.

Name: Campus für Christus
Description:
Wir tragen Gottes Liebe ganzheitlich in die unterschiedlichen Bereiche der Gesellschaft hinein.

Durch unsere nationalen und internationalen Arbeitsbereiche sowie City-Hubs schaffen wir Räume, in denen Menschen aus den unterschiedlichsten gesellschaftlichen Milieus und Nationen gemeinsam mit uns Gott erleben und besser kennenlernen können.

Wir inspirieren und befähigen Menschen, mit Jesus Christus in eine Beziehung zu treten und seinem Vorbild zu folgen. Dieser Lebensstil spiegelt Gottes Liebe in Wort und Tat wider.

Name: Römisch-Katholische Kirche im Aargau
Description:
Die Römisch-Katholische Kirche im Aargau – das sind rund 185'000 Katholikinnen und Katholiken, die sich in 92 Kirchgemeinden und verschiedenen Missionen der Anderssprachigen organisieren. Etwa 10'000 Freiwillige leisten jedes Jahr über 1.5 Millionen Stunden Freiwilligenarbeit. 

Die Römisch-Katholische Kirche im Kanton Aargau ist dual aufgestellt. Die Seelsorge wird durch die pastoral verantwortlichen Personen in den Pfarreien und in den verschiedenen Fachstellen geleistet. Als Landeskirche umfasst sie alle Römisch-Katholischen Kirchgemeinden des Kantons und ist eine selbständige Körperschaft des kantonalen öffentlichen Rechts mit Sitz in Aarau. Sie fördert und unterstützt die pastoralen Tätigkeiten der Römisch-Katholischen Kirche im Kanton Aargau, im Bistum Basel und in der ganzen Schweiz. Zudem unterstützt sie die Aargauer Kirchgemeinden bei der Erfüllung ihrer Aufgaben. Sie ist für die Mittelbeschaffung und den Einsatz der dafür nötigen Ressourcen verantwortlich. 

Die Römisch-Katholische Landeskirche führt eigene Fachstellen zur Unterstützung der Seelsorge in den Bereichen Bildung und Propstei, Diakonie, Jugend und junge Erwachsene, Katechese und Medien sowie Spezialseelsorge. Sie verantwortet die Seelsorge in kantonalen oder regionalen Institutionen mit und dient als Dienstleistungszentrum für die Kirchgemeinden. Darüber hinaus beheimatet sie die Anderssprachigenseelsorge (Missionen) und fördert damit die kulturelle Vielfalt und Integration.  Auf diese Weise sind wir täglich gemeinsam mit dem Bistum, den Pfarreien und den Kirchgemeinden für unsere Mitglieder und für die kirchennahen und kirchenfernen Menschen in unserer Gesellschaft unterwegs.

Name: Church at The Mill
Description: Church at The Mill, located in Spartanburg County, South Carolina, is one church currently meeting on three campuses. Each campus is led by a pastor who preaches live and joins a campus staff in shepherding and leading the members and guests of that location. Each Campus Pastor leads under the guidance of our Senior Pastor, who preaches weekly at our Central Campus.

Name: First Baptist Church of Lincoln Gardens
Description: How can we serve you?

Name: St. Mary Catholic Church
Description:
St. Mary Catholic Church is a Roman Catholic church in Hagerstown, MD, within the Archdiocese of Baltimore.

We are a Eucharist-centered community, inspired and guided by the Holy Spirit, nourished and strengthened by the sacramental life of the Church, who welcomes, accepts and celebrates our unity, as persons created in the image and likeness of God.

We proclaim the gospel of Jesus Christ, through our thoughts, words and actions, with faith, hope and charity.

As disciples of Jesus Christ, we faithfully follow the teachings of Pope Benedict XVI, and his successors, and the authority of the Church.

We respect the human dignity of all persons from conception to natural death.

We are called to evangelize, with our time, talent and treasure, serving all people, those in our Church community and those who have not yet experienced the Catholic faith.

Name: Heart Keys
Description:
At Heart Keys, we recognise the unique challenges and responsibilities that come with pastoral roles. As a Christian ministry, we are dedicated to providing Professional Pastoral Supervision and Prayer Ministry services to clergy, church workers, practitioners and Christians, offering them a supportive space to reflect, grow, and engage in intentional inner healing while uplifting those called to the scared space of Ministry.

Professional Pastoral Supervision: Nurturing Pastoral Excellence 
Our Pastoral Supervision services aim to create a structured and intentional space for clergy, church workers, and practitioners to reflect on their pastoral practice. Led by a skilled professional, our supervision sessions serve as a platform to debrief, plan, and strategise, allowing individuals to prioritise their well-being and ensure best practices in ministry.

Generally taking just an hour per month, our sessions focus on guiding individuals in the pastoral space to navigate their roles with wisdom, resilience, and spiritual insight. We believe that intentional reflection fosters personal and professional growth, creating a solid foundation for effective ministry.

Heart Keys Prayer Ministry: Inner Healing in Union with the Trinity 
Heart Keys Prayer Ministry is dedicated to helping Christians navigate the inner healing space in union with the Trinity (Father, Son & Holy Spirit). Rooted in scriptural principles, our prayer ministry is pastoral in nature, facilitated through active listening, listening prayer, and the gifts of the Spirit.

At Heart Keys, we believe in the transformative power of pastoral supervision and prayer ministry, and we are committed to companioning alongside individuals on their journey for healing, growth, and the advancement of the Kingdom.

Join us as we unlock the doors to spiritual renewal, healing and restorative love.

Name: Minnesota Hillel
Description: To inspire every student to make an enduring commitment to Jewish life, learning, and Israel.

Name: Celestial Church of Christ Worldwide
Description: Celestial Church of Christ Worldwide came into the world through divine inspiration from heaven by DIVINE ORDER on the 29th of September 1947 in PORTO NOVO, REPUBLIC OF BENIN (formerly REPUBLIC OF DAHOMEY) through a single individual, a man who is the founder of the Church, the Reverend Pastor Prophet Founder SAMUEL BILEHOU JOSEPH OSHOFFA. The Church has its National (Diocesan) Headquarter-, at 12/15 Church Street, Makoko, Yaba, Lagos state, the Supreme Headquarters in Porto Novo, Republic of Benin and with International Headquarters at Mission House, Ketu, Lagos state, Nigeria.

www.goodnews.or.kr
www.biblecrusade.com

Name: Christ Church At Grove Farm
Description: Christ Church At Grove Farm is a religious institutions company based out of 249 Duff Rd, Sewickley, Pennsylvania, United States.

Name: Annunciation Catholic Church
Description: Annunciation Catholic Church is a Catholic Parish in the Diocese of Orlando.

Name: Midway Church
Description:
Since July, 1847, Midway has been home to thousands of people in the West Georgia region: people of every race, age, economic background, and stage of life. Midway is experiencing its greatest days since its inception. Under God’s watchful eye and Pastor Todd’s leadership, Midway has grown from 200 in weekly Worship to over 2,000 today. 

Midway has seen many changes over the years, which include a couple of name changes. Midway was founded under the name of The Baptist Church of Christ at Macedonia. Today Midway Macedonia Baptist Church of Carroll County, Ga., Inc. is simply known as Midway Church or “Midway”.

Burdened by the statistics that 85% of our surrounding community is not attending church and only 4% of those under the age of 28 claims to know Christ, Midway was driven to turn these stats around.  In recent years, Midway has aggressively positioned itself to target these groups, organizing itself to be strategically outward-focused. 

Midway today is a vibrant, racially diverse congregation of over 3,200 believers that follows an Acts 1:8 Strategy to be “on-mission” in our Jerusalem, Judea, Samaria and Ends of the Earth. We weekly experience life change through decisions for Christ and baptisms, through serving with one of our local ministries, or through serving with our mission partners around the world.

Midway is committed to being a church that is outwardly focused and welcoming to all, regardless of their background. Pastor Todd refers to us as “a hospital for sinners, not a hotel for saints.” We desire to meet people right where they are, following the example set by Christ. Our mission as God’s church will never change – He has instructed us to “GO” and tell the world about Him and then teach new believers how to grow in Him.

Description: SEMINARIO REFORMADO is a religious institutions company based out of PO BOX 741034, Orange City, Florida, United States.

Description: The Crossing Church is led by Pastor Greg Dumas with multiple campuses in the Tampa area. Our vision is to see a church intensely Kingdom focused, compassionately loving people, fervently praying, and relentlessly pursuing God's presence.

Name: Roman Catholic Diocese of Helena
Description: The Roman Catholic Diocese of Helena was established on March 7, 1884, and initially included the entire state of Montana. Its present boundaries were established on May 18, 1904, when the Diocese of Great Falls (now Great Falls-Billings) was formed from its territory. The diocese covers 51,922 square miles of western and north central Montana, encompassing 21 counties and parts of two others. The diocese’s 57 parishes and 38 missions are structured into six deaneries: Bozeman, Butte, Conrad, Helena, Kalispell, and Missoula. Its diocesan church is the Cathedral of St. Helena, which was dedicated in 1914 and is located in Helena.

Name: Faith Matters
Description: Exploring an expansive view of the Restored Gospel.

Name: Exodus Place
Description:
Exodus Place is a facility for men coming out of difficult life situations. Our objective is to help restore the lives of men that have been faced with almost hopeless situations. We provide affordable housing, offer mentoring, counseling, encouragement, assistance with employment resources, Bible studies and fellowships. We believe in offering a hand up, not a hand out.

Follow us on:

Facebook at -
http://www.Facebook.com/ExodusPlace

YouTube at - 
http://www.youtube.com/user/ExodusPlace?feature=mhum

Description: Adas Israel Congregation is a religious institutions company based out of 2850 Quebec St NW, Washington, null, United States.

Name: Missouri Conference of The United Methodist Church
Description:
The United Methodist Church in Missouri is made up of approximately 600 local churches, approximately 1,000 retired and active clergy and more than 65,000 weekly worshippers. Learn more about the United Methodist Church in Missouri at www.moumethodist.org.

The Missouri Annual Conference is united around a common purpose. We celebrate Unity in Christ and Diversity in the Kingdom.

MISSION
Making disciples of Jesus Christ for the transformation of the world.

VISION
The Missouri Conference will relentlessly lead our churches to become outwardly focused and spiritually centered Christ followers.

RACE AND CULTURE PURPOSE STATEMENT (Read more at www.moumethodist.org/race-and-culture)
The Missouri Conference desires to be a brave, empowering place for people from diverse cultures and generations so that we can become a Church for all God’s people. We seek to do this by equipping Conference leaders and local churches for multicultural leadership, cross-cultural connection and healing justice.

CORE PRACTICES
Congregational Excellence
Leadership Excellence
Missional Justice

EXPECTATIONS
The Missouri Conference has six expectations of all clergy and lay members: Christ-centeredness, accountability, collaboration, excellence, diversity and fruitfulness. These values are central to our pursuit of Unity in Christ through Diversity in the Kingdom.

Name: Karura Community Chapel
Description:
Vision
“Becoming Christ-like disciples engaging the world”

Mission Statement

“To be a worshiping community, helping one another to be obedient to God’s Word and to be Ambassadors of Jesus Christ in a needy world”

Core Values

The Core Values of the Church are expressed in the following statement of the Church DNA :

Commitment to the Great Command to love God and to love our neighbors (Luke 10:25-37)
Commitment to Biblical transformation (Rom. 12:1,2; Col. 1:28,29)
Commitment to empowering leadership (Ephesians. 4:11,12) (open, unthreatened, team oriented, unity seeking, willing to pass on to others)
Commitment to Contextual Relevance (1 Cor. 9:19 to 23)
Commitment to Church Planting (Matt. 28:19,20)

Name: St. Laurence Catholic Church
Description:
St. Laurence Catholic Church, Sugar Land, Texas is a vibrant and strong community of 6,300 registered families celebrating our 25th year since its establishment in the Archdiocese of Galveston-Houston.

"We the people of St. Laurence Catholic Church are centered in the love of Christ, and live to glorify God in all that we do."

Name: First Southern Baptist Church
Description: We desire to see Jesus Christ radically transform our neighborhoods, our city and our community.

Name: St. Gregory the Great Parish & School
Description: St. Gregory the Great Parish is a Roman Catholic Church in Buffalo, NY. Formed in 1958, St. Gregory's is a part of the Diocese of Buffalo. St. Gregory's School features early childhood education from Pre-K up to Grade 8.

Name: St. Luke's Anglican Church & Community Centre
Description: St. Luke’s is your community hub in North-West Oakville. Our church is part of the Anglican Diocese of Niagara and has been operating since 1845, offering inspiring worship and spiritual nourishment to the Oakville Community. Our Community Centre has been offering programs and events for the whole community since 2016. St. Luke’s is proud to partner with community groups, not-for-profits, government, and businesses to provide programs, services, and events that meet needs and interests identified by YOU. People of all ages, ethnicities, faiths, sexual orientations, gender expressions, and abilities will find that they belong at St. Luke’s.

Name: Mosaic Middle East
Description:
Mosaic Middle East is a UK-based charity bringing hope, help and healing to vulnerable and persecuted minorities. As a Christian charity, we believe that God cares for the people of Iraq. The Charity provides medical care and humanitarian relief as well as promoting reconciliation amongst different religious groups.

Please consider making a regular contribution to support our important humanitarian relief, medical, educational and reconciliation work.  https://mosaicmiddleeast.org/how-to-help/give

Mosaic Middle East is registered as a charitable company in the United Kingdom and Wales No: 1133576 and your donations are eligible for GiftAid.  For US citizens tax deductible contributions can be made through American FRRME, a 501(c)3 corporation.

VISION: Our work promotes hope and forgiveness, to counter prevailing attitudes of bitterness and retaliation, and break the cycle of hate begetting hate. We're guided by these core principles:

- Religion and politics are intimately linked in the Middle East, so a religious track is essential to a balanced peace process

- Any peace talks need to be jointly owned by each of the major faith groups within the region

- To be fully effective, the religious peace process should engage both the senior leadership and the grassroots of a community

- Conflict causes destruction and poverty, which can breed resentment and create further division. As well as treating victims of violent conflict, our humanitarian relief work helps to establish the conditions necessary for lasting reconciliation. 

Visit our website at www.frrme.org for more information and to sign-up and subscribe for new blog posts, prayer updates and e-newsletters from the FRRME Charity.

Name: St. Bartholomew Church and School
Description: St. Barthlomew is an outstanding Parish and School on the Northwest side of Chicago at Cicero and Addison.

Name: Dallas Baptist Association
Description: Dallas Baptist Association churches worship, serve, share the Gospel and make disciples in almost every neighborhood of Dallas and Rockwall Counties--from the urban core to outlying suburbs. Our churches are found in homes, parks, storefronts, movie theaters, and traditional church facilities. The congregations range in size from fewer than a dozen members to several thousand. The ethnic diversity of the Dallas area is reflected in our churches. Although most DBA congregations worship in English, on any given Sunday worship and Bible study are conducted in more than 30 languages and dialects. Dallas Baptist Association churches believe that we can share the Gospel of Jesus Christ better, in both word and deed, by working together.

Name: Cross City Church
Description: Cross City Church is a baptist church located in the Mid-Cities area between Dallas and Fort Worth our goal is to develop fully devoted followers of Christ.

Name: Gereformeerde Bond in de Protestantse Kerk in Nederland
Description: De Gereformeerde Bond in de Protestantse Kerk zet zich in voor de vorming en toerusting van gemeenteleden, kerkenraadsleden, predikanten en studenten, in gehoorzaamheid aan de Bijbel en gebonden aan de belijdenis van de Reformatie. Dit doen we onder meer door onze wekelijkse uitgave van ons weekblad De Waarheidsvriend. Daarnaast willen wij studenten ondersteunen en present zijn op universiteiten, zodat zij juist daar geestelijk worden begeleid en bemoedigd. Ook dragen wij zorg voor kwetsbare kerkelijke gemeenten in ons land, door hen bij te staan waar dat nodig is en hen te helpen hun roeping als gemeenschap van Christus waar te maken.

Name: United Faith Christian Academy
Description:
For 35 years, United Faith Christian Academy has offered a Christ-centered community and balanced approach to 
academics, athletics, fine arts, service and leadership. 
As a non-denominational private Christian school, United Faith is college-prep accredited and serves Pre-K through 12th grade.  
Our students are inspired to discover their God-given talents and thrive academically, spiritually, and socially with an average 10:1 student-to-teacher ratio.   
Why you belong at United Faith:
100% college acceptance
65% athletics participation -  middle & high school
72% fine arts participation - middle & high school 
$1.5M+ offered in scholarships annually
Only school in region to offer JROTC of Christian Schools - Navy
Leadership & Community Service built into curriculum
Integrated biblical worldview
Charlotte Business Journal ranked Top 10 private school 2020
Affordable solution for South Charlotte families

Name: New Life Covenant Ministries
Description:
We truly believe that Jesus provided the best example for each of the unique challenges we face in life. We commit as a family to model His lifestyle through believing the Bible, seeking Him in prayer, and honoring Him through worship.

New Life Covenant Church believes in the importance of genuine friendships. We commit, as a community of believers to a lifestyle of encouraging, challenging, and discipling each other.

At New Life Covenant Church, we believe that every individual plays an important role in the body of Christ. We commit to creating opportunities for every individual to get involved in the mission of the church through service, outreach, and giving.

Name: Elim Fellowship
Description: Elim Fellowship, is a world-wide fellowship of ministers and international workers.  The office of International Ministries oversees Elim's career International workers located throughout the world, and promotes short-term missions through the Short-Term Enlistment Program.  EFIM Member Care Facilitators are volunteers who work to sustain and nurture international workers from the beginning through the end of their service.

Name: St. Thomas Aquinas Catholic Church
Description:
Welcoming everyone to proclaim and live the joy of the Gospel.


A Roman Catholic Church of the Archdiocese of Atlanta.

Name: Chabad at Indiana University
Description: Welcome to Chabad of Bloomington and Indiana University – Your Hub for Jewish Life on Campus and Beyond!  Join our vibrant community of current students and esteemed alumni as we connect, celebrate, and grow together. Explore our profile, network with fellow students and graduates, and stay up-to-date with our exciting events and programs. Whether you're reminiscing about your time on campus or seeking to stay connected, we're here for you every step of the way. Reach out to us anytime – we can't wait to meet and connect with you!

Name: Transform Minnesota
Description:
Transform Minnesota is a network of churches and ministries, we exist to connect Christian leaders, develop Biblical solutions and equip churches that transform communities.

Connect

As a broad and diverse network of churches, ministries and individuals, Transform Minnesota facilitates connections between evangelicals to exchange ideas and ministry resources. Representing nearly 1/3 of the state’s Christians; from the small church in rural Minnesota to the large suburban congregation in the Twin Cities, Transform Minnesota acts as a convener to connect and equip the Church.

Through connection and collaboration, Transform Minnesota helps evangelical churches and leaders across many denominations to advance the Gospel of Jesus in their communities. We bring together church leaders, ministries and individuals across denominations and cultures in order to provide a stronger, more diverse—and unified—voice on pivotal cultural, religious and social issues, including a biblical view of marriage, religious liberty, immigration reform, reconciliation efforts and legislative policy.

Equip

Centered on Biblical beliefs, Transform Minnesota provides a neutral, shared platform where evangelicals can wrestle with critical and important issues facing our community, and collaborate to develop and apply Biblical solutions.

By delivering high-quality conferences, publications, trainings and seminars, Transform Minnesota provides practical resources that help equip the Church to transform the state. Building partnerships, pooling resources and exchanging ideas strengthen all of us. When we do this effectively and strategically, we are more successful in our mission to glorify Christ and see His Kingdom expand in Minnesota and beyond.

Transform Minnesota provides these resources and opportunities through our five initiatives:
1) Coming Together 
2) Holy Sexuality 
3) Ministry Equip 
4) Church Impact
5) Muslim Outreach

Name: Uniao Nordeste Brasileira Da Igreja Adventista Do S Dia
The Province of St. Mary of the Capuchin Order
Description: The Province of St. Mary promotes the equality of all people within our fraternity, in the churches dedicated to the gospel of Christ, and in the larger world. Capuchin friars minister in a wide variety of settings. Our service to people of a variety of cultures in North America and around the globe have allowed us to witness to God at work in countless ways.

Name: Congregation Neveh Shalom
Description:
Congregation Neveh Shalom is a vibrant and energetic egalitarian Conservative congregation dedicated to providing a home for traditional observance, study, and gathering for Portland´s Jewish population. For 150 years, we have worked together to build a community of connection, a house for people of all backgrounds, orientations, genders, and viewpoints. 

***Our Vision***
A more humane world cultivated through Jewish living, learning, and celebration.

***Our Mission***
To foster and preserve an inclusive, authentic, and engaging experience of Conservative Judaism in the Pacific Northwest.

Through meaningful lifecycle events, spiritual, educational, and social experiences, we become our best selves, strengthen connections to each other, and forge positive change in the world.

***Our Values***
Community – Kehillah
Acting with courage, honesty, and integrity, we share meaningful moments where new relationships are forged, and old ones live on that shape our lives with connections to each other.

Innovation – Chidush
Bringing the best of our creative Jewish wisdom, we encourage and develop new and innovative solutions to close the gap in our vision between what is and what can be.

Jewish Life – L’Dor V’Dor
Embracing tradition and change, we engage in Jewish ritual, celebration and prayer, becoming an enduring family within our synagogue, keeping a vibrant relationship with Israel and the greater Jewish world, now and for generations to come.

Inclusivity – K’lal
With acknowledgment of the divine spark in each of us, we create a culture of belonging where people of all diverse backgrounds and identities—such as religious observance, race, ethnicity, ability, gender, or sexuality—are all embraced.

Transformative Learning – Talmud Torah
We strive to deepen our understanding of Judaism on the lifelong path to becoming our best selves and creating a more humane world.

Name: Emmanuel Episcopal Church
Description: We are a congregation of The Episcopal Church serving La Grange and the surrounding area.

Name: The Experience Community
Description: We are a group of Christ-Followers that are focused on living lives as closely as we can to Jesus. We are a non-denominational, completely self-sufficient community that teaches Biblical principles of grace, mercy, love, and community service. If you are intimidated or uncomfortable in traditional churches and desire genuine worship and community, this might be a great chance for you to experience a true and authentic encounter with God.

Name: Assumption Church Syracuse
Description:
This magnificent church, steeped in the history of Syracuse, is a place where all are welcome to make their home, hear the positive message of God's love, and in turn spread that love, in service, to others.

Assumption Church and its outreach ministries, brings the Gospel message of peace, reconciliation and love to our neighbors on Syracuse’s Northside. Assumption Church is home to a parish dating back to 1845.

Today this community draws members from all over CNY who worship and form community in the inclusive, down-to-earth spirit of Saints Francis and Clare of Assisi.

Be sure to follow us on Instagram, Facebook and Twitter!

Name: Ward Church
Description:
A community of Christ followers who seek to love God passionately, serve others joyfully, and intentionally share our faith with people across the street and around the world.

Ward Church was founded more than 60 years ago, and has roots as far back as 1842. Ward has always been about an authentic community that loves God passionately, serves others joyfully, and shares faith with people across the street and around the world.

Name: Buncombe Street Methodist Church (Greenville, SC)
Description:
Since 1834 Buncombe Street Methodist Church has been a church on a mission to share God’s love and power. The center of our life is Worship, which feeds a steadfast commitment to faith formation for every age and a need to be in mission both around the corner and around the world.

Thank you for visiting us! We invite you to join us on our journey of Christian Discipleship. Our hope is that the information on this site will allow you to catch a glimpse of who we are, and we would love to greet you personally next Sunday.

Name: Faithtree Resources
Description:
We create Orthodox Christian resources that help families and parishes learn, live, and share the Faith. From parenting guides and teen mental health curriculum to Vacation Bible School programs and prayer tools, our mission is to equip the Church with trusted, Christ-centered resources for real life.

 Learn more and check out our resources at https://www.faithtree.org/.

Name: First Presbyterian Church of Tulsa
Description:
WORSHIP Elevator access from the south tower entrance on Boston Avenue, and from the Atrium via north and south entrances.

Mission Statement
We believe God acted decisively in Jesus Christ to bring healing and wholeness to all. We believe God’s Spirit is at work today, changing minds and hearts and transforming human lives for the glory of God.

The First Presbyterian Church of Tulsa, born by grace in 1885, exists to introduce Jesus Christ as a way of life, that many may come to “taste and see that the Lord is good.” (Psalm 34:8)

Name: Christians in Parliament APPG
Description:
Christians in Parliament is an All-Party Parliamentary Group which exists to support all Members and staff in their work in the Houses of Parliament. We do this through Bible study and prayer groups, one-to-one pastoral contact, chapel services, speaker events, political theology discussions and the annual National Parliamentary Prayer Breakfast.

Disclaimer: This is not an official social media page of the House of Commons or the House of Lords. It has not been approved by either House or its committees. All-Party Parliamentary Groups are informal groups of Members of both Houses with a common interest in particular issues. The views expressed in these webpages are those of the group.
